subject: Add hover Copy button on assistant messages
body:
PR18 of the UI audit. Assistant messages frequently end up pasted into
notes, IDEs, or other chats; without a Copy affordance the user has to
manually select the bubble and Cmd+C, which is awkward once the text
includes long code fences.

- `MessageCopyButton` lives inside each assistant `.maka-bubble-
  with-actions` and yanks the raw markdown source (not the rendered
  HTML) to `navigator.clipboard`. Falls back silently if the clipboard
  isn't available so it never crashes the bubble.
- Copy → Check icon swap for 1.4 s after a successful copy, with the
  border + tint switching to the existing success-text token so the
  feedback is unmistakable but transient.
- Visibility uses opacity transitions guarded by the bubble's `:hover`
  / `:focus-within` and the button's own `:focus-visible`, so keyboard
  users can reach it without the mouse and visual noise stays low.
- User messages intentionally don't get the button — the user wrote
  them already.

subject: Consolidate 2026-05-21 feature patch updates
body:
Compacted commits:
- Add syntax highlighting for fenced code blocks
- Wrap App in a React ErrorBoundary with a recoverable fallback
- Add a discoverable keyboard shortcuts cheat sheet

subject: Add end-to-end session CRUD: pin / archive / rename / delete
body:
PR23 of the UI audit. Until now the sidebar could only open sessions —
there was no way to pin a chat to the top, archive an older thread,
rename a session whose auto-generated name was wrong, or get rid of
something you didn't want any more. The Pinned and Archived filters
existed but you couldn't toggle the underlying state.

Storage
- `SessionStore` gains `unarchive`, `setFlagged`, `rename` (with empty
  rejection + 80-char cap) alongside the existing `archive` / `remove`.
- `unarchive` clears both `isArchived` and `archivedAt` via the same
  atomic-write path used elsewhere, so a session can round-trip
  through the archive without leaving stale timestamps.
- New `packages/storage/src/__tests__/session-store.test.ts` covers all
  four behaviors against a real tmp workspace (4 node:test cases,
  brings storage workspace to 8 passing tests).

Runtime
- `SessionManager` mirrors the new store surface with `unarchive`,
  `setFlagged`, `renameSession`, updating any active backend's cached
  header so a renamed/pinned active session reads back correctly.

Main process
- New IPC channels: `sessions:archive`, `sessions:unarchive`,
  `sessions:setFlagged`, `sessions:rename`, `sessions:remove`.
- Preload + `global.d.ts` expose them on `window.maka.sessions`.

UI
- `SessionListPanel` now splits each row into a primary `<button>`
  (open) and a slide-in `.maka-list-row-actions` cluster with four
  Lucide icon buttons: Pin / Unpin (PinOff), Rename (Pencil), Archive
  / Unarchive (ArchiveRestore), Delete (Trash2, destructive tint).
  Actions are revealed on hover or `:focus-within`, never lay on top
  of the open button, and each carries a proper `aria-label` + tooltip.
- Rename uses `window.prompt` for now (acceptable in Electron and
  matches the no-extra-dialog scope); inline edit is a follow-up.
- Delete is gated by `window.confirm` so an errant click can't
  silently nuke a thread.
- A small gradient fade on the action strip makes the transition from
  primary content to actions readable without an extra border.
- `A…

subject: Add in-app toast notifications + Promise-based confirm dialog
body:
PR24 of the UI audit. The renderer was still relying on native
`window.alert` / `window.confirm` / `window.prompt` for any non-modal
feedback. Those block the renderer event loop, can't be themed, and
behave inconsistently across macOS versions and IME states. PR24
replaces them with a proper in-app primitive.

@maka/ui
- New `toast.tsx` exposes a `<ToastProvider>` + `useToast()` API:
  - `toast({title, description, variant, duration, action})` returns
    an id; `success/error/info/warning` are friendly shorthands.
  - `confirm({title, description, confirmLabel, cancelLabel,
    destructive})` returns a `Promise<boolean>` that resolves on the
    user's accept/cancel click. Escape closes via the existing
    `useModalA11y` hook (resolves to false); Enter accepts.
  - Internal `<ToastViewport>` renders the stack bottom-right with
    `@starting-style` for slide+fade, variant-coded left borders,
    optional action button, and a dismiss X.
  - `<ConfirmDialog>` renders an `alertdialog`-role modal that shares
    the existing `.maka-modal` recipe.
- Exported from `packages/ui/src/index.ts` for renderer consumers.

apps/desktop
- App wraps `<AppShell>` in `<ToastProvider>` so any descendant can
  call `useToast()`. The shell pulls the API once.
- PR23's session delete flow now calls `toastApi.confirm({...,
  destructive: true})` and surfaces a success toast after removal,
  replacing the previous `window.confirm`. SessionRow becomes
  presentation-only — confirmation lives at the App layer.

All workspace tests green (core 37 / storage 8 / runtime 52 / desktop
5 = 102) and full build + typecheck pass.

subject: Inline-edit session rename instead of opening a native prompt
body:
PR25 of the UI audit. PR23 used `window.prompt` for renaming, which is
the same native-dialog smell the toast system fixed. PR25 swaps it for
an inline editor.

- Each `SessionRow` keeps a local `editing` boolean. Entering edit mode
  swaps the name display for an `<input>` autofocused and pre-selected
  (so the user can either tweak or overwrite without an extra Cmd+A).
- Edit triggers: the existing Pencil action button OR double-clicking
  the row body. Both feel discoverable; the action button stays the
  primary affordance because it's keyboard-reachable, while
  double-click matches Finder/Sidebar conventions for free-text
  renaming.
- Commit: form submit (Enter) or blur. Empty / unchanged values cancel
  silently. Escape cancels without committing. An IME-composition
  guard prevents Enter from committing while a CJK candidate is still
  open.
- While editing, the row's hover actions hide so the input has the
  full width; a faint accent underline + accent border make the edit
  state unmistakable.
- The store-side rename validator (added in PR23) still trims and caps
  the name on persist, so even a hostile pasted input stays bounded.

subject: Wire arrow-key navigation and focus ring through the session list
body:
PR26 of the UI audit. The session list was Tab-reachable (browser
default), but every row was its own tab stop, so getting to the 5th
chat meant ~6 Tabs past the filter nav. Now arrow keys move focus
inside the list the way Mail / Slack / Telegram do.

- New `handleListKeyDown` on `.maka-list-stack` intercepts ArrowUp /
  ArrowDown / Home / End, finds the current `.maka-list-row-main`
  index in the focusable list, and moves focus to the right neighbor
  with `scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest' })` so the focused row never
  ends up off-screen.
- Enter on a focused row still triggers the native button click — no
  custom handler needed.
- New focus-visible style: the row gets a soft accent border + 2 px
  ring via `:has(.maka-list-row-main:focus-visible)` so keyboard
  navigation is visually obvious without poisoning mouse focus states.
- The keyboard nav is already documented in the cheat sheet (PR22
  "会话列表" section), so users hitting `?` discover it the same way
  they discover the panel resize keys.

subject: Surface prompt suggestions on the empty chat hero
body:
PR27 of the UI audit. The empty chat said only "What should we work
on?" — accurate but unhelpful. New users had no idea what Maka could
actually do, and existing users had to retype the same template
opener every time they started a new session.

- Six tappable prompt chips for the recurring use cases: 总结代码库 ·
  解释这段代码 · 规划一个新功能 · 调试一个 bug · 写单元测试 ·
  Code review. Each chip carries a label + a one-line preview of the
  full prompt body.
- Composer becomes a `forwardRef<ComposerHandle>` with a tiny
  imperative API: `setText(text)` replaces the buffer, autosizes the
  textarea, focuses, and moves the caret to the end so the user can
  keep typing without an extra click. `focus()` is exposed for future
  callers (e.g. tools/skills that want to nudge the user back to
  input).
- ChatView accepts an optional `onPromptSuggestion` callback; the App
  routes it to `composerRef.current?.setText(prompt)`. Chips show on
  both the no-session-selected hero and the in-session empty state
  (e.g. brand-new chat with no messages yet).
- New `.maka-prompt-suggestions` grid + `.maka-prompt-chip` recipe in
  styles.css. auto-fit minmax(220px) so the grid collapses to one
  column on narrow chat widths.

subject: Show real connection and model on the chat tab
body:
PR28 of the UI audit. The chat tab previously showed `fake` or `ai-sdk`
in the subtitle — internal backend kind, not what the user picked. Now
it shows the human-readable provider + model.

- `ChatTab` swaps the `backend` prop for `{ subtitle, subtitleHint }`.
  The hint becomes the native `title` so a hover reveals
  `<session name> · <connection> · <model>` without inventing a
  tooltip primitive.
- `ChatView` accepts `activeConnectionLabel` + `activeModelLabel`.
  Either or both can be undefined (fake backend); the tab gracefully
  drops the subtitle when both are missing.
- `App` resolves the active connection from the loaded connections
  list and computes the display labels. SessionSummary doesn't carry
  the resolved model (it lives on the SessionHeader), so we fall back
  to `connection.defaultModel` for the display — accurate for the
  common case where a session uses its connection's default.
- The `Ask mode` pill now carries a `title` describing the actual
  behavior ("Sensitive tool calls require explicit allow/deny before
  running"), making the static label informative on hover instead of
  decorative.

subject: Make the permission dialog scannable per-tool with risk tone
body:
PR30 of the UI audit. The permission prompt was a generic dialog with `
{JSON.stringify(args)}
` for every tool. Bash commands, file writes, and arbitrary tool calls all rendered as a blob of JSON that the user had to mentally parse before deciding allow/deny — even though there's enough structure on the event to render a real summary. - New `REASON_PRESETS` lookup maps each `PermissionRequestEvent.reason` to a Lucide icon, human-readable headline, and tone (info / caution / destructive). The header swaps the raw reason enum (e.g. `fs_destructive`) for the friendly label and lights up the icon / border in the matching tone. - `renderPermissionSummary` produces a per-tool summary block at the top of the body: - `Bash`: "即将运行 shell 命令：" + the command in a framed code block + optional timeout. - `Write`: target path as a pill + byte/line count + a truncated content preview. - `Edit`: target path + side-by-side `删除 / 写入` diff blocks tinted via the existing destructive / success tokens. - Unknown tools fall back to the existing JSON view. - The full raw args block lives in a `
` titled `查看完整参数` so power users can still inspect the underlying payload without drowning the typical case. - The Allow button switches to the destructive variant for `fs_destructive` / `git_destructive` / `privileged` reasons and the label changes to `我已确认，允许` so users can't muscle-memory their way through a dangerous operation. - Copy refresh: dialog labels become `需要确认权限 / 拒绝 / 允许 / 本轮对话内记住选择` to match the Chinese-leaning Settings surface, and the dialog now has role="alertdialog" so AT announces it more forcefully than a regular dialog.

subject: Add a ⌘K command palette across sessions, settings, and theme
body:
PR31 of the UI audit. Maka was missing the one shortcut every modern
chat / IDE app has: ⌘K to jump anywhere. With six Settings sections,
three theme states, and a growing session list, the user had to
mouse to the sidebar for everything. The palette gives them a single
keyboard entry point.

- New `apps/desktop/src/renderer/command-palette.tsx` with three
  pieces:
  - `useCommandPalette()` — owns the global ⌘K / Ctrl+K listener +
    open state.
  - `buildCommandList(args)` — pure function that composes the
    available commands from the App's current state: New chat, Open
    Settings, Open keyboard help, three theme toggles, and every
    non-archived session as an "open" entry. Each command carries an
    icon, group label, optional hint, and a `keywords` array for
    cross-lingual search.
  - `CommandPalette` — modal with focus-trap (via the existing
    `useModalA11y`), a search input that highlights via fuzzy
    subsequence match, Arrow / Home / End / Enter / Esc keyboard
    navigation, and a footer cheat sheet.
- App wires the palette: state from `useCommandPalette`, command list
  rebuilt each render from the live session/theme state. Theme switch
  goes through the existing `setThemePref` so PR10's persistence is
  preserved. Sessions are scoped to the currently filtered view so
  Archived sessions stay out of "jump to" unless the user is already
  in the Archived filter.
- Cheat sheet (PR22) will be extended in a follow-up to mention ⌘K
  too; ⌘K is also discoverable from the palette itself via the
  Settings → keyboard help command.
- Renderer bundle: 615 KB -> 626 KB (gzip 189 -> 192 KB), all
  application code, no new heavy dependencies.

subject: Real session search box (PR32) and shortcut cheat sheet update for ⌘K
body:
The session list had a `.maka-session-search` div marked `aria-hidden`
with placeholder text — it looked interactive but did nothing. The
keyboard cheat sheet also still missed the new ⌘K palette from PR31.

PR32 fills the mock in:
- `SessionListPanel` now owns a `searchQuery` state and filters its
  incoming `sessions` prop on case-insensitive substring against
  `session.name`. Empty query shows everything.
- The search box becomes a real `<input type="search">` with our own
  clear (`×`) button (we hide the WebKit native cancel UI). Esc empties
  the field when there is a query; the field auto-focuses via ⌘F /
  Ctrl+F so users muscle-memory their way in. Filter is scoped to the
  Sessions section, so ⌘F doesn't fight other future surfaces.
- A new "没有匹配的会话" empty state replaces the regular list when the
  filter returns zero rows, with a hint to press Esc to clear.
- `.maka-session-search:focus-within` lights up the border via the
  accent token; the clear button reuses the existing icon-button
  recipe.

Cheat sheet (PR22): adds a `⌘K` entry to the 通用 section so the new
palette is discoverable from the same `?` overlay.

subject: Wire the chat header permission-mode switcher to the new backend API
body:
PR29 of the UI audit. Hooks the renderer up to @xuan's
`sessions:setPermissionMode` work so the previously decorative `Ask
mode` pill becomes a real Explore / Ask / Execute switcher.

@maka/ui
- New `PermissionModeSwitcher` component: a 3-up segmented control
  (`Explore` / `Ask` / `Execute`) styled like a radio group on a pill
  background. Each option carries an explanatory `title` so hover
  reveals the policy boundary in plain language. The active option
  picks up a tone-coded foreground (info / accent / caution) backed by
  existing tokens so dark mode stays correct.
- ChatView's static `Ask mode` pill is replaced by the switcher, fed
  the live `activeSession.permissionMode`. While `streamingText` is
  active the switcher disables itself and surfaces the reason via a
  `title` tooltip (matches the runtime's "reject mid-turn" behavior).
- Pre-session header still shows a disabled `Ask` switcher so the
  layout stays consistent before the first message lands.

apps/desktop
- App owns `setPermissionMode(mode)`: calls the new IPC, patches the
  session in-place from the returned `SessionSummary` so the chat
  header updates instantly, and surfaces a success toast carrying the
  plain-language description of the new policy boundary. Errors from
  the runtime (e.g. trying to switch mid-stream) become an error toast
  with the underlying message so users learn why it was blocked
  instead of silently failing.

Validation
- Tests: 110 passing (core 37 / storage 9 / runtime 59 / desktop 5).
- Build + typecheck green across all workspaces.

subject: Guard mode switching across overlapping turns
Use real proxy tests for Settings network toast
Consolidate 2026-05-21 same-day local updates
body:
Compacted commits:
- Force timeout stuck proxy connection tests
- Test current Settings proxy form values

subject: P0: hide system_note noise from chat, surface fake-backend banner, swap font + grain
body:
The screenshot @WAWQAQ shared shows the actual problems: chat is
flooded with `SYSTEM session_resume` / `SYSTEM mode_change` blocks
between every real message, and the user can't tell why "real" replies
look like `Fake backend received: …`. PR35 lands the focused fixes.

UI clarity (P0)
- `materializeChat` no longer emits a `system` ChatItem for every
  `system_note`. The audit JSONL still records every kind for replay;
  the chat surface only renders kinds in `VISIBLE_SYSTEM_NOTES`
  (empty for now). `mode_change` / `session_resume` / `connection_locked`
  stop polluting the conversation.
- ChatView shows a yellow info banner above the message stream
  whenever the active session is on `backend: 'fake'`, telling the
  user exactly what to do — open Settings · 模型 and add an API key
  for a real provider. Without this, the assistant's
  `Fake backend received: …` echo reads like a broken product.

Premium feel (taste-skill + redesign-skill upgrades)
- Bundled `@fontsource-variable/geist` + `@fontsource-variable/geist-mono`
  and pointed `--font-sans` / `--font-mono` at them. system-ui stays as
  a fallback for CJK + first-paint, but the primary face now has the
  characterful weights both skills recommend (the taste-skill
  explicitly bans Inter and the generic system font stack for premium
  surfaces).
- Added a fixed `body::after` SVG noise overlay at 4-6% opacity with
  `mix-blend-mode: overlay`, pointer-events: none, and reduced-motion
  off. Per both skills this is the cheap atmospheric upgrade that
  breaks digital flatness without GPU cost.

Skills landed in workspace
- Installed `Leonxlnx/taste-skill`, `Leonxlnx/redesign-skill`, and
  `anthropics/skills/frontend-design` under `~/.../skills/` so future
  sessions can ground UI decisions in them.

subject: Consolidate 2026-05-21 copy and polish updates
body:
Compacted commits:
- Alma-inspired chat polish: distinct user bubble, half-block caret, code pill
- Ship UI density modes (compact / comfortable / spacious)

subject: Add first-run onboarding hero when no real connection is configured
body:
PR38 of the UI audit. With xuan's P0 fix (7ce8f30) blocking silent
fake fallback, a first-run user now sees an error toast the moment
they hit send — that's the right hard-fail behavior, but the empty
chat itself still showed the generic "What should we work on?" hero
with prompt suggestions. The user could spend a minute clicking
chips before learning Maka can't actually answer them. PR38 fixes
the first-run UX so the path forward is visible from the moment the
window opens.

@maka/ui
- `ChatView` gains an `emptyOverride` slot. When provided, it replaces
  the prompt-suggestion `EmptyChatHero` in both the no-session and
  empty-session states. The default behavior is unchanged for users
  who already have a connection wired.

apps/desktop
- New `OnboardingHero.tsx` renders the welcome surface: an eyebrow
  label, a left-aligned bold headline ("把一个真实的 LLM 接进来，
  再开始第一条对话"), explanatory body copy, and a responsive grid of
  six featured providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, GLM Coding Plan, Kimi,
  DeepSeek, Ollama) — each card shows the real provider brand SVG
  (extracted from ProvidersPanel via newly-exported `ProviderLogo` +
  `providerDisplay`) plus a one-line description. Tapping any card
  opens Settings · 模型. A secondary "先用 FakeBackend 走一遍流程" link
  refocuses the composer for the rare debug use case.
- App computes `needsOnboarding = !connections.some(c => c.enabled)`
  (matches @kenji's `needs_onboarding` state in the provider-readiness
  3-state contract) and passes the new hero in via `emptyOverride`.
- `.maka-onboarding-*` recipes in styles.css use the existing accent /
  border / background-elevated tokens so dark mode renders correctly.
- ProvidersPanel exports `ProviderLogo` and `providerDisplay` so the
  onboarding hero stays in sync with the catalog without duplicating
  the SVG marks.

subject: Show the real provider brand mark in the chat tab header
body:
PR39 of the UI audit. The chat tab still rendered the generic Lucide
MessageSquare on the left of every session, regardless of which model
was actually answering. Now connected sessions show the provider's
real brand mark (Anthropic / OpenAI / GLM / Kimi / DeepSeek /
Moonshot / Z.ai / Ollama) — same SVG used by the Settings provider
catalog and the new OnboardingHero (PR38), so the brand identity
threads coherently through the app.

@maka/ui
- `ChatView` accepts two new optional props: `activeProviderType` +
  `renderProviderMark(type) => ReactNode`. The desktop app supplies
  the renderer so @maka/ui stays unaware of the provider SVG library.
- `ChatTab` swaps its leading icon: provider mark when supplied,
  MessageSquare when not. Fake-backend / pre-session keep the
  MessageSquare so the visual still implies "this is a chat" rather
  than nothing.

apps/desktop
- App resolves the active session's `connection.providerType` and
  passes it + a `ProviderLogo` renderer into ChatView. The brand mark
  inherits the existing per-provider color tokens automatically.
- Small `.maka-chat-tab-provider` recipe in styles.css sizes the
  inline mark down to 18px square (vs. the 32px+ catalog tile) and
  drops the box shadow so it reads as part of the tab text run.

subject: Sidebar row polish: left accent bar, unread halo, micro-translate hover
body:
PR40 of the UI audit. Three small CSS-only changes that bring the
session sidebar to parity with mature chat apps (Slack, Linear,
Telegram) without any new IPC.

- Active row gets a 3px accent bar pinned to the left edge via
  `::before`, with a 12px top/bottom inset so it reads as a "rail"
  alongside the row rather than a full border. The row name also
  tints to `--accent` so the active selection is unmistakable.
- Unread dot grows to 8px and gains a `oklch(from --accent ... /
  0.15)` halo via box-shadow — same trick the running tool status
  dot uses in PR13, but smaller and static (sidebar items aren't a
  motion surface).
- Hovering a row translates the inner button 1px right (160ms
  ease-out-strong from the token system). Active rows hold at 0px so
  there's no jump when the user clicks. Hover also lifts the row
  name to `--foreground` for slightly stronger affordance.

All token-driven, no hardcoded colors. Test suite stays 115 green.

subject: Friendlier message labels and a top scroll scrim on the chat surface
body:
PR41 of the UI audit. Two small polish moves that compound with PR36 +
PR40 to give the chat a finished, branded feel.

- Message role labels swap the raw `USER` / `ASSISTANT` all-caps enum
  for friendlier copy: `你` (CJK "you") for user messages and `Maka`
  for the assistant. System messages keep their kind. The label also
  loses its uppercase / wide tracking treatment, picks up tone colors
  (accent for Maka, foreground-60 for the user, info-text for
  system), and bumps to 11px so the heading reads at the same weight
  as the message body without shouting. Combined with the brand SVG
  in the chat tab (PR39), the surface now consistently feels like
  "this is Maka, that's you" instead of an enum dump.
- A 24px scrim gradient sits at the top of `.maka-chat-shell` (from
  --background to transparent) so the chat header / mode switcher
  read as a distinct strata above scrolling content. The bottom edge
  stays uncovered so the composer keeps a clear line to the last
  message and the jump-to-bottom pill (PR17) still floats above it.

subject: Composer focus ring on the wrapper, not the textarea
body:
PR42 of the UI audit. The composer's focus state was the browser's
default textarea outline — a thin solid box that fights the rest of
the design system. The shell itself never showed it was focused, so
keyboard users with no caret visible (paste-in-progress, etc.) had no
visual cue that the composer was active.

- `.maka-composer-inner` gains an explicit 1px border + 14px radius so
  it reads as a single surface around both textarea and toolbar.
  Padding lifts from 10/12 to 12/14 for a slightly more generous feel.
- `:focus-within` on the wrapper switches the border to an accent
  tint and adds a 3px outer ring + shadow blend so the focus is
  unmistakable but not aggressive. Falls back gracefully without
  `:focus-within` in older browsers (Electron 39 supports it).
- The textarea's native `outline` is suppressed inside the composer
  since the wrapper now owns the focus visual.
- Toolbar `kbd` tags pick up a tinted foreground-3 background so
  shortcut labels read at a glance instead of blending into the
  toolbar text.

subject: Replace 个性化 'coming soon' card with a real personalization page
body:
PR43 of the UI audit. The 个性化 Settings page has been showing a
ComingSoon card since PR14. PR43 replaces it with a functional form
that ships two persisted preferences and threads the first one
through the chat surface.

Schema (@maka/core)
- `PersonalizationSettings { displayName: string; assistantTone: string }`
  added to AppSettings. `UpdateAppSettingsInput` /
  `createDefaultSettings` / `mergeSettings` / `normalizeSettings`
  carry the new branch.
- Index re-exports the new type so renderer code can import it.

Settings UI
- `PersonalizationSettingsPage` renders two fields:
  1. `显示名称` — single-line input bounded at 60 chars, shown to the
     user in the chat as the label on their messages. Empty falls
     back to the default 「你」.
  2. `助手语气偏好` — multiline textarea (500 char cap, resize-y)
     intended to be appended to the model's system prompt so the
     model knows how to address the user. Backend ingestion is
     xuan's surface; UI ships the storage now so the contract lands.
- Save button + success toast for both fields. Failure surfaces as
  an error toast with the underlying message.
- The 个性化 nav item drops its `comingSoon: true` flag; the matching
  entry in `COMING_SOON_PAGES` is removed so the section now routes
  to the real page.

Chat propagation
- `ChatView` gains an optional `userLabel` prop. `messageRoleLabel`
  uses the prop for user-role messages (trim + non-empty), falling
  back to "你". Assistant stays "Maka".
- App owns `userLabel` state, seeds it from `settings.get()` on
  mount, and exposes `onUserLabelChange` to SettingsModal. The modal
  calls it whenever `personalization.displayName` is part of the
  update patch, so the chat header relabels immediately without a
  reload.

subject: Surface lastMessagePreview in the session sidebar rows
body:
PR44 of the UI audit. @xuan's ad7f078 added `SessionSummary
.lastMessagePreview` derived in the storage layer (no renderer N+1
IPC, internal events skipped, attachments fallback to '附件',
grapheme-safe 96-char truncation). PR44 wires it into the sidebar.

- SessionRow's primary content wraps name + meta in a new
  `.maka-list-row-text` grid so the preview line sits between the
  name and the relative-time meta with consistent baseline alignment.
- `.maka-list-row-preview` clamps to a single line, ellipsis on
  overflow, foreground-60 default → foreground-70 when the row is
  active so the preview reads through the accent wash. Hover lift +
  unread halo (PR40) compose cleanly.
- Sessions with no preview (empty store-derived value) fall back to
  the meta-only display, so brand-new chats without any user message
  still render as before.

subject: Skills sidebar lists real ~/.../skills/ folders via skills:list IPC
body:
PR45 of the UI audit. The Skills sidebar entry has been a static
empty-state placeholder since PR9. PR45 makes it a real surface that
scans the workspace `skills/` directory and opens each folder in
Finder/Explorer on click.

Main (apps/desktop)
- New `listInstalledSkills(workspaceRoot)` + `parseSkillFrontMatter`
  helpers scan `{workspaceRoot}/skills/<dir>/SKILL.md`, extract `name`
  + `description` from the YAML front-matter (regex-light, no new dep),
  and return `{ id, name, description, path }[]`. Errors per skill
  fall through silently so one malformed folder can't blank the list.
- New IPC `skills:list` exposes the helper.
- New IPC `app:openPath` lets the renderer ask Electron's shell to
  reveal a path in the OS file manager. Path is whitelisted to live
  inside `workspaceRoot` so a compromised renderer can't probe the
  whole disk.

Preload + types
- `window.maka.skills.list()` and `window.maka.app.openPath(target)`
  wired through preload + `global.d.ts`.

UI
- New exported `SkillEntry` type on @maka/ui.
- `SessionListPanel` accepts `skills` + `onOpenSkillFolder` props.
  The skills section renders each entry as a `.maka-skill-row` row
  with the same name / preview / meta layout as session rows (PR40 +
  PR44 styling cascades cleanly). Click reveals the folder in Finder.
- Falls back to the original empty state if no skills are found.
- App reads `window.maka.skills.list()` on startup, routes the
  open-folder callback through `app.openPath`.

subject: Real 数据 settings page: workspace path + open-in-Finder + copy
body:
PR46 of the UI audit. 数据 was the last 'Coming Soon' settings page
that had concrete renderer-side work behind it; the others (每日回顾
/ 语音模型 / 开放网关 / 搜索服务) genuinely depend on V0.2 features.
PR46 ships the data page using the `app:info` + `app:openPath` IPCs
landed in PR45.

- New `DataSettingsPage` renders two rows (workspace path, storage
  engine) plus an action row with two buttons:
  - 在 Finder / 资源管理器中打开 — calls
    `window.maka.app.openPath(workspacePath)` which Electron whitelists
    to inside `workspaceRoot`. Result toast on success, error toast
    on failure.
  - 复制路径 — `navigator.clipboard.writeText` with a green toast.
- A subtle Settings notice card documents what V0.2 will add (.maka.zip
  export / schemaVersion-aware import) without lying about current
  capability.
- 数据 nav item drops its `comingSoon: true` flag; the `data` entry
  in `COMING_SOON_PAGES` is removed so the section now routes to the
  real implementation.

subject: Consolidate 2026-05-21 same-day local updates
body:
Compacted commits:
- Real 账号 settings page with live connection counts
- Group session sidebar by time buckets (今天 / 昨天 / 过去 7/30 天 / 更早)
- Reset chat scroll pin to bottom when switching sessions

subject: Account page lists each connection by name with honest status copy
body:
PR50 of the UI audit. PR47's account page summarized connections as
"X 已启用 / Y 总数" — accurate, but it gave no way to see *which*
connections are wired or distinguish "configured" from "verified".
PR50 expands the page with a per-connection list, following the
status taxonomy in @kenji's accounts-credentials-status contract.

- Each LlmConnection renders as a row showing:
  - Connection display name (left)
  - `<providerType> · <defaultModel>` subtitle
  - Status label on the right
- For now the right-hand status is `已启用，未验证` or `已禁用` —
  matching the contract's distinction between "configured" (saved
  but not tested) and "verified" (last test ok). The page footer
  notes that the full 5-state taxonomy (已配置 / 已验证 / 需要重新
  登录 / 错误) lights up when the backend adds `lastTestStatus`.
- Empty state when no connection is registered points the user
  toward 设置 · 模型 to add one.
- Stays aligned with @kenji's "UI shows status, never the secret"
  rule — no API key fingerprints, no key length, no base URL leakage.

subject: Surface SKILL.md declared/requested tools on Skills sidebar rows
body:
PR51 of the UI audit. The Skills sidebar now reads each SKILL.md's
`allowed-tools` front-matter (alongside `name` + `description`) and
shows it on the row. Per @kenji's skills-ingestion-contract, the UI
labels these as "declared / requested" — never granted — so users
understand PermissionEngine is still the only authority.

Main / preload / global.d.ts
- `parseSkillFrontMatter` now also handles `allowed-tools` in either
  inline (`[A, B, C]`) or YAML block-list (`- A`) form. Returns
  `allowedTools: string[]` (empty when not declared).
- `skills:list` IPC + preload + global type carry the new field.
  `declaredTools` is a non-empty array only when the skill explicitly
  declared one.

UI
- `SkillEntry` gets an optional `declaredTools: string[]` field with
  inline JSDoc reminding readers it's a request, not a grant.
- Skill row's meta line appends a small `requests` pill + the comma-
  separated tool list when declared. Hovering the row shows a fuller
  title attribute: `<path>\n\nRequests: <tools>\nPermissionEngine
  still applies — this is a declaration, not a grant.`
- New `.maka-skill-tools-label` recipe — info-tinted 9px uppercase
  pill, matches the Settings nav 'Soon' badge styling.

No change to the empty state — a fresh workspace still shows the
how-to copy directing the user to drop a SKILL.md folder.

subject: SessionListPanel keyboard lifecycle: Left/Right cycles filters, Delete confirms, Pinned floats
body:
PR52 of the UI audit. Implements three rules from @kenji's
session-list-lifecycle contract that PR40/PR48/PR49 left open.

- Left/Right inside the list cycles the active filter
  (`chats` → `flagged` → `archived` → `chats`). Skills section keeps
  its own logic and isn't affected. The cycle preserves keyboard
  focus inside the panel so a power user can scan the whole sidebar
  without the mouse.
- Delete / Backspace on a focused session row opens the existing
  toast.confirm() destructive dialog (via `rowActions.onDelete`)
  instead of triggering a silent delete. Lifecycle contract is
  explicit: never delete without confirmation.
- New `groupSessionsForFilter` floats pinned (flagged) sessions to a
  dedicated `已置顶` section at the top of the Chats filter, separate
  from the date buckets. Pinned + Archived filters keep their flat
  date-bucket layout because the filter itself is already the
  semantic grouping there.
- Added `data-session-id` on `.maka-list-row-main` so the keyboard
  handler can resolve the focused session id when Delete fires
  without re-querying React state.

subject: Inline relative timestamp on each chat message + hover absolute
body:
PR53 of the UI audit. Chat messages had no time information surfaced
anywhere — users couldn't tell whether a conversation paused for
five minutes or five hours. The StoredMessage carries a `ts` already;
PR53 plumbs it through `materializeChat` and renders a small
relative-time chip next to the role label.

- `ChatItem` adds `ts?: number`; materializeChat copies it from
  user / assistant / surfaced system_note messages.
- Each rendered `<article>` carries a `title` with the absolute date+
  time (`Intl.DateTimeFormat` with `dateStyle: medium`, `timeStyle:
  short`), so hover always reveals the precise time without burning
  pixels.
- Inline next to the role label: a small `.maka-message-time` chip in
  `--foreground-40` with tabular numerals, using
  `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` (numeric: 'auto', style: 'narrow') so it
  reads naturally in both English ("5m") and Chinese ("5 分钟前").
  Guarded fallback keeps the function safe in SSR / non-DOM test
  environments.
- Tests stay green across all workspaces.

subject: Persist sidebar nav selection and last Settings section across restarts
body:
PR54 of the UI audit. Two small but high-touch state items reset on
every restart: the sidebar filter (Chats / Pinned / Archived /
Skills) and the Settings modal section. Returning users had to
re-navigate every time. PR54 persists both to localStorage.

- App seeds `navSelection` from `readNavSelection()` and writes back
  to `maka-nav-selection-v1` on every change. The reader validates the
  parsed JSON against the discriminated-union shape, so a corrupted
  value falls back to `{ section: 'sessions', filter: 'chats' }`
  without throwing.
- SettingsSurface seeds its `section` state from
  `readLastSettingsSection()` and writes to `maka-settings-section-v1`
  on each change. The reader checks the value against the live
  `SETTINGS_NAV` ids, so a removed section (e.g. an old beta page
  in a future build) falls back to `'models'`.
- Both readers / writers are guarded with `try/catch` for environments
  where `localStorage` is unavailable (some Vite dev workers).

subject: Coming Soon Settings pages: 4-section product stance per V0.2 contracts
body:
Refactors the four Coming Soon pages (每日回顾 / 语音模型 / 开放网关 /
搜索服务) so they read as deliberate disabled-by-default product
stance rather than empty placeholders. Layout per @kenji's
suggestion: 当前状态 / 会包含什么 / 不会做什么 / 下一步需要配置什么.

Each page now surfaces the hard rules from the V0.2 contract notes
(notes/maka-*-contract.md), most importantly:

- daily-review: aggregates only Maka conversation JSONL + usage; does
  NOT screenshot, NOT keylog, NOT read other apps' data
- voice-models: mic permission deferred until user grants OS-level
  permission; no audio upload before UI discloses scope
- open-gateway: disabled-by-default, token-required (Authorization:
  Bearer), 127.0.0.1 only by default, CORS allow-list, scoped to
  model/health/aliases only (no settings/tools/files/skills/bots),
  no fake fallback, logs only status/latency/model/token-hash
- search: per-query opt-in, routes through configured network proxy,
  no query body persisted, privacy mode skips cache

Adds a badge slot on the hero (e.g. "V0.2 · disabled-by-default ·
token-required") and tone-tagged section cards (status/include/
exclude/config) with light token-derived backgrounds. The "不会做
什么" list uses a red ✗ marker so non-goals visually pop as hard
boundaries.

Typecheck + build green.

subject: Consume structured openPath result in renderer; surface skills-folder failures
body:
Builds on @xuan's 9c12f46 (Harden app open path allowlist) by tightening
the renderer-side contract for `window.maka.app.openPath`:

- Narrow the preload/global.d.ts return type from
  `{ ok: false; reason: string }` to the closed enum
  `unknown-key | not-allowed | missing | not-a-directory | open-failed`
  so missed callsites are caught by typecheck.
- Lift `openPathFailureCopy` out of SettingsModal into a new
  `renderer/open-path.ts` shared helper, plus an `openPathActionLabel`
  helper for the toast title ("无法打开工作区目录" vs "Skills 目录").
- Wire the sidebar "open skill folder" button (previously a silent
  `void window.maka.app.openPath('skills')`) through `openSkillsFolder`
  so missing-dir / open-failed results raise a toast instead of looking
  like the button did nothing.

Typecheck + build green; existing 20 desktop tests still pass.

subject: About Settings page: brand hero + 本地优先 privacy stance + copy-env button
body:
Replaces the row-only About page with a real product surface. Top-level
hero shows the Maka mark + version pill + "本地开发版" channel tag and a
short tagline. Beneath the hero, a 本地优先 / 隐私默认 card surfaces the
five privacy properties that already hold today:

- All sessions / settings / credentials / skills stay on this machine
- API keys are encrypted via Electron safeStorage
- No telemetry — Maka only talks to the provider you explicitly enable
- Permission policy classifies tool calls; high-risk ones require auth
- Per-session JSONL records messages / tool calls / permission decisions
  / mode changes locally and never leaves the machine

A new "复制环境信息" button writes a markdown-formatted summary
(version + Electron / Node / Chrome / OS / arch) to the clipboard so
bug reports include the right details without manual typing. Deliberately
excludes workspacePath (which can leak the OS username).

Typecheck + build green.

subject: Return personalization warning enums from settings update
Tool error display: inline red banner + copy-error button + expand-by-default
body:
When a tool call comes back with isError, materialize.ts sets the
ToolActivity status to 'errored'. The wrapper already paints a red
border via [data-status="errored"], but the body content was the same
generic OverlayPreview blob as a successful result — easy to mistake
for normal output.

This adds three layers so failures are unmistakable:

1. ToolErrorBanner — a red-tinted inline banner at the top of the body
   with an AlertOctagon icon, a "工具调用失败" heading, and the first
   240 chars of the failure text. Extracts the right field per
   ToolResultContent kind (text/json/terminal.stderr/file_diff/etc.).
2. Copy-error button — single-click clipboard write of the full error
   text with a transient "已复制" confirmation. Useful for pasting into
   bug reports or asking another agent for help.
3. Expand-by-default — isOpenByDefault now returns true for 'errored'
   so the user sees the failure without an extra disclosure click.
   Successful + interrupted calls still collapse by default.

Typecheck + ui/desktop build green.

subject: Personalization warning toast: single toast + generalized policy copy
body:
Refines the warning surface for `settings:update` per @kenji's guidance
in the personalization-prompt-contract.

- Collapse the two-toast pattern ("success" then "warning") into a
  single toast — either "个性化已保存" on a clean save, or
  "已保存并做安全清理" with the warning lines when sanitize fired.
- Generalize the per-enum copy so each line is a policy statement
  describing what was done + which invariant still holds. Never echo
  or name the matched phrase / keyword class:

  - override-attempt → "检测到可能尝试改变助手行为的内容，已按低优先级
    偏好处理；权限策略不受影响。" (no "system/developer/permission
    override" enumeration as before)
  - sensitive-pattern → "检测到疑似敏感凭据，已避免在提示或日志中
    回显原文。" (no "密钥/token/密码" enumeration)
  - control-chars → unchanged (was already in this form)

Typecheck + build green; full desktop test suite 27 passing.

subject: Defensive UI-level secret masking for tool error display (PR58 follow-up)
body:
Per @kenji's review of PR58: tool stderr and raw provider error text can
slip credential paths, bearer tokens, or API keys past main-side
redaction. The copy-error button in particular is dangerous since the
user might paste it into a bug report.

Adds `packages/ui/src/redact.ts` as a defensive UI-level mask applied
to ToolErrorBanner's display text *and* the clipboard write. Patterns
(in priority order):

1. `Authorization: Bearer/Basic/Token <token>` → mask token portion
2. URL query secrets — `?key=`, `?api_key=`, `?token=`, `?access_token=`,
   `?secret=`, `?signature=` etc. → mask value
3. HTTP header form `X-API-Key:` / `api-key:` (anchored to line start or
   whitespace so it doesn't double-eat the URL-query form above)
4. Provider key prefixes — OpenAI `sk-`, Anthropic `sk-ant-`,
   Google `AIza`, GitHub `ghp_/gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_`, Slack `xox[abprs]-`
5. Long high-entropy hex/base64 strings (40+ chars) as a catch-all

Backend (main) remains the authoritative redactor — settings, logs, and
persisted state are sanitized there per the contracts. This module is a
second layer specifically for runtime strings that surface in the UI.

Verified against 8 representative inputs (Bearer token, X-API-Key
header, `?api_key=` query, `sk-proj-` provider key, GitHub `ghp_`
token, long hex hash, plain "Connection refused"). All credential
patterns are masked; benign text passes through unchanged.

Typecheck + ui/desktop build green.

subject: Consolidate 2026-05-21 same-day local updates
body:

subject: Generalize outbound error messages before persistence
Add inline Test Connection button to AccountSettingsPage rows
body:
Each connection row now exposes a 测试连接 button that calls
`window.maka.connections.test(slug)` and surfaces the result via
toast — success shows latency + tested model, failure shows the
generalized errorMessage. After the test resolves, the page calls
`onRefresh()` (threaded through SettingsModal -> SettingsSurface ->
AccountSettingsPage) so the freshly-persisted `lastTestStatus`,
`lastTestAt`, and `lastTestMessage` re-render the row's derived
status without closing and reopening Settings.

While testing one connection the other rows' buttons are disabled
(canTest = testingSlug === null) to prevent racing IPC calls.
Disabled connections show the button disabled to make the action
order explicit: enable first, then test.

Pairs naturally with @xuan's 5ca1f8a (persistent test status) and
01b533f (generalized error helper) — UI test action now produces real
persisted state visible at-a-glance via PR61's tone-tagged badge.

Typecheck + build green; 40 desktop tests still pass.

subject: Command palette: jump to any Settings section directly via ⌘K
body:
Adds one palette command per SETTINGS_NAV entry (设置 · 通用 / 个性化 /
主题 / 每日回顾 / 模型 / 使用统计 / 语音模型 / 开放网关 / 机器人对话 /
搜索服务 / 网络 / 数据 / 账号 / 关于). Coming Soon pages are flagged with
a 即将推出 hint but still navigable — they're real targets, just without
final feature behaviour.

Wiring details:
- SETTINGS_NAV is exported from SettingsModal so the palette can read
  labels + icons from a single source of truth (no parallel list to
  drift).
- New SettingsModal prop `requestedSection?: SettingsSection`. When it
  changes (or is set on initial mount), SettingsSurface mirrors it into
  the internal `section` state via an effect, so an already-open Settings
  modal switches tabs without close/reopen.
- main.tsx tracks `settingsRequestedSection` separately from
  `settingsOpen`; `openSettingsSection(s)` writes localStorage (cold-open
  fallback per PR54) AND sets the prop (warm-switch).

Typecheck + build green; 40 desktop tests still pass.

subject: Generalize runtime error events and logs
Surface needs_reauth / error connection alert in chat header
body:
When the active session's connection has `lastTestStatus = needs_reauth`
or `lastTestStatus = error`, the chat header now shows a small clickable
pill that jumps straight to Settings · 账号 to fix it. Tone follows the
6-state ConnectionUiStatus tokens for continuity:

- needs_reauth → warning tone ("需要重新登录")
- error        → destructive tone ("上次连接失败")

Without this signal, a user could keep typing into a chat whose
provider has been silently rejecting calls and only discover it after
the next send fails. Now the lifecycle status from PR61's persistent
schema is visible at the moment they look at the chat.

Shape:
- New `ChatHeaderAlert` interface (`tone | label | onClick?`) exported
  from @maka/ui so callers can hand the chat header any actionable
  status pill without ChatView knowing the source.
- `ChatHeaderAlertBadge` renders the pill (button when clickable, span
  otherwise) with AlertTriangle + label.
- main.tsx derives the alert via `useMemo` from
  `activeConnection.lastTestStatus`; the onClick re-uses PR64's
  `openSettingsSection('account')` so warm-switch works.

Typecheck + ui/desktop build green; 40 desktop tests pass.

subject: PermissionDialog: localize reason labels + destructive emphasis note
body:
Two small polish passes on the permission surface so it reads
consistently with the rest of the Chinese-leaning UI:

1. REASON_PRESETS labels translated. The dialog title / buttons /
   summary lines were already Chinese, but the reason label sandwiched
   in the subtitle still read "Shell command (review carefully)",
   "Filesystem destructive (cannot undo)", etc. Now matches the rest:

   shell_dangerous    → 高风险 shell 命令 · 请仔细确认
   file_write         → 写入或创建文件
   fs_destructive     → 不可恢复的文件系统操作
   git_destructive    → 不可恢复的 Git 操作
   network            → 对外网络请求
   privileged         → 特权操作 (sudo / su)
   custom             → 自定义请求

2. Destructive-tone requests now show an extra emphasis note under the
   "记住本轮" checkbox: "这类操作不可恢复，确认前请再读一遍上面的参数。"
   Forces an extra beat before the user hits 我已确认，允许 without
   adding an extra click.

3. Tightened the "记住本轮" caption to spell out the lifecycle:
   "本轮对话内记住选择（同类型工具不再询问，关闭/切换对话后失效）".

subject: Localize remaining English titles + session-name fallback
body:
PR70 covered aria-labels but missed the `title=` tooltips on the
sidebar row actions (Rename / Archive / Unarchive / Delete) and the
two places `'New Chat'` slipped through as a session name fallback:

- ChatTab title when activeSession is undefined → "新建对话"
- main.tsx activeSessionForView placeholder → "新建对话"
- main.tsx createSession name fallback (text.slice || 'New Chat') →
  "新建对话"

  - Archive / Unarchive → "归档" / "取消归档"

Last meaningful English string in the user-visible UI as far as I can
see; smoke + product surface now reads as one language end-to-end.

subject: Lock down deriveConnectionUiStatus invariants with node:test
body:
The Account page renders one of six derived states per connection
(disabled / not_configured / configured / verified / needs_reauth /
error) via `apps/desktop/src/renderer/connection-status.ts`. Backend
landed `5ca1f8a` (persistent test status) + `01b533f` (generalized
error). UI test gate was missing — until now the helper was only
exercised by build typecheck.

Adds 10 node:test cases covering the invariants in @kenji's status
contract:

1. **Priority order, highest wins.**
   - disabled overrides every other signal (no "disabled + verified")
   - not_configured beats stale lastTestStatus when secret missing
   - not_configured beats stale lastTestStatus when defaultModel missing

2. **lastTestStatus mapping.** Each persistent enum value (verified /
   needs_reauth / error) maps to the matching display status; undefined
   falls back to configured.

3. **Ollama / authKind === 'none' path.** Skips the hasSecret gate
   (Ollama on localhost has no API key) but still requires a
   defaultModel — a no-secret local provider with no model picked
   should NOT render as ready.

4. **Failure-does-not-disable invariant.** A connection that just
   errored stays enabled with status = error. The regression we'd
   catch here is the UI ever writing enabled=false on its own.

Bumps desktop test count 41 → 51, all passing.

body:
Compacted commits:
- Settings nav: 5 grouped sections instead of flat 14-item list
- Tool result rendering: real diff coloring + terminal stdout/stderr split

subject: Message avatar slot + tool output redaction & truncation
body:
PR77 (message meta) + PR76 follow-ups @kenji flagged:

**Message meta**: replaces the bare role-label `<span>` with a
`MessageMeta` component that renders [avatar][role name][timestamp]
inline. Avatar is a small (22px) tinted circle with the initial
glyph — uses the first non-ASCII codepoint when available, so userLabel
"建文" → 建, "JK" → J, "🦊 fox" → 🦊. Assistant gets a fixed "M". Per
role data-attr the circle uses the matching token (chat-user-bg for
user, accent-tinted for assistant, info-tinted for system).

**Tool output redaction (@kenji PR76 follow-up #1)**: `OverlayPreview`
now runs `redactSecrets` over `text` / `json` payloads and the
`FileDiffPreview` body. `TerminalPreview` redacts cwd-adjacent `cmd`
string + stdout + stderr — `$ curl -H "X-API-Key: ..." …` lines and
provider dumps go through the masker before display. Pairs with the
existing copy-error redaction in ToolErrorBanner (PR58 / PR60); both
display and copy paths are now consistent.

**Tool output truncation (@kenji PR76 follow-up #2)**: introduces
`TOOL_LINE_CAP = 500` + `capLines()`. file_diff body, terminal
stdout, terminal stderr, and plain text all slice to 500 lines and
append a "… N more lines hidden" marker. Prevents a single
megabyte-scale `npm test` failure from creating 10k React elements
and drowning the chat surface.

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build + tests green.

subject: Return structured provider model discovery results
Settings nav: group-level status summaries
body:
Follows up @kenji's PR75 review ("group labels should also act as
navigation summaries, not just visual dividers"). Each non-trivial
group now renders one short live-state line below its label, tinted
by urgency:

  基础         (no summary — preference-only)
  AI           "尚未启用任何连接" (info)
              "N 个连接出错"     (destructive)
              "N 个需重登"       (warning)
              "未设默认模型"     (warning)
              "N 个连接可用"     (neutral)
  集成         "代理已开 / 直连 · N 个机器人"
  数据与账号    "N 个凭据需处理"   (warning/destructive)
              "凭据本地加密"     (neutral)
  其他         (no summary)

Copy stays under ~14 chars per @kenji's PR78 review: "不要让 nav 变成
第二个详情页". One sentence each, no enumerations.

`navGroupSummary()` computes from `connections` + `defaultSlug` +
`settings`; nav renderer threads them in. Tones reuse existing
info/warning/destructive tokens for visual continuity with the chat
header alert (PR65) and Account 6-state badges (PR61).

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build green.

subject: Thinking block: explicit "not the final answer" + dedicated copy + task list checkboxes
body:
Two follow-ups from @kenji PR83 review:

1. **Thinking block clarity**: the summary line now has two parts — the
   clickable "查看思考过程" + a small uppercase "模型推理草稿, 不是最
   终答案" note next to it. Makes it unambiguous that the contents are
   model working notes, not the response.
2. **Dedicated "复制思考过程" affordance**: thinking-block body now
   includes its own labelled MessageCopyButton so the user can pull
   the reasoning when they want to verify it, but the main message
   Copy button (above) continues to only carry `assistant.text` — no
   thinking ever leaks into a normal copy-answer flow.

MessageCopyButton gains an optional `label` prop. With a label it
becomes a labelled inline button (`data-labelled="true"` — static
position, always visible, 4×10 padding, label + icon row). Without a
label it stays the absolute-positioned floating copy button used on
each assistant bubble.

Bonus: **Task list checkboxes** in markdown. remark-gfm already
parses GFM task lists into `<input type="checkbox" disabled>`, but
we never styled them. Adds custom accent-tinted checkbox styling
inside `.maka-bubble-assistant li.task-list-item` so `- [x]` /
`- [ ]` lines read as proper checkboxes. Disabled by design — Maka
doesn't edit assistant markdown content from the renderer.

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build green.

subject: Provider model source: show "X 拉取" suffix from persisted modelsFetchedAt
body:
Backend `94b482b` persists `modelsFetchedAt` on the connection record.
This wires it into the model-source label so users can see how fresh
the live list is at a glance:

  实时拉取的 7 个模型（5 分钟前拉取）
  实时拉取的 7 个模型（3 小时前拉取）
  静态备用列表（3 项）。点「从 API 刷新」拉取该 provider 的真实模型清单。

`formatFetchedAtSuffix` uses `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` with `style: 'short'`
to localize naturally; falls back to `toLocaleString()` for engines
without RTF. Returns empty string when `modelsFetchedAt` is undefined
(legacy connections from before backend `94b482b`) so we don't render
"(undefined 拉取)" or similar artifact.

Pairs with `connection.modelSource ?? 'fallback'` initial-state read
that already landed in `94b482b`. UI now relies entirely on backend
fact instead of inferring from `models.length > 0`.

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build + tests green (60 pass).

subject: UI-02: replace model picker dropdown with a real ModelTable workspace
body:
@kenji's UI-02 backlog item ("Provider model workspace, not a form
field"). The connection editor's model picker was a `<select>` paired
with a "从 API 刷新" button — fine for 3-item fallback lists, fragile
for the live 30+ model catalogs that real providers return.

New `<ModelTable>` workspace:

- **Header row**: bold "模型" + tone-coded source/fetched-at line
  ("实时拉取的 7 个模型（5 分钟前拉取）" success / "静态备用列表（3
  项）" info / fetched-empty branch) + refresh button anchored right.
- **Search input** appears when the catalog has more than 6 entries
  so a long list (e.g. OpenAI's 100+ models) stays browseable.
- **Per-row radio + capability chips**: each row is a `role="radio"`
  click target. Default model has tinted background + filled radio +
  "默认" badge. Capability chips render when `ModelInfo.capabilities`
  has truthy `vision`/`reasoning`/`functionCalling`, plus a compact
  context window chip ("200K ctx" / "1M ctx") when present. Chips
  stay empty when the provider didn't populate them — no fabricated
  state.
- **Empty states**: explicit copy for "fetched returned 0" vs
  "haven't fetched yet" vs "search matches nothing" — three distinct
  shapes per the source-of-truth contract.

ConnectionDetail passes connection.modelsFetchedAt through so the
"X 前拉取" subline reads the persisted backend timestamp (PR90 +
xuan 94b482b).

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build + tests green (60 pass).

subject: ModelTable: ARIA radiogroup keyboard nav + hidden-default search hint
body:
Two PR91 follow-ups @kenji called out:

1. **Keyboard nav inside the radiogroup**: implements the standard
   ARIA radiogroup pattern — ArrowUp/Left moves focus to previous
   radio, ArrowDown/Right to next (wraps both ends), Home/End jumps
   to first/last. Only the active radio is in the tab order
   (tabIndex=0); the rest are tabIndex=-1, so Tab leaves the group
   instead of trapping. Space/Enter on a focused radio still triggers
   the native button click.
2. **Hidden default hint**: when a search filter excludes the
   currently-selected default model, a one-line accent-tinted hint
   appears above the (filtered) list: "当前默认 `glm-4.5` 不在搜索
   结果中 · 点这里清空搜索". The whole hint is a button — clicking
   clears the search and brings the default back into view. Prevents
   the "I searched for X and now I can't tell which is default"
   confusion.

No backend changes; pure renderer polish on top of PR91's ModelTable.

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build green.

subject: ModelTable arrow keys: move focus AND select (ARIA radiogroup pattern)
body:
@xuan PR92 review: arrow / Home / End were only moving focus, but the
standard ARIA radiogroup pattern selects on focus move. Safe to do here
because `onPickDefault` updates local form state only — persistence
happens on "保存修改", so scanning models with arrow keys doesn't write
to disk on every keystroke.

Adds the `onPickDefault(next.id)` call right after focus/scroll in the
arrow/Home/End branch. Space/Enter on a focused radio continues to
work via the native button click handler — no change there.

subject: Extract ModelTable kbd helper to a pure module + 14 node:test cases
body:
Per @kenji PR93 follow-up: "ModelTable 的 keyboard transition 抽成纯
helper（currentId + visibleIds + key → nextId），用 node:test 钉住
逻辑，React 只负责调用 helper." Lock down the ARIA a11y behavior
without dragging React Testing Library / jsdom into the desktop test
runner.

Shape:
- New `apps/desktop/src/renderer/settings/model-table-keyboard.ts`
  exports `isRadioGroupNavKey(key)` and `nextRadioId(currentId,
  visibleIds, key)`. Pure .ts (no React import), so the desktop main
  test runner can import it without JSX.
- `ProvidersPanel.tsx`'s `onListKeyDown` shrinks to: derive
  `visibleIds` + `currentId` from DOM, call `nextRadioId`, focus + call
  `onPickDefault(nextId)`. The selection transition logic is now in
  the helper.
- Each rendered radio gets `data-model-id={model.id}` so the DOM →
  current-id lookup is robust to wrapping spans / icons. (Previously
  we read the focused button index by reference — fine when nothing
  re-orders, but the helper signature is cleaner.)

Tests (14 cases, lifts desktop test count 60 → 74):
- isRadioGroupNavKey: positive for Arrow/Home/End, negative for
  Space/Enter/Escape/printable/Tab
- ArrowDown/Right: advance, wrap, enter-from-undefined → first
- ArrowUp/Left: retreat, wrap, enter-from-undefined → last
- Home/End: jump to first/last
- Non-nav keys → null (caller skips preventDefault)
- Empty radio set → null
- Single-radio group: returns same id (caller's no-op guard handles it)
- Filtered-out currentId (search hid the default): falls back to first
  visible — matches the hidden-default search hint UX from PR92

subject: Smoke gate: extend with 4 new paths for this wave's surfaces (UI-07)
body:
Per @kenji's UI-07 follow-up: this wave (PR75-94) added several user-
facing surfaces — Settings nav groups, typed tool result rendering,
chat turn grouping + summary chips, ModelTable workspace, sidebar
streaming pulse, command palette diagnostics, Markdown export. The
original 5-path smoke.md covered only credential lifecycle / streaming
deletion / permission dialog. Adds 4 paths so each new surface has an
explicit verification gate that doesn't drift back to "looks good":

- **Path 6 ModelTable workspace (UI-02)**: source label tone,
  hidden-default search hint behavior, ARIA radiogroup Arrow/Home/End
  with select-on-focus, default radio + badge follow active row,
  wrapping semantics. Fail signal calls out the silent-fallback
  regression PR91 closed.
- **Path 7 Chat turn narrative (UI-04)**: turn grouping into a single
  `<section class="maka-turn">`, summary chip strip
  (model/tools/duration/tokens), in-progress duration "进行中" pill,
  thinking block "草稿" framing + dedicated copy + no leakage into the
  main copy-answer flow.
- **Path 8 Sidebar streaming indicator (PR85)**: pulsing accent dot
  on the active-streaming session's row, "Maka 正在思考…" preview
  override, streaming > unread priority.
- **Path 9 Command palette (UI-05) + export (PR86)**: five-group nav,
  test/export commands triggerable from palette, warm-switch
  `requestedSection` jumps Settings directly to model, Markdown
  export structure + thinking-exclusion invariant.

Updates the trigger list with the new types touched this wave
(`TurnViewModel`, `nextRadioId`). Run time bumped to ~ 8-10 minutes
for the nine-path sweep.

Pure docs change.

subject: Block in-app navigation away from renderer; hand external URLs to OS
body:
Catches a real bug: clicking an assistant-emitted markdown link
(http/https to docs, GitHub, provider sign-up pages, etc.) would
either replace the renderer's view with the remote page (breaking the
app) or — for `target="_blank"` — open a new BrowserWindow with full
Node integration. Both are vectors for losing trust.

Two layers in `createWindow`:

1. **`setWindowOpenHandler`**: intercepts `target="_blank"` + JS
   `window.open(url)`. If the URL is in the allow-list (http/https/
   mailto), `shell.openExternal(url)` hands it to the OS browser.
   Otherwise the action is denied. Returns `{ action: 'deny' }`
   always so no in-app BrowserWindow is ever opened.
2. **`will-navigate`**: catches plain `<a>` clicks that would
   change `window.location`. Allows the initial Vite-dev-server /
   packaged file:// load through; for any subsequent navigation to
   an external URL, `event.preventDefault()` + `shell.openExternal`.

Whitelist is enforced by `isExternalUrl()` (new
`apps/desktop/src/main/external-link-guard.ts`):

  - allow: http / https / mailto
  - reject: file://, javascript:, electron:, chrome-extension:,
    about:, data:, non-URL strings

Tests (`__tests__/external-link-guard.test.ts`, 6 cases, lifts
desktop test count 74 → 80):

  - http/https/mailto pass
  - file:// rejected (FS exfiltration vector)
  - javascript:/case-variants rejected (XSS vector)
  - internal schemes (electron://, chrome-extension://, about:, data:)
    rejected
  - garbage strings rejected without throwing

Pure renderer security baseline. No protocol change.

subject: External-link guard: drop non-allowed navigations + drag-drop + harden webPreferences
body:
Three follow-ups in one commit per @kenji's PR96 review on defense-in-
depth + the drag-drop hole I noticed while auditing:

1. **`will-navigate` now blocks ALL non-initial navigation** instead of
   only intercepting external URLs. Previously file:// and other
   non-allow-listed schemes silently passed through, which means
   dropping a file onto the window would navigate to its `file://`
   URL. Now: initial Vite/file:// load passes, anything else gets
   `event.preventDefault()`; external URLs additionally
   `shell.openExternal(url)`.
2. **In-window drag-drop suppression**: after `did-finish-load`, inject
   a document-level listener that calls `preventDefault` on dragover
   and drop. Prevents the dropEffect flicker + ambiguity, in addition
   to the navigation block above.
3. **Tighten `BrowserWindow.webPreferences` defense-in-depth**:
   - `sandbox: true` — renderer + preload run in the OS sandbox
   - `webSecurity: true` — explicit (default but worth pinning)
   - `allowRunningInsecureContent: false` — no mixed content
   - `contextIsolation: true` + `nodeIntegration: false` already set

Preload uses only `contextBridge` + `ipcRenderer.invoke`, both available
in sandboxed renderers, so `sandbox: true` doesn't break the existing
window.maka surface. Typecheck + build + tests green (80 desktop).

subject: Application menu: localize custom labels to match the in-app Chinese UI
body:
The renderer was localized end-to-end through PR69/70/71 (visible text,
aria-labels, tooltips, session-name fallback). The Electron application
menu — top-of-screen on macOS — was still showing "File / Edit / View /
Window" English labels alongside "Preferences… / Hide Maka / Quit Maka".
That broke the visual consistency every time the user reached for the
menu bar.

Localizes the explicit `label` strings to Chinese:
- "Maka" submenu: 关于 Maka / 设置… / 隐藏 Maka / 退出 Maka
- "文件" / "编辑" / "视图" / "窗口"

Role-based items (cut/copy/paste/reload/resetZoom/etc.) keep their
default labels — Electron auto-translates those to the system language,
which is the right behavior. Only the explicit-label entries are
touched here.

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build green.

subject: Add deterministic visual smoke fixture mode
Persist window bounds + maximized state across launches
body:
The BrowserWindow was hardcoded to 1240x820 on every launch — users
who resize / position the window had their layout reset every restart.
Mature desktop apps remember window geometry; we should too.

Adds `apps/desktop/src/main/window-state.ts` with `sanitizeBounds`,
`readSavedBounds`, `writeSavedBounds`. State persists to
`{workspaceRoot}/window-state.json` as `{x, y, width, height,
isMaximized}`. Restore goes through `sanitizeBounds()` which:

- Returns defaults for null / non-object / corrupt JSON
- Returns defaults when width<480 or height<320 (safe minimum;
  catches zero/negative + pre-fullscreen sentinels)
- Drops bad x/y (non-finite) but keeps the valid width+height
- Floors fractional pixel values (high-DPI screen restores)
- Forwards isMaximized only when actual boolean

Write path is debounced (400ms after the last resize / move /
maximize / unmaximize event) so a drag doesn't write on every frame;
flushed synchronously on `close` so the final geometry always
persists. Maximize state is captured as `{ normalBounds, isMaximized:
true }` so restoring un-maximizing gives the right size.

7 node:test cases pin the sanitize invariants (lifts desktop test
count 80 → 87). No `electron-window-state` dep — the module is small
enough that a pure helper + tests are more auditable than a
third-party library.

body:
Compacted commits:

subject: Add scripts/check-console.mjs as a workspace audit gate
body:
@kenji PR101 follow-up: "build a lightweight console.* check, not a
full ESLint plugin." This is that script.

Walks `apps/` and `packages/` for `.ts` / `.tsx` files (excluding
`__tests__/`, `dist/`, `node_modules/`), greps for `console.<method>(`,
flags any match whose file isn't in the explicit ALLOW map. ALLOW
carries a one-line reason per allowed file so future audits can see
WHY each exception exists.

Initial allow-list = the 5 sites from the audit + the script itself:
- `error-boundary.tsx` — React boundary, DevTools-only
- `main/main.ts` — dev-gated (PR100)
- `bots/bot-registry.ts` — generalizedErrorMessage
- `telemetry/record-llm-call.ts` — generalizedErrorMessage
- `telemetry/record-tool-invocation.ts` — generalizedErrorMessage

Wired into `apps/desktop/pretest` so any new console site breaks
`npm test` immediately. Standalone usage: `node scripts/check-console.mjs`.

To add a new legitimate site: append to ALLOW with a one-line reason.
To remove an offender: route it through `generalizedErrorMessage` or
gate on `NODE_ENV=development`.

No new dependency.

subject: Eliminate dark-theme FOUC: apply cached theme synchronously before React mounts
body:
Dark-theme users saw a brief light-mode flash on every launch while
`window.maka.settings.get()` resolved and `applyTheme(pref)` fired
inside a useEffect — that effect runs after the first paint. The
BrowserWindow's `backgroundColor: '#f3f3f5'` (light) compounded the
problem because it set the chrome background before any CSS landed.

Fix: persist the user-facing theme preference to `maka-theme-v1` in
localStorage whenever `applyTheme` runs, then read it synchronously at
the top of `main.tsx` (before `createRoot`) to toggle `.dark` on
`<html>` and set `colorScheme`. Standard FOUC-prevention pattern.

The pre-React paint reapplies the `auto` branch itself via matchMedia
so a system-dark user with `pref: auto` also gets a clean first paint.
On first-ever launch (no cached value) we fall back to system
preference, which matches the eventual `applyTheme('auto')` default.

Runs inside the same JS bundle as the rest of the renderer, so the
existing `script-src 'self'` CSP rule is unchanged — no `unsafe-inline`
needed.

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build + tests green.

subject: BrowserWindow initial bg also follows theme — kills the chrome FOUC frame
body:
@kenji PR103 follow-up: the renderer applies `.dark` synchronously
before React mounts (so the DOM doesn't flash), but the OS-level
BrowserWindow chrome shows its `backgroundColor` for the first frame
before the renderer paints. Hardcoded `#f3f3f5` (light) meant dark-
theme users saw a light flash at the window level even after PR103.

Reads `settingsStore.get()` before constructing BrowserWindow — the
store is already initialized at module top, this just awaits its
first read. Combined with `nativeTheme.shouldUseDarkColors` for the
`auto` branch:

  pref='dark' OR (pref='auto' AND system-dark)  → bg = #1c1d21
  otherwise                                      → bg = #f3f3f5

`#1c1d21` matches the dark theme's `--background` token (oklch(0.20
0.005 270)) closely enough that the renderer's first paint is a no-op
visual transition. Light value unchanged from before.

@maka/ui + desktop typecheck + build green.
