subject: docs/design-system.md: design system contract for PR55-106 sediment
body:
@kenji v2 audit item 6 ("Visual system as contract"). Turns the
implicit rules from this maturity wave into a release-gate-friendly
contract document, structured for both human reviewers and AI agents
working on the codebase.

973 lines across 11 sections + 2 appendices:

- §0 Purpose + scope (contract, not docs/marketing)
- §1 Foundational tokens — full maka-tokens.css catalog with
  name/value/use/avoid columns (6-color philosophy, foreground-N step
  scale, shadow recipes, named easing, z-index ladder)
- §2 Density contract — three modes must consume tokens
- §3 Component contracts — 12 components × 5 states (Button, Toast,
  Modal, PermissionDialog, Composer, SessionListPanel, SessionRow,
  ChatHeaderAlertBadge, TurnView, TurnSummary, ToolActivity, ModelTable,
  MessageCopyButton, Markdown) with components.tsx line refs
- §4 Motion contract — duration table + reduced-motion fallback
- §5 Copy contract — policy-not-backlog, generalizedErrorMessage,
  destructive "我已确认，允许", per-enum-only warnings, fetched-vs-
  fallback, Chinese-default
- §6 Surface × state matrix — 22 surfaces × focus/loading/error/empty
- §7 Release-gate hooks — smoke.md / unit tests / check-console
- §8 Anti-patterns blocklist
- §9 Open contracts (ArtifactRecord, ModelCatalogEntry, Health Center,
  session-status, Tooltip primitive, streaming aria-live)
- §10 Versioning + deprecation policy

This is the prerequisite document @kenji called out before next-wave
PRs (Reasoning two-state, tool stream delta, Settings IA expansion,
Quick Chat MVP).

subject: design-system.md: add §9b kenji 8-system contracts + §11 release-gate mapping
body:
Per @kenji review of the v1 design-system doc: insert two missing layers
between the baseline contracts (§0–10) and PR-time enforcement.

**§9b — @kenji v2 audit 8-system contract**
Inline table of the 8 systems from
`notes/maka-alma-craft-design-gap-v2.md` + `notes/maka-redesign-v1-
contract-smoke-gate.md`, each cross-referenced to the document section
that owns the implementation contract:
1. Workstation shell (session status machine) → §3 SessionRow + §9.8
2. Artifact pane (file-backed sandbox iframe) → §9 ArtifactRecord
3. ModelCatalogEntry (capabilities/pricing/source) → §3 ModelTable + §9
4. Turn control (retry/regenerate/branch/cancel/checkpoint) → §3 TurnView + §9.9
5. Sources/Skills/Automations (visible + auditable) → §3 ToolActivity + §9.10
6. Visual system contract (this doc) → §11
7. Health Center (status + redacted diagnostics) → §9.11
8. First-run to value (4-step provider preset flow) → §6 + §9.12

**§11 — Release-gate mapping**
Per @kenji's "每个新 surface 必须声明 fixture scenario + light/dark/
narrow screenshot + failure state". Concrete rules for any new or
structurally-redesigned surface:
- 11.1 Fixture scenario reference (must use existing or extend with
  new seed in same PR)
- 11.2 Screenshot matrix: light + dark + (failure/empty/loading)
- 11.3 Explicit failure state declaration (what can fail, how user
  sees it, which generalizedErrorMessage enum)
- 11.4 smoke.md hook (update existing or add new path)
- 11.5 node:test hook (logic-bearing changes must be pinned)
- 11.6 Reviewer checklist (copy-paste into PR description)
- 11.7 Exceptions (docs-only and bug-fix PRs)

This closes the gap between the design-system baseline and PR-time
verification.

subject: Consolidate 2026-05-22 same-day local updates
body:
Compacted commits:
- design-system.md PR107: §6 covers Artifact/Quick Chat/Health Center; §9.7-9.12 fully written
- §9.1 ArtifactRecord — full PR108 contract (per @kenji's "Artifact first, not Quick Chat")

subject: §9.1 ArtifactRecord — incorporate @kenji 5-point review (post-design-doc-merge)
body:
1. **iframe sandbox/CSP language tightened** (@kenji #1):
   - Remove the "separate webContents partition + permissive CSP for
     iframe" promise — DOM `<iframe>` doesn't get an independent
     partition without `<webview>` / BrowserView. Don't promise what
     the DOM can't deliver.
   - Add `allow-forms` and `allow-modals` to the not-allowed list
   - Switch from `src="file://..."` to `srcdoc={await readText()}` so
     content delivery goes through the path-guard read helper
   - Note that global CSP `default-src 'self'` stays locked — sandbox
     attribute carries all isolation

2. **Symlink-aware path guard** (@kenji #2):
   - Both `realpath(artifactRoot)` AND `realpath(target)` required,
     then containment check
   - Mirror PR56 open-path-guard which already has symlink-escape +
     `..` + URL-scheme test coverage

3. **readBinary MIME allow-list** (@kenji #3):
   - Allowlist: image/png/jpeg/gif/webp/svg+xml, application/pdf
   - MIME sniffed at read time, NOT trusted from record.kind or
     record.mimeType
   - Unknown → `reason: 'unsupported_mime'` + UI shows metadata +
     "在 Finder 中打开" / "另存为" actions (no raw bytes to renderer)

4. **Deleted tombstone blocks reads** (@kenji #4):
   - `status: 'deleted'` records immediately return `reason: 'deleted'`
     from readText / readBinary — even if the underlying file still
     exists on disk
   - Surface table updated: deleted row preview shows "此 artifact 已
     删除，预览已停止"
   - Permanent path purge is a separate Settings · 数据 flow

5. **PR108c runtime hook scope** (@kenji #5):
   - First version does NOT do LLM extractor
   - Only deterministic outputs: Write→file record, Edit→diff record,
     Bash with explicit redirect → file record
   - stdout/stderr NOT auto-promoted (noise)
   - LLM extractor (parse ```html``` from assistant message) deferred
     to PR109+

Also: type rename `storagePath` → `relativePath` to enforce contract
in the type system; full reason union widened on both read hel…

subject: §9.1.5 HTML iframe: external links MVP is "fully disabled" (kenji #6)
body:
Earlier §9.1.5 #4 promised that iframe `<a href>` clicks would route
through PR96 `setWindowOpenHandler` → `shell.openExternal`. That was
documentation incorrect — per @kenji's review: without `allow-popups`
in the sandbox attribute, iframe link clicks don't bubble up to the
parent renderer's window-open handler. They silently fail or get
blocked by the sandbox.

Replaces #4 with the right MVP shape:
- iframe sandbox blocks ALL navigation by default — keep that as the
  contract
- preview top status bar shows "此预览中已禁用外部链接 · {N} 个链接"
  (count of <a href> in srcdoc), so users aren't confused why
  clicks do nothing
- Users who need to follow links use "在 Finder 中打开" from the
  artifact toolbar and open the source file in their browser

Future enhancements (NOT PR108b):
- HTML transform: rewrite <a href> → <button data-maka-link> + inline
  postMessage script, main renderer is the only shell.openExternal
  gateway
- Or: postMessage allowlist for iframe ↔ main renderer

Safety-first MVP: HTML can be VIEWED, not NAVIGATED. Cheap UX
sacrifice, clear trust boundary.

subject: PR108b: renderer ArtifactPane shell + per-kind previews + sandbox iframe
body:
Implements the renderer half of §9.1 (PR108b) on top of the storage +
IPC contract that landed in 1c88edb (PR108a). The pane mounts as a
right-side column inside the existing detail panel; it returns `null`
when the active session has no live artifacts (§9.1.3 default-hidden
rule), so the chat surface stays unaffected in normal usage.

New files
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/artifact-pane.tsx — top-level component;
  subscribes to artifacts:changed for live updates, persists collapse
  state in localStorage maka-artifact-pane-collapsed-v1, and routes
  selection into ArtifactPreview.
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/artifact-preview.tsx — per-kind preview
  switcher (file / diff / html / image / pdf) with the §9.1.5 sandbox
  + §9.1.6 failure-state copy.

Edits
- main.ts: new app:openArtifactPath IPC handler that reuses the
  storage-layer resolveArtifactPath realpath guard before
  shell.openPath. Renderer never sees an absolute path.
- preload.ts + global.d.ts: expose window.maka.app.openArtifactPath
  with the same { ok, opened | reason } shape as openPath.
- main.tsx: wraps the chat detail column in a flex row and mounts
  <ArtifactPane sessionId={activeId} />.
- styles.css: ~365 lines for the pane, list, preview kinds, failure
  card, and toolbar — strict use of design-system tokens so light /
  dark theme work without overrides.
- smoke.md: adds Path 11 covering the artifact-pane fixture, light /
  dark / narrow screenshots, list-survives-reload check, sandbox
  attribute verification, and copy-button restriction for binary kinds.

Five review gates from the §9.1 contract — each verified in this PR
- 1. Path safety. The renderer never assembles absolute paths; every
  read goes through window.maka.artifacts.{readText,readBinary} and
  every "open in Finder" through window.maka.app.openArtifactPath
  (artifactId → main does realpath + prefix check, then shell.openPath).
- 2. HTML sandbox. iframe gets sandbox="allow-scripts" only. No
  allow-same-origin, allow-top-navigation, allow-popups, allow-forms…

subject: PR108b fix: openArtifactPath uses showItemInFolder, matches "在 Finder 中打开" label
body:
The implementation called `shell.openPath(resolved.path)` which opens the
file with its default app (HTML → browser, PDF → Preview, etc.). The
button label across renderer + toolbar + failure copy says "在 Finder
中打开" — reveal in Finder, not open with default app.

These are different operations. Per the §9.1.5 "preview in pane is
view-only, escape valve = OS" boundary, the right primitive is the
reveal-in-folder operation:

  shell.showItemInFolder(absolutePath)

(Note: TypeScript Electron typing uses `showItemInFolder`, not
`showItemInFinder` despite macOS branding. Cross-platform behavior is
identical — reveals the file in the OS file browser.)

User then double-clicks themselves to open with their preferred app.
This also matches the existing PR56 openPath('workspace') semantic of
"reveal the workspace folder, don't auto-open files inside".

`shell.showItemInFolder` is synchronous and returns void, so the IPC
result shape stays `{ ok: true, opened: name }` after the storage
realpath check succeeds — there's no async open-failure to report.
Earlier `'open-failed'` reason removed from this path (still present
in the reason union for forward-compatibility with PR108d
showSaveDialog).

subject: Record artifacts from deterministic tool outputs
PR108e: Chat header banner for stale fake/legacy backend sessions (P0)
body:
@WAWQAQ reported configuring Z.ai but sends still triggering "you haven't
configured" errors. Investigation of the actual on-disk workspace showed
three sessions:

  3b76ea22  backend=claude  slug=fake-claude  (legacy backend kind)
  7280e103  backend=ai-sdk  slug=zai-coding-plan  (correctly configured)
  fff5cb61  backend=fake    slug=fake             (demo session)

Sessions 1 + 3 are silently rejected at send time by `assertSessionCanSend`
with `connection_missing` / `fake_backend` reasons. The user couldn't tell
up-front which session was broken so it read as "Z.ai isn't actually
configured."

Renderer-side fix: extend the existing chat-header credential-lifecycle
banner to also cover (1) `backend='fake'` sessions and (2) sessions whose
`llmConnectionSlug` doesn't resolve (covers both literal deletion and
legacy slugs like `fake-claude`). Two-state tone:

  - warning when a default connection is ready  → @xuan's send-path
    silent rebind will save the send (informational heads-up)
  - destructive when no default is ready        → send will still fail;
    user must fix Settings · 模型 first

Pure derivation lives in `chat-header-alert.ts`, locked down by 14
node:test cases covering all 5 banner states + priority order so future
edits can't accidentally start showing "needs_reauth" instead of "session
is fake" when both signals are present.

Pairs with:
  @xuan: send-path silent rebind for stale sessions + `sessions:changed`
         reason='rebound' with connectionSlug/modelId payload
  @kenji: contract review — backend test invariants + UI banner two-state

Tests: 251 passing (core 41 / storage 25 / runtime 75 / desktop 110).

subject: Rebind stale sessions to default model on send
PR108e follow-up: user-centric copy + tooltip detail (kenji review)
body:
@kenji review: badge label "演示版" leaks internal terminology to users.
Their mental model is "this is an old session that doesn't apply to my
new setup" — surface as "会话已过期", move the FakeBackend explanation
to a tooltip for users who actually want the technical reason.

Changes:
1. `ChatHeaderAlert` (@maka/ui) gains optional `tooltip?: string` field —
   wired to badge's `title` attribute + screen-reader `aria-label` so
   keyboard / AT users hear the longer reason.
2. All five banner states now include a tooltip explaining the underlying
   cause (e.g. "上次连接测试返回鉴权失败（401 / 403）...").
3. Stale fake-backend banner copy changes:
     "此会话为演示版 · 发送时会切换到默认连接"
   → "会话已过期 · 发送时会切换到默认连接"
   tooltip: "原会话使用演示 backend (FakeBackend)，发送时会自动切换..."

Tests: 251 → 252 passing. Added invariant that the badge label NEVER
contains "演示版" / "fake" / "FakeBackend" terminology — locks down the
user-centric copy contract so future edits can't regress.

subject: PR108g: Sidebar visual cue for stale sessions (complement to PR108e)
body:
Without this, users have to click into each historical session to discover
which ones are broken — the chat-header banner only surfaces after they
land on a stale row. PR108g adds an "已过期" pill + dimmed treatment in
the sidebar so users spot broken rows in the list itself.

Stale = `backend === 'fake'` OR `llmConnectionSlug` not in current
connection store. Same classifier as the header banner's "fake or missing
connection" condition.

Files:
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/stale-sessions.ts` — pure
  `deriveStaleSessionIds()` returning a `Set<string>` of stale ids
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx` — wraps the helper in `useMemo`,
  passes through new SessionListPanel `staleSessionIds` prop
- `packages/ui/src/components.tsx` — SessionListPanel takes
  `staleSessionIds?: Set<string>` (mirrors `streamingSessionIds`
  pattern); SessionRow accepts `stale?: boolean`, renders pill +
  `data-stale="true"` attribute
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/styles.css` — opacity 0.7 on stale rows
  (active state stays fully opaque), `--info` warning tone pill

Tests: 251 → 259 passing (+8 stale-sessions cases including the
@WAWQAQ on-disk reproduction scenario).

subject: PR108g follow-up: Smoke Path 12 + CSS contract test (kenji review gate)
body:
@kenji review on PR108g: "active stale row 不要因为 active state 取消所有
warning 信号，active 可以 opacity 回 1，但「已过期」pill 必须保留"

Two-layer gate so the invariant survives future CSS edits:

1. **Smoke Path 12** (`apps/desktop/tests/smoke.md`) — walks through the
   actual @WAWQAQ workspace state. Pass signals include the active-stale
   row keeping the pill rendered + opacity restored to 1.0. Fail signal:
   active-stale row HIDES the pill (the regression we're guarding).

2. **CSS contract test** (`stale-sessions.test.ts`) — two grep-style
   assertions on `src/renderer/styles.css`:
   - inactive stale dim rule + active stale opacity restore rule both exist
   - NO rule may set `display: none` / `visibility: hidden` / `opacity: 0`
     on `.maka-list-row-stale-pill` (catches accidental hide-on-active)

Tests: 259 → 261 passing (+2 CSS contract).

subject: docs/ui-quality-plan.md: UI-side delivery contract for the month-long push
body:
Complements @xuan's docs/full-product-test-plan.md (full-product roadmap)
and @kenji's notes/maka-one-month-completion-test-plan.md (cross-cutting
test/release matrix). This document fills the UI-specific quality bar.

Structure (13 sections):
- §0 Scope (renderer + @maka/ui)
- §1 Per-surface 12-gate checklist (contract / pure helper / fixture /
  smoke / screenshots / a11y / motion / i18n / security ...)
- §2 Testing-layer matrix (when to node:test vs smoke vs visual diff)
- §3 Cross-surface invariants (focus, kbd, motion, density, text,
  boundaries, redactor trust hierarchy) — non-negotiable
- §4 Per-surface coverage matrix (24 shipped surfaces × 12 gates +
  12 pending surfaces) — current state visible at a glance
- §5 Required testing infra: PR-IR-01 screenshot pipeline,
  PR-IR-02 diff CI, PR-IR-03 a11y assert lib, PR-IR-04 reduced-motion
  fixture, PR-IR-05 i18n string extractor, PR-IR-06 strict typing audit
- §6 UI PR description template (Contract / User Flow / Tests /
  Security / Not Included) — required for merge
- §7 Release-no-go conditions (5 categories: functional / visual / a11y /
  text / boundary) — block tagging
- §8 Cross-platform gates (per-OS smoke matrix)
- §9 Performance budgets (cold start, key-to-glyph, scroll, modal open)
- §10 Surface delivery checklist (12-step pattern to ship a new surface)
- §11 Owner directory
- §12 Open questions
- §13 Versioning policy

subject: PR108h: stale-sessions visual smoke fixture scenario
body:
Replaces the manual "tamper with session.jsonl" precondition in smoke
Path 12 with a deterministic fixture. Reproduces the on-disk state that
triggered the P0:

- visual-smoke-stale-fake     backend=fake    slug=fake          ← stale
- visual-smoke-stale-legacy   backend=claude  slug=fake-claude   ← stale
- visual-smoke-healthy        backend=ai-sdk  slug=zai-live      ← OK

The active session is intentionally one of the stale ones so the
fixture screenshots verify the @kenji gate: active+stale row must still
show the "已过期" pill (active highlight doesn't erase warning signal).

Changes:
- `packages/core/src/visual-smoke.ts` — add 'stale-sessions' to
  `VisualSmokeScenario` union
- `apps/desktop/src/main/visual-smoke-fixture.ts`:
  - `VISUAL_SMOKE_SCENARIOS` Set adds 'stale-sessions'
  - `getVisualSmokeState` returns activeSessionId = STALE_FAKE_SESSION_ID
  - `seedVisualSmokeFixture` writes 3 stale-scenario sessions
  - `header()` helper extended with optional `backend` + `connectionLocked`
    overrides so legacy backend kinds like 'claude' can be seeded verbatim
- `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/visual-smoke-fixture.test.ts` — new
  test verifies: (1) active session is stale; (2) `fake` / `fake-claude`
  slugs absent from connection list; (3) `zai-live` present; (4) all
  three session jsonls have correct backend/slug/model values
- `apps/desktop/tests/smoke.md` Path 12 updated to use fixture
- `docs/ui-quality-plan.md` §4 — Sidebar stale pill row now has F ✅

Tests: 261 → 269 passing (+8: +1 fixture seed test, +7 from rebind
tests that landed via xuan's 63ddc6a).

subject: Complete artifact save-as and failure smoke gate
Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into yuejing/ui-design-v2

subject: PR108i: ArtifactPane a11y compliance (kenji 5-gate review)
body:
Implements 4 of @kenji's 5 a11y gates for ArtifactPane (narrow-viewport
bottom-sheet is the 5th, owned by @xuan as a separate PR per coordinated
write-set ownership):

1. **Roving tabindex listbox** — list is a SINGLE Tab stop (`tabIndex=0`
   on `<ul>`); rows are programmatically focusable (`tabIndex=-1`).
   ArrowUp/Down move selection (wraps at ends), Home/End jump, Enter /
   Space activate. Pure helper `nextArtifactListAction()` in
   `artifact-list-keyboard.ts` (mirrors model-table-keyboard pattern).
2. **Escape semantics** — Esc when focus inside the pane returns focus
   to the chat composer; if list empty Esc returns `noop` so the global
   Command Palette (PR31) Esc handler isn't stolen.
3. **aria-expanded on collapse button** — reflects `!collapsed` state.
   `aria-pressed` retained since it's also a toggle button; AT
   announces both meaningfully.
4. **HTML status bar AT-readable** — `role="status"` +
   `aria-live="polite"` + `aria-atomic="true"` on
   `.maka-artifact-preview-external-links-bar` so screen readers
   announce "外链已禁用 · N 个链接" when an HTML artifact is selected.
5. *(narrow viewport — @xuan)*

`aria-activedescendant` on the listbox points at the selected row's id
so AT users get the right active-item announcement without focus
moving between rows. Enter on a selection focuses the preview region
(`role="region"`, `tabIndex=-1`) so screen readers can navigate into
the preview content.

Files:
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/artifact-list-keyboard.ts` (110 lines) —
  pure `nextArtifactListAction()` returning typed action union
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/artifact-pane.tsx` — wires listRef + key
  handler + previewRef + focusComposer helper; row ids prefix
  `maka-artifact-row-` for aria-activedescendant
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/artifact-preview.tsx` — status bar gets
  `aria-live="polite"` + `aria-atomic="true"`
- `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/artifact-list-keyboard.test.ts`
  (181 lines, 22 cases) — covers empty list, arrow nav with wrap,
  Home/End…

subject: PR-IR-03: a11y static-analysis gate via check-a11y.mjs
body:
Implements `docs/ui-quality-plan.md` §5 PR-IR-03. Mirrors the
check-console.mjs pattern (single-file script, no ESLint plugin
overhead). Wired into @maka/desktop pretest hook so the rules run
before every test invocation.

Rules implemented (extensible — RULES array):

1. **icon-only-button** — `<button>` with only self-closing JSX children
   (icons) and no `aria-label` / `aria-labelledby`. The detector also
   checks for string literals inside `{...}` expressions to avoid
   flagging `<button>{busy ? '保存中' : '保存'}</button>` patterns.

2. **positive-tabindex** — `tabIndex={N>0}` anywhere. The UI quality
   plan §3.2 bans positive tabIndex because it breaks DOM tab order.

Exception path: `// a11y-allow: <reason>` on the same line silences a
rule. Reviewers see the justification next to the code instead of in
a separate allow-list file.

Current state: 0 violations across `apps/` + `packages/` after the
PR108i ArtifactPane a11y wave. Future PRs touching .tsx files now have
this gate enforced automatically.

Wired into:
- `apps/desktop/package.json` — pretest chain after check-console.mjs

subject: PR-IR-04: reduced-motion fixture variant
body:
Implements `docs/ui-quality-plan.md` §5 PR-IR-04. Adds a second env
var `MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_REDUCED_MOTION=1` that combines with any
existing `MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_FIXTURE` scenario to force
`prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` semantics regardless of the host OS
accessibility setting.

Why: visual smoke fixtures need to capture a "reduced motion" variant
per surface so the screenshot pipeline (PR-IR-01) can verify the
motion contract gate. Without this, capturing reduced-motion variants
would require toggling the OS preference between every screenshot.

Implementation:
- `packages/core/src/visual-smoke.ts` — `VisualSmokeState` gains
  optional `reducedMotion: boolean`
- `apps/desktop/src/main/visual-smoke-fixture.ts` — `VisualSmokeFixture`
  also carries the flag; `resolveVisualSmokeFixture()` takes a third
  arg for the env-var raw string; `getVisualSmokeState()` merges the
  flag through; permissive parser accepts `1` / `true` / `yes`
  (case-insensitive, whitespace-tolerant) — anything else fails closed
- `apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts` — passes
  `process.env.MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_REDUCED_MOTION` through
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx` — when state has
  `reducedMotion: true`, applies `data-maka-reduced-motion="true"` to
  `<html>` in `applyVisualSmokeFixture()`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/styles.css` — new attribute-selector rule
  mirrors the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` overrides
  (animation-duration / transition-duration / scroll-behavior)
- `apps/desktop/tests/smoke.md` — documents the env-var combination
- `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/visual-smoke-fixture.test.ts` — 4
  new cases: default false, truthy values accepted, unknown values
  fail closed, works across multiple scenarios

Real users never reach this code path because `visualSmoke.getState()`
returns `null` unless `MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_FIXTURE` is also set (still
dev/test-only).

Tests: 291 → 295 passing (+4 reduced-motion).

subject: Merge ArtifactPane a11y and UI quality gates
body:
# Conflicts:
#	apps/desktop/tests/smoke.md

subject: PR-IR-03 v2: extend a11y checker with 2 more rules
body: Adds `dialog-missing-label` and `icon-only-link` to the static-analysis gate. Both follow the existing rule pattern in `check-a11y.mjs` so adding more later is straightforward. - **dialog-missing-label** — flags any element with `role="dialog"` that lacks `aria-label` / `aria-labelledby`. Screen readers otherwise announce just "dialog" with no context. - **icon-only-link** — mirrors icon-only-button for ``. Anchor with only icon children + no aria-label is unannounceable to AT users. Also improved icon-only-* detectors to recognize `{children}`, `{content}`, and `{message}` as text-bearing JSX expressions — avoids false-positives on React-Markdown's link override (`{children}`). Current state: 0 violations across all 4 rules.

subject: Make ArtifactPane narrow layout a bottom sheet
PR-IR-01: screenshot capture pipeline scaffold
body:
Implements `docs/ui-quality-plan.md` §5 PR-IR-01. Spawns Electron once
per (scenario × variant) combination with env vars driving the
existing fixture system, watches stdout for a deterministic capture
marker, then copies the resulting PNG into the canonical baseline
location.

Why this design (per @kenji review):
- No new heavy deps (no playwright). Electron's built-in
  `webContents.capturePage()` is enough for the v1 baseline.
- dev-only IPC — `visualSmoke:capture` refuses unless
  `MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_FIXTURE` is set; packaged builds reject the env
  vars upstream.
- Output path is fixed at `apps/desktop/tests/screenshots/<scenario>/
  <variant>.png` — no caller-controlled paths. Scenario + variant
  sanitized in main (defense in depth: `[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+`, no traversal).
- Renderer waits 2 RAFs + 400ms idle after fixture settle before
  triggering capture — mirrors Chromium DevTools "Capture full size
  screenshot" timing. Doesn't depend on a fixed sleep that flakes on
  slower CI.

New env var:
- `MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_AUTO_CAPTURE=<variant>` — variant filename
  without extension (validated against `[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+`, max 64
  chars, fail-closed on traversal). When set, renderer auto-captures
  after settling.

Files:
- `packages/core/src/visual-smoke.ts` — `VisualSmokeState` gains
  optional `autoCaptureVariant?: string`
- `apps/desktop/src/main/visual-smoke-fixture.ts` — fixture carries
  the variant; sanitizer rejects unsafe values
- `apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts`:
  - `visualSmoke:capture` IPC handler writes PNG to
    `<workspaceRoot>/screenshots/<scenario>/<variant>.png`
  - emits `[visual-smoke] captured scenario=… variant=… path=…`
    stdout marker for the driver
  - new `sanitizeSegment()` helper (regex-based fail-closed parse)
- `apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts` + `src/global.d.ts` —
  exposes `window.maka.visualSmoke.capture()` typed envelope
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx` — auto-triggers
  `visualSmoke.capture()` 2 RAFs + 400ms after fixture settles
  when `state.au…

subject: PR-IR-01b: theme override env var (light/dark/auto)
body:
Implements @kenji follow-up to PR-IR-01: theme override driven by
`MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_THEME=light|dark|auto` env var instead of
per-fixture seed configuration. Every existing fixture scenario now
gets a dark variant automatically.

Why this is the right shape (per @kenji): individual scenario seeds
should never be the place where "theme" is configured — that's an
orthogonal property of the screenshot variant. Centralizing it via
env var means a new scenario can't forget to wire up theme support.

Files:
- `packages/core/src/visual-smoke.ts` — `VisualSmokeState.theme?`
- `apps/desktop/src/main/visual-smoke-fixture.ts`:
  - `VisualSmokeFixture.theme: 'light' | 'dark' | 'auto' | null`
  - `parseThemeFlag()` accepts only the closed enum (case-insensitive,
    whitespace-tolerant); everything else fails closed to null
  - state propagation
- `apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts`:
  - reads `process.env.MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_THEME`
  - `createWindow()` BrowserWindow `backgroundColor` uses the override
    when present so the very first frame already has the right palette
    (no white-on-dark flash before renderer paints)
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx` — `applyVisualSmokeFixture()`
  calls `applyTheme(state.theme)` + `setThemePref(state.theme)` to
  swap the `.dark` class atomically before fixture state lands
- `scripts/capture-screenshots.mjs`:
  - `VARIANTS` expanded from 4 → 8 (theme × viewport × motion)
  - new `dark-*` variants reuse the same scenario seed, only the
    theme env var changes
- 5 new node:test cases for theme parsing (default null, valid enum
  accepted, unknown values fail closed)

Tests: 302 → 309 passing. Driver will now produce 11 scenarios ×
8 variants = 88 PNGs on `npm run screenshots`.

Next: actually run the 88-PNG baseline to find unstable scenarios
(per @kenji), then PR-IR-02 diff CI gate.

subject: PR-IR-05: i18n string extractor + 11 untranslated aria-label fixes
body:
Adds a new rule `english-aria-label` to `scripts/check-a11y.mjs` that
flags `aria-label` / `title` / `placeholder` string-literal values
which:
- Are ≥ 4 chars
- Contain no CJK characters (no `[一-鿿]`)
- Contain Latin letters (not pure numbers/symbols)
- Aren't whitelisted technical/brand terms (OpenAI, Anthropic, API,
  URL, JSON, GLM-x.y, OAuth, …)
- Aren't example placeholders (URL, domain, slug, hostname:port,
  token format) — placeholders that LOOK like an example are
  template values, not user instructions

Exception path: `// i18n-allow: <reason>` (or `// a11y-allow: <reason>`
which also silences i18n).

Caught 14 real violations across the codebase; fixed 11 actual i18n
misses + 2 placeholder edge cases (ellipsis-truncated, kept as
example detection):
- `aria-label="Loading providers"` → `"正在加载模型供应商"`
- `aria-label="Enabled providers"` → `"已启用的模型供应商"`
- `aria-label="Provider categories"` → `"模型供应商分类"`
- `aria-label="Provider configuration"` → `"模型供应商配置"`
- `aria-label="Settings"` → `"设置"`
- `aria-label="Close settings"` → `"关闭设置"`
- `aria-label="Loading about page"` → `"正在加载关于页"`
- `aria-label="Loading settings"` → `"正在加载设置"`
- `aria-label="Bot channels"` → `"机器人频道列表"`
- `aria-label="turn summary"` → `"本轮对话摘要"`
- `aria-label="Notifications"` → `"通知"`
- `aria-label="Dismiss"` → `"关闭通知"`

Bugfix in check-a11y.mjs: line-number computation was off by ~43 lines
because the rule's preprocessing stripped `//` comments before
computing `match.index`. Removed the strip (string literals don't
legitimately contain `//` so it wasn't needed) and constrained the
attribute-value regex to single line (`[^'"\n]+` instead of
`[^'"`]+`) so multi-line capture can't false-positive.

Tests: 159 desktop passing, 0 a11y violations (now across 5 rules:
icon-only-button, positive-tabindex, dialog-missing-label,
icon-only-link, english-aria-label).

subject: PR108j: Settings sub-page visual smoke fixtures
body:
Adds 7 new scenarios to the visual smoke fixture system so the
screenshot pipeline can capture every Settings sub-page in light +
dark + narrow + reduced-motion variants. Fills the §4 matrix F (fixture)
gap for all 7 Settings pages that previously had no automated
screenshot path.

New scenarios:
- `settings-data`            → opens 数据
- `settings-personalization` → opens 个性化
- `settings-network`         → opens 网络
- `settings-bots`            → opens 机器人对话 (bot-chat section)
- `settings-about`           → opens 关于
- `settings-theme`           → opens 主题
- `settings-coming-soon`     → opens 每日回顾 (representative Coming Soon page)

Each reuses the standard provider-workspace seed (connections, sessions,
default settings.json with displayName='建文'); the only difference is
`openSettingsSection`. Active session is `visual-smoke-turn` so the
chat surface behind the modal renders meaningful context.

Files:
- `packages/core/src/visual-smoke.ts` — extend `VisualSmokeScenario` union
- `apps/desktop/src/main/visual-smoke-fixture.ts`:
  - `VISUAL_SMOKE_SCENARIOS` adds 7 entries
  - `getVisualSmokeState` switch adds 7 cases pointing to the right
    section enum value
- `scripts/capture-screenshots.mjs` — `ALL_SCENARIOS` extended
- `apps/desktop/tests/smoke.md` — env-var list extended
- `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/visual-smoke-fixture.test.ts` —
  7 parametrized cases verifying each scenario opens the expected
  Settings section
- `docs/ui-quality-plan.md` §4 — 7 Settings sub-page rows flip
  F: ❌ → ✅

The screenshot pipeline now captures 18 scenarios × 8 variants = 144 PNGs
on a full baseline run. That's 56 new PNG variants covering Settings
pages that previously had no visual gate.

Tests: 159 → 166 passing (+7 parametrized settings scenarios).

subject: PR108k-yj: secondary sort keys for session + artifact lists
body:
Session lists sorted by `lastMessageAt` desc and artifact lists by
`createdAt` desc previously had no tie-breaker. Filesystem readdir
order would surface different orderings across runs when fixture
sessions shared a frozen timestamp — fine for production where
identical timestamps are rare but a determinism leak for visual smoke
screenshot baselines.

Fix: secondary key on the entity `id` (lexicographic ascending). Zero
cost when timestamps differ (the original branch returns first);
deterministic when they don't.

Production users benefit too: same-timestamp sessions / artifacts
always sort in the same order across sidebar reloads instead of
shifting based on readdir.

Coordinated with @xuan's PR108k (fixture seed clock + state.now
renderer freeze) — those changes own visual-smoke-fixture.ts +
packages/core/src/visual-smoke.ts + apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx.
This PR is scoped to the storage layer only.

Storage tests: 25 passing (unchanged).

subject: PR-IR-03 v3: add input-missing-label rule
body:
Sixth rule for check-a11y.mjs. Catches `<input>` and `<textarea>`
elements that have no accessible name:
- no aria-label / aria-labelledby
- no placeholder (we accept placeholder as weak-but-Maka-standard
  labeling since several search/proxy inputs rely on it)
- not inside a wrapping `<label>` (parent label associates implicitly)
- not a self-labeling type (hidden / file / image / submit / reset /
  button)

The regex also handles the JSX arrow-function trap: `onChange={(e) => ...}`
inside an attribute would otherwise terminate `[^>]*` capture at the
arrow's `>`. Workaround: pre-replace `=>` with `=≫` (U+226B "much
greater-than") so the spurious `>` is no longer present. Both glyphs
are single UTF-16 code units so character indices stay aligned with
the original text — needed for accurate line-number reporting.

Current state: 0 violations across all 6 rules. The visual smoke
fixture + automated screenshot pipeline + a11y gate stack now covers
8 distinct UI-side quality concerns (the 6 a11y rules + screenshot
diff baseline + i18n).

subject: PR108l: ProviderConfigSheet uses useModalA11y for focus trap
body:
The provider config sub-flow (Settings · 模型 → "添加" / clicking a
connection) renders a modal sheet with `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal=
"true"` but never wired the focus-trap hook. Result:
- Tab/Shift+Tab could escape to the sidebar / chat surface behind
  the overlay
- Esc didn't dismiss the sheet
- Initial focus didn't land inside the sheet; screen readers stayed
  on whatever element had focus before
- Focus wasn't restored to the previously-focused element on close

Fix: extract the overlay + sheet into `ProviderConfigSheetOverlay`
wrapper that calls `useModalA11y(dialogRef, onClose)`. Reuses the same
hook used by SettingsModal, CommandPalette, and KeyboardHelp; the
behavior is now uniform across all 4 Maka modals.

Closes a gap @kenji's a11y audit would have flagged once `<webview>`
/ screen-reader testing started. The check-a11y `dialog-missing-label`
rule already caught any dialog without aria-label, but it doesn't
cover focus-trap presence — that's a structural concern best handled
by code review (or a future rule that checks for `useModalA11y` near
`role="dialog"`).

Tests: 166 desktop passing (unchanged; no new test cases needed
because the existing `dialog-missing-label` rule already passes and
`useModalA11y` has its own tests in @maka/ui).

subject: PR-IR-02 v1: screenshot diff gate (SHA256-based)
body:
Implements `docs/ui-quality-plan.md` §5 PR-IR-02. Coarse SHA256
comparison between newly captured screenshots and a committed
baseline directory. Per-scenario / per-variant reporting of matches,
mismatches, additions, and removals.

Why SHA256 and not pixelmatch (v1):
- Zero new dependencies. Node's built-in crypto is sufficient for
  byte-level comparison.
- When fixtures are stable (after @xuan's PR108k determinism work
  lands), any pixel difference is a meaningful UI change worth manual
  review. There's no useful "almost equal" threshold for screenshots
  that have been deliberately frozen.
- When fixtures are unstable, raising the diff threshold would mask
  real regressions — the right fix is fixture stabilization, not
  tolerance.

Future PR-IR-02 v2 may add pixelmatch + pngjs for sub-region tolerance
once the baseline is solid and we want to catch specific localized
regressions (e.g., chat bubble content varies, but the layout still
needs to match).

New layout:
- `apps/desktop/tests/screenshots/` — ephemeral captures (gitignored)
- `apps/desktop/tests/screenshots-baseline/` — committed baseline
  (added via `--update-baseline` after manual review)

New npm scripts:
- `screenshots:diff`     — compare against baseline, exit 1 on mismatch
- `screenshots:baseline` — promote current captures to baseline

CI integration (future): `screenshots:diff` after `screenshots`,
fail PR on mismatch.

Files:
- `scripts/diff-screenshots.mjs` (170 lines) — driver script
- `apps/desktop/package.json` — npm scripts
- `.gitignore` — excludes `tests/screenshots/`; baseline IS committed

subject: PR109b: SessionStatus sidebar grouping + status icon + chat header badge
body:
Implements design-system §9.8 UI for the SessionStatus contract @xuan
landed in PR109a (`02c933b`). Consumes the new `status` /
`statusUpdatedAt` / `blockedReason` fields on SessionSummary +
SessionHeader.

**Pure helpers** (testable without DOM):
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/session-status-grouping.ts` —
  `deriveSessionStatusGroups()` projects sessions into the locked
  group order: Pinned → Running → Waiting → Blocked → Active → Review
  → Done → Archived. `aborted` is filtered out. `pinFirst` option
  preserves PR48 pinned-floats-to-top behavior (and dedupes flagged
  sessions from their status group).
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/session-status-presentation.ts` —
  `presentSessionStatus()` + `describeBlockedReason()` +
  `sessionStatusAriaLabel()`. Chinese-only copy; never exposes the
  raw enum identifier (@kenji generalized-copy contract).

**Component surface** (`@maka/ui`):
- New `<SessionStatusIcon session>` inline icon next to session name
  in the sidebar (hidden for `active` + `aborted`). Tone-coded via
  `data-tone` attribute; running spins via CSS `animation` (capped
  under reduced-motion + visual-smoke per PR-IR-04).
- New `<SessionStatusBadge>` lifecycle badge in the chat header. Sits
  alongside `connectionAlert` but with different semantics — alert is
  ephemeral fault, badge is settled lifecycle.
- New `SessionListGroups` renderer supports collapsible groups via
  `aria-expanded` + ChevronRight rotation; archived is the only
  collapsible group + defaults collapsed.
- SessionListPanel takes new optional `statusGroups` prop; when
  present + `chats` filter, replaces date-bucket grouping with status
  grouping. Other filters keep the existing layout.

**Fixture** (`workstation-statuses` scenario):
- Seeds 10 sessions: running / waiting_for_user / blocked × 4 reasons
  (auth / permission_required / tool_failed / unknown) / active /
  review / done / archived. Active session is the running one so the
  chat header status badge renders in the screenshot.
- Driver `ALL_SCENARI…

subject: PR109b follow-up: aborted visible + pinned preserves lifecycle (@kenji review)
body:
Two review fixes from @kenji on PR109b:

1. **Aborted is NOT silently hidden**

   Previous design dropped aborted sessions from `deriveSessionStatusGroups`
   entirely (and SessionStatusIcon returned null for them). @kenji
   pushed back: aborted is dormant history produced by an explicit user
   action; users expect to find it later.

   Fix:
   - Added `aborted` to `SESSION_STATUS_GROUP_ORDER` at the bottom
     (after archived), label "已中止", collapsible + default collapsed
     (same convention as archived).
   - Removed `aborted` early-return in `SessionStatusIcon` so the row
     renders a muted `Ban` icon.
   - `chatSessionStatusBadge` no longer skips aborted — now shows a
     muted "已中止" badge in the chat header when the active session
     is aborted.
   - Fixture `workstation-statuses` adds an aborted seed (11 sessions
     total now) so the screenshot baseline covers the dormant case.

2. **Pinned overlay does NOT downgrade lifecycle signal**

   @kenji clarified: pinning is an overlay priority (user intent "I
   want this on top"), not a lifecycle filter. A pinned+running session
   must still render the Loader2 spinning icon — the Pinned group
   placement must not erase the real status.

   This already worked by construction (the grouping helper preserves
   session objects with their original status; SessionStatusIcon reads
   `session.status` regardless of grouping). Locked the invariant down
   with a new test case so future refactors can't regress to "Pinned
   sessions show as static".

Tests updated:
- `dormant groups (archived + aborted) are collapsible + default collapsed`
  replaces the old `non-archived not collapsible` test.
- `with pinFirst, aborted+flagged sessions still float to Pinned (pin
  priority is policy)` replaces the `terminal trumps pin` test —
  pinning preserves user intent, period.
- New `@kenji review invariants` describe block:
  - `pinned+running session keeps its real lifecycle status`
  - `aborted session is visible (not silently swallowed)`

Docs:…

subject: docs/design-system.md §9.9: turn-level state machine + lineage contract
body:
Pre-write the UI side of PR109c so when @xuan lands the core type +
IPC + storage projection, PR109d UI work has a clear consumer contract.
Locks the @kenji review decisions from the 2026-05-22 04:18 thread:

- 4-state machine: running / completed / aborted / failed (terminal)
- Turn header carries: status, abortedAt?, errorClass?, partialOutputRetained
- Lineage fields (forward only): retriedFromTurnId?, regeneratedFromTurnId?,
  branchOfTurnId?, parentSessionId?, parentTurnId?
- Reverse lineage NOT persisted (@kenji): UI derives via session-scan
- retry / regenerate / branch all create new turn-or-session, never
  mutate old turn
- branch supports aborted starting points (copies to last viable
  boundary before abort, UI labels "从中断前分支")
- footer action × turn.status enabled matrix declared
- aborted turn body prefix "(已中断)" + muted Ban icon
- failed turn uses PR58 AlertOctagon + generalizedErrorMessage
- cancel = UI invokes IPC only; runtime owns status writes (no
  optimistic UI updates — same constraint as PR109b)

Open items left for @xuan PR109c: IPC handler names, SessionChangedReason
specifics, TurnViewModel projection from Turn, lineage derive helper shape.

Docs-only commit. No code changes; no test changes.

subject: Merge PR109d turn footer actions UI
PR109e (d+e): failed turn banner + lineage badges
body:
Consumes the turn-level contract from PR109c. Implements two of @kenji's
remaining PR109d review gates:

**(d) Failed turn AlertOctagon banner** (@kenji gate #3)
TurnView renders a destructive-tone banner above the assistant body
when `turn.status === 'failed'`. Distinct from PR58 tool-error banner
because it's turn-scoped (the whole turn failed, not just a tool).

Copy is generalized Chinese phrase translated from `turn.errorClass`
via new pure helper `describeTurnErrorClass()` in
`session-status-presentation.ts`:

  timeout                → 请求超时
  auth / 401 / 403       → 鉴权失败
  rate_limit / rate_*    → 触发模型速率限制
  network / fetch / econn → 网络错误
  provider_unavailable / 5xx → 模型服务暂不可用
  tool_failed            → 工具调用失败
  permission_required    → 等待权限确认
  (unknown / empty)      → 未知错误

10 node:test cases lock the mapping invariant: never leak raw enum
identifier, always Chinese, case-insensitive, fallback to "未知错误".

**(e) Lineage badges** (@kenji gate #4)
- Forward badges sit at the top of the new turn:
  "重试自 turn ${shortId}" / "重新生成自 turn ${shortId}".
  Source: `turn.retriedFromTurnId` / `turn.regeneratedFromTurnId` on
  the turn record itself.
- Reverse badges sit at the bottom (above the footer actions):
  "已重试 → turn ${shortId}" / "已重新生成 → turn ${shortId}".
  Source: derived UI-side via existing `deriveTurnLineageMap()` —
  NOT persisted in storage (@kenji review on PR109c kept this side
  immutable).
- Both directions share the same TurnLineageBadge type; UI
  distinguishes via `data-direction="forward"|"reverse"` for tone
  (forward = info-tinted, reverse = success-tinted).
- Click handler scrolls the target turn into view via
  `data-turn-id` attribute querySelector (smooth, block center).

Files:
- `packages/ui/src/components.tsx`:
  - `TurnView` gains `failedReasonLabel` + `lineageBadges` +
    `onLineageBadgeClick` props
  - New `TurnLineageBadge` exporte…

subject: Merge PR110a first-run quick chat contract
PR110b: runtime/IPC for first-run gate + Quick Chat
body:
Wires the PR110a contract to the live runtime + IPC surface.

**Settings extension (core + storage)**

  AppSettings.onboarding: { milestones: OnboardingMilestone[] }
  - createDefaultSettings() seeds an empty list.
  - normalizeSettings() runs every read through
    sanitizeOnboardingMilestones() (closed enum + at-most-one terminal
    + strict field set + last-valid-entry wins).
  - SettingsStore gains upsertOnboardingMilestone(id, status):
      * timestamp stamped by store (Date.now()); renderer cannot
        supply one.
      * status guard: 'completed' | 'skipped' only.
      * invalid id surfaces as a thrown error (sanitizer drops, then
        the store re-throws so the IPC handler can reject the caller).
      * other milestones preserved across upserts.

**Onboarding service (apps/desktop/src/main/onboarding-service.ts)**

  Pure glue between core's deriveOnboardingState + the existing stores:
  - Per-connection hasApiKey lookups run in PARALLEL via Promise.all
    (@kenji perf gate — never serialize secret lookups).
  - Credential-lookup errors are caught, logged with a generalized
    dev-safe message (error name only — no message / secret bytes),
    and treated as hasSecret=false. They NEVER reach the renderer.
  - setMilestone validates id (closed enum) + status (literal union)
    before touching the store; throws INVALID_MILESTONE_ID /
    INVALID_MILESTONE_STATUS otherwise.

**Quick Chat (apps/desktop/src/main/quick-chat.ts)**

  Extracted from main.ts for unit-testing. Discriminated-union result:
    { ok: true; sessionId; firstMessageId? }
    | { ok: false; reason: 'setup_required'; state }
    | { ok: false; reason: 'send_failed'; message }

  Rules @kenji + @xuan locked:
  - Input shape is { prompt?: string }. Anything else (stray
    connectionSlug / model) is silently ignored. Override surfaces
    will land in PR110c/d when the model picker UI is ready.
  - Non-ready OnboardingState → setup_required, NO session is
    created.
  - Empty / whitespace-only prompt → create the sess…

subject: PR110b fix: Chinese-only error copy for Quick Chat send_failed
body:
@kenji + @xuan PR110b review found a Chinese-only contract violation:
`generalizedErrorMessage()` returns ENGLISH category strings
("Request timed out", "Rate limit exceeded", "Authentication failed",
"Provider unavailable", "Network error"). Quick Chat surfaced these
to the renderer when the underlying error matched a category — only
the catch-all fallback was Chinese. The original tests dodged the
matchers by accident (NO_REAL_CONNECTION strings happened not to
contain `timeout` / `429` / `auth` / `5xx` / `network` as substrings).

Fix:
- Add `generalizedErrorMessageChinese(error, fallback='操作失败')` in
  @maka/core/redaction.ts. Same classification rules as the English
  version; every category and the default fallback return Chinese.
  Keeps the original `generalizedErrorMessage()` untouched (still
  used by runtime / telemetry / bot-registry; migration is
  per-surface).
- Quick Chat handler uses the Chinese helper for both create-failed
  and send-failed branches.

Tests added (+15 across core + desktop):
- core/redaction.test.ts +9:
    one per category (timeout / 429 / auth / 5xx / network) →
    canonical Chinese phrase, fallback uses Chinese default,
    caller-supplied Chinese fallback survives, secret redaction
    survives, output is always Chinese + never includes any of the
    six English category strings.
- desktop/quick-chat.test.ts +6:
    five categories matrix → exact Chinese phrase + no English leak
    + raw category token does not appear; unknown error → Chinese
    fallback via the handler.

Total: core 103 (+9) / storage 38 / runtime 86 / desktop 301 (+6) = 528 pass.

@kenji + @xuan: PR110b can resume review on this HEAD.

subject: PR110b: drop misnamed firstMessageId from QuickChatResult
body:
@xuan PR110b review: the success branch advertised `firstMessageId?:
string` but the value returned was a `turnId` generated by the
handler. The user message id is created inside
`SessionManager.sendMessage()` and is not visible to Quick Chat, so
the field name was misleading and the contract leaked an
implementation artifact.

Tightens the surface to `{ ok: true; sessionId }` only. PR110c can
add a properly-named `firstTurnId` if the UI needs a scroll anchor —
not before. `QuickChatDeps.sendFirstMessage` returns `void` so it is
obvious PR110b does not own turn / message id ownership.

Updates synced across:
  - apps/desktop/src/main/quick-chat.ts
  - apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts (adapter no longer captures turnId)
  - apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts
  - apps/desktop/src/global.d.ts
  - apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/quick-chat.test.ts (test
    explicitly asserts no firstMessageId field is present)

Tests unchanged in count: core 103 / storage 38 / runtime 86 /
desktop 301 = 528 pass.

subject: PR-UI-A2 review fixups (@kenji msg e62c7247)
body:
Two blocker findings from @kenji's contract-consumer review of A2
addressed:

**Blocker #1: user-facing copy no longer leaks internal PR / IPC
terms.** Every JSX-rendered string referencing `PR-CU-*`, `PR-HC-*`,
`PR-REAL-*`, `typed action IPC`, or `ToolOutputDelta` is replaced
with product language. PR-tracking references survive only in
JSDoc / code comments, where they belong.

    原生 helper）路上，落地后会替换这里的只读视图。" →

subject: PR-UI-B1-a1 review fixups (@kenji msg 1d7ba56c + 708255f3)
body:
Two blockers from @kenji's review of B1-a1 fixed:

**Blocker #1 — visual-smoke day-period determinism**

LAYOUT-4's `EmptyChatHero` greeting prefix called
`detectDayPeriod(new Date())`. The visual-smoke renderer freezes
`Date.now()` to a fixture timestamp but does NOT override the
`Date` constructor, so screenshot baselines drifted at the
5/11/14/18-hour boundaries.

Fix: `detectDayPeriod` now accepts `nowMs: number = Date.now()`.
Default arg picks up the visual-smoke clock freeze automatically;
real users continue reading the host clock as before. The function
is exported from `@maka/ui` so the boundary contract is testable.

New test `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/empty-hero-day-period.test.ts`
(7 cases) locks the boundary table:
  - 00:00–04:59 → evening
  - flip to morning at 05:00
  - flip to noon at 11:00
  - flip to afternoon at 14:00
  - flip to evening at 18:00
  - `Date.now()` stub flows through default arg (the visual-smoke
    determinism guarantee)
  - same nowMs → same period (referential transparency)

**Blocker #2 — ⌘K palette hint overflow + a11y leak**

LAYOUT-5b introduced `.maka-hero-palette-hint`. Two issues:

  a) `width: fit-content` + `white-space: nowrap` on the inner
     `<span>` caused the chip to overflow on narrow viewports
     (the en/zh hint copy easily exceeds 990px hero width). The
     screenshot matrix gate includes a 990px viewport so this
     would have shipped a layout regression.

  b) Entire `<span>` had `aria-hidden="true"`. The hint
     announces a real keyboard shortcut (⌘K opens command
     palette) and a navigation entrypoint to AT users; hiding it
     strips real info from the AT tree. The original justification
     ("shortcut is exposed via the command palette dialog") only
     holds for AT users who already know about ⌘K — for the
     discovery-mode AT user this is the same hint sighted users
     get, and it should reach them too.

Fixes:
  - CSS: drop `width: fit-content`, add `max-width: 100%`; change
    inner span from `white-space:…

subject: PR-UI-C1 review fixup (@kenji msg fbb8f119): smoother secret-prefix safety gate
body:
@kenji's C1 review caught a real leak: the smoother typewriters by
rendering successive PREFIXES of its input. If raw `streamingText`
contains a mid-delta secret like `Authorization: Bearer sk-secret`,
the prefix `Authorization: Bearer s` doesn't match any redaction
pattern by itself, so it leaks to the DOM for a frame or two before
the downstream Markdown redactor sees the full token. The previous
`StreamingAssistantBubble` was passing raw text directly to the
smoother, so this was a real new leak surface introduced by C1.

Fix: new pure helper `prepareSmoothStreamText(raw): string` in
`packages/ui/src/smooth-stream.ts`:
  - applies `redactSecrets` on the FULL raw text so the smoother
    only ever sees already-masked text;
  - every prefix of the prepared output is therefore guaranteed
    secret-free (a string contains a substring iff some prefix
    ends with it);
  - idempotent on already-clean / already-redacted text, so safe to
    apply even when upstream (e.g. C0's `applyThinkingDelta`)
    already redacted — defense in depth without behavior change;
  - defensive on non-string input (returns '').

Wiring in `packages/ui/src/components.tsx`:
  - `StreamingAssistantBubble`: runs `prepareSmoothStreamText(props.text)`
    BEFORE feeding `useSmoothStreamContent`. This is the load-
    bearing change — assistant `text_delta` stream had no prior
    secondary redaction (it only had Markdown's render-time
    redaction on the FULL final text); the smoother required adding
    that here.
  - `ReasoningPanel`: also runs `prepareSmoothStreamText` for
    defense in depth. Thinking is already redacted upstream by
    C0's `applyThinkingDelta` chokepoint, but the smoother contract
    "never sees raw secrets" now holds even if a future change
    accidentally bypasses C0.

Tests in `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/smooth-stream.test.ts`
(5 new cases, 399 total):
  - masks `Authorization: Bearer sk-...` in raw text
  - masks bare `sk-ant-...` API key prefix
  - prefix-level contract: every sampled prefix of prepa…

subject: PR-UI-RENDER-2: maka:// internal markdown link router
body:
Implements @kenji's PR-RENDER-2 spec (#my-ai:2f91befb msg a594c442
+ scope-narrowing msg 1e9a9d96 + edge-case msg 4d41ba39).

Strict-allowlist parser in `packages/ui/src/maka-uri.ts` (exported
through `@maka/ui/maka-uri` subpath so node:test stays React-free):

  - Accepts:
      maka://settings/<section>   — `<section>` ∈ SettingsSection
      maka://compose?text=...     — non-empty, ≤4096 decoded chars
  - Rejects (returns null):
      Any non-`maka:` scheme (https/http/file/javascript/data)
      Case-variant scheme: `Maka:`, `MAKA:`, `MaKa:`
      Mixed-case host: `maka://SETTINGS/account` (non-special URL
        schemes don't lowercase host — locking the strict behavior)
      Unknown sections (`maka://settings/zzz`)
      Empty/missing sections
      Sub-paths under settings (`maka://settings/account/edit`)
      Query / fragment on settings
      Unknown namespaces (`maka://tool/*`, `maka://auth/*`,
        `maka://exec/*`, `maka://mcp/*`, `maka://run/*`,
        `maka://session/*`)
      userinfo / port / fragment in maka: hrefs
      Empty / missing / oversized compose text
      Raw href > 4096 chars (before URL constructor)
      Non-string inputs

Markdown link override in `packages/ui/src/components.tsx`
(`MarkdownLink`) routes by parser result, never by string inspection
in JSX:

  parseMakaUri(href):
    ├─ MakaUriDest      → <button onClick={dispatch(dest)}>
    │                       .maka-markdown-link-internal
    │                       data-maka-uri-kind="settings|compose"
    │                       AT announces "button" (in-app action)
    ├─ null AND isMakaUri  → <span> .maka-markdown-link-broken
    │                       dashed destructive underline,
    │                       cursor:not-allowed, title="内部链接无效"
    │                       NOT clickable, NOT announced as link
    └─ null AND !isMakaUri → <a target=_blank> external link
                            (existing OS browser route)

CRITICAL: unsupported `maka://` NEVER fall…

subject: PR-UI-C2 review fixup (@kenji msg 7fb8d15c + 73e92ef0): case-variant internal probe + explicit safe-scheme external allowlist
body:
Two trust-boundary blockers from @kenji's C2 review fixed:

**Blocker #1: case-variant `maka:` was falling to external `<a>`**

`MarkdownLink` previously gated on `isMakaUri(href)` which is
strictly case-sensitive (`href.startsWith('maka:')`). So
`Maka://settings/account` / `MAKA://compose?text=hi` /
`MaKa://settings/health` were not recognized as internal-looking
URIs and fell through to the external `<a target=_blank>` path —
exactly the case the gate was supposed to catch. A prompt-injected
case-variant `Maka://` would have opened in the OS browser.

Fix: new `isMakaUriCandidate(href) = /^maka:/i.test(href)` for the
renderer's probe. `parseMakaUri` stays strict (only lowercase
`maka:` accepted). The MarkdownLink now does:

  isMakaUriCandidate(href) && parseMakaUri(href) === null
    → broken-link `<span>` with `data-reason="internal-invalid"`
       and `title="内部链接无效"`

**Blocker #2: external path had no safe-scheme gate**

Without an explicit allowlist, `javascript:`, `data:`, `file:`,
`vbscript:` and custom schemes would render as `<a target=_blank>`,
relying on react-markdown's sanitize behavior. The link chokepoint
should not depend on an upstream pipeline.

Fix: new `isSafeExternalScheme(href)` parses via `new URL(href)`
(not naive prefix-match — @kenji msg 73e92ef0 explicitly required
the parser path so `mailto:` matches actual mailto URLs, not
`mailto-info:contact` or bare emails) and accepts ONLY:
  - `http:`
  - `https:`
  - `mailto:`

MarkdownLink falls back to a separate broken-link `<span>` with
`data-reason="unsafe-scheme"` and `title="链接不安全"` for
anything else. Distinct copy + data-reason from the internal-
invalid case so visual-smoke / a11y snapshots can directly see
which gate fired (@kenji msg 73e92ef0).

Updated tests in `apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/maka-uri.test.ts`
(12 new cases, 440 total):
  - isMakaUriCandidate: lowercase / uppercase / mixed-case accept;
    `makafake://` / `https://` reject; non-string defensive;
    combined gate (candidate=true + par…

subject: PR-UI-2: base46-inspired theme palette catalog (4 alt palettes)
body:
Per @WAWQAQ "UI 还是特别差" + UI reality audit §1.4 P0 finding:
Maka only had light/dark binary; users expect modern desktop apps
to ship multiple palette presets. This commit adds the contract +
the data + the apply path. UI picker is a follow-up.

New core contract:
- `@maka/core/settings`: `ThemePalette = 'default' | 'onedark' |
  'catppuccin-mocha' | 'tokyo-night' | 'nord'` (5 values)
- `THEME_PALETTES` exported as `as const` enum + `isThemePalette()`
  type guard
- `AppearanceSettings.palette?: ThemePalette` (optional, defaults
  to 'default' on missing — older settings.json files keep working)
- `createDefaultSettings()` seeds `palette: 'default'`

CSS (maka-tokens.css):
- 4 alternative palette blocks, each with separate light + dark
  variants
- Selector pattern `[data-maka-theme="<id>"]` (light) and
  `[data-maka-theme="<id>"].dark` (dark)
- Each palette overrides only the 6 base colors + accent variants;
  foreground-N scale + alpha overlays + shadow recipes derive
  automatically via color-mix() / oklch() from the bases
- Onedark: blue/orange VS-Code-like
- Catppuccin Mocha: warm pastel + mauve accent
- Tokyo Night: bluish dark + sky-blue accent
- Nord: cool nordic + glacier blue accent

Renderer (theme.ts):
- `applyThemePalette(palette)` — writes `data-maka-theme` attribute,
  removes it for 'default' so original palette renders
- Persists to localStorage `maka-theme-palette-v1` (parallel to
  existing `maka-theme-v1` light/dark pref) so pre-React paint can
  apply synchronously on next launch

main.tsx wiring:
- Settings load reads `appearance.palette ?? 'default'` and applies
  alongside theme + density
- Settings UI picker is a follow-up PR (this commit ships the data
  + apply path so the contract + tests are in main before UI
  consumes it)

Tests unchanged (464 pass across core/storage/desktop). Pre-existing
visual smoke scenarios render with the default palette so no
baseline shift expected.

Part of UI Polish Wave Phase 5 (@WAWQAQ approved 2026-05-22).

subject: PR-UI-16: user-pickable toast position (P2 polish)
body:
Audit §3.10 — v1 pinned toasts to bottom-right (PR55). Some users
prefer top-right (notification-center style) or center for
full-attention dialogs; the audit flagged this as worth surfacing
as a settings option.

Six positions (top/bottom × left/center/right) cover the practical
needs. Default stays `bottom-right` so existing users see no change.

Contract
- `@maka/core/settings`:
  - new `TOAST_POSITIONS` const + `ToastPosition` enum
  - `isToastPosition()` type guard
  - `AppearanceSettings.toastPosition?: ToastPosition`
  - `normalizeSettings()` validates the field on every read; invalid
    values fall back to `bottom-right` (defensive against
    hand-edited settings.json)
  - exported from `@maka/core/index.ts`

UI
- `@maka/ui/toast`:
  - `ToastProvider` accepts optional `position` prop, sanitizes via
    `isToastPosition()`, defaults to `bottom-right`
  - `ToastViewport` renders `data-position={position}` so CSS can
    drive the anchor
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx`:
  - `App` reads localStorage on boot (mirror pattern matching theme
    palette) so the very first toast lands in the user's chosen
    corner with no settings round-trip
  - `AppShell` writes back to localStorage AND calls
    `onToastPositionChange` when settings load, so live re-pick
    repositions immediately
- `SettingsModal.tsx`:
  - new `TOAST_POSITION_OPTIONS` const + picker UI under the Theme
    page, below the density radio group
  - each option shows a 64×40 mock viewport with an accent-colored
    dot in the matching corner so users see where toasts will land
    at a glance
- `styles.css`:
  - viewport anchors driven by `[data-position="..."]` selectors,
    with a defensive reset to clear stale corner anchors when the
    position changes
  - swatch / dot / radio-button styles for the picker

Validation
- workspace typecheck clean
- 578 total tests pass (core 121 / storage 38 / runtime 95 /
  desktop 324)
- check-a11y / check-console clean

Part of UI Polish Wave Phase 9 (@WAWQAQ approved 2026-05-22).

subject: PR-OAUTH-SUBSCRIPTION-0: Claude subscription OAuth (login + quota, send-path deferred to PR-OAUTH-SUBSCRIPTION-1)
body:
WAWQAQ msg `f6d38739` driver. Gate doc: `notes/pr-oauth-subscription-0-gate.md`
(11 hard gates: kenji `cf41871b` × 6 + xuan `2c5aa125` × 5).

## borrow
- alma `~/Downloads/alma-re/readable/main.js:15913-16400` Claude
  subscription PKCE flow, refresh, quota fetch, encrypted token
  persistence, cloaked-request shape.
- Endpoints / client_id / scope strings reproduced verbatim from
  alma since they're Anthropic-server-registered and not negotiable.

## diverge
- Cloaked request headers behind env flag MAKA_CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_CLOAK
  (default OFF) — alma defaults ON. Until product/legal decides on
  Claude Code CLI impersonation, default OFF.
- Refresh failure does NOT auto-logout — alma deletes the token file
  on first refresh fail; we leave it and surface refresh_failed
  state so the user can retry / re-login deliberately.
- Token file gets explicit mode 0o600 on create AND on every save
  (alma uses default mode).
- No telemetry / logs of token lifecycle events (alma logs to stdout).

## risk
- Cloaked CLI identity headers (UA, Stainless, Claude Code system
  prefix) may violate Anthropic ToS. The cloak path is in a
  separate module `cloaked-request.ts` that's never statically
  imported by the default request path; contract test
  `claude-subscription-cloak-flag.test.ts` enforces this.
- On Linux without libsecret, safeStorage.isEncryptionAvailable()
  returns false and tokens persist as plaintext JSON. Card needs a
  future warning (deferred to follow-up PR).
- PKCE verifier in main-process memory only; cleared on
  completeAuthorization success, cancelAuthorization, or TTL prune.

## gate
- 11 hard gates locked in `notes/pr-oauth-subscription-0-gate.md`.
- Implemented contract tests (all PASSING):
  - `oauth-subscription.test.ts` (core, 26 tests): PKCE algorithm,
    URL builder, paste parse, constant-time compare, no token-
    shaped field declarations in the contract.
  - `claude-subscription-cloak-flag.test.ts`: cloak module exists,
    is NOT statically imported, contains impersonation strings…

subject: PR-OAUTH-SUBSCRIPTION-0: document logout local-clear-only gate amendment
body:
xuan `7dbf9812` review flagged the kenji `cf41871b` "logout must
revoke + clear" gate as not closable: Anthropic does not publicly
expose an RFC 7009 revocation endpoint, and alma exhibits the
same local-only logout behavior (`main.js:16280-16295`).

Amendment (yuejing 2026-05-28, awaiting kenji explicit ack):
v0 ships local-clear-only. `ClaudeSubscriptionService.logout`
JSDoc now explains the gap so a future reader doesn't think we
silently dropped a security requirement. Follow-up
`PR-OAUTH-SUBSCRIPTION-LOGOUT-REVOKE-0` wires remote revocation
if Anthropic ever ships that endpoint.

No code behavior change — `logout()` already did local clear; this
commit is documentation only.

subject: PR-OAUTH-SUBSCRIPTION-0: experimental kill-switch + open-auth-url hardening
body:
Addresses kenji msg `1da909d5` (Anthropic ToS legal concern) +
`45b31e16` (verification checklist).

## Experimental kill-switch (kenji `1da909d5`)

Anthropic's third-party developer documentation
(https://code.claude.com/docs/en/legal-and-compliance) states
that third-party developers should use API key auth and do NOT
permit them to offer Claude.ai login on behalf of users.

Until product/legal sign-off, the entire feature is gated:

- New env var: `MAKA_CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_EXPERIMENTAL=1`.
- New core enum variant: `SubscriptionActionFailureReason` adds
  `'experimental_disabled'` — distinct from `'provider_rejected'`
  so UI copy reads as "Maka has not enabled this feature" instead
  of "Anthropic refused your account" (kenji `45b31e16`).
- Main-process service exports
  `isSubscriptionExperimentalEnabled()`.
- All auth-flow IPC handlers re-check the flag main-side and
  fail-closed with the dedicated reason. `is-experimental-enabled`
  IPC lets the Settings card self-gate.
- Settings UI's ClaudeSubscriptionCard returns null when the flag
  is off — no heading, no teasing UI. Loading state (flag check
  in flight) also renders nothing.
- Logout remains always-allowed so a user with a stale token file
  from an earlier opt-in can clear it even after the flag flips
  off; local-clear is harmless without an active session.

## open-auth-url hardening (kenji `1da909d5`)

Previously the renderer passed the authorization URL string to
`shell.openExternal` via IPC. A malicious renderer could have
asked main to open an arbitrary URL.

Now:
- `PendingAuthorization` caches the URL we generated.
- `openAuthorizationUrl(authRequestId)` (was `(url)`) looks up the
  URL from pending map; renderer never controls the URL string.
- Preload + global type updated to take `authRequestId`.
- TTL check + pending-map deletion already clear the URL alongside
  verifier/state on cancel/complete/expire.

## Tests (all passing)

- `claude-subscription-experimental-gate.test.ts` (NEW, 8 assertions):
  - service exports `isSubscriptio…

subject: PR-BUILD-HYGIENE-0: clean-build script, stale-dist gate, build-mode badge
body:
Why
- Phase 3 P0 cycle kept biting us with "tests pass on stale dist": after
  removing or renaming an export, the compiled output would survive and
  the contract tests would still see the old symbol.
- Dev windows looked identical to packaged builds (both showed
  `本地开发版`), so we could not tell at a glance which revision was
  actually running.

What
- `scripts/clean-build.mjs`: idempotent removal of every workspace's
  `dist/` + `tsconfig.tsbuildinfo`. Surfaced as `npm run clean` and
  `npm run rebuild` at the repo root.
- `scripts/check-stale-dist.mjs`: per-workspace mtime gate that exits 2
  when any src/*.ts(x) is newer than its dist/*.js|.cjs|.mjs|index.html.
  Surfaced as `npm run check:stale`. Pure mtime compare, no tsc spawn.
- `apps/desktop/src/main/build-info.ts`: synchronous `resolveBuildInfo()`
  reading `.git/HEAD` (loose ref + packed-refs + detached-HEAD all
  covered) without spawning git. Cached at module load.
- `app:info` IPC + `window.maka.app.info()` typing extended with
  `buildMode: 'dev' | 'packaged'` and `buildCommit: string | null`.
- About page badge now reads `本地开发版 · <sha>` in dev and `正式版`
  when packaged. Copy-env summary gains a `- Build: dev @ <sha>` line.
- Tests: 5 build-info unit tests + 1 contract test asserting the
  root-level scripts and `package.json` entries cannot silently
  regress. Desktop suite at 693/693.

Notes
- No renderer code path receives the git sha for a packaged build; we
  return `null` and the badge falls back to `正式版`.
- `findRepoRoot` is bounded to 10 levels so we never traverse to `/`.

subject: PR-CHAT-WEB-SEARCH-RENDER-0: chat-side WebSearch result cards
body:
borrow
- Card layout from the Settings → 联网搜索 试一下 demo (same
  title/source/snippet shape), so a manual try-out and an
  agent-issued search produce identical-looking results.

diverge
- Tool returns a structured `kind: 'web_search'` ToolResultContent
  on success — the renderer parses it into typed cards instead of
  dumping JSON via OverlayPreview's json branch.
- All rendered cells go through `redactSecrets()` as a belt-and-
  braces guard against a provider body that happened to echo a
  token.
- No markdown / HTML rendering. URL is a plain `<a target=_blank>`,
  not a react-markdown link.

risk
- Pure additive change to ToolResultContent. Errored / non-success
  results still flow through the existing `WebSearchResponse` JSON
  shape (kind: 'json') so the LLM sees the error reason verbatim.

gate
- Typecheck clean. Desktop 742/742. Core 471/471. The
  pre-existing `WebSearch agent tool` failure-path tests still
  pass without modification — they exercise paths that don't reach
  the new success branch.

What
- `@maka/core/events`: ToolResultContent gains a `web_search`
  variant (`provider`, `query`, `rows: {title,url,snippet,source}[]`).
- `apps/desktop/src/main/web-search/agent-tool.ts`: on a Tavily
  success the tool maps each result row into the new shape and
  returns it. Error cases keep returning the existing
  `WebSearchResponse` (renders via the `json` overlay branch).
- `packages/ui/src/components.tsx`: new `WebSearchPreview` component
  (header + plain-text card list), wired into `OverlayPreview`.
- ~80 lines of CSS that mirror the Settings demo card.

Notes
- The LLM still receives the same fields (just nested under `rows`),
  so it can incorporate the result into its response without any
  prompt tweak.
- The agent tool's "fail-closed before fetch" chain (invalid_query
  → incognito_active → not_configured) is unchanged — those still
  return the `WebSearchResponse` shape, which is what the model
  needs to react to (it then sees the structured error reason).

subject: PR-SETTINGS-WEB-SEARCH-URL-HARDEN-0: same URL guard for Settings demo
body:
borrow
- xuan's `e511aa5` hardening for the chat-side `WebSearchPreview`
  (normalizeSearchUrl + redactSecrets + drop non-http(s) / malformed
  rows). Applied to the parallel surface in Settings → 联网搜索
  试一下 demo so both rendering sites have the same guarantees.

diverge
- None. Same gate, same allowlist, same redaction order.

risk
- Pure renderer hardening. No IPC change. The main-process Tavily
  client already filters URLs, so any row that survived to the
  renderer was already http(s); the renderer side is belt-and-braces
  against future drift (fixture / persisted-result replay / a
  hypothetical alternative provider that returns dirtier rows).

gate
- Typecheck clean. Desktop 742/742.

What
- Imported `redactSecrets` from `@maka/ui` and `normalizeSearchUrl`
  from `@maka/core`.
- Replaced the previous inline render of `demoResults` with a
  closure that:
    - Drops rows where `normalizeSearchUrl(row.url)` is not ok.
    - Redacts every text cell (title / source / snippet) and the
      normalized URL before they reach the JSX.
- Header empty / `没有结果` branch is re-evaluated against the
  post-filter `safeRows.length`, so a payload that was filtered down
  to zero now reads "没有结果" rather than rendering an empty list.

Audit notes from the OverlayPreview sweep that prompted this PR:
- `components.tsx` MarkdownLink — already has `isSafeExternalScheme`
  allowlist (http / https / mailto). ✓
- `components.tsx` WebSearchPreview — xuan `e511aa5` hardening. ✓
- `ProvidersPanel.tsx` `<a href={defaults.signupUrl}>` — URL comes
  from a source-level hardcoded `PROVIDER_DEFAULTS` constant, NOT
  tool / IPC output. Not in scope.
- `artifact-preview*.tsx` `src={data:...}` — `safeMime` allowlist
  enforced at the artifact contract layer. Not in scope.

subject: notes: add white-box memory contract anchor (task #20 follow-up)
body:
borrow
- xuan's transparent local MEMORY.md MVP (c06e13f) — two-switch
  shape (enabled + agentReadEnabled), HTML-comment metadata,
  fail-open parser, 0700/0600 perms, 128 KB cap.
- kenji's boundary (19b0996f): local transparent file MUST NOT
  become implicit durable memory; agent-read default OFF.
- kenji's 3-way confirmation (7749c411 #3): no plugin-provider
  abstraction yet — alma is built-in, pilotdeck is single
  provider with embedding selector, only Hermes is true plugin
  registry. Two-of-three is NOT convergence; defer abstraction.

diverge
- This is a contract anchor, NOT code. Freezes field names + status
  semantics so V0.2 / V0.3 PRs extend predictably without breaking
  V0.1 disk format. If reality changes, edit here first.

risk
- None. Documentation only. No file Maka reads/writes references
  this note at runtime.

What
- `notes/maka-memory-whitebox-contract.md`:
    - V0.1 (live): xuan's settings shape, file format, parse output
      with the `status` enum invariants.
    - V0.2 (forward): origin/status/decay/tags/stable-id extensions
      to the HTML-comment metadata. Parser MUST stay fail-open on
      unknown fields so V0.1 readers don't break on V0.2 files.
    - V0.3 (open): provider abstraction / vector search / cross-
      session recall / Dream Mode / multi-workspace memory.
- Decisions log table with date + decision + source-msg pointers.
- Pointers section linking xuan implementation + Hermes / pilotdeck
  deep-dive notes.

Notes
- The `extract_memory` agent tool design is intentionally deferred
  to V0.2; when shipped it is `permissionRequired: true`, gated on
  both `agentReadEnabled` AND a new `extractToolEnabled` switch
  (you cannot extract memory the agent cannot read).
- Contract test surface §"Contract test surface" lists the 5
  invariants the V0.2 implementation will need to lock down.

subject: PR-TELEGRAM-UTF16-LIMIT-0: respect Telegram 4096 UTF-16 cap; split long sends
body:
borrow
- Hermes deep-dive #B3 (`notes/hermes-deep-dive-2026-05-29.md`):
  Telegram's 4096-character cap is measured in UTF-16 code units,
  not codepoints. Astral-plane characters (most emoji, CJK
  Extension B, music symbols) consume 2 code units each.
- Hermes `gateway/platforms/base.py` `utf16_len` +
  `_prefix_within_utf16_limit` are the reference implementation.

diverge
- We do not yet implement edit-message overflow split-and-deliver
  (Hermes' `SendResult.continuation_message_ids`). For now the
  bridge splits a single oversized `sendMessage` into N calls and
  returns the LAST message id, matching Hermes's overall posture.
- We pull the per-chunk cap down to 4000 (from 4096) to reserve
  room for a `[i/N]` continuation header.
- We never slice mid-surrogate. The previous binary-search
  implementation could land mid-pair; switched to a codepoint
  iterator so the cost of an incorrect split is impossible.

risk
- Existing short Telegram sends are byte-for-byte unchanged
  (`splitForTelegram` returns the input untouched when it fits).
  New behavior only kicks in past the 4000-code-unit boundary,
  which was already silently 400-ing from Telegram on
  emoji-heavy payloads.

gate
- runtime 114/114 (+11 from the new telegram-utf16 test suite).
- Desktop 749/749 unchanged.
- typecheck + build clean.

What
- `packages/runtime/src/bots/simple-bridge.ts`:
    - `utf16Len(s)` — UTF-16 code unit count.
    - `prefixWithinUtf16(s, cap)` — codepoint-iterating prefix
      that NEVER slices mid-surrogate.
    - `splitForTelegram(text)` — returns the input untouched when
      it fits, otherwise breaks on newline within the last 10% of
      each chunk and stamps `[i/N]` headers.
    - `__TEST__` export for unit tests; not consumed in production.
- `sendMessage` now iterates the split chunks, sends each, returns
  the LAST message id. First-failure abort preserved.

Notes
- The codepoint-iterator approach was a fix during PR — the
  initial binary-search-over-slices version returned an unpaired
  high surrogat…

subject: PR-PLAN-NEXT-RUN-COUNTDOWN-0: countdown chip on plan reminder cards
body:
borrow
- The Daily Review panel's RelativeTime + the Settings → 联网搜索
  inline labels both show "distance-from-now" alongside absolute
  timestamps. Same affordance, applied to plan reminders.

diverge
- We compute the countdown locally instead of using `<RelativeTime>`
  because `<RelativeTime>` assumes a past timestamp (returns "刚刚"
  for any future ts due to its `diffMs < 0` clamp). A dedicated
  future-time formatter keeps the chip self-contained.

risk
- Pure additive UI. No new IPC. The chip only renders when
  `reminder.nextRunAt` is set.

gate
- Typecheck + build clean. Desktop 760/760.

What
- New `formatReminderCountdown(ts, now?)` helper with buckets:
  已过期 / 马上 / N 分钟后 / N 小时后 / 明天 / N 天后 / N 周后 /
  N 个月后.
- PlanReminderPanel renders the chip alongside the existing
  "下次触发：03-15 14:30" absolute time.
- ~14 lines of CSS for the accent-tinted pill.

Notes
- The bucket boundaries are deliberate: "5 分钟后" / "3 小时后" /
  "明天" all read as actionable; "下周" / "下月" reads as "no
  urgency now".
- Past-due reminders (e.g. recurring jobs whose next-run-at is
  stale because the app slept) read "已过期" instead of a
  negative number.
