subject: feat(desktop): add dev:hmr launcher for live renderer reload (#88)
body:
* feat(desktop): add dev:hmr launcher for live renderer reload

The main process already loads the Vite dev server when
VITE_DEV_SERVER_URL is set (main.ts), but nothing wired up that flow,
so the default dev loop rebuilt the renderer on every change.

Add `scripts/dev-hmr.mjs`, which starts the Vite dev server, waits for
its URL, then launches Electron pointed at it — renderer edits under
apps/desktop/src/renderer now hot-reload without a rebuild. Exposed as
`npm run dev:hmr` (root delegate + @maka/desktop chain that first builds
the workspace libs, main, and preload). Extra args are forwarded to
Electron (e.g. --remote-debugging-port).

Binaries are resolved by walking up to the nearest node_modules/.bin so
the launcher also works from a git worktree (third-party deps live in
the main checkout). Verified: Electron renderer loads from
http://localhost:5173 instead of file://.

Scope note: HMR covers the desktop renderer only; main/preload changes
still need a restart, and @maka/* package edits need that package
rebuilt — neither is a Vite concern.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01MGFGxccBskW4pwKQEf8wyd

* fix(desktop): forward dev:hmr args through the root delegate

The root `dev:hmr` ran `npm --workspace @maka/desktop run dev:hmr`
without a trailing `--`, so `npm run dev:hmr -- --remote-debugging-port=N`
had its arg parsed as an (unknown) npm config and dropped before reaching
the launcher — the documented usage silently did nothing. Append `--`
so trailing args pass through to the workspace script (and on to Electron
via the launcher). Verified end-to-end: with the fix, the forwarded
--remote-debugging-port reaches Electron (CDP port responds); a bare
`npm run dev:hmr` is unaffected.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01MGFGxccBskW4pwKQEf8wyd

* refactor(desktop): drive dev:hmr via Vite JS API

Replace the spawn-vite + stdout-regex + startup-timeout state machine
with Vite's programmatic API: createServer().listen() then read the URL
from server.resolvedUrls. Drops the britt…

subject: Taste Pass A — off-black CTAs, list stagger, spring transitions
body:
Apply design-taste-frontend + high-end-visual-design + minimalist-ui rules
across the highest-traffic interactive surfaces. Bundle of taste-grade
upgrades; no JSX or contract changes.

Off-black CTAs (pure #000 is banned per all 4 taste skills):
- .maka-plan-new-task-button: #000/#1f1f1f hex -> oklch(0.18 0 0) /
  oklch(0.26 0 0) hover, plus translateY(-0.5px) lift + scale(0.97) :active
  press feedback. Inner highlight tightened to oklch(1 0 0 / 0.15).
- .maka-composer-send-button: gradient #222->#000 -> oklch(0.24 0 0) ->
  oklch(0.14 0 0). Hover gradient brightens to oklch(0.32)->oklch(0.20).
  All transitions unified to cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1). :active drops shadow
  to inset 1px highlight only (machined-hardware feel).
- .maka-composer-send-button:disabled: flat #000 -> oklch(0.18 0 0).

Session list stagger entrance (.maka-list-row):
- New @keyframes maka-list-row-enter (opacity 0 + translateY 3px -> 0,
  240ms cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1)).
- animation-delay: calc(min(var(--maka-row-index, 0), 11) * 32ms) so each
  row cascades up to the 12th row; capped to avoid long visible delays on
  big sidebars. Renderer can opt-in to staggered ordering by setting the
  CSS custom prop; without it, every row gets the same fade-up but no
  cascade (still upgrade over instant mount).
- prefers-reduced-motion + visual-smoke fixture both drop the animation.
- Existing background-color transition unified to cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1).

Premium curve discipline:
- All new transitions on the touched surfaces use cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1)
  (the maka-tokens.css `--ease-out-strong` curve) instead of bare `ease-out`.
  Spring-physics feel per design-taste rule 5 (tactile feedback) and per
  high-end-visual-design rule 5 (motion choreography).

1468/1468 contract tests still pass.

subject: Taste Pass B — sweep ease-out to spring curve token; tabular nums on count badges
body:
Per WAWQAQ "粒度不要太细" — bigger bundled pass. Two coordinated taste fixes
across the whole CSS file.

(1) ease-out -> --ease-out-strong sweep (46 instances)
- Replace every bare `Nms ease-out` in transitions / animations with
  `Nms var(--ease-out-strong)` (the maka-tokens.css cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1)
  curve, already used by some surfaces).
- high-end-visual-design rule 5 bans `linear` and `ease-in-out` and biases
  toward custom cubic-bezier; design-taste-frontend rule 5 + minimalist-ui
  §7 enforce the same premium curve discipline.
- Net effect: every hover / click / list mount / tooltip slide reads as
  one coherent spring instead of N different curves.

(2) Tabular nums on count + meta surfaces
- .maka-nav-count: gets `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` so the unread
  badge digits sit on a monospaced grid (no shifting layout when 9 -> 10).
- .maka-list-row-meta: same — relative-time stamps don't visually jump
  every time the time advances ("3m" -> "4m") because the digits keep
  fixed advance widths.
- Joins the existing tabular-nums coverage on .maka-message-time / others.

1468/1468 contract tests still pass.

Cumulative this turn:
- 22b7b47d Taste Pass A (off-black CTAs, list stagger, spring physics on
  send button + plan CTA)
- this commit: Taste Pass B (full sweep + count tabular nums)

subject: Taste Pass C — skeleton primitives + modal double-bezel inner highlight
body:
Per WAWQAQ "继续, 粒度不要太细" — bundled visual-system pass:

(1) Skeleton primitive library
- New `.maka-skeleton-row` / `.maka-skeleton-text` / `.maka-skeleton-circle`
  building blocks with a shared shimmer (linear-gradient swept via
  translateX(-100%) -> 100% over 1.6s var(--ease-out-strong) infinite).
- Pre-composed `.maka-skeleton-list-row` matches the dense `.maka-list-row`
  geometry (18px circle + 1fr name line + 28px meta line) so the session
  list / skill list / plan list all have a ready loading placeholder
  instead of a flat empty area.
- `.maka-skeleton-text[data-width]` data-attr variants — short / medium /
  long — give the rendered placeholder visual variety without ad-hoc
  inline styles.
- prefers-reduced-motion + visual-smoke fixture both freeze the shimmer.
- Implements design-taste-frontend rule 5 ("Loading skeletons matching
  layout sizes, avoid generic circular spinners") and minimalist-ui §7
  (subtle shimmer over premium curve).

(2) Modal double-bezel inner highlight
- `.maka-search-modal`, `.maka-palette-modal`, `.maka-help-modal` all pick
  up `box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 oklch(1 0 0 / 0.05)` — a faint 1px inner
  top highlight that reads as physical hardware sheen.
- Implements high-end-visual-design §4A (Doppelrand / Double-Bezel) — the
  outer chrome lives on the Dialog primitive, the inner sheen is added
  here so each modal feels machined rather than printed.

1468/1468 contract tests still pass.

Cumulative this turn:
- 22b7b47d Taste Pass A (off-black CTAs + list stagger + spring physics)
- d10113a2 Taste Pass B (ease-out token sweep + tabular nums)
- this commit (Taste Pass C — skeleton system + modal sheen)

subject: Phase 1 — reference layout atlas (systematic RE)
body:
Produce notes/reference-atlas.md, a single source-of-truth document for the
visual / token / DOM / motion system of the external reference layout
that Maka's UI is targeting. Built from a beautified expansion of the
extracted bundle's globals CSS (8032 lines) plus index.html and the
main JS chunk's string literals.

Coverage:
- §1 Theme system (8 themes, macOS vibrancy default, font runtime bootstrap)
- §2 Font tokens, weight scale, type scale, runtime data-font-size controller
- §3 Color tokens for light-parchment / light / dark families
- §4 Page architecture tokens (--agents-content-area-radius=6px,
  --agents-content-area-gap=4px), DOM hierarchy inferred from selectors,
  the universal card recipe (1px border-tertiary + 6px radius + 4% lift)
- §5 Shadow vocabulary (5 patterns: single 4% lift + 3 floats + composer
  triple-glow)
- §6 All 13 keyframes catalogued, marked as ported / not-ported
- §7 Page-level component names extracted from the main JS bundle
- §8 Data-attribute state vocabulary
- §9 Per-surface gap analysis: Maka now vs reference recipe
- §10 Phase 2 plan: Plan/Reminder page + Settings restructure +
  shared primitives + out-of-scope follow-ups
- §11 Source pointers + re-runnable grep recipes

Pure documentation commit — no code changes. Naming hygiene preserved
throughout: every section uses neutral terms ("reference layout",
"upstream design") in the artifact itself; the product name appears
only where unavoidable for identification, and the doc explicitly
flags that the name must not leak into commits / JSDoc / shipped CSS
comments.

This sets up Phase 2: atlas-driven rewrites of the Plan and Settings
pages to close the specific deltas identified in §9.

subject: Phase 2A — adopt reference page-card recipe on Plan + Skills + module headings
body:
Apply notes/reference-atlas.md §10 to the two top-level module pages. Single
PR per WAWQAQ "粒度不要太细" + "一定要做的美观".

(1) New page-card recipe tokens in maka-tokens.css
- --card-radius: 6px            (atlas §4: --agents-content-area-radius)
- --card-gap: 4px               (atlas §4: --agents-content-area-gap)
- --card-border-color: oklch 8% (atlas §4: --color-border-tertiary)
- --card-bg: var(--background)
- --card-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch 4%   (atlas §5: the universal card lift)
- --card-highlight: inset 0 1px 0 oklch white 4%   (Doppelrand inner sheen)

This is the single source of truth for the lifted "page card" chrome —
matches the reference layout's most-repeated pattern (every workbench-card,
aux-panel, and agents-chat-panel uses this exact recipe).

(2) Apply page-card chrome to .maka-plan-shell and .maka-skill-library
- Both shells now render with the 6px / hairline-border / 4% lift / inner
  highlight combo. Internal padding 18 20 20 with 14px gap between sub-
  sections (closer to the reference's tight 4-6px than the old loose
  18px stack, while still readable on long pages).
- Both pick up the existing maka-page-shell-enter 320ms entrance via the
  --ease-out-strong token.

(3) Page heading scaled to display tier (atlas §2 type scale + §9 gap)
- .maka-module-main-header h2: 24/650 -> 30/600 with letter-spacing
  -0.012em + line-height 1.15 — picks up the cinematic page-heading weight
  reference uses for module page H1s.
- .maka-plan-heading h2: same upgrade (24/650 -> 30/600 / -0.012em / 1.15).

1468/1468 contract tests pass.

This sets the foundation for Phase 2B (Settings page modal -> inline
restructure + further internal aux-panel pattern reuse).

subject: Phase 2B — Settings chrome rewrite to reference-card recipe
body:
Apply the page-card recipe from notes/reference-atlas.md §4 + Phase 2A tokens
to the Settings surface. Three visible upgrades, all CSS, no JSX touch.

(1) Settings page H1: display-tier scale
- .settingsHeader h1: 16/600 -> 30/600 with letter-spacing -0.012em + line-height
  1.15. Matches reference layout's --text-3xl page heading (atlas §2). Settings
  page now reads as a real top-level surface, not a section panel.

(2) Settings header bar: tighter + reference-aligned chrome
- .settingsHeader: 52px min-height -> 60px (to balance the new 30px heading),
  padding 12/16/10 -> 18/22/16 (more breathing room around the display H1),
  border-bottom now uses --card-border-color (atlas hairline tertiary) instead
  of --border (which was 0.10 alpha — too heavy), background var(--background)
  instead of --foreground-2 tint.

(3) Settings modal chrome: --card-* recipe applied
- .settingsModal radius 18 -> 8 (reference layout uses 6-8px on all big
  surfaces; 18 reads as soft pillow, not premium card).
- Single 1px hairline border via --card-border-color.
- Shadow stack rebuilt: single 4% lift (--card-shadow) + 1px inner highlight
  (--card-highlight) + a soft 10% bottom drop. The previous 18%+10% double
  black shadow was modal-popup-heavy; the new layered approach reads as a
  premium card sitting on a dimmed canvas.
- Transitions kept on --ease-out-strong (Phase B sweep).

NOTE on inline-page restructure: full Modal -> inline-page conversion (Settings
rendered inside .maka-panel-detail, like Skills/Plan) is queued for Phase 2C.
That involves rewiring main.tsx's render tree, prop-threading the close
handler through the module nav, and adjusting focus-trap a11y. Phase 2B above
makes Settings VISUALLY reference-aligned without that structural risk;
2C is the routing change.

1468/1468 contract tests pass.

Cumulative this turn:
- 22b7b47d Taste Pass A (off-black CTAs + list stagger + spring physics)
- d10113a2 Taste Pass B (ease-out -> --ease-out-strong sweep + tabular nums)
- bd00a0d7 Taste Pass C (skeleton primitives…

subject: Phase 2C — deep RE addendum + 2 keyframe ports + composer card + settings narrow variant
body:
Append atlas §12 (deep-RE follow-up) and apply a multi-surface visible pass.
Single PR per WAWQAQ "一次性多做点" + "逆向的彻底一点".

(0) Atlas deep-RE addendum (notes/reference-atlas.md §12)
- 12.1 Secondary entry-point HTMLs (quickpick / voice-overlay / artifact-
  preview / mcp-app-preview): quickpick is transparent body + native
  vibrancy chrome; reference's premium floating treatment is half CSS,
  half native-window territory.
- 12.2 Data-* state vocabulary catalogue: [data-resizable-sidebar],
  [data-workbench-root], [data-workbench-card], [data-workbench-stack],
  [data-settings-nav-column], [data-canvas-dialog], [data-banner-logo].
  The reference's pattern is "structural state on data-*, theming on
  [data-theme=…], route on [data-agents-view=…]".
- 12.3 Telemetry naming as a structure proxy: reference's Settings has
  Profile / General / Preferences / LegoKit tabs (not a single rail
  like Maka); Cron has separate task / run-logs surfaces; Sidebar
  supports nested workspace / chat / IM / cron groups.
- 12.4 Verified streaming-fade-in + indeterminate-slide exact specs
  (CSS-only port lands in this PR).
- 12.5 Settings narrow-viewport variant pattern
  (.agents-settings-page-title-narrow → @media query land in this PR).
- 12.6 Revised Phase 2C scope + explicit deferred list.

(1) Port streaming-fade-in keyframe (atlas §12.4)
- New @keyframes maka-streaming-token-fade-in (180ms ease-out,
  opacity 0 -> 1).
- Utility class .maka-streaming-token-fade-in for wrapping streamed
  Markdown tokens. CSS only; wiring lives in a future Markdown-renderer
  PR.

(2) Port indeterminate-slide keyframe + .maka-indeterminate-bar primitive
- New @keyframes maka-indeterminate-slide (1.4s ease-in-out infinite,
  left -40% -> 100%).
- Primitive .maka-indeterminate-bar: 2px tall track with foreground-4%
  fill + accent-tinted 40%-wide sweeping head. Ready to drop into
  retry-load pending / plan reminder run-now in-flight / modal footer
  in-flight rows.
- prefers-reduced-motion + visual-smoke fixture…

subject: remove sidebar account widget — maka has no account system
body:
Per WAWQAQ msg cad3dec4: "现在左下角怎么还有账户部分，我们没有账户". The
sidebar footer's `.maka-sidebar-account` widget showed a "jakevin / Free Plan"
account chip, falsely implying a subscription model maka does not have.

Changes:
- packages/ui/src/components.tsx
  - SessionListPanel footer: drop the `.maka-sidebar-account` button and the
    `accountLabel` / `accountInitial` locals. Settings gear becomes a labeled
    `.maka-sidebar-settings-button` ("设置") so the footer is purely a single
    settings affordance.
  - SessionListPanel props: drop the `onOpenUpdate(): void` requirement —
    no longer used now that the account widget is gone.
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx
  - Drop the `onOpenUpdate={() => openSettingsSection('about')}` prop pass.
    About / version info is still reachable via the regular Settings gear → 关于.
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/styles.css
  - Drop ~70 lines of `.maka-sidebar-account*` CSS (avatar, copy, hover,
    nested children).
  - Add a clean `.maka-sidebar-settings-button` rule (full-width row with
    24px icon col + label, collapses to icon-only when the sidebar is
    collapsed via the existing `[data-collapsed="true"]` selector).
- apps/desktop/src/main/__tests__/sidebar-version-info-contract.test.ts
  - DELETED. The contract encoded the account widget shape ("Free Plan",
    `.maka-sidebar-account`, `onOpenUpdate`) — the entire concept is gone.
  - About-page-reachability is preserved via the existing settings flow.
  - Test count goes 1468 -> 1464 (only the 4 assertions in this file dropped).

1464/1464 contract tests pass after the rewrite.

subject: sidebar glass material on macOS — Electron vibrancy + CSS translucency
body:
Per WAWQAQ msg 0fb2afd1: "现在感觉Sidecar这个左边栏做的不是很好看，好像
我们没有学习qoderwork的那种玻璃质感". The upstream reference layout
defaults to `light-glass` on macOS (atlas §1 + §12.1), painting the
sidebar against the OS-level vibrancy material via Electron's
BrowserWindow vibrancy option. Maka was opaque on every platform; this
PR closes that gap on darwin.

Main-process change (apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts):
- BrowserWindow construction: add `vibrancy: 'sidebar'` on darwin only.
  Other platforms get no extra option (no-op there). The macOS WM
  paints the sidebar material behind the window when vibrancy is set.

Renderer-process change (apps/desktop/src/renderer/main.tsx):
- New useEffect reads `window.maka.app.info().platform` and sets
  `<html data-os="darwin">` so CSS can scope glass treatment to macOS.
  Defensive try/catch — failure leaves the attr unset and CSS falls
  back to opaque chrome.

CSS rules (apps/desktop/src/renderer/styles.css, ~55 lines appended):
- `html[data-os="darwin"]`: window + body + .maka-shell-2col background
  go transparent so the OS vibrancy material is visible.
- `.maka-session-panel`: a 172° warm gradient overlay (matches the
  reference's `--agents-layout-bg` parchment gradient direction) over
  a 62%-translucent `--surface-canvas` base + 12px backdrop blur with
  140% saturation. Reads as a defined column of glass-frosted material,
  not raw translucency.
- `.maka-session-panel-footer`: small bottom gradient + hairline
  divider to anchor the settings button against the glass.
- `.maka-panel-detail`: explicitly kept on solid `var(--background)` so
  chat text + lifted cards stay fully legible. Glass material is
  sidebar-only — matches the reference layout's pattern of mixing
  vibrancy chrome with opaque content cards.

Non-macOS hosts (Windows / Linux): no change. CSS rules are all gated
on `[data-os="darwin"]`, vibrancy option is gated on the same platform
check in main.ts, so opaque chrome remains the default.

1464/1464 …

subject: audit punch-list — tab heights, hover shadows, empty-state copy, glass legibility
body:
Apply 6 targeted fixes from an audit pass (Explore agent walked styles.css +
components.tsx against notes/reference-atlas.md). No architectural changes,
no contract test updates needed.

1. Plan tabs height 30 -> 28 (.maka-plan-tab)
   30 was the display-heading height (atlas §2); tabs should sit on the
   24/28/32 control grid. 28 is the standard size for module tabs.

2. Skill filter pill height 30 -> 28 + radius 7 -> 6 (.maka-skill-filter-pill)
   Same off-grid issue; radius also pulled to the universal --card-radius
   value for consistency with the post-Phase-2A token set.

3. Plan card hover shadow 32% -> 8% (.maka-plan-card:hover)
   Reference's universal card lift is 4% (atlas §5); 32% read as "modal
   popping out", not "card responding to hover". 8% with a tighter spread
   (4px / 12px / -8 spread vs 10/22/-22) reads as a premium lift instead.

4. Skill market card hover shadow 30% -> 8% (.maka-skill-market-card:hover)
   Same fix family; both card grids now share the same hover physics.

5. Empty state body 11 -> 12 + foreground-50 -> foreground-45
   (.maka-empty-state-body)
   11/50% read as caption-tier, not "helpful onboarding". 12/45% holds
   readability on the warm canvas + the new glass sidebar without losing
   the secondary-text hierarchy.

6. Glass sidebar legibility (audit §6 — concrete WCAG concern)
   The new HEAD `2dd8fc24` glass material had a 62% translucent
   --surface-canvas + 12px blur. Audit measured ~2.5:1 contrast on the
   row text — WCAG AA failure on bright desktops. Tightened:
   - 172° wash 0.04 -> 0.08 alpha at top, fades to 0.02 at bottom (was
     fading to transparent). Bottom now subtly anchored so the column
     doesn't visually disappear into the chat behind it.
   - base translucency 0.62 -> 0.50 (more vibrancy room, less window
     content bleed-through).
   - backdrop-blur 12 -> 8 px (12 was making small UI text soft).
   - Force `color: var(--foreground)` on .maka-session-panel-header,
     .maka-session-list, .maka-nav-row, .maka-list-row so glass
    …

subject: deep RE round 3 — sidebar / composer / banners / resize-handle (atlas §13)
body:
Per WAWQAQ msg f31b4611. Append §13 (7 sub-sections) to reference-atlas.md
covering pattern families I hadn't decoded in §1-§12. Atlas now at 729 lines.

§13.1 Sidebar two-mode chrome
- The reference sidebar swaps between vibrancy mode and
  `.agents-sidebar-floating-glass` mode (opaque card variant for
  parchment themes). The renderer doesn't branch — CSS lights the right
  mode via `[data-theme]` selector. This is the answer to "how to
  support parchment alongside glass": same component, conditional class.
- Sidebar resize uses react-resizable-panels (`[role=separator]`); the
  separator has transparent bg + no focus outline; user only sees the
  column edge. `[data-resizing=true]` drops box-shadow during drag.

§13.2 Composer 3-layer wing glow (verified)
- `.chat-input-primary-glow` ::before runs forward, ::after runs
  reverse; each carries 3 shadow layers (bottom-drop + L wing + R wing).
- Maka's `maka-composer-glow-pulse` matches the pattern; parity good.
- Parchment composer has its own palette via
  --chat-input-parchment-{glow,halo,edge} tokens (warm amber). Maka
  composer is single-palette; will need overrides when we adopt
  parchment theme.

§13.3 Banners — three mascot families
- `[data-skills-banner]` hosts `.animate-pointer-poke` SVG mascot (4s
  linear infinite micro-shake).
- `[data-connectors-banner-visual]` has `[data-connectors-lightning]`
  pulsing on 1.4s ease-in-out infinite.
- `[data-plugins-banner]` has multi-part character mascot
  (body / hand / tags-layer / dynamic-visual).
- All three apply parchment-theme SVG color-rewrites:
  `[fill*=color-primary]` -> `var(--color-fill-secondary)` and
  `[stroke*=color-primary-border]` -> `var(--color-border-tertiary)`.
  This neutralizes brand-blue to parchment-friendly tones without
  per-theme asset variants.
- Maka has no equivalent banner mascots. Real "delight" gap.

§13.4 Theme-tinted brand motion
- `[data-parchment-brand-motion]` rule: parchment themes get
  `filter: hue-rotate(-85deg) saturate(.65)`; dark themes get
  `filter…

subject: skip vibrancy under visual-smoke fixture
body:
Auto-capture renderer cannot reliably paint native macOS vibrancy material
in the headless smoke environment; the compositor stalls waiting for frames
that never settle, and `capture_marker_not_seen` fires after 60s.

Guard the constructor option on `process.env.MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_FIXTURE`
being absent. Real-world Electron launches keep the glass material;
visual-smoke captures get the opaque fallback (and finish in seconds).

1464/1464 contract tests pass.

subject: Phase 3 — chrome user-select hygiene + parchment brand-motion tint + atlas §14
body:
Per WAWQAQ msg 7f3ca067 ("继续学习…多解决现有前端的问题，设计 布局的问题").
Append atlas §14 (Settings palette, popover translate, Settings layout
specifics — 5 new sub-sections, atlas now 839 lines) and ship 5 Phase 3
quick-win patches.

Atlas §14 notes (no code change for these — research only):
- 14.1 Settings has its own primary-color palette (mint green #8ee5a1 in
  light themes, deep charcoal #202116 in light-parchment). Adopting a
  Settings-only token override would let Save/Apply CTAs feel visually
  distinct from chat — a premium pattern Maka doesn't currently match.
- 14.2 Reference's Settings nav column has `background: 0 0` (inherits
  from modal palette).
- 14.3 Popover positioning uses Tailwind data-* variants
  (`.data-[side=top]:-translate-y-1`); Maka has the tooling installed
  but hasn't shifted from hand-rolled rules.
- 14.4 Reference globally defaults chrome to `user-select: none` and
  opts content surfaces in via `.allow-text-selection`. Maka does the
  opposite, which is why click-drag on a nav row can begin a text
  selection. This commit closes that gap on chrome surfaces.
- 14.5 Additional polish gaps (cmd+k cold-open auto-focus, glass-effects
  toggle, canvas-dialog variant) — noted for future PRs.

Ship-now code changes:

(1) Chrome surfaces opt out of text selection
- `user-select: none` on .maka-session-panel, .maka-session-panel-header,
  .maka-session-panel-footer, .maka-nav-row, .maka-list-row,
  .maka-module-main-header, .maka-plan-heading, .maka-skill-tab,
  .maka-plan-tab, .maka-sidebar-search-button.
- Selectable text remains on Markdown bodies, composer textarea, and
  any readonly pre/code content (browser-default inheritance).

(2) `[data-parchment-brand-motion]` global theme-tint rule
- light-parchment + dark-parchment → filter: hue-rotate(-85deg) saturate(.65)
- dark + dark-glass + classic-dark → filter: brightness(.8)
- Any third-party brand motion (Lottie / GIF / branded SVG) tags itself
  with the attribute and auto-tints across a…

subject: Phase 4A — sidebar group hierarchy + meta typography polish
body:
Per WAWQAQ msg 3c9e1db1 ("现在侧边栏的这个设计感觉完全不够美观") +
d3e43c8b ("侧边栏这一块一定要学习好啊，你们不用考虑向前兼容").

This commit is the visual-layer polish. The structural layer (nested
workspace/group/chat tree + new IPC) is the larger Phase 4B PR coming
next — needs IPC + data model + JSX, not just CSS.

(1) Group label retypography (.maka-list-group-label)
- 11/500 lowercase neutral copy -> 9.5/600 uppercase tracking 0.08em
  with tabular-nums. Reads as "data-tier section header" matching the
  reference's `.sidebar-section-title` pattern. Mirrors the warm-canvas
  "data display" typography rather than caption tier.
- Padding 1/6/3 -> 0/6/3 so the label hugs the divider above it.

(2) Group + group divider (.maka-list-group + .maka-list-group)
- margin-top 6 -> 10, padding-top 8, plus a 1px hairline
  `oklch foreground 5%` border-top. Each section now has a visible
  data-break above its label, no longer running together as a single
  flat list. Closes a major visual gap WAWQAQ called out.

(3) Row meta typography (.maka-list-row-meta)
- 11/foreground-50 -> 9.5/foreground-42 with tracking 0.01em + weight
  500 + tabular-nums. The date stamp ("2026年8月5日 03:30") was
  reading too loud relative to the title; pulled to the secondary-meta
  tier so the title leads.

1464/1464 contract tests pass. Visible change in sidebar density,
hierarchy, and breathing room.

Note on Phase 4B (deferred, larger work — see WAWQAQ msg d3e43c8b
"don't worry about backward compat"):
- Adopt reference's nested sidebar tree: Workspace > ChatGroup > Chat,
  with IM hub + cron group as siblings. Atlas §12.3 captures the
  reference's full nesting.
- New IPC: workspace.*, group.*, chat.moveToGroup.
- New <SidebarTree> component with collapse/expand + drag-reorder.
- One-time migration of flat sessions into "default workspace > ungrouped".
- Phase 4B will land as its own PR; this commit closes the visual
  delta now so the polish is visible while 4B builds.

subject: Phase 4A — hero typography (Geist variable axes) + atlas §15
body:
(1) Hero title uses Geist variable axes per atlas §15.1 finding
- .maka-hero h1 / .emptyChat h1: 28/600 with line-height 1.12 ->
  32/550 with letter-spacing -0.018em + font-variation-settings
  "wght" 550, "wdth" 105.
- Reference uses Instrument Sans @ wdth:100/wght:500 on .welcome-title /
  .login-title for hero typography distinction. Geist Variable also
  has wdth + wght axes; tuning them gives the "wider, slightly
  heavier" hero feel without shipping a second variable font asset.
- Lede paragraph 14 -> 14.5 line-height 1.5 -> 1.55 for breathing.

(2) Atlas §15 (deep RE round 5, atlas now 934 lines)
- 15.1 Instrument Sans variable font on hero — applied above.
- 15.2 [data-gradual-blur-layer] overlay primitive — reference uses a
  fixed pointer-events: none scrim with linear-gradient mask +
  backdrop-blur to fade content edges into chrome. Maka has no
  equivalent; would close a polish gap on sticky chat headers.
- 15.3 Font-display swap parity (Maka already aligned via
  @fontsource-variable/geist).
- 15.4 [data-os][data-glass-effects=off] per-platform fallback pattern.
- 15.5 Phase 5 candidates 11-14 (variable hero, blur-layer, glass-off
  toggle, login surface).

1464/1464 contract tests pass.

subject: batch polish — Daily Review page card + gradual blur layer primitive
body:
Per WAWQAQ msg fabeb1c7 ("多做一点修复类和打磨类…批量做"). Low-risk batch of
polish wins, all pure CSS, no JSX touch, no contract test impact.

(1) Daily Review page picks up the universal page-card recipe
- .maka-module-main .maka-daily-review-panel was flat (transparent, no
  chrome), reading orphaned next to Skills + Plan + Settings (all of
  which already use --card-*). Now matches: bg-card, 1px hairline border,
  6px radius, 4% lift + Doppelrand inner highlight, 320ms entrance.
- Internal padding 6/12/16 -> 18/20/20 with gap 10 -> 8 (matches Plan +
  Skills internal cadence).

(2) Daily Review day header (.maka-daily-review-day)
- 12/600 -> 13/600 with tabular-nums + 0.005em tracking. The day label
  is data; tabular-nums prevents date digit shifting when navigating
  day-to-day.

(3) New [data-gradual-blur-layer] overlay primitive (atlas §15.2 ported)
- Fixed pointer-events: none scrim with linear-gradient mask + 6px
  backdrop-blur. Use case: drop on a positioned div inside any scroll
  container; pass data-side="top" or "bottom" to choose fade direction.
- Honors prefers-reduced-motion (drops backdrop-filter) and the
  visual-smoke fixture's [data-maka-visual-smoke="true"] root.
- CSS-only; future PRs can sprinkle the attribute on sticky chat
  headers / sidebar list edges / settings nav column overflow.

1464/1464 contract tests pass.

subject: Merge pull request #93 from maka-agent/pr/visual-refresh-and-sidebar
body: Visual refresh — atlas-driven design system + sidebar Phase 4

subject: PR-DAILY-REVIEW-FULL-0 — contract types for config + archive
body:
Add the missing data contracts on top of MVP-0: DailyReviewConfig,
DailyReviewArchive, DailyReviewArchiveSummary, mode/status enums,
default config, and normalize/archive-id helpers. No IPC, no UI, no
behavior — just the shared shape that core, main, preload, and
renderer will all import in subsequent commits.

The interface was locked in the maka-dev-kabi thread; backend and UI
land concurrently against this header.

subject: PR-DAILY-REVIEW-FULL-0 — sidebar nav entry for 每日回顾
body:
Top-left primary nav now lists 每日回顾 between 新任务 and 技能, using
the LineChart glyph. Section was already wired in MODULE_NAV_LABEL /
selectModule / props.mode === 'daily-review'; the missing piece was

Rides along: small sidebar-adjacent polish — uppercase skill section

.maka-skill-examples / .maka-skill-installed cadence, tighter
.maka-search-modal-result hover (translateX 1px), and onboarding

subject: PR-DAILY-REVIEW-FULL-0 — info banner + manual trigger row in panel
body:
DailyReviewBridge gains optional pipeline methods (runOnce, list/get/
delete archive, fetch/update config). The panel now renders an info
banner explaining the feature and, when the bridge exposes runOnce,
a "生成每日回顾" + "生成深度分析" trigger row that calls the bridge
and refreshes the panel.

Backend wiring (IPC + archive + cron) is xuan's lane; the runOnce
button stays hidden until that ships.

subject: PR-DAILY-REVIEW-FULL-0 — real Settings → 每日回顾 config form
body:
The Settings → 每日回顾 page is no longer a static "shipped features"
list. It now renders the live config:

- 启用每日回顾 + 执行时间 (HH:mm, time input)

Each control writes through `window.maka.dailyReview.setConfig`

subject: PR-PERMISSION-PAGE-REDESIGN — actionable Permission Center
body:
Replaces the engineering-flavored 5-column status grid with a clean
permission-row layout: icon + label + purpose + impact + status badge
+ action button. Per-permission action buttons actually trigger system
settings (or the OS consent dialog where supported) — the old page
was read-only.

**New IPC**
- `permissions:openSystemSettings(permId)` — deep-links into the
  matching macOS System Settings → Privacy & Security pane via
  `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_*`.
- `permissions:requestAccess(permId)` — uses
  `systemPreferences.askForMediaAccess('microphone')` for mic,
  `new Notification(...).show()` for notifications, falls back to
  `openSystemSettings` for accessibility / screen_recording /
  automation (macOS has no programmatic consent dialog there).

**New UI shape**
- Page intro leads with the action affordance, not "只读快照".
- 4-tile permission summary above the list (已授权 / 等待授权 /
  已拒绝 / 未知-不支持) — answers "what's already configured" at a
  glance, which was previously invisible.
- Per-permission row uses lucide glyphs (Accessibility / Monitor /
  Mic / Bell / MousePointer2) + heading row with status pill +
  purpose + new "影响功能" impact chip + reason text.
- 请求授权 / 前往系统设置 buttons render only when `canRequest` /
  `canOpenSettings` from the snapshot — so unsupported platforms
  don't show inert buttons.
- Capabilities section moved below permissions and the 5-column
  internal-state detail (功能开关 / 配置 / 操作审批 / 记忆写入 /
  运行态探测) is collapsed by default behind a "展开详情" toggle.
  Power-users still get the diagnostic view; the casual user sees
  the readiness pill and impact, not the state machine.

**Visual fidelity (atlas-driven)**
- Card recipe (1px hairline + 6px radius + 4% lift + inner highlight)
  on rows and summary tiles.
- Status-tinted left border for denied / waiting / granted states.
- Icon glyph tinted to match permission state (granted = succe…

subject: PR-USE-SHADCN-BASE-UI-SWITCH — drop bespoke settings switch
body:
The Settings module had its own `function Switch(...)` (in
SettingsModal.tsx) that rendered a `<Button role="switch">` with a
single `<span/>` thumb, styled by ~50 lines of hand-rolled
`.settingsSwitch` CSS. That was a hand-rolled re-implementation of
the very same Base UI Switch we already ship via `@maka/ui`.

This PR keeps the local `Switch(...)` adapter (same external
`{ ariaLabel, checked, onChange, disabled, ariaDescribedBy }` shape,
so the 15+ callsites stay unchanged) and switches the implementation
to the shared `BaseUiSwitch` (`Switch` from `@maka/ui`, which wraps
Base UI `Switch.Root` + `Switch.Thumb` with Tailwind utility styling
on the project's semantic tokens — `data-[checked]:bg-primary`,
focus ring tied to `--ring`).

Why an adapter, not a callsite rewrite: the 15+ usages live inside
the 6900-line settings module that several lanes touch in parallel;
keeping the local shape lets us swap implementation without merge
conflicts.

The `.settingsSwitch` CSS block (~50 lines) is removed — the new
underlying component carries its own styling.

Visual delta is intentionally minimal: same track + thumb geometry,
same brand-deep checked state via `--color-primary` (which our
`@theme inline` block already aliases to `--accent`). Keyboard,
focus, and `[role="switch"]` semantics are now provided by Base UI
instead of a `<button>` with manual `aria-checked` / `data-checked`
attributes.

1464 desktop tests pass; renderer + main typecheck clean.

subject: PR-USE-SHADCN-BASE-UI-BADGE — pill spans become semantic badges
body:
The 6 status chips in `SettingsModal.tsx` were rendered as
`<span className="pill" data-tone="...">` — but there's no `.pill`
rule anywhere in `styles.css`, so they were shipping as plain
unstyled inline spans (text-only, no badge geometry, no tone color).

This PR:
- Re-exports the canonical shadcn `Badge` primitive from `@maka/ui`
  as `PrimitiveBadge` (renamed so it doesn't collide with the legacy
  `Badge` from `ui.tsx`). Variants: default / destructive / error /
  info / outline / secondary / success / warning.
- Adds a small `statusBadgeVariant(tone)` adapter mapping the
  project's status-tone vocabulary onto `PrimitiveBadge` variants:
  success → success, warning → warning, destructive → destructive,
  info → info, neutral → secondary.
- Replaces all 6 `<span className="pill" data-tone="X">` callsites
  in Capability Center, Permission Center, Health Center, and the
  Health Center summary blockers with `<PrimitiveBadge variant=…>`.

Visual: from unstyled inline spans → real Badge geometry with
status-tinted background tied to semantic Tailwind tokens
(`bg-success/8 text-success-foreground` etc.). Major upgrade in the
default state since the previous "pill" wasn't actually rendering
as a pill at all.

1464 desktop tests pass; renderer + main typecheck clean.

subject: PR-DELETE-DEAD-PRIMITIVES — drop 27 unused shadcn cva files
body:
`packages/ui/src/primitives/` had 54 shadcn-style cva primitive files
copied wholesale from upstream during the early "use shadcn" pivot.
Only a curated subset was re-exported from `@maka/ui/index.ts` and
imported by `components.tsx`; the rest sat in the bundle as dead
weight.

This commit deletes the 27 files that have zero non-self consumers
in the source tree:

  accordion, alert-dialog, avatar, breadcrumb, card, checkbox,
  checkbox-group, collapsible, combobox, context-menu, dialog,
  field, fieldset, form, label, meter, number-field, otp-field,
  popover, progress, radio-group, select, slider, switch, toast,
  toggle, toggle-group

= 3,316 LOC removed.

Verification done before deleting:
- `grep -rE "from .*primitives/<name>" --include='*.tsx' .` returns
  zero matches outside the file itself for each.
- Inter-primitive deps audited: `autocomplete`, `separator`, `sheet`,
  `skeleton`, `textarea`, `tooltip` were initially flagged dead but
  are pulled in by live primitives (command / group / sidebar /
  input-group) so they stay.
- `@maka/ui` builds clean.
- `apps/desktop` typecheck (main + renderer) clean.
- `apps/desktop` test suite: 1464 / 1464 pass.

The remaining 27 primitive files in `packages/ui/src/primitives/`
are all either re-exported from `index.ts` or transitively required
by something that is.

Real component implementations Maka actually renders (Button,
Switch, Badge, Card, Dialog, Tabs, Toggle, etc.) live in
`packages/ui/src/ui.tsx` — not the deleted primitive files — and
are untouched by this PR.

Per CLAUDE memory `feedback_bigger_prs.md`: one big bundled
deletion PR, not 27 tiny per-file PRs.

subject: PR-MOVE-PERMISSION-MODE: Move permission picker into composer dropdown (#129)
body:
WAWQAQ pushed back on the chat-header `只读 / 确认 / 执行` radiogroup:
they read as obscure jargon (`只读` "显然没有任何意义" for normal chat),
and live in the wrong place — the reference product expects the mode
picker where you actually start typing, not in the page chrome.

Changes:

- Drop the chat-header PermissionModeSwitcher entirely. The static `通用`
  role chip in the composer's left-controls becomes a 2-option Menu
  dropdown (base-ui), labelled by the active mode with a ChevronDown.

- Rename modes for legibility: `询问权限 / 自动执行`, with multi-line hints
  inside the popup explaining what each mode means.

- Retire `explore` (read-only) from the picker. Deep-Research sessions
  still set it internally, but it has no useful runtime toggle for
  normal chat — the picker collapses an `explore` session to display as
  `ask` so the user only sees the two modes that matter.

- Move the disabled-reason computation from ChatView to the <Composer/>
  call site in main.tsx (where the streaming + activeSession + pending
  signals already live). The chip surfaces the disabledReason as its
  tooltip; the menu auto-closes when disabled.

- Delete the orphan PermissionModeSwitcher function + its custom
  radiogroup keyboard handler. base-ui Menu carries arrow / Home / End
  navigation natively.

Test contract updates: `session-status-presentation.test.ts` now pins
the disabledReason wiring on main.tsx and the composer Menu pattern on
components.tsx (instead of the deleted chat-header switcher).

Verified visually via `turn-narrative/light-1280-motion`: chat header is
now a thin chrome strip with no mode chips, composer left shows the
`询问权限 ⌄` dropdown chip.

subject: PR-NAV-NEW-TASK-WEIGHT-0: unbold 新任务 + harmonize nav row size to reference (#130)
body:
WAWQAQ flagged two sidebar nav-row issues:

1. "新任务" was rendered with `font-weight: 600` permanently — it read as
   if it were the active page, even when nothing was selected. Active
   state already carries weight: 600, and reference nav rows are all
   normal weight at rest. The SquarePen glyph already signals "create",

2. `.maka-nav-row` was at 13px / 1.25 line-height (≈16.25px line). The

   reference sidebar uses. Bump to 14px / 1.43 to match.

subject: PR-SETTINGS-IA-CONSOLIDATE-0: 16→12 Settings nav items (reference IA) (#133)
body:
WAWQAQ flagged 网络代理 as "single-purpose page is wasteful" and asked
us to study the reference's settings shape. 4 parallel Explore agents
mapped reference's Preferences modal: 9 visible tabs, one giant
`Preferences` page that aggregates the system-level toggles (autoLaunch
/ proxy / notifications / preventSleep / etc.), rather than splitting
them across narrow pages. See notes/reference-settings.md for the full
RE.

Maka was sitting at 16 nav items, several of them single-block pages
that mirror exactly the pattern the reference avoids. Consolidation:

  - `网络` (proxy only)              → folded into 通用 as a sub-section
  - `主题` + `个性化`                → 外观 (Appearance)
  - `记忆` + `每日回顾`               → 记忆与回顾 (Memory & Review)
  - `语音模型` + `开放网关`           → 语音与网关 (Voice & Gateway)

16 → 12 visible nav rows. Each merge keeps both original page
components and renders them stacked inside the new section, so the
existing form-row logic / IPC wiring stays untouched.

Section id mapping (lock for url deeplinks):
  network         → general
  personalization → appearance
  theme           → appearance
  memory          → memory-review
  daily-review    → memory-review
  voice-models    → voice-gateway
  open-gateway    → voice-gateway

Notes/reference-settings.md (~400 lines) is the RE artifact — captures
the shell layout (256px rail + max-w-3xl content), the row/card
visual vocabulary, the per-tab control inventory, and the design
tokens. Future PRs convert each page body to the flat-row recipe;
this PR only moves the nav.

Renames touched: SettingsSection type (core), ALLOWED_SETTINGS_SECTIONS
(ui/maka-uri), SETTINGS_NAV + render switch (SettingsModal),
visual-smoke scenarios (`settings-network` → `settings-general`,
`settings-theme/personalization` → `settings-appearance`,
`settings-daily-review` → `settings-memory-review`),
FirstRunChecklist deeplinks, openSettingsSection callsites,
4 contract tests + 1 visua…

subject: feat: shared fault-tolerant Edit replacer (#131)
body:
One self-contained computeEditedSource (exact -> line-trimmed -> whitespace ->
escape) is the single matcher for both Edit surfaces: the in-process builtin
imports it; the isolated headless Edit embeds its .toString() into a node -e
script. Fuzzy is guarded (unique full-span match, reject too-short/
disproportionate/oversized/binary); exact is never gated. new_string is written
verbatim (no indentation migration, matching opencode). Headless Edit reads raw
bytes: valid-UTF-8 runs the shared matcher, binary files allow only a unique
exact byte-level replacement (no corruption). The native editFile fork was
removed so Edit unconditionally uses the shared replacer.

Re-land of #107 (reverted in #127) as Option A, with binary-safety, temp-file,
and single-source-of-truth fixes from independent review.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01KBnwuJ4pkhwcPjDubpFgiP

subject: PR-SETTINGS-PAGE-BODY-0: page body to reference recipe (max-w-3xl + flat rows) (#134)
body:
WAWQAQ msg 2c810f2d pushed back that PR-SETTINGS-IA-CONSOLIDATE-0
moved the nav but left the page body looking nothing like reference.
Specifically called out: Content Surface left/right padding is visibly
wider in reference; "the aesthetic is still not as good as theirs."

Second-pass pixel RE confirmed two structural deltas:

1. Reference content column is `mx-auto max-w-3xl pt-10 pb-16` (768px
   centered, 40px top, 64px bottom). Maka's `.settingsStructuredPage`
   had `gap: 16px` and no max-width — rows stretched edge-to-edge of
   the right pane, eating the whitespace WAWQAQ wanted. At a 1280px
   viewport with the 256px nav rail, reference's 768px centered column
   leaves ~128px whitespace on each side.

2. Reference rows are flat — no border-bottom between rows, no per-row
   plate, no shadow. Maka was:
   - `.settingsFormRow` had `border-bottom: 1px` + `padding: 6px 0`.
   - `.settingsRow` was sharing `.providerCard` bordered + tinted +
     shadowed chrome, which made every SettingRow render as a "box".

subject: feat(runtime/headless): bounded, recoverable Bash output (#92 P0)
body:
One shared runShellWithBoundedTail streams stdout/stderr into a memory-bounded
tail (last ~1MB per stream) and never kills a command for output size — used by
BOTH the in-process builtin Bash and the Harbor/local isolated executor (which
previously killed >10MB output mid-run and returned only the head). truncateToolOutput
bounds what the model sees to its budget with a recovery marker. Read/Glob/Grep
keep full, head-first output (bounded tail is Bash-only, via an opt-in flag).

Hardening (review rounds):
- Cap live emitOutput forwarding so a runaway command can't flood the event stream.
- Timeout/abort SIGTERM -> grace -> SIGKILL the whole process TREE (POSIX process
  group; Windows taskkill /T) and resolve only once the child has actually exited
  — no orphaned process, and no runner hang when a child holds the stdout pipe.
- Failures (incl. timeout/abort) carry the captured bounded tail + a conventional
  exit code (124/130), so the model can see why a command failed.
- Shared output-recovery hint across truncation/oversized-line markers; binary-safe
  Edit; redaction-safe tail boundaries.

Closes #92 (P0 tool-fidelity: Bash output).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01KBnwuJ4pkhwcPjDubpFgiP

body:
Final round of the 15-loop. Bundles 6 high-convergence findings from

`notes/ui-skills-deep-read-2026-06-23.md`):

1. **Shared motion tokens** — `--motion-fast: 160ms` +

   balance (no awkward 2-word breaks), subtitle gets pretty (no
   orphan last word).
6. **Hint floor 13 → 14px**, line-height 1.45 → 1.5 — crosses the
   ≥14px legibility floor that baseline-ui + UIDB + Impeccable
   `/typeset` independently require.

with 15 ships covering shell layout, IA consolidation, page body,

subject: feat(headless): add heavy-task benchmark evidence tools
feat(runtime): loop-gate repeated identical failing tool calls (#92 P1)
body: Block a tool call once the same tool+args has failed N consecutive times in a turn (#92: "same tool+args failing N times"). A success or any different call resets the streak, so polling and iterate-then-retry are never gated. Guard/permission rejections count as failures, so a repeatedly not-loaded tool is also gated. Non-zero terminal exit codes (headless Bash, which returns rather than throws) are classified as failures at deriveToolResultStatus() — the shared classification point for isError, telemetry, and the loop-gate. ToolRuntime per-turn state resets at the start of each turn, not only at cleanup.

subject: feat(desktop): headless visual-smoke capture, no foreground window (#161)
body:
Visual-smoke captures launched a real Electron window that took
foreground focus on every (scenario × variant), so the harness was
intrusive to run during interactive work — and it could not run from a
git worktree at all.

- main: under MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_FIXTURE, create the window with
  `show: false` and hide the macOS dock icon, so the capture process
  never shows a window or steals focus. `capturePage()` still returns a
  painted frame on a hidden window (paintWhenInitiallyHidden defaults to
  true). Verified across empty / modal / settings scenarios.
- main: resolve the fixture defensively. An unknown scenario (a stale
  build whose scenario set lags the capture script, or a typo'd env)
  previously threw during top-level module evaluation, which surfaced a
  blocking native error dialog and made the driver wait out its full
  ~60s marker timeout. It now logs a parseable line and exits fast with
  no dialog.
- scripts/capture-screenshots: resolve the electron binary via Node's
  upward module resolution instead of hard-requiring
  REPO_ROOT/node_modules/electron, so a git worktree (whose node_modules
  holds only the workspace's own @maka/* packages) finds electron in the
  parent checkout.

Real (non-capture) runs are unchanged — every branch is gated behind
MAKA_VISUAL_SMOKE_FIXTURE.

subject: feat(ui): add Item row primitive; fix first-run provider list overflow
body:
The first-run provider tiles dropped a 32px provider logo into an 18px
grid track, clipping the logo and overlapping the title. Rebuild the
list on a new shared `Item` primitive (shadcn Item rewritten onto Base
UI `useRender`) whose media slot is `shrink-0` with no fixed width, so
it sizes to the icon it wraps and that class of mismatch cannot recur.

- packages/ui: new primitives/item.tsx (Item / ItemMedia / ItemContent /
  ItemTitle / ItemDescription / ItemActions); exported from index. The
  clickable row hover stays neutral (a faint foreground wash), reserving
  the brand `accent` for active/selected rows.
- OnboardingHero: provider list now renders through Item; the dead
  .maka-onboarding-card grid CSS that caused the clip is removed.
- tests: re-target the hover-neutral and shared-primitive contracts to
  the new primitive instead of the deleted CSS class.

subject: feat(headless): Grep prefers ripgrep, falls back to find/awk (#92 P1) (#110)
body:
* feat(headless): Grep prefers ripgrep, falls back to find/awk (#92 P1)

The isolated/benchmark Grep walked every file with find + awk: no binary
skipping and only awk's ERE regex. GREP_SCRIPT now prefers ripgrep when
on PATH (skips binaries, far richer regex, fast) and falls back to the
original find/awk walk, unchanged, when rg is absent (minimal task
images). Output stays "<relpath>:<line>:<text>" with the per-file (50) /
total (200) caps.

rg runs with --no-ignore --hidden so it searches the same file set as the
fallback: results do not depend on whether rg happens to be installed.
--with-filename keeps the path prefix on single-file searches. rg is
always given an explicit path ("." for the whole workspace) so it never
blocks reading from an unclosed stdin pipe; the "./" it then prefixes is
stripped to preserve the bare relative-path contract. rg exit >1 (a real
error) surfaces instead of masquerading as "no matches". The existing
inside-workspace + no-symlink-escape validation runs before both paths.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01LarGqzE9nUj38RoCD1RdP9

* fix(headless): harden ripgrep Grep — no config/symlink escape, single glob dialect (#92)

Review follow-ups on the ripgrep Grep path:

- P1: pass --no-config --no-follow to rg. RIPGREP_CONFIG_PATH passes through the
  Harbor local executor (childProcessEnv spreads process.env), so a host-set rg
  config could inject --follow and make rg follow a workspace-internal symlink
  out of the workspace. --no-config ignores that config; --no-follow matches the
  find/awk fallback (find without -L). Closes the workspace-escape hole the PR
  assumed was already guaranteed.

- P1: use rg only when no glob is requested. rg's gitignore-style --glob is a
  different dialect from the fallback's ERE (globPatternToEre treats {}[],! as
  literals), so the same Grep returned different files with vs without rg. Gate
  the rg branch on [ -z "$glob" ] so every glob goes through the single fallback
  dialect; results are now identical regardless of whether r…

subject: feat(onboarding): selection-led first-run + real brand icons
body:
Redesign the needs_connection first-run hero around picking a provider:
title leads, the three setup steps compress into one quiet numbered
stepper, and a scrollable provider list (Item rows) is the subject.

- Vendor official brand SVGs from @lobehub/icons-static-svg (MIT) in
  provider-brand-marks.tsx; ProviderLogoMark now delegates to them, so
  Settings 模型 rows get the same real icons (Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/
  DeepSeek/Moonshot/Zhipu/Ollama) instead of hand-drawn SVGs.
- Neutral, theme-aware provider plate (drops hardcoded light tints).
- De-version provider copy: highlight the provider, not model versions
  (no "GPT/Responses", no "V3"); update the OAuth-section contract test
  expectations to match.
- Remove now-dead .maka-onboarding-grid block and the orphaned
  maka-card-enter / maka-agents-fade-in keyframes.

Brand marks ship under their owners' trademarks; SVGs are MIT-licensed.

subject: fix(settings): wrap the 已启用模型 strip instead of clipping chips
body:
The enabled-models band was a horizontal scroller; once a few models
were enabled the chips ran off the right edge and got cut mid-word.
Put the header on its own row and let the chips wrap below, with a
max-height + vertical scroll for long lists, so every enabled model
stays fully visible.

subject: fix(settings): align enabled-models chips + clean tab selected state
body:
- Enabled-models strip: a flex-wrap left every chip a different width
  (sized to its own text) so rows never lined up. Lay the chips on an
  even auto-fill grid and truncate long names, so columns align and the
  band reads as one tidy group.
- Catalog pill tabs: hide Base UI's sliding rounded-md indicator. Our
  pills supply their own fill, and the indicator's square corners poked
  out from behind the 999px pill, making the selected state look warped.

subject: feat(headless): RSI prompt-optimization loop, validated end-to-end (#64) (#149)
body:
* fix(headless): make ai-sdk Harbor cell cost and prompt hash controller-consistent

The prompt-optimization controller round-trips two values it reads back from the
Harbor cell output, but the ai-sdk cell path broke both, so every task would fail
plumbing classification once wired to the real controller (unit tests passed only
because they used self-consistent fake runners):

- Prompt hash: harborCellSystemPrompt() prepended a fixed preamble to
  config.systemPrompt before the runtime hashed it, so the emitted
  systemPromptHash was stableHash(preamble+candidate) while the controller
  expects hashSystemPrompt(candidate) -> prompt_hash_mismatch every task. An
  explicit prompt is now passed through byte-for-byte (the preamble remains only
  as the default for prompt-less ad-hoc cell runs). This also makes "the prompt
  being optimized" equal "the prompt that runs".

- Cost: builtin pricing has no deepseek-v4-flash entry, so computeCost(null)
  returned 0 -> costUsd=0 with tokens>0 -> zero_cost_with_tokens plumbing failure,
  and the budget ceiling would never trip. The cell now honors the same
  MAKA_TRIAL_*_USD_PER_1M env the Python adapter (trial_pricing.py) already reads,
  so one pricing source feeds both runtime cell cost and Harbor trial cost.

Tests cover explicit-prompt pass-through and the pricing override.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_015yqs8KqJo3DB5DtkGeiNTA

* feat(headless): secret-safe API key files + trial pricing into the ai-sdk cell

Two more prerequisites for running the prompt-optimization loop on the real
ai-sdk + DeepSeek path:

- Key files: the ai-sdk cell now resolves an API key from `<NAME>_API_KEY_FILE`
  (e.g. DEEPSEEK_API_KEY_FILE) in addition to the raw env var. The Harbor adapter
  already forwards the *_API_KEY_FILE path, so the secret can stay in a mounted
  file and only its path travels through the Harbor CLI / job config — never on a
  command line. A raw key still takes precedence. (Resolves the deferred
  AI-SDK _API_KEY_FILE support noted in PR #85.)

- T…

subject: feat(ui): add governed Accordion primitive (Base UI)
body:
Wrap Base UI's Accordion (Root / Item / Header / Trigger / Panel) the
same way the Tabs primitive is wrapped (cn + data-slot), and export the
Primitive-prefixed aliases from @maka/ui. Gives the design system a
single reusable, governed disclosure component instead of hand-rolled
expand/collapse.

subject: refactor(settings): enabled models as provider→connection accordion
body:
The enabled-models list flattened providers and connections into one
peer level, which read wrong (a connection and its provider looked like
siblings) and felt cramped. Rebuild it as a Base UI Accordion: each
provider is a collapsible row that owns its connections, with the worst
connection status rolled up to the header so problems show while
collapsed. The provider holding the default connection and any failing /
needs-relogin provider start expanded; healthy ones stay compact.

Connection rows render on the shared Item primitive; the old bespoke
chip grid and its CSS are removed. Contract tests updated: the @maka/ui
import check tolerates multi-line formatting and now also pins the
Accordion + Item primitives, and the no-raw-button rule allows Base UI's
`render={<button/>}` polymorphic target.

The zai-coding-plan provider reused the legacy 智谱 (Zhipu) molecule mark,
but the product surfaced here ('Z.AI Coding Plan') carries the Z.ai

subject: refactor(settings): enabled models as a seamless aligned list
body:
The enabled-models accordion sat in a bordered, filled card — the
heaviest element on the 模型 page, clashing with the catalog grid below
and reading as 'two visual languages'. Drop the card box for a seamless
hairline-bounded list (whitespace + logo plates carry the structure),
and align the columns: connection rows now sit directly under the
provider name (pl 49 = pad-x 6 + logo 32 + gap 11) with their status
right edge aligned to the provider rollup (pr 32 = pad-x 6 + chevron 15
+ meta gap 11). Healthy statuses stay quiet; only problems take color.

subject: refactor(settings): converge provider catalog onto the Item primitive
body:
The 模型 catalog (国内/海外/本地 cards) and the OAuth login cards were
hand-written: a Button (or, when gated, a raw <div>) wrapping <span>
copy styled by bespoke .providerCatalogCard CSS. They were the last part
of the page not on the shared shadcn Item primitive, so the page still
mixed two component languages.

Render every catalog + OAuth entry as a governed Item (ItemMedia ·
ItemContent[ItemTitle/ItemDescription] · ItemActions) — the same
primitive the enabled connections use — in a seamless single-column
hairline list. Gated providers become an inert div Item (no render prop)
with a real trailing state badge instead of an absolute ::after, so the
provider-surface gate still keeps them unclickable. Removes the dead
.providerCatalogCard CSS skin.

The two contract tests that pinned the old card geometry (8px radius,
fixed 172px height, auto-fill 198px grid, .providerCatalogCard/Copy
class names, ::after badges) are repointed to the new intent: governed
.providerCatalogRow chrome shared across all tabs, single-column
seamless list, squared (non-pill) badges. Typecheck + all 1467 desktop
tests pass.

subject: fix(settings): vertically center catalog row trailing badge
body:
The category / preview / login badge sat inside ItemTitle (the row's
first line), so it pinned to the title baseline instead of the row
center on two-line rows. Move it into ItemActions next to the chevron —
a direct child of the row's items-center flex — so badge and chevron
share the row's vertical center.

subject: refactor(settings): declutter the model page (live-test feedback)
body:
Two papercuts found while testing the running app:

- The model page opened with a '.settingsPageIntro' row that paired a
  non-actionable hint ('如果配置遇到问题，可以查看配置指南。' — plain
  text, no link) with a 'N 个模型' badge that just restated the count
  already shown by the 已启用模型 header right below it. Removed the
  whole intro row (and its now-orphan CSS); the page starts at the
  enabled-models list.

- The unified row lists carried BOTH a container top+bottom border AND
  inner row separators, so the catalog stacked a line under the tabs and
  doubled a line against the 自定义供应商 border. Drop the container
  borders on all three lists (enabled / market / oauth); inner hairlines
  between rows now carry the separation on their own.

subject: refactor(settings): pilot Base UI Field on the model config sheet
body:
Convert ConnectionDetail's 连接标识 / 服务地址 / 模型密钥 rows from the
hand-written <label><span/> markup to the governed Base UI Field
primitive (FieldRoot + Label + FieldDescription from @maka/ui), which
already existed but had no consumers. The label tone is pinned to the
established Settings caption (text-xs / foreground-60) so the rows stay
visually identical; the parenthetical state hints (OAuth 固定 / 已设置…)
now render on their own FieldDescription line under the label.

Scoped to this one sheet as a pilot: AddProviderForm and every other
Settings form stay on the legacy markup. The model-oauth copy contract
is repointed to the new Field structure while keeping the copy
Chinese-first.

subject: fix(settings): keep model config header clear of the close button
body:
The provider config sheet's close button is absolutely positioned in the
top-right corner (.providerConfigSheetClose, 30px at right:14px), but the
ConnectionDetail header's right-aligned status badges (默认 / 国内) were
laid out flush to the content edge and slid underneath the X. Reserve the
corner with a 36px right padding on .providerEditor header so the badges
(and any long provider title) clear the close affordance.

subject: refactor(settings): drop inert maka-button class from ProvidersPanel buttons
body:
Every governed @maka/ui <Button> in ProvidersPanel also carried a
className="maka-button". That legacy class is defined in maka-tokens.css
under @layer components, but the Button primitive's own styling lives in
@layer utilities, which wins under Tailwind v4 layer ordering — so the
maka-button class was inert dead weight on these buttons (colors, size,
border, radius, hover, focus and disabled all came from the Button
variant, not maka-button).

Remove all 18 occurrences. Repoint the three model-oauth contract
assertions that pinned the class, and add an a11y-contract guard so a
governed Button can't silently regrow the legacy class. The legacy
maka-button system stays in place for the raw <button> holdouts in other
surfaces (composer, memory, network gateway) that still rely on it.

subject: fix(settings): align model config sheet header + flush the refresh button
body:
Three layout fixes on the provider config sheet, verified live via CDP:

- Refresh button stranded ~36px from the card's right edge. Root cause:
  the close-button clearance rule was written as the descendant selector
  `.providerEditor header`, which also matched the nested
  `.modelTableHeader` and leaked its `padding-right: 36px` onto the model
  card, capping the right-aligned button short of the edge. Scope it to
  the direct child `.providerEditor > header` so only the sheet's own
  title/badges header reserves the corner.
- Model table header switched to `align-items: flex-start` so the refresh
  button sits on the title line instead of floating mid-block, and the
  grid gets an explicit `minmax(0, 1fr)` column so the header spans the
  full card width.
- Close button raised to `top: 10px` so it centers on the header's first
  line, lining up with the title and the right-aligned status badges.

subject: fix(settings): give the model config close button its hover back
body:
.providerConfigSheetClose hand-rolled rest/hover/focus styles on top of the
governed Button but leaned on --settings-surface-* / --text* tokens that were
never defined, so hovering the X did nothing. Strip the class down to position
+ circular shape and let the Button's variant="quiet" size="icon-sm" own the
rest/hover/focus states.

Update the contract test to assert the close button reuses the quiet icon
Button, replacing the assertion on the now-removed hand-written focus outline.

subject: feat(headless): Read numbers lines, caps output, guards binaries (#92 P1) (#116)
body:
* feat(headless): Read numbers lines, caps output, guards binaries (#92 P1)

The isolated/benchmark Read tool dumped raw file contents with no line
numbers, no cap (a huge file flooded the context), and no binary guard.
READ_SCRIPT now matches Claude Code / opencode:

- cat -n line numbers (absolute, so offsets stay meaningful)
- a default 2000-line cap, with a hint to continue via a larger offset
- over-long lines (>2000 chars) clipped with a marker
- a NUL-byte binary guard that reports "[binary file: N bytes, ...]"
  instead of dumping garbage (command substitution strips NULs anyway)

Formatting lives in the script path, which is the one the benchmark/Harbor
executor uses: no executor provides the optional native readFile hook, and
an executor that does provide it owns its own formatting.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01LarGqzE9nUj38RoCD1RdP9

* fix(headless): Read enforces #92's 50KB byte budget, add per-line clip test (#92 P1)

Review follow-ups on the rebased Read change:
- P1 (must-fix, grounded in #92 'default 2000 lines / 50KB'): READ_SCRIPT had only
  the line cap + per-line column clip, no total byte budget — a 1500-line medium
  file (~1.2MB) could still flood the model. The awk output loop now accumulates
  emitted bytes and stops at ~50KB (whichever of the line/byte caps comes first),
  always emitting at least one line, with the same offset continuation hint.
- Reformulate the Harbor P1 head-first regression test for the byte-budget regime:
  it now asserts the head (HEAD_MARKER) is returned, the tail (TAIL_MARKER) beyond
  the budget is not, output is bounded (<64KB), and a continuation hint is present.
  Preserves the original 'not tail-bounded' guard.
- P3: add a precise per-line clip test (one 2500-byte line -> line number + exactly
  2000 bytes kept + '... [line truncated]' marker).

P2 (native executor.readFile bypasses the new contract) deferred: Read/Write/Glob/
Grep all share a symmetric optional native fast path, none implemented in
production today; routing them thro…

subject: fix(settings): rename 模型连接 section and stop mislabeling lapsed OAuth logins
body:
The Settings → 模型 band was titled "已启用模型" but grouped and counted
every connection, and labeled OAuth subscription logins that had lapsed
(stored as enabled:false + needs_reauth) as "已禁用". A connection that
only needed re-login read as user-disabled, and the enabled-only rollup
left a group holding only a lapsed login showing idle at the header.

- Rename the band to「模型连接」(it is the configured-connection list);
  count and empty state stay on the full list, now honestly named.
- chipStatusText: needs_reauth wins over the disabled check (需要重新登录);
  a bare enabled:false (legacy migration only) reads 暂不可用, never 已禁用.
- rollupForGroup spans the full group, so a lapsed-only group raises a
  warn header and auto-expands instead of reading idle.
- Extract chipStatusText/rollupForGroup into a pure provider-connection-status
  module with direct behavioural tests; relax the contract test to verify the
  dedicated helper import.

The deeper enabled/auth state-model decoupling and the chat-readiness /
health copy stay as the documented follow-up.

subject: feat(headless): Glob enumerates via ripgrep, falls back to find (#117)
body:
* feat(headless): Glob enumerates via ripgrep, falls back to find (#92 P1)

The isolated/benchmark Glob walked the tree with `find -type f`, which is
slow on large trees. GLOB_SCRIPT now enumerates with `rg --files` when on
PATH and falls back to find otherwise. rg runs with --no-ignore --hidden
so it sees the same file set as find, and the glob match itself stays the
existing ERE (applied in awk), so results are byte-identical with or
without rg — only enumeration speed changes, never which files match.

rg is given an explicit relative path (so it never blocks on its
never-closing stdin pipe) and runs from root so its output is
root-relative; the "./" it prefixes is stripped to match find's paths.
--no-config makes rg hermetic so a stray RIPGREP_CONFIG_PATH (a dev's
global --follow/--sort/--glob) cannot change its file set or output format
while find stays unaffected. Both branches search the same symlink-resolved
base (rel_base, derived from existing_target's $base) so a search cwd
reached through a symlinked parent resolves consistently. existing_target
still validates the search base before both paths.

The 200-result cap is applied AFTER a fixed-locale sort in both branches,
not during enumeration: rg and find traverse in different orders, so
capping mid-walk would return different first-200 subsets when more than
200 files match. Sorting first makes the truncated set deterministic and
identical across both paths.

The test exercises a >200-match workspace to prove rg and find return the
same capped set, and pins the find branch to a temp bin holding only the
coreutils GLOB_SCRIPT needs (not rg), so the parity check stays real even
where rg lives in /usr/bin.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01LarGqzE9nUj38RoCD1RdP9

* fix(headless): Glob surfaces ripgrep runtime errors, trims rationale (#92 P1)

Review follow-up on the Glob rg branch:
- Check rg's exit code (rc>1) and surface a real error instead of swallowing
  it into an empty result, mirroring the merged Grep rg branch. The rc check
  r…

subject: feat(runtime): serialize file writes per path with withFileWriteLock (#92 P1)
body:
The AI SDK runs a step's tool calls concurrently, so a model emitting two
Edits (or an Edit and a Write) to the same file in one step races on the
read-modify-write and silently loses an update — exactly what opencode's
per-file Semaphore prevents.

Add a minimal `withFileWriteLock(key, fn)` (runtime, file-write-lock.ts) that
serializes async work per key and reclaims keys once their chain drains. It is a
low-level primitive: the headless package reaches it only through the
`@maka/runtime/file-write-lock` subpath and it is marked `@internal`, kept off
the public barrel so external callers cannot join this process-global queue by
accident. Apply it to the file-mutating tools on both surfaces that write
workspace files:

- runtime builtin Write + Edit (`builtin-tools.ts`)
- benchmark/headless Write + Edit (`headless/src/tools.ts`)

Each surface keys on the path — the builtin on the resolved absolute path, the
headless on a JSON [cwd, path] pair across the executor boundary — derived
identically for Write and Edit so they lock the same file and spellings ("a",
"./a", "d//a") collapse. The key is lexical (realpath only on the cwd), so it is
stable across a file's creation and never splits one file across two keys
mid-flight. Edit and Write share the key, so an Edit and a Write to one file
also serialize; different files stay parallel. Path resolution now runs inside
the lock, so the containment check and the read-modify-write form one atomic
critical section per file.

Known limitations, matching opencode's lexical (path.resolve) Semaphore key:
one file reached under two names — via a symlinked parent dir, a hard link, or a
case-insensitive filesystem ("a.txt" vs "A.txt") — takes two keys and is not
merged; Bash is not serialized, since a per-file lock cannot key arbitrary shell.

Tests: withFileWriteLock unit suite (serialization, parallel distinct keys,
no-lost-update, rejection recovery); concurrent-Edit no-lost-update and
mixed-spelling serialization on both the runtime and headless paths; a headless
ex…

The PR removes the per-suggestion dismiss / bulk restore flow,
removes the inline `导入文件内容` / `导入文件夹目录` buttons from the
first-run composer, switches suggestion clicks from append-to-draft
to replace-the-draft, and narrows the composer card from 680 → 620.
All four are intentional design changes (confirmed against the PR
description and the new UX).

Existing contract tests pinned the old shape literally and were
failing as a result. This commit retargets them to lock in the
new design instead of the deleted one:

  - `first-run-task-suggestions.test.ts`
    - "dismissible and restorable…" test → rewritten to assert
      visibleSuggestions = FIRST_RUN_TASK_SUGGESTIONS, nextDraft =
      prompt (replace, not append), and no leftover dismiss/restore
      UI strings remain in the source.
    - "gates first-run import actions" anchor moved from the now-
      removed `const importTextFile` to `const importActionBusy`;
      drop/paste assertions kept (those still exist via the textarea
      wrapper); file/folder button-click assertions dropped (UI
      removed).
  - `text-file-import.test.ts`
    - "appends prompt suggestions instead of replacing existing
      drafts" renamed to "appends in main composer but replaces in
      first-run hero" and split to pin the two separate behaviors
      explicitly.
    - "wires the import action…" — dropped the two `/导入文件内容/`
      / `/导入文件夹目录/` assertions against onboardingSource (UI
      strings removed). Drop/paste handler wiring still pinned.
  - `project-context-badge.test.ts`
    - workspace-row width pin 680 → 620, comment updated.

Tests: 1484/1484 pass against the PR's new shape. Full build clean.
