  match the split nav (separate `voice` + `open-gateway` items) and
  the new Settings-navigation copy.
- `maka-uri.test.ts`: section enu…

subject: PR-GENERAL-DEFAULTS-CONFIGURABLE-0: real General-page default-model control (#307)
body:
* PR-GENERAL-DEFAULTS-CONFIGURABLE-0: turn read-only General-page rows into real controls

WAWQAQ msg `d3ea9a33` 2026-06-26: "通用里面的很多选项现在都完全无法配置，
显然都应该是可以配置的。" The General page used to ship a `<SettingsRows>`
block with three read-only `<SettingRow>` lines — 启动 = "已启用" /
新对话模式 = "询问权限" / 默认模型 = current default-slug as text only.
They read like settings but had no configurable backing: the static
text was the entire UI.

Fix — drop the two without backing storage, make the third real:

- 启动 (Resume last session): no `AppSettings` field backs this, the
  renderer always restores last session. The row was just labelling
  the hardcoded behavior. Drop it; adding a real "open to last
  session vs. new chat vs. home" choice needs new persisted state
  and is its own PR.
- 新对话模式 (Default permission mode): permission mode is already
  configurable per-session in the composer's permission chip. There
  is no persisted "default for next chat" setting and adding one
  cuts across the composer + chat session subsystems. Drop the row.
- 默认模型 (Default model connection): `connections.setDefault(slug)`
  IPC already exists. Replace the read-only row with a new
  `<GeneralDefaultsCard>` component — a real `<SettingsSelect>` that
  lists every enabled `LlmConnection`, persists the choice via the
  existing IPC, refreshes the parent connection list on success, and
  surfaces a toast on failure.

The component carries the same mounted-ref + savingRef synchronous
ownership pattern the rest of SettingsModal uses (PR-STOP-ERROR-SURFACE-0
/ PR-BOT-RESTART-RACE-0 lineage) so:
  - duplicate selects don't race,
  - in-flight saves can't write React state after Settings closes,
  - failures surface a localized toast instead of an unhandled
    promise rejection.

Verifies green:
  apps/desktop$ npx tsx --test src/main/__tests__/*-contract.test.ts
  → 439 pass / 1 pre-existing flaky (subagent-ui SSR; unchanged on ma…

subject: PR-PALETTE-SWATCH-VISIBLE-0: define per-palette swatch background on Appearance picker (#308)
body:
WAWQAQ msg `d3ea9a33` 2026-06-26: "现在外观里面的颜色都没有显示出来，显然
是有bug的。" The Appearance palette picker composes each swatch class as
`settingsPaletteSwatch settingsPaletteSwatch-${palette}` (e.g.
`settingsPaletteSwatch-coral`), but `styles/settings/theme-preview.css`
only declared the base `.settingsPaletteSwatch` recipe (size, border,
shadow) — no per-palette `background-color`. Result: every swatch
inherited the default `transparent` and read as an empty grey circle.

Fix: add a `.settingsPaletteSwatch-{name}` rule for every entry in
`THEME_PALETTES`, each carrying the palette's light-mode `--accent`
value verbatim from `maka-tokens.css` so the picker previews the
palette's brand identity even when the current page is on a different
palette.

Also added a safety-net fallback on the base `.settingsPaletteSwatch`
rule: if a future palette lands in `THEME_PALETTES` without a matching
swatch rule, the swatch falls through to the active `--accent` instead
of going invisible (so the regression is visible but soft).

Contract follow-up:
- `renderer-style-pruning-contract.test.ts`: allowlist the 11
  `settingsPaletteSwatch-*` selectors as dynamic style hooks. They
  never appear verbatim in renderer source (template-string class
  composition), but they're consumed at runtime by ThemeSettingsPage.

Verifies green:
  apps/desktop$ npx tsx --test src/main/__tests__/*-contract.test.ts
  → 439 pass / 1 pre-existing flaky (subagent-ui SSR; unchanged on main).

subject: PR-PERMISSIONS-UNIFIED-CARD-0: unify OS permission rows + horizontal actions (#309)
body:
* PR-PERMISSIONS-UNIFIED-CARD-0: unify OS permission rows into one grouped card + horizontal actions

WAWQAQ msg `d3ea9a33` 2026-06-26 (two halves of the same issue):

> "权限与能力，里面的权限部分都是应该并在一块的，现在不是一个单独的权限组件，
> 和其他的组件不一样。其他的模式，这里面相关的东西都是并在一块的，而不是中间
> 有间隔和空白。"

> "现在权限与能力里面，权限的部分有些地方有两个按钮，这个按钮的设置太不美观了。
> 一个是请求授权，一个是前往系统设置，它们的颜色感觉配置不美观，位置也配置的
> 不美观，需要重新设置。"

## Unify the rows

The OS permission list used to render each row as its own bordered
card with a 12px gap between, so the section read as 4 floating chips
instead of one grouped settings card like the rest of Settings
(`.settingsRows` on General / Bot Chat / Daily Review).

- `.settingsOsPermissionList` is now the single grouped card — owns
  the outer border, 12px radius, background, `overflow: hidden`.
- `.settingsOsPermissionRow + .settingsOsPermissionRow` contributes a
  6%-foreground top hairline divider (matches the existing
  `.settingsRow` divider treatment elsewhere).
- Per-row border / radius / shadow / hover transition dropped — the
  status accent now lives as a 3px left-edge stripe (`::before`)
  tinted by `--destructive` / `--warning` / `--success` so denied /
  not_determined / granted still read at a glance without re-introducing
  the per-row cards.

## Re-do the two buttons

The actions stack used `flex-direction: column / align-items: flex-end / gap: 6px`
with the request button as `default` primary and the open-settings
button as `secondary`. Two same-weight buttons stacked vertically read
as two competing options instead of a primary + secondary pair.

- `.settingsOsPermissionActions`: horizontal flex, `justify-content: flex-end`,
  8px gap, centered alignment so the row stays flush.
- JSX order flipped: 前…

subject: PR-UI-DEAD-EXPORT-SWEEP-0: delete 26 unused ui.tsx exports + lock-contract (#325)
body:
Net: -222 lines from ui.tsx (797 → 590), +112 lines for the new
dead-export contract.

Background: `packages/ui/src/ui.tsx` accumulated ~30 named exports that
no source file outside ui.tsx itself imported. Most were Base UI primitive
re-exports (`DialogTrigger = BaseDialog.Trigger`) or shadcn-style wrappers
that components.tsx + every renderer/primitive consumer ignored. They
cost autocomplete noise, bundle weight, tsc work, and design-discussion
clarity ("we have a Card variant" — except nothing uses it).

Deleted (no external consumer, no internal use):
- Card family: Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent
- Avatar family: Avatar, AvatarImage, AvatarFallback
- Accordion (entire section): all 5 — `packages/ui/src/primitives/accordion.tsx`
  already owns the alive duplicates exported as `PrimitiveAccordion*`
- ScrollArea (entire section): ScrollAreaRoot, ScrollAreaViewport,
  ScrollAreaScrollbar — `primitives/scroll-area.tsx` owns the alive impl
- Popover (entire section): PopoverRoot/Trigger/Portal/Backdrop/Positioner/Popup
- Dialog sub-pieces: DialogTrigger, DialogTitle, DialogDescription
- Field sub-pieces: FieldControl, FieldError
- Tooltip sub-pieces: TooltipRoot, TooltipPortal, TooltipPositioner
- Sheet sub-piece: SheetRoot
- Form re-alias of BaseForm

Demoted from `export` to internal `const` (used only inside ui.tsx by
DialogContent/SheetContent/SheetPopup wrappers):
- DialogPortal, DialogBackdrop, DialogPopup
- SheetSide type

Also removed 6 now-unused Base UI imports (Accordion, Avatar, Form, Popover,
ScrollArea, Toast) and 1 unused icon (`ChevronRight`).

Locked the rule with `ui-tsx-dead-export-contract.test.ts`:
- every named export in ui.tsx must have a consumer outside ui.tsx OR be
  in `ALLOWED_PENDING` with a justification (empty by default; capped at 5)
- second assertion prevents `ALLOWED_PENDING` from rotting unmaintained

Updated existing `ui-tsx-design-contract.test.ts`: z-50 count 5 → 4
(PopoverPopup deletion), `transition-[height]` entry removed (Accordion
dele…

subject: PR-RENDERER-CSS-DEDUPE-0: remove 7 lines of dead CSS dupes + lock contract (#326)
body:
Scanned all of apps/desktop/src/renderer/styles for selectors that
declare the same property twice in the same block. Found 3 blocks
with 6 dupes total — last-wins means the earlier declaration is dead.

Fixed:
- settings/bot.css:389-392 — `.settingsRow` first did `display: flex` +
  3 flex props (align/justify/gap=10), then `display: grid` + grid props
  (align/gap=16). The flex setup is leftover from before the grid
  rewrite. Deleted 4 lines.
- onboarding.css:83-84 — `.maka-onboarding-ready header` first set
  justify-items:start + text-align:left, then center for both. Deleted
  the 2 dead lines.
- onboarding.css:542 — `.maka-onboarding-quickchat-submit` had
  `min-width: auto` then later `min-width: 40px`. Deleted the dead one.

Locked the rule with `renderer-css-duplicate-decl-contract.test.ts`:
parses every .css under styles/, tracks brace depth + selector context,
flags any block that declares the same non-custom property twice. Skips
at-rule wrappers (`@media` / `@layer` / `@supports`) since their
redeclarations are intentional. Skips CSS custom properties (`--*`).

Companion to PR-UI-DEAD-EXPORT-SWEEP-0 — both are the "清理" (cleanup)
lane: delete dead code that survived a refactor, lock the rule so it
doesn't recur. Surfaced by @maka-审美专家's punch-list-organize.md
finding #7 + grep generalization suggestion.

Tests: contract passes (0 dupes remaining). Full desktop suite unaffected.

subject: PR-SIDEBAR-GLASS-VIBRANCY-0: make appFrame transparent on darwin so vibrancy shows (#329)
body:
WAWQAQ msg c356717b → 6c0b8a7c: the reference layout's `light-glass` /
`dark-glass` theme is what we set out to model on macOS, but the
classic-light look is what's actually rendering. Diagnosis:

- `main.ts:1724` already enables `vibrancy: 'sidebar'` on BrowserWindow
- `theme-glass.css` already sets `body { background: transparent }`,
  `.maka-shell-2col { background: transparent }`, sidebar panel as
  translucent + backdrop-blur on macOS
- But PR-CHAT-CHROME-FIX-1 (commit comment in sidebar.css) made the
  `.appFrame` root explicitly opaque `var(--surface-canvas)` to kill a
  separate gradient bleed. That outer opaque cover sits between the
  Electron vibrancy substrate and the rest of the chrome, so the
  native blur material is masked away even though it's enabled

Fix: gate `.appFrame { background: transparent }` on
`html[data-os="darwin"]`. The macOS surface chain becomes
`vibrancy substrate → transparent appFrame → transparent shell →
translucent session panel (with backdrop blur)`, while the chat / settings
right pane keeps `.maka-panel-detail { background: var(--background) }`
so chat content stays solid + legible.

Non-darwin builds are unaffected (rule scoped to `[data-os="darwin"]`).

Tests: `tsc --noEmit` clean. Pure-CSS, single-rule scope so no logic
changes; visual verification needs a real macOS window.

subject: PR-BOT-ICONS-REAL-ANTD-0: swap 4 hand-drawn bot icons for real Ant Design paths (#328)
body:
PR-BOT-LOGO-NEUTRAL-PLATE-0 (#300) shipped iOS-app-icon style brand tiles
with white silhouettes on brand-color discs, but my locally-composed SVG
bodies for wechat / wecom / dingtalk / qq were hand-sketched approximations
that drifted in 4 visible ways:

- `wechat` double-bubble path extended past the 24×24 r=12 circle bounds,
  so the white shape got clipped at the disc edge
- `wecom` was essentially a WeChat-shaped double-bubble — no distinctive
  Tencent Enterprise WeChat brand mark
- `dingtalk` "D-wave" silhouette was a stylized approximation, not the
  recognizable Alibaba Dingding mark
- `qq` penguin was positioned in the lower-right quadrant (path mis-
  centered) and used `#EB1923` red, which is the older legacy palette —
  the canonical QQ identity is the light-blue penguin

This PR vendors the real Ant Design Icons (MIT) brand paths for all 4:
  - `wechat`         filled  → 1024-grid two-bubble + eye dots
  - `wechat-work`    filled  → 1024-grid (with translate(112,112)), real
                                Tencent Enterprise mark
  - `dingtalk`       outlined → 1024-grid official DingTalk silhouette
  - `qq`             outlined → 1024-grid penguin (no outer circle), so
                                our brand disc stays visible

Each entry now has the full 1024 viewBox, with `<circle cx=512 cy=512
r=512 fill=BRAND/>` as the disc and the brand path in `fill=#fff`.

Brand color corrections (#EB1923 red → #12B7F5 light blue for QQ):
  - `bot-brand-icons.ts` disc fill
  - `bot-brand.ts` PROVIDER_BRAND.qq.color (drives the status-dot tint)
  - `bot.css` `.settingsBotHero[data-provider="qq"]` (drives the hero
    wash + child accent inheritance)

Untouched (already real-source, working):
  - telegram (Logos collection, multi-color gradient)
  - discord (Logos collection, filled blurple Clyde)
  - feishu (3-tier staircase from feishu.cn brand kit)

* feat(ui): govern tool-result preview surfaces onto @maka/ui previewVariants (#332 PR4)

Retire the bespoke OverlayPreview family shell CSS onto a single literalize
table, the last result-preview island in the chat conversation flow.

What moved onto `previewVariants` (packages/ui/src/primitives/chat.tsx):
- the shared `.maka-overlay-preview` base + `.maka-overlay-close`
- file-diff, terminal, office-document, explore-agent + subagent, web-search cards
- the bare json/text/rive `<pre>` dumps

Each retired declaration becomes a 1:1 literal arbitrary utility, so the cva
source string is the computed-style proof. Descendant rules on bare tags are
folded into their container part via `[&_tag]:` variants, so call sites swap
only the container className. Structured cards apply `cn(overlay, kind)`; the
base's white-space / font-family are arbitrary so the kind overrides win by
tailwind-merge, reproducing the retired two-class source-order cascade.

`previewVariants` is exported on the @maka/ui barrel: the file-diff parts have a
second, cross-package consumer — apps/desktop's non-chat artifact-preview.tsx,
which co-migrates here so the shared `.maka-tool-diff*` shell retires without a
temporary shared layer. The card-entrance `@keyframes maka-tool-card-enter`
moves to maka-tokens.css (the canonical motion home).

Zero visual change: locked by a new chat-preview cascade contract (retired
selectors gone, keyframe relocated, escape literals + barrel export pinned,
scale-drift banned). tool-output.css drops 535 lines, tool-stream.css 105.
The PR3b contract releases `.maka-tool-diff*` / `.maka-tool-terminal*` (now
legitimately retired); visible-copy / office / explore / subagent contracts

subject: PR-MODEL-ROW-AND-OAUTH-EMAIL-FIX-0: 2 of 6 bug bundle (WAWQAQ c71b4dcb / 77221a77) (#373)
body:
Two of WAWQAQ's screenshots flagged 6 issues. This PR ships the 2 with
clean root causes (1 CSS + 1 backend); 4 others split out per scope:

### Bug 1 fixed: model-row second line clipped (1 of 4 model-list bugs)

`.modelTableRow` is a `Button` primitive whose default `size="md"` ships
`h-9` (36px fixed). Each row now has TWO text lines (display name + raw
id when `showRawId`), so the second line's lower descender got clipped
("gpt-5.4" → "ant 5.4" in the screenshot). Override on the recipe:

  height: auto;
  min-height: 44px;
  padding: 8px 10px;  /* was 4px 8px */

This also resolves the apparent "middle row has a different background"
bug — the user just hovered/focused that row; with the taller row it
reads as expected hover state, not a separate visual.

### Bug 6 fixed: OAuth display name leaks user email

`apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts` was constructing the connection display
name as `Claude OAuth · ${state.profile.email}` (and the symmetric Codex
path). That value is persisted as `connection.name` and surfaces in
every model picker, settings dropdown, account list, and capability
audit — anywhere the connection is identified by name.

The email belongs on the Account · 模型 page, not in model identity.
Both paths now use brand-only labels:

  const displayName = 'Claude OAuth';
  const displayName = 'Codex OAuth';

The email is still available via the OAuth state for the dedicated
account surfaces that legitimately need it. Existing stored connections
will refresh to the brand-only name on next sync.

### NOT in this PR — split out for scope

- **Bug 5 (default-model dropdown lacks model selection)** — requires a

subject: PR-QODERWORK-GLASS-RESHIP-L3-L6-L7: re-ship the 3 layers that never landed (#375)
body:
The original PR-QODERWORK-GLASS-LAYERED-0 push tried to ship 5 layers
(L1+L2+L3+L6+L7) but only L1 (vibrancy unblock) actually landed on main
via the squash-merge of PR #329 (`7d008d51`). The follow-up commit
`023b90b2` was force-pushed to the same branch but the merge happened
before that, so for the past 3 days users have been seeing the
QoderWork "classic" look despite the substrate being correctly enabled.

This re-ships L3 + L6 + L7 from that delta:

**L6 — warm token vocabulary**
New CSS custom props on `html[data-os="darwin"]`: `--color-bg-layout
#fdfcfa`, `-highlight #c9c4b8`, `-container #faf9f6`, `-element
#f5f3ee`, `-state-selected #8ee5a1`, `-text-quaternary 45%-fg`. Notable
rename per @maka-审美专家 token-naming critic: was going to be
`--color-row-selected` / `--color-primary`, now `--color-state-selected`
so it's reusable for chip / tab / nav selected states without renaming
later.

**L3 — selected vs hover finally distinguishable**
Active `.maka-list-row[data-active="true"]` paints `var(--color-state-
selected)` (mint `#8ee5a1`) instead of the 6% foreground wash that was
identical to `:hover`. Font-weight stays 500 — the color block IS the
signal. Active session reads at a glance instead of "where's the cursor
again". Same root cause as punch-list-polish-2 #S2 (hover/selected
indistinguishable) which has been stuck for 3 days because L3 never
shipped.

**L7 — un-stylize group label**
`.maka-list-group-label` was 9.5px UPPERCASE + 0.08em tracking on the
sidebar; reads as "Windows admin tool" not "macOS native sidebar".
QoderWork uses plain quaternary text at body size. Drop the size /
transform / tracking on macOS; the natural color step is enough.

NOT in this PR:

- **L2 (blur 8px → 24px)** — needs real-window verification of the blur
  strength before pinning a number
- **L1 (move blur from .maka-session-panel to .agents-layout-root)** —
  needs coordinated move of the existing 8px rule, separate concern
  from this "re-ship what was missing" PR

All gated on `[data-os="darwin"…

subject: feat: speed up dev startup with incremental tsc + default HMR mode (#374)
body:
- tsconfig.base.json: add incremental:true for all packages,
  tsc generates .tsbuildinfo caches, subsequent builds only
  recompile changed files (~3s vs ~30s).

- package.json: switch default 'dev' script from full production
  build to Vite HMR dev server, skipping the 10s vite build step.
  Old behavior preserved as 'dev:full'.

- apps/desktop/scripts/dev-hmr.mjs: fix Windows electron binary
  resolution — node_modules/.bin/electron is a POSIX shell script
  that Node spawn() cannot execute on Windows; resolve directly
  to electron.exe instead.

fix(desktop): recognize OAuth-subscription connections in onboarding (#389)
body:
* fix(desktop): recognize OAuth-subscription connections in onboarding

Onboarding's hasApiKey only checked the API-key credential store, so
a Claude/Codex Subscription connection set as default was always
reported as missing credentials — even when Settings showed it
verified and default — leaving the first-run flow stuck on "set a
default connection". Route onboarding's secret resolution through the
same resolveConnectionSecret the send-path already uses, which
special-cases OAuth-subscription connections.

* fix(desktop): make onboarding's credential check read-only

Address review on #389 (@Astro-Han):

- Split resolveConnectionSecret's OAuth-refreshing path from a new
  read-only hasConnectionSecret / hasStoredCredential check, so
  onboarding's getSnapshot can no longer trigger a token refresh
  (network call + local token-state mutation) just by being read.
  Send/test/fetch-models paths keep the refreshing resolver.
- Rename OnboardingServiceDeps.hasApiKey to hasCredential and change
  its signature to take the LlmConnection directly instead of a slug,
  avoiding a redundant connectionStore re-read and keeping onboarding
  state derived from one consistent connection snapshot.
- Add regression coverage: a source-pattern contract test proving
  hasConnectionSecret never calls the refreshing
  getAccessTokenInternal(), plus onboarding-service unit tests
  proving getSnapshot doesn't trigger OAuth refresh and that the
  bound hasCredential receives the identical connection object.

subject: refactor(ui): split module-panels.tsx into per-domain sibling files (#408)
body:
* refactor(ui): split module-panels.tsx into per-domain sibling files

module-panels.tsx grouped three unrelated domains (skills, daily-review, plan-reminder) by form rather than domain. The domain boundary already exists in the helpers and types; this moves each panel's render body into its own sibling file so the layout matches.

Move-only: no behavior, props, types, or helpers changed. Drops the dead Count component (no call site). chat-view imports the three panels from their new files. Source-contract tests that read the panel source by path are repointed; skills.test SkillsModuleMain regex boundary changes from the next same-file export to end-of-file; two scan-style tests expand to all three new paths.

ui typecheck + build pass; renderer bundle byte-identical (5990.55 kB); 12 contract assertions re-resolve against the new files; no module-panels references remain.

* test(desktop): replace stale function-block sentinels with brace-matching helper

The module-panels split left contract tests reading the new per-domain panel files but still using the old end sentinels: daily-review-copy-feedback used function PlanReminderPanel (now in a different file) as the DailyReviewPanel boundary, silently producing empty panelBlock strings; skills.test used EOF as the SkillsModuleMain boundary, imprecise against future additions.

Adds extractFunctionBlock: a test-only helper that tracks paren depth past the parameter list, then brace-matches the function body (skipping strings, template literals, and comments). Throws on miss so a stale extraction fails loudly instead of degrading to empty-string assertions.

Repoints all six panelBlock extractions and the skillsModuleMain extraction to the helper. Adds assert.ok on three derived blocks (gateBlock, manualRunBlock, archiveLoadBlock) so a missing inner pattern also fails immediately.

Verified: daily-review-copy-feedback 9/9, daily-review-export-file 3/3, skills 17/17 — all pass against compiled dist/ output.

* test(desktop): use extractFunctionBlock for PlanReminde…

subject: fix(renderer): clear leftover ring-shadow vars on wrapper-owned textareas (#391)
body:
* fix(renderer): clear leftover ring-shadow vars on wrapper-owned textareas

Several inputs use the "outer wrapper owns the visible chrome, inner
Textarea/Input stays bare" pattern via JSX overrides like `border-0
shadow-none focus-visible:ring-0`. Those overrides only remove the
utility classes from the className string — they don't clear the
base Textarea/Input's `focus-visible:ring-2` custom properties
(`--tw-ring-shadow` / `--tw-ring-offset-shadow`), which can
re-composite into `box-shadow` on focus and render as a second
"framed" border nested inside the wrapper's own border.

- Onboarding quick-chat textarea (`.maka-onboarding-quickchat-input`):
  add explicit `border: none; box-shadow: none;` plus the ring-var
  reset.
- Composer textarea (`.maka-composer-textarea`): same explicit reset
  — it had only the ring-var clear, not `border`/`box-shadow`.
- Skill search input (`.maka-skill-search input`): already had
  `border: 0; box-shadow: none;` from a prior fix
  (PR-SKILL-SEARCH-DOUBLE-BOX-0), but relied on an unrelated global
  `:where(input, select, textarea):focus { ... !important }` fallback
  to suppress the leftover ring vars. Add the same explicit reset so
  it doesn't depend on that fallback rule staying scoped the way it
  is today.

Verified against a live app window: the composer's nested border is
gone.

* fix(renderer): make wrapper-owned textarea resets survive focus

The previous fix's border/box-shadow resets weren't !important, so
the global `:where(input, select, textarea):focus { ... !important }`
rule in module-pages.css (which paints its own inset accent ring on
every focused field) always won regardless of the more specific
selector, repainting the exact nested-border bug on focus — the state
every screenshot of this bug was actually taken in. Verified live via
Chrome DevTools Protocol against both the production build and the
Vite HMR dev server: computed box-shadow on the composer/onboarding/
skill-search inputs is `none` while focused, and a CDP screenshot in
the focused state…

subject: fix(desktop): stop chat header status badges overlapping the workspace toolbar (#419)
body:
* fix(desktop): stop chat header status badges overlapping the workspace toolbar

The session-status / connection / event-stream alert badges rendered
inline in .maka-chat-header, right-aligned via the header's flex
spacer — which put them directly underneath the absolutely-positioned
.maka-workspace-top-actions toolbar (top-right corner) once more than
one badge was showing at once. Moved the badge cluster out of the
header's flow into its own row, anchored directly below the toolbar
and sharing its right:24px baseline. Also trimmed the status pill's
padding/font-size so its visual weight matches the neighboring alert
badge and toolbar icons.

* fix(desktop): cap chat status cluster width and dedupe toolbar geometry

Addresses review feedback on #419 (Astro-Han):

- .maka-chat-status-cluster had no max-width or wrapping behavior.
  With three badges (session status + connection alert + event-stream
  alert) and long Chinese labels in a narrow window, the shrink-to-fit
  row could grow past the left edge of the panel/window. Added
  `max-width: min(60vw, 320px)` + `flex-wrap: wrap` so an oversized
  row wraps onto a second line (still right-aligned) instead of
  overflowing horizontally.

- --maka-workspace-top-actions-bottom duplicated .maka-workspace-top-
  actions' `top`/`right` geometry inline, and .maka-chat-status-cluster
  hard-coded its own `right: 24px` on top of that -- one layout
  contract in two places. Split the token into
  --maka-workspace-top-actions-{top,height,right,bottom} in
  maka-tokens.css so both rules read from the same source, and updated
  sidebar.css's .maka-workspace-top-actions to consume the new tokens
  instead of its own inline calc.

- Dropped .maka-chat-header-status's now-redundant `margin-right: 6px`
  -- spacing between badges comes from .maka-chat-status-cluster's
  `gap` since the badges moved out of the header's flow in the
  previous commit.

Verified the wrap fix with a static repro using the real CSS (three
long-label badges in a 460px panel): pre-fix the row grows t…

subject: fix(desktop): stop chat header mode pill overlapping the top-actions toolbar (#423)
body:
* fix(desktop): stop chat header mode pill overlapping the top-actions toolbar

The .maka-chat-header-mode-pill (read-only "深度研究/只读探索" mode pill) and the
model switcher render right-aligned inside .maka-chat-header, which reserved
only 10px of right padding. The .maka-workspace-top-actions toolbar in the
top-right corner is position:absolute (out of flow, z-index 4), so the header
never reserved horizontal space for it and the right-aligned pills flowed
underneath the toolbar — the help / health-center icons rendered on top of the
mode pill text.

PR-CHAT-HEADER-STATUS-CLUSTER-0 (#419) fixed the same class of collision for the
status badge cluster only; the in-header pills were left overlapping. Add a
--maka-workspace-top-actions-inset token (the toolbar's shared right offset plus
its 24px icon-button footprint) and reserve it as the chat header's right
padding so in-flow right content clears the toolbar. Adds a layout contract test
mirroring chat-status-cluster-layout-contract.

Verified via CDP against the running app: mode pill right edge moved from x=1392
(under the toolbar at x=1268-1382) to x=1252, a 16px gap; no overlap.

subject: refactor(ui): govern all border-radius via --radius-* tokens (#406 gap 4) (#418)
body:
* refactor(ui): govern all border-radius via --radius-* tokens (#406 gap 4)

Migrate every hardcoded border-radius literal (Npx) in renderer CSS
and every rounded-[Npx] arbitrary value in TSX to the token system
(--radius-control 6px / --radius-surface 8px / --radius-modal 12px /
--radius-pill 999px). Delete the --card-radius and
--agents-content-area-radius intermediate variables; their call sites
now reference the canonical tokens directly.

Intermediate values (2/3/4/5/7/9/10px) collapse to the nearest
component-type tier: button/input/chip/kbd/inline-code → control,
card/popover/code-block/toast/notice/table → surface, modal/composer
→ modal, pill/badge/dot → pill. Non-symmetric shorthand (e.g.
blockquote 0 8px 8px 0) now uses per-corner token references.

The radius-converge-contract test now scans the full CSS and TSX
source tree, banning any bare Npx border-radius or rounded-[Npx]
that is not a var(--radius-*) reference (0/50%/inherit/initial
exempt). Six existing contract tests that asserted literal Npx
values are updated to assert the token references.

Closes #406 gap 4.

Address code review on PR #418:
- P2: replace permissive --radius-* regex with explicit whitelist

  private tokens now fail the contract
- P2: scan border-{top,bottom}-{left,right}-radius longhand and
  rounded-{t,tr,tl,b,br,bl,l,r}-[...] directional utilities, not just
  shorthand and rounded-[...]
- P2: pin each canonical token's resolved px value (6/8/12/999) so a
  silent edit of --radius-modal is caught
- P3: collapse CSS collection to a single readAllRendererCss() entry —
  it already recursively unfolds styles.css imports (which include
  maka-tokens.css and the styles/ tree); remove the duplicate scans
- P3: sync the stale header comment (was '8px modal / 10px code block',

* fix(radius)…

subject: refactor(ui): ban bare ease keywords, enforce var(--ease-*) tokens (#431)
body:
* refactor(ui): ban bare ease keywords, enforce var(--ease-*) tokens

Add a new invariant to motion-token-converge-contract.test.ts banning
bare ease/ease-out/ease-in-out/linear timing keywords in renderer CSS
and @maka/ui TSX. Replace all bare keywords with var(--ease-out-strong)
or var(--ease-in-out-strong). linear in infinite-loop animations
(spinners/shimmer) is whitelisted.

- Fix dead --ease-maka reference in ui.tsx (→ --ease-out-strong)
- Update chat-tool-card-cascade-contract assertion to match token usage

Part of #430.

* test(ui): cover bare ease-in, tighten linear infinite whitelist

Codex review findings on #431:
- [P2] BARE_EASE skipped hyphenated values, so bare `ease-in` slipped
  through the contract. Add BARE_EASE_IN.
- [P2] linear whitelist skipped the whole line when both `linear` and
  `infinite` appeared, letting a `linear` in a same-line `transition`
  pass. Whitelist per match, not per line.

* test(ui): single longest-match timing matcher, segment-scoped linear whitelist

Address code review on PR #431:

P1: the five separate regexes let bare `linear` bypass the contract.
`ease-linear` (Tailwind class → `transition-timing-function: linear`)
fell between `ease` and `linear` regexes (both rejected by the
hyphen lookbehind/lookahead). A same-line `transition ... linear;
animation ... infinite` was shielded entirely because the whitelist
skipped the whole line on any `infinite` match.

Replace with one longest-match-first matcher using matchAll:
`ease-in-out | ease-linear | ease-in | ease-out | ease | linear`.
Split each line into `;`-delimited declaration segments and scan
each independently — `linear` is whitelisted only when its own
segment contains `infinite`, so a transition `linear` on the same
line as an infinite animation is still flagged.

P3: `ease-in-out` was reported twice (as `ease-in` AND
`ease-in-out`) because the separate `ease-in` regex matched the
prefix. The single ordered matcher resolves this — longest token wins.

Add a fixture `it` locking the four review cases:…

subject: fix(ui): finish --ease-linear in animation catalog stories (#434)
body:
Follow-up to #431: the merged PR tokenized shimmer but left the
status pulse as bare `ease-in-out` and the Easing Scale story
without the new `--ease-linear` entry. Finish the catalog so all
developer-facing easing examples use `var(--ease-*)`.

subject: Fix/startup white screen (#429)
body:
* fix: add inline skeleton loader in index.html to prevent white screen on startup

- Add inline CSS + HTML skeleton in index.html that renders before
  React mounts and external CSS/JS loads
- Skeleton matches .maka-onboarding-loading design with hardcoded
  theme-aware colors (light/dark via prefers-color-scheme)
- No external dependencies — visible immediately after HTML parse
- Eliminates blank white/gray screen during 355KB CSS + 5.8MB JS window

* review: fix failing contract tests, radius tokens, and scope leaks (#429)

- startup-loading-shell contract: the second test asserted a
  Suspense/StartupFallback/maka-preload wiring in app.tsx that was never
  implemented (app.tsx imports AppShell eagerly). Repoint it at the actual
  fix — the inline preload skeleton in index.html — so it guards real
  behavior instead of a red, aspirational contract.
- onboarding.css: the new .maka-onboarding-loading skeleton used bare
  border-radius: 12px/999px, violating the radius-token governance
  contract (#406/#418). Use var(--radius-modal)/var(--radius-pill).
- package.json: restore root `dev` to the governed `dev:hmr` fast-HMR path
  (real-window-smoke contract); the new scripts/dev.mjs launcher stays
  available via `@maka/desktop run dev`.
- .gitignore: drop the machine-specific `.suncode/` IDE dir leak.

Full suite green: core/storage/runtime/headless/ui + 1671 desktop tests.

---------

Co-authored-by: jackwener <jakevingoo@gmail.com>

subject: fix(ui): keep first-run CTA above the fold; unsquash skills capability-audit strip (#436)
body:
Two visual-audit fixes from fresh 1280x800 screenshot review:

- first-run: three stacked paddings (home-surface chatContent
  clamp(72,10vh,116) + onboarding-stack clamp(40,7vh,76) + a 56vh
  provider list) pushed the primary CTA below the viewport — the very
  first screen a user sees rendered a half-clipped button. Flatten the
  outer padding stack for .maka-firstrun only, bound the provider list
  with clamp(240px,42vh,420px), and compress rows py-3 -> py-2 so all
  six providers plus the CTA fit at the default window size.
- module-skills: .maka-module-main defines two grid rows but the skills
  page stacks three direct children, so the capability-audit strip
  collapsed into minmax(0,1fr) at ~0 height and its caption bled over
  the banner below. Any module main hosting the strip directly now gets
  a third row via :has().

Desktop suite green (1671 tests).

subject: fix(ui): stop daily-review empty state floating over reports; repair stale-session banner layout (#438)
body:
Round 2 of the visual screenshot audit:

- daily-review: the .maka-daily-review-summary-empty override lived in
  @layer components, but the shared .maka-empty-state base is unlayered
  position:absolute centering — unlayered rules always beat layered
  ones, so the override silently never applied and the '等待记录今天活动'
  card rendered as a translucent overlay on top of the archived report
  text. Move the override out of the layer with panel-scoped
  specificity.
- stale-session banner: the Alert primitive ships w-full, which plus
  the 14px horizontal margins made the banner 28px wider than the pane
  (right edge clipped against the card); align-items:center parked the
  warning icon on the middle of three lines; and AlertDescription's
  flex-col blockified the inline copy around <strong> into three
  stacked fragments. width:auto + flex-start + display:block restore a
  single inline-flowing line with the icon on it.

Desktop suite green (1671 tests); before/after fixture screenshots
verified for module-daily-review + stale-sessions.

subject: fix(ui): pin artifact toolbar separator to a 1px hairline (#439)
body:
At narrow artifact-pane widths the toolbar wraps; the ToolbarSeparator
rendered as a ~15px bordered pill next to the delete button — visual
noise that read as an empty broken button. Pin it to flex-basis 1px
with no padding/border/radius so wrapped layouts show at most a quiet
hairline. Markup untouched (the separator element is locked by
artifact-pane-lifecycle-contract).

Verified via artifact-errors fixture screenshots; desktop suite green.

subject: fix(ui): pin skills banner copy to fixed inks for dark mode (#440)
body:
The featured-skills banner keeps its light-blue gradient in both themes,
but its copy followed the theme foreground: in dark mode the title
rendered near-white on the pale gradient (unreadable ghost text) and
the floating art cards showed white-on-white copy. Pin the banner and
art-card text/border/shadow to fixed dark inks matched to the banner's
200-224 hue so the card reads identically in both themes.

Verified via module-skills light+dark fixture screenshots; desktop
suite green.

subject: fix(ui): stop leaking the raw absolute MEMORY.md path in settings (#442)
body:
The memory settings meta row rendered the full absolute path
(/private/var/folders/…/MEMORY.md) as a full-width mono line, which
shoved the sibling status words (上一版备份 / 草稿已保存 / N 条生效)
into a cramped vertical stack — and leaked a raw absolute path into
the renderer against the UI quality plan (§3.6).

Display layer only: displayMemoryPath() shows the meaningful trailing
…/<workspace>/memory/MEMORY.md segments, title= keeps the full path on
hover, and the copy-path action still copies the real path. The meta
row wraps so status words sit on their own line, with a nowrap/ellipsis
safety net on the path span.

Contract kept: 等待创建 MEMORY.md stays inline in MemorySettingsPage
(local-memory-ui-contract). Desktop suite green (1671).

subject: fix(ui): artifact file list crushed to 40% of its own content height (#444)
body:
Fixes #443.

.maka-artifact-list had max-height: 40%, but the list sits in an
auto-sized grid row — Chromium resolves the percentage against the
track's own content height, so the list was always crushed to 40% of
ITSELF: a single-file pane showed ~12px of its one row (filename
unreadable, preview surface overlapping it), and the six-file
artifact-errors fixture showed only ~2.2 rows.

Move the cap onto the grid track as fit-content(40%), which resolves
against the pane's definite height and means what the original rule
intended: content-sized up to 40% of the pane, then scroll.
minmax(auto, 40%) is wrong too — the maximize-tracks step inflates it
to the full 40% even for one row.

Verified via DOM geometry dumps (list 16px -> 39px for one file;
208px with all six rows visible for the errors fixture) and fixture
screenshots. Desktop suite green (1671).

subject: refactor(ui): split --accent into semantic aliases, lock call-site governance (#441)
body:
Closes #406 (PR1 + PR5).

Migrate every raw var(--accent) call site to its semantic alias
(--link, --focus-ring, --status-running, --nav-active, --toast-accent,
--action, --control) and add a block-aware contract that bans
var(--accent) outside token definition blocks and palette preview.

- maka-tokens.css: ~24 component call sites hidden inside the token
  file migrated to semantic aliases; --bot-brand-default added so
  bot.css stops referencing --accent directly.
- All styles/*.css + packages/ui/src + apps/desktop/src/renderer TSX:
  ~200 raw var(--accent) references replaced with matching alias.
- design-system-governance-406-contract.test.ts: new contract bans
  var(--accent) by scanning CSS block nesting (must be in :root/.dark/
  [data-maka-theme]/@theme inline) + token-name allowlist + renderer
  TSX scan. Cross-platform via basename()/relative().
- Dark mode contrast fix: onboarding submit button and markdown
  task-list checkbox moved from bright --accent fill + white text
  (contrast ~2.4:1) to --action/--control with --action-foreground/
  --control-foreground (clears 4.5:1).
- Hero empty-state decorations use --brand-deep, not --link.

Final var(--accent) count: 39 references, all in maka-tokens.css
(definitions), styles.css (@theme bridge), theme-preview.css (palette).

subject: fix(desktop): keep collapsed topbar actions clickable
body: Keep collapsed titlebar actions visually in place while carving overlapping drag regions so clicks reach the buttons.

subject: refactor(ui): converge font-size to two-tier token scale (#430 PR2) (#437)
body:
* refactor(ui): converge font-size to three-tier token scale (#430 PR2)

Ban bare Npx/Nrem font-size across all renderer CSS. 413 sites collapse
into three tiers: --font-size-base (15px), --font-size-ui (13px),
--font-size-caption (11px). Bubble h1-h4 switch from px to em off body 15.

Mapping:
  9/10/10.5/11/11.5px + sub-0.8rem  -> --font-size-caption (11px)
  12/12.5/13/13.5/14px + 0.8-0.95rem -> --font-size-ui (13px)
  15px                               -> --font-size-base (15px)

Tailwind: --text-xs→caption, --text-sm→ui, --text-base→base.
Contract test bans bare px/rem; pins 15/13/11; verifies alias mappings.
Docs synced to three-tier scale.

* fix(ui): bump settings hint to --font-size-base, slim contract test (#430 PR2)

- settingsField small: --font-size-ui (13px) → --font-size-base (15px)
  to clear the 14px legibility floor noted in the prior comment.
- contract test: remove unused EM_RE and duplicate bare px/rem scan;
  8 tests → 7, same coverage.

* test(ui): catch font: shorthand bypass in typography contract (#430 PR2)

Scanner now flags bare px/rem inside font: shorthand (e.g.
'font: 12px/1.4 var(--font-sans)') so the bypass route is locked.
Added positive and negative cases; 9 tests, all pass.

subject: fix(ui): stop showing healthy legacy sessions as 已阻塞; compact sidebar timestamps; localize bypass label (#452)
body:
Three fixes from a real-app screenshot report (main window, 2026-07-03):

1. Blocked display semantics. The #410 terminal-ledger invariant marks
   legacy sessions missing a terminal fact as blocked/unknown. The
   sidebar groups by raw status, so intact, resumable conversations
   piled up under an 已阻塞 group with shield icons and the chat header
   badge read 已阻塞 — for sessions where the model had answered and
   nothing needed the user's attention. Session-level blocked is now a
   display-layer distinction: only actionable reasons
   (NO_REAL_CONNECTION / auth / permission_required) keep the blocked
   presentation; tool_failed / unknown normalize to active at the
   renderer state boundary (commitSessions / upsertSessionSummary), so
   grouping, row icon, and header badge agree. Runtime status writes
   are untouched. Regression: session-status-display-normalization.test.ts.

2. Sidebar timestamps. Sessions older than 7 days rendered the full
   medium date + time (2026年6月20日 16:33, ~150px) which crushed the
   title next to it to ~2 characters. New formatCompactTimestamp in
   @maka/core: relative inside 7 days, date-only beyond (6月20日 within
   the year, 2025年6月20日 across years). Sidebar-only; wide surfaces
   keep formatRelativeTimestamp.

3. i18n consistency. The composer permission-mode pill showed English
   'Bypass permissions' between two Chinese modes; now 跳过确认 in the
   composer, command palette, session settings actions, and the
   account settings copy.

Desktop suite: 1685/1685; typecheck clean.

subject: fix(ui): revive dead darwin glass palette, fix token typos, dark mint contrast, and review fallout (#454)
body:
Full frontend code-review sweep (3 parallel deep reviews: renderer /
@maka/ui+settings / CSS system). This lands the CSS findings plus two
cross-cutting fixes:

- theme-glass.css: the darwin warm-glass palette sat inside @layer base
  while reference-shell.css defines the same custom properties
  unlayered - unlayered beats layered, so the documented
  parchment-glass look NEVER shipped. Moved out of @layer and added
  dark-mode variants; --color-state-selected mint #8ee5a1 deepens to
  oklch(0.42 0.075 152) in dark so the selected session row is no
  longer near-white text on light mint.
- Undefined-token typos silently dropping styles in 5 places:
  var(--surface)/var(--text)/var(--text-muted)/var(--surface-hover) do
  not exist - search-modal input row, command-palette input, browser
  panel nav/address/empty, artifact image matte now use real tokens.
- styles.css --shadow-maka-panel blur layers: fixed rgba(15,23,42,*)
  navy literals to oklch(from var(--foreground)) so the panel shadow
  tracks the theme.
- module-pages.css .maka-skill-add-button: hex CTA recipe to oklch
  equivalents (same rendered look, one token language).
- FirstRunChecklist: optional-call-then-nullish-fallback fired the
  fallback unconditionally (void return) - explicit branch; contract
  updated to pin the correct pattern.
- storybook form-controls stories: 9 english section labels to Chinese
  (check-a11y was failing on main).

Desktop suite: 1685/1685; check-a11y clean.

subject: feat(design): refinement roadmap from 4-source skill study + P-SHADOW recipes (#457)
body:
Docs: docs/design-refinement-roadmap-2026-07.md distills a systematic
study of four design-skill sources (taste-skill, superdesign,
ui-skills.com ecosystem incl. emil-design-eng / interface-design /
impeccable / make-interfaces-feel-better, vercel-labs agent-skills /
web-interface-guidelines) against what maka already shipped in the
2026-06-24 skills round. It records the four owner decisions (4pt
spacing system over pixel-parity; dark mode as a first-class theme;
fast-and-precise motion personality; no signature element yet) and an
ordered delivery queue (P-SHADOW ... P-STATE), plus an explicit
"not adopting" list with CJK adaptations.

Code: P-SHADOW, the first queue item.
- Light recipes: every blur layer in --shadow-minimal/-medium/-modal
  was pure-black rgba. On the warm gray shell a pure black shadow
  reads as a smudge (taste-skill ban: shadows must carry the surface
  hue). All layers now derive from the warm foreground via
  oklch(from var(--foreground)) so ring, blur, and ink share one
  light source. Opacities unchanged.
- Dark collapse (decision D2): blur stacks are invisible-but-costly
  on a dark shell, and foreground-derived layers turn white-ish and
  glow. Dark mode now carries elevation with border rings
  (minimal/flat/medium), modal keeps one deep drop for backdrop
  separation.

Verified with light+dark fixture screenshots (turn-narrative,
settings-general); desktop suite 1685/1685.

subject: feat(design): P-INSET input step-down + P-RADIUS concentric corners (#458)
body:
Queue items 2-4 of docs/design-refinement-roadmap-2026-07.md.

P-INSET (interface-design "inputs sit slightly darker = type into
here"):
- palette input row + search-modal input row stepped DOWN from
  background-elevated to a 3% foreground wash - they sit ON an
  elevated modal surface, so elevated-on-elevated read as no
  hierarchy at all.
- generic Input/Textarea (shared inputClasses): bg-background -> 2%
  foreground wash. Foreground-derived, so dark mode adapts free.
- composer deliberately NOT changed: it is the command deck, not a
  form field - its elevated lift is the intended reading
  (PR-UI-LAYOUT-8).

P-RADIUS (concentric formula: inner = outer - inset):
- palette modal and search dialog are 12px shells with 8px insets;
  the input rows' 6px control radius read "too round" against the
  shell corners. Now calc(var(--radius-modal)-8px) = 4px. NOTE: the
  radius contract's calc allowlist splits values on whitespace, so
  the no-space form is required.

P-MOTION audited as already-compliant (no code): duration tokens
120/150/180/280ms match the Emil table at the low end, decorative
entrance animations were already removed by #406 gap 3, remaining
keyframes are functional streaming indicators (D3 exemption).

Roadmap statuses updated. Desktop suite 1685/1685.

subject: feat(design): P-4PT spacing ratchet contract + P-TEXT orphan tier cleanup (#459)
body:
Queue items 5-6 of docs/design-refinement-roadmap-2026-07.md
(owner decision D1: 4pt grid, incremental migration).

P-4PT: new spacing-4pt-ratchet-contract.test.ts scans renderer CSS
padding/gap/margin px values for 4pt-grid compliance (0 allowed,
1px/2px hairline exempt). The 442 legacy off-grid values are frozen
per file and may only shrink; new files are born clean; a slack check
keeps baselines honest so the ratchet cannot rust.

P-TEXT (first pass): the -20/-30 foreground tiers were orphans (8 call
sites) sitting between the surface washes (2..10) and the real text
ladder (40..80):
- onboarding group count text 30 -> 40 (also a contrast fix: 30% ink
  fails the 4.5:1 line)
- chat.tsx status dots 30 -> 40
- resize handle / bot status dot / context-plus border: inlined their
  color-mix (decorative one-offs, not text tiers)
- tier definitions + @theme bridge removed; design-tokens story scale
  updated (20 -> 50 so the displayed ladder matches the real one)

Desktop suite 1687/1687 (adds 2 ratchet tests).

subject: docs(design): mark refinement queue round 1 complete (P-SHADOW..P-STATE) (#460)
body:
All eight queue items resolved across #457/#458/#459: four landed as
code (shadow recipes + dark collapse, input inset step-down,
concentric radii, 4pt ratchet + text tier cleanup) and four audited
as already-compliant or covered (motion durations, dark semantics,
state coverage). Records next-round candidates.

subject: refactor(ui): converge spacing to --space-* scale, ban bare px (#430 PR3) (#448)
body:
* fix(ui): --spacing 3.75px bug -> 4px ruler + define --space-* scale (#430 PR3)

The --spacing token was 0.25rem, which under the 15px root font-size
equals 3.75px, not the 4px the comment claimed. This silently broke
alignment between Tailwind's spacing scale (p-2 = 7.5px) and hand-written
px (padding: 8px) — two unrelated scales.

Fix the ruler to absolute 4px in both :root and @theme inline so Tailwind
utilities (p-N, gap-N, m-N) and hand-written var(--space-N) share one
source. Define a 14-value scale covering the codebase's real spacing
distribution: 0/2/4/6/8/10/12 below 16 (even numbers, dense UI needs the
6 and 10 half-steps), 16/20/24/32/40/48/64 at and above (pure 4px
multiples). 1px stays a literal (hairline). Deliberately absent: 14/18/
22/26/28/30/34/36/52/56 — snapped to the nearest scale value in the
follow-up commits to kill magic numbers.

Per the #430 adversarial review, spacing has no single-property anchor
like border-radius, so the upcoming contract scopes to padding/margin/
gap only (not width/height/inset/grid).

* refactor(ui): replace all bare px spacing with var(--space-*) tokens (#430 PR3)

Replace every bare px value in padding/gap/margin across renderer CSS
(689 sites) and TSX arbitrary spacing utilities (125 sites) with
var(--space-*) tokens or Tailwind scale utilities. Snap non-scale values
to the nearest scale tier: 3->2, 5->4, 7->6, 9->8, 11->12, 14->12,
18->16, 22->24, 26->24, 28->24, 30->32, 34->32, 36->40, 52->48, 56->64,
72->64. Negative margins use calc(var(--space-N) * -1). 1px stays a
literal (hairline). Responsive clamp()/max() bounds stay literal (they
are viewport parameters, not spacing beats).

Update 6 contract tests that pinned the old bare-px literals to expect
the new var(--space-*) or scale-utility forms. The chat-header inset
contract's pxDeclaration helper now resolves var(--space-*) tokens to
their px equivalents so the geometric invariant (buttons x width + gaps
+ clearance = inset) still holds.

* test(contract): spacing-converge-contract bans bare px in…

subject: docs(design): record P-4PT handoff from ratchet to #448 converge ban (#461)
fix(ui): clear the outstanding UI-review P0s and follow-ups (#463)
body:
Closes out every remaining item from the 2026-07-03 three-way code
review instead of leaving them as suggestions:

- Search modal focus yank (P0): selecting a result navigated to the
  chat turn but useModalA11y unconditionally restored focus to the
  sidebar search trigger on unmount, defeating the call site's
  restoreFocus:false intent. The hook now takes an optional
  suppressFocusRestoreRef; SearchModal sets it when navigation owns
  focus. Lifecycle contract updated to the new signature/structure
  (StrictMode still-mounted guard unchanged, one line later).
- Skills view switcher ARIA (P0): role=tablist/tab promised the ARIA
  tabs keyboard contract (roving tabindex, arrow keys, linked panels)
  that plain buttons never implemented. Now honest aria-pressed
  segmented buttons.
- Bot platform nav freeze (P0, amended per existing contract): the
  contract intentionally locks platform switching while a provider
  action runs, but all seven rows froze with NO explanation. Kept the
  lock, added the missing title (当前操作进行中，完成后可切换平台).
- Dead filter pills (P2): 全部 / 排序：热门 were disabled buttons
  visually promising interactivity; now static spans (CSS gains
  explicit inline-flex so the span keeps the 28px pill geometry).
- Motion contract TSX scanner (#431 review P2): strip //-line and
  /*-block comments before scanning so prose "ease"/"linear" in doc
  comments cannot false-positive — closes the CSS/TSX asymmetry.

Desktop suite 1696/1696.

body:
* Add fork sync GitHub Actions workflow

* Change fork sync to hourly

A coordinated cold-start pass so the window shows an interactive shell
within milliseconds of launch.

Main process:
- main.ts: createWindow() now runs before runBackgroundStartup();
  credential migration, connection bootstrap, telemetry/pricing,

  remark-* + rehype-highlight (highlight.js) pipeline behind lazy so
  it's only parsed when a message actually renders.

- OnboardingHero.tsx / command-palette.tsx: import the fine-grained
  provider-display / settings-nav leaves instead of the full

First-paint data — bundle into the onboarding snapshot to skip
redundant IPC round-trips:

subject: perf(ui): content-visibility on committed turns for long-session first render (#468)
body:
vercel react-best-practices rendering-content-visibility (second-pass
full read of all 70 rules): off-screen turns skip layout/paint for a
~10x first-render win on long sessions — no virtualization library,
native Cmd+F and text selection preserved. contain-intrinsic-size
'auto 250px' keeps a placeholder until first render, then remembered
actual size prevents scrollbar jumps. The streaming turn is pinned
content-visibility:visible: it grows every frame and anchors
auto-scroll, so it must never be skipped.

Desktop suite 1696/1696; turn-narrative + turn-control-history fixture

body:

sections, v1 §9 motion specs, research/laziness), all 126 ui-skills
registry entries (~110 unique docs), all 88 vercel-labs files (70
react-best-practices rules), superdesign in full. Adds the deduped
delta rules (motion thresholds, width-locking, gray-on-color ban,
OKLCH laws, icon governance, copy discipline, React perf queue with

subject: feat(storybook): add P1 UI primitive stories (Progress, Radio, Toggle, Badge, Menu, Accordion, Spinner, Empty, Kbd) (#453)
body:
Add independent Storybook stories for 9 P1 UI primitive components in packages/ui: Progress, Radio/RadioGroup, Toggle/ToggleGroup, Badge (ui + PrimitiveBadge), Menu, Accordion, Spinner, Empty, Kbd. Covers all variants and key states for visual regression and component documentation.

- Progress: 0/30/70/100% + indeterminate (value=null)
- Radio/RadioGroup: vertical/horizontal/disabled/uncontrolled (display:flex for horizontal)
- Toggle/ToggleGroup: states (off/on/disabled/controlled) + single-select / multi-select (toggleMultiple)
- Badge: ui.tsx Badge (6 variant) + PrimitiveBadge size×variant matrix (3 size × 8 variant)
- Menu: Basic interactive + OpenMatrix (basic/shortcuts/checkbox/radio/submenu/icons, all open with accessible trigger labels)
- Accordion: single-open / multiple-open / all-collapsed (SampleAccordion helper, defaultValue as array)
- Spinner: sizes 16/20/24/32px
- Empty: icon-only / title / title+description / with-action / loading (Spinner media)
- Kbd: single keys / combos (⌘K, ⇧⌘P) / arrow group (KbdGroup)

build-storybook verified green after each story and each review fix.

subject: feat(design): check-copy lint + record round-2 decisions D5-D8 (#470)
body:
D8: check-copy.mjs enforces the machine-checkable half of the
four-source copy rules for Chinese UI strings — pressure words
(轻松/一键/只需, per writing-guidelines' never-easy/simple/quick) and

subject: feat(design): waiting-state spectrum 1 — toast overshoot + streaming sweep (D6) (#471)
body:
First installment of the D6 full-spectrum decision (taste-skill v1 §9
'AI product feel' motion specs):

- Toast arrival overshoot: toasts previously popped into existence
  with zero motion. 240ms rise + slight over-scale settle
  (maka-toast-enter) reads as 'something arrived' — Emil's toast tier
  is 200-300ms; the global reduced-motion clamp applies. Exit motion
  needs a dismissal window in toast.tsx (instant unmount today) —
  deferred to a component-level pass.
- Composer streaming sweep: while the assistant streams, a 1px
  status-running light sweep crosses the composer shell top edge —
  'working' stays visible exactly where the user's attention returns.
  Functional infinite motion, status-token derived.
- Both animation names added to the #406 functionalMotion whitelist
  with rationale; composer-inner gains the data-streaming attribute.

Desktop suite 1696/1696; streaming-answer fixture verified.

subject: perf(ui): memoize the turn derivation chain and TurnView (#472)
body:
vercel react-best-practices rerender rules (round-2 full read), the
core of 'streaming never re-renders old turns':

- visibleMessages/chat/storedTools/tools/turns were derived fresh on
  EVERY render, so during plain-text streaming (dozens of updates/sec,
  the hottest path) every turn object changed identity and the whole
  transcript re-rendered per token. All five derivations are now
  useMemo'd on (messages, tools) — text streaming leaves them
  untouched; tool-activity updates still invalidate legitimately.
- TurnView is memo()'d. Its two function props route through
  identity-stable wrappers backed by refs (advanced-use-latest), so
  upstream handler recreation cannot pierce the memo; the per-turn
  inline closure moved inside TurnView (signature now carries turnId).

Desktop suite 1696/1696; turn-control-history + streaming-answer
fixtures verified.

subject: perf(ui): defer command-palette filtering off the keystroke path (#473)
body:
React perf queue item 3 (vercel rerender-use-deferred-value): the ⌘K
palette filtered its command list synchronously per keystroke; with
content-search commands merged in, fast typing paid the full list
re-render before the input echoed. Filtering now keys off
useDeferredValue(query) — keystrokes stay urgent, the list follows at
deferred priority. First useDeferredValue in the codebase (was 0).

search-modal already debounces (180ms) and the model switcher has no
text filter, so the palette is the only surface needing this.

Desktop suite 1696/1696.

subject: feat(design): neutral-cool zinc palette — retire the warm parchment cast (D7') (#474)
body:
Owner call (2026-07-03, supersedes D7): the warm hue-75 ink and ivory
darwin glass palette made the whole app read beige — fighting the
glass/vibrancy language the shell is built on. Reference target is a
clean zinc sidebar (pure-neutral canvas + charcoal ink).

- --foreground light/dark: hue 75 -> 286 (zinc family, same chroma).
  Every color-mix gray tier and oklch(from) derivation follows free.
- --surface-canvas light + dark bg/canvas trio: neutralized.
- darwin glass palette: ivory hexes (#fdfcfa/#faf9f6/#f5f3ee/#c9c4b8)
  -> zinc equivalents at the same lightness steps (#fcfcfd/#fafafa/
  #f4f4f5/#d4d4d8); dark glass hue 96 -> 286. Mint selection stays —
  sage is the accent, not the neutral.
- Default-theme warm stragglers neutralized (hero avatar plate,
  provider-config overlay, two dark scrims). The `sand` opt-in theme
  keeps its warm identity by design.

Sampled proof: light composer area (249,249,249) — was (249,248,245)
warm; dark card (23,23,25) cool zinc. Note: the theme-preview contract
already banned "the old warm parchment hue" — core tokens now agree
with it. Desktop suite 1696/1696; light+dark fixtures verified.

subject: fix(onboarding): make onboarding one-time + add skip exit for blocked states (#466)
body:
* feat(core): add initial_onboarding milestone + hasSettledInitialOnboarding helper

New milestone id 'initial_onboarding' gates whether the onboarding hero
should render. hasSettledInitialOnboarding(milestones) returns true when
the milestone has completedAt or skippedAt, so onboarding is a one-time
guide rather than a gate that revives when sessions are deleted.

* refactor(desktop): gate showOnboardingHero by initial_onboarding milestone

Replace sessions.length === 0 gate with hasSettledInitialOnboarding
check so onboarding is a one-time guide. Deleting all sessions no
longer revives the onboarding hero. Also export
hasSettledInitialOnboarding from @maka/core barrel.

* feat(desktop): add skip button to needs_*/blocked onboarding heroes

All five setup-blocked states (needs_connection, needs_default_connection,
needs_connection_credentials, needs_default_model, blocked) now show a
'跳过，先逛逛' button. Clicking it writes initial_onboarding as skipped
and refreshes the snapshot, so the hero disappears and the user enters
the app without configuring a provider.

* feat(desktop): mark initial_onboarding completed on first quick-chat send

When quickChat.start succeeds, write initial_onboarding as completed
so the onboarding hero never reappears for this user.

* feat(desktop): backfill initial_onboarding for existing users

getSnapshot auto-marks initial_onboarding as completed when the user
already has sessions but no initial_onboarding milestone (upgraded from
before this PR). Ensures existing users never see the onboarding hero.

* fix(desktop): match skip button size to other onboarding footer buttons

Remove size=sm so the skip button uses the default md size, matching
the primary and secondary CTAs in the same footer.

* fix(desktop): use ghost variant for skip button to match footer visual weight

quiet (muted text) made the skip button read as a footnote rather than
a peer action. ghost matches the secondary CTA's weight; position and
copy differentiate intent.

* fix(desktop): address review P1+P…

subject: feat(design): toast exit window + lucide stroke governance (D5/D6) (#483)
body:
Two queue items from the round-2 study:

Toast exit (completes #471's lifecycle): dismissal is now two-phase —
the entry is marked exiting, a 180ms shrink/fade plays (exit = enter
240ms x 75%, roadmap motion rule), then the entry unmounts. Toasts no
longer vanish mid-glance; exiting toasts drop pointer-events. Exit
keyframes whitelisted in the #406 functional-motion contract.

Lucide stroke governance (decision D5): call sites had EIGHT different
strokeWidth values (1.5/1.6/1.65/1.7/1.75/1.8/1.85/2) across 143
usages plus the default 2 wherever unspecified. One rule
(svg.lucide { stroke-width: 1.75 }) overrides every SVG presentation
attribute, unifying all glyphs at the distribution's modal weight with
zero call-site churn. icon-system-contract gains the positive
assertion; its size-hammer ban regex gets (?<!-) so the governed
stroke-width rule doesn't false-positive on "width".

Markdown parse caching (queue item) evaluated and DOWNGRADED: memoized
MessageBody already prevents re-parsing committed turns; the streaming
turn's re-parse is essential work. Noted here so it isn't re-proposed.

Desktop suite 1697/1697 (+1 contract).

subject: fix(dev): run esbuild through its JS API — bin/esbuild is a native binary (#484)
body:
npm run dev crashed on any machine where esbuild's postinstall had
run: dev.mjs spawned `node node_modules/esbuild/bin/esbuild`, but
postinstall replaces that file with a platform-native executable
(Mach-O on darwin), so node threw SyntaxError on byte one and the
preload/main bundles never built.

Both esbuild invocations now use the JS API (esbuildBuild with
equivalent options: bundle/platform/format/packages/external), which
is binary-agnostic, cross-platform, and skips a child process. The
dev-startup contract now asserts the API options instead of CLI flags,
with the rationale inline.

Verified: npm run dev boots end-to-end (incremental build 0.2s, vite
on :5173, Electron launches). Desktop suite 1708/1708.

body:
* build(storybook): typecheck stories to catch prop drift

Add tsconfig.storybook.json and wire typecheck:stories into the
desktop typecheck script so tsc --noEmit covers story files.

Story files were previously excluded from the @maka/ui tsc build
and never type-checked: build-storybook uses Vite which strips
types without checking. This meant a component could change its
props and the story would silently keep using the old API.

Fix three drift issues caught by the new typecheck:

- toggle.stories.tsx: toggleMultiple -> multiple (Base UI renamed
  the prop; the story still used the old name)
- settings-pages.stories.tsx: UsageSummary fixture fields updated
  to match the current interface (range/fromMs/toMs/requestCount/
  errorCount -> totalRequests/totalCostUsd/totalTokens/etc)
- onboarding.stories.tsx: stop mutating readonly AppSettings fields
  via spread-based fixture construction
- maka-bridge.tsx: narrow the window cast so delete-on-optional
  and MakaGlobal assignability errors go away

* test(storybook): assert every public UI component has story coverage

Add a contract test that scans all story files and asserts each
public UI component name (Button, Badge, Input, ..., Toolbar,
ToastProvider) appears in at least one story. Catches the 'new
component, no story' drift class without manual grep audits.

Verified red/green: deleting toolbar.stories.tsx turns the test red
(Toolbar missing), restoring it turns green.

* fix(storybook): use mergeSettings instead of shallow spread in settings story

Replace { ...createDefaultSettings(), ...patch } as ReturnType<...>
with mergeSettings(createDefaultSettings(), patch) in the settings
story bridge mock. The real IPC handler uses mergeSettings for deep
merge and normalization of nested settings (webSearch, localMemory,
botChat). The shallow spread + type assertion could return settings
with undefined nested fields, diverging from production behavior.

* build(storybook): narrow tsconfig.storybook.json to story/config entries

The previous tsconfig included ent…

current-turn large tool-result pruning. Explicit off/false still opts
out. Adjusts the existing explicit-false-like-boolean test (stale/archive
off no longer implies an empty context budget) and adds default-on and
explicit-off coverage.

* refactor(desktop): drop activeToolResultPrune threshold env alias

Keep only the canonical MAKA_CONTEXT_ACTIVE_TOOL_RESULT_MAX_ESTIMATED_TOKENS
env. The MAKA_CONTEXT_ACTIVE_TOOL_RESULT_PRUNE_MAX_ESTIMATED_TOKENS alias
was a half-alignment with headless (only the threshold alias, not the
enable/disable aliases); drop it per review feedback to avoid carrying
headless historical compatibility baggage on desktop.

* docs: changelog activeToolResultPrune default-on

Document the desktop+headless default-on behavior, the 2048-token
threshold, the metadata-only placeholder behavior for oversized tool
results, the opt-out env, and the #340 non-inferiority evidence basis.
Per review feedback on the headless default behavior change.

--nav-active, --toast-accent, --selection, --brand-deep, --bot-brand-default)
move from sage green (oklch 0.70 0.135 152) to the logo blue sampled from
apps/desktop/assets/icon.png (oklch 0.70 0.135 250). --success stays an
independent green, so connected/ok semantics are unchanged. The primary CTA
and checked controls keep green for now; the next commit migrates them to
the blue family with WCAG tuning.

The #406 governance contract still passes: it pins --action/--control
literals and contrasts, which this commit does not touch.

* feat(design-system): migrate primary CTA and checked controls to logo blue

Direction A: --action becomes a pale blue chip (oklch 0.85 0.08 250) with
deep-blue text (--action-foreground oklch 0.30 0.06 250), a light, fresh
button replacing the dark-green solid. --control moves to oklch 0.65 0.135
250, one step below the accent L0.70, so the near-white glyph clears WCAG
2.1 SC 1.4.11 non-text contrast 3:1 (3.09:1); the logo L0.70 only reaches
2.55:1. Dark mode uses the same action/control values for consistency.
control-foreground stays near-white; --success stays green.

Update the #406 governance contract: action-foreground literal becomes
oklch(0.30 0.06 250), and the control/control-foreground contrast bar moves
4.5 -> 3.0 with a comment citing WCAG 1.4.11 (the pair paints graphical
objects — checkbox check, switch knob, radio dot, progress fill — not text;
action keeps the 4.5:1 text bar). action contrast is 8.64:1.

Verified: full desktop suite 1821 pass / 0 fail; storybook renders the
default primary button as a pale-blue chip + deep-blue text and the
checked checkbox at L0.65.

* fix(design-system): repoint residual hardcoded greens to logo blue

Two greens survived the accent migration because they were hardcoded
literals, not --accent aliases, so they never tracked the brand color.

- Sidebar selected-row fill (theme-glass.css -…

subject: feat(providers): add MiniMax M-series (Global + China) (#464)
body:
* feat(providers): add MiniMax M-series (Global + China)

Add MiniMax as a first-class provider with the M3 model, wired over the
Anthropic-compatible protocol (same path as kimi-coding-plan). Two access
entries: MiniMax (Global, api.minimax.io) and MiniMax-cn (China,
api.minimaxi.com).

Model facts (context 1M, max output 128K, pricing $0.30/$1.20 per 1M)
sourced from models.dev, the registry this repo already tracks.

- llm-connections: ProviderType + PROVIDER_DEFAULTS + ready/catalog lists
- model-metadata: M3 static facts + curated fallback
- model-factory: createAnthropic routing + providerOptions
- headless harbor: MINIMAX_* env wiring + key-file mapping
- telemetry: builtin M3 pricing
- desktop UI: provider display, brand mark, onboarding entry

* fix(minimax): send MiniMax over Bearer auth instead of x-api-key

MiniMax's Anthropic-compatible API accepts both x-api-key and Bearer,
but documents Bearer as recommended and gives it precedence when both
are present. Pass the key as `authToken` so createAnthropic emits
`Authorization: Bearer`, matching the repo's claude-subscription path.

Add a regression test asserting the MiniMax branch uses authToken (not
the bare apiKey shorthand) while keeping its provider-specific base URL.

* fix(minimax): match Harbor env passthrough against lowercased provider

buildPiCliEnv lowercases the provider name before testing it against each
PI_PROVIDER_ENV_RULES entry's `includes` values, so the rule value
`MiniMax` (with uppercase letters) never matched — MINIMAX_API_KEY,
MINIMAX_API_KEY_FILE and MINIMAX_BASE_URL were silently dropped from the
pi CLI cell env. Lowercase the rule value to `minimax` so both
minimax and minimax-cn resolve it.

Add regression tests asserting the rule values stay lowercase (matching
the normalized provider) and that buildPiCliEnv keeps lowercasing.

---------

subject: fix(desktop): treat draining assistant text as settled for live-only chrome
body:
* fix(desktop): treat draining assistant text as settled for live-only chrome

Distinguish the streaming 'streaming' phase from the 'draining' phase so
final assistant text smoothing through the smoother no longer keeps
session event health polling alive, drives the sidebar pulse, disables
the composer, or blocks permission mode transitions.

- Introduce activeStreamingLive (phase === 'streaming') in app-shell.tsx
  and use it for composer streaming flag, permission mode disabled
  reason, and sidebar streamingSessionIds.
- Switch useSessionEventHealthPolling to depend on activeStreamingLive
  instead of activeStreaming.length, so draining text does not hold the
  event stream health checker open.
- Remove deferMessageRefresh in the 'complete' session event handler so
  committed history is always refreshed even while final text drains.
- Add an effect that settles the draining slot once the committed
  assistant message arrives in messages, without requiring a session
  switch.
- Add readMessagesWithSettleRetry with [120, 360]ms delays to tolerate
  read-model settle races during refreshMessages.
- Update session-event-health, session-message-lifecycle-contract,
  session-status-presentation, and streaming-handoff contract tests to
  lock in the new behavior.

* fix(desktop): wait for committed assistant handoff

---------

subject: feat(desktop): add global default permission-mode setting (#513)
body:
* feat(desktop): add global default permission-mode setting

New sessions always started on 询问权限 (ask), even after the composer's
per-session permission-mode picker landed -- there was no way to change
what a *new* chat starts on. Adds Settings → 通用 → 默认权限模式, right
below 默认模型.

- packages/core: new `chatDefaults.permissionMode` field on `AppSettings`
  (excludes `explore`, which is Deep-Research/bot-incoming-only), with
  closed-enum fail-closed normalization matching the existing
  appearance.palette / personalization.uiLocale pattern.
- packages/ui: exported `PERMISSION_MODE_META`/`PERMISSION_MODE_ORDER`
  (previously private to composer.tsx) via components.tsx, so the
  Settings picker's labels/hints can never drift from the composer's own
  picker.
- apps/desktop: General settings page renders a rich `Menu`-based picker
  (not a plain `<SettingsSelect>`) reusing the composer's own
  `.maka-composer-mode-menu*` styling -- every option shows its label +
  full hint text in the dropdown before picking, not just the selected
  one. The row's own description is a fixed explanation of what the
  setting does (previously it echoed the *selected* option's hint,
  duplicating the dropdown).
- main.ts: sessions:create (both branches) and Quick Chat's non-Deep-
  Research path now fall back to chatDefaults.permissionMode instead of
  a hardcoded 'ask'.
- app-shell.tsx / app-shell-chat-actions.ts: the actual "send first
  message in a new chat" path has its own renderer-side fallback
  (pendingNewChatPermissionMode ?? defaultPermissionMode) since it always
  sends an explicit value and never hits the main-process fallback.
  defaultPermissionMode is loaded on mount and re-synced when Settings
  closes (settings-surface.tsx keeps independent AppSettings state and
  never notifies app-shell.tsx directly -- same pattern already used for
  onboarding/memory-pill refresh on close).

Also fixes a real bug found while testing this interactively: opening
Settings during an active stream and cl…

variant, dead data-[selected] active selectors — Base UI sets data-active)

TabsRoot/TabsList/TabsTrigger/TabsPanel from primitives/tabs so every tab
surface gets the maka-tab class + underline/pill variants + the correct

subject: feat(cli): make TUI controls usable (#515)
body: Split the TUI runner into focused modules (tui-ansi, tui-autocomplete-layout, test terminal mock); serialize model/session/permission switches through runControl; scope session resumes to the startup cwd/connection inside the driver; keep the permission prompt retryable; restore the Edit tool. 68/68 tests green.

subject: fix(storage): prevent write-queue memory leak (#524)
body:
SessionStore / AgentRunStore / RuntimeEventStore each kept a per-key
write queue (a Map of in-flight promises) whose entries were never
removed. Deleting a session left its entry behind, and every run left
a permanent entry since the run stores have no removal path at all.
Each leaked entry holds a settled promise + its key string (~200
bytes), so this grew without bound over a long-lived desktop process.

Extract a shared chainWrite() helper (matching the existing
botConversationQueues pattern): once a key's write chain finishes and
no new write is queued behind it, the entry is removed; an identity
check avoids deleting an entry a newer write already replaced. The
three withQueue methods now just validate ids and delegate to it.

Add unit tests for chainWrite: entry cleanup after the chain drains,
the identity guard while a write is queued, serialization order, and
error propagation. Confirmed the cleanup tests fail when the .finally
is removed (50 entries remain instead of 0) while serialization still
passes.

subject: refactor(ui): converge typography onto --leading-*/--font-weight-*/--tracking-* tokens (#520 PR1) (#526)
body:
* refactor(ui): converge line-height onto --leading-* tokens (#520 PR1)

Add 4 --leading-* tokens (none/tight/snug/normal = 1/1.25/1.375/1.5) aligned to a Tailwind leading-scale subset (drop relaxed/loose, zero sites). Bridge --leading-* to Tailwind leading-* utilities in @theme inline. Replace ~160 bare line-height values across renderer CSS with var(--leading-*), incl. the 20px magic (plan-reminders.css) and 1.68 bubble-assistant. Snug is the one tier that can't be cut: 40 sites at 1.3-1.4 (13/11px small-text dense zones) collapse to tight/normal at ±0.1-0.2, visible on small text. Tracking issue: #520.

* test(ui): lock line-height converge contract (#520 PR1)

Add line-height-converge-contract.test.ts mirroring typography-converge-contract: ban bare line-height numbers/px/rem/normal, whitelist 4 --leading-* tokens + em + inherit/initial/unset/revert/0; pin token values; assert Tailwind @theme inline bridging. Tracking issue: #520.

* refactor(ui): converge font-weight + letter-spacing + font-family minor (#520 PR1)

Add --font-weight-* (4-tier: normal/medium/semibold/bold = 400/500/600/700) + --tracking-* (4-tier: normal/wide/wider/widest = 0/0.025em/0.05em/0.1em; no tight — CJK-first, roadmap §1.6 bans tightening CJK) + --font-serif tokens; bridge to Tailwind font-*/tracking-* utilities in @theme inline. Replace ~205 font-weight (550/620/650→semibold, 680→bold) + ~62 letter-spacing (negatives→normal) values across renderer CSS. Drop font-variation-settings "wght" 550 "wdth" 105 on .maka-hero h1 (wght folds into font-weight token, wdth one-off dropped). Fix font-family: var(--mono-font,…)→var(--font-mono), redundant fallbacks→var(--font-mono), Georgia,serif→var(--font-serif). Tracking issue: #520.
