subject: feat(cli): inject system prompt, AGENTS.md, and environment into TUI
body: Sinks workspace-instructions, personalization, project-context, and session-environment prompt builders from desktop into @maka/runtime so the CLI/TUI can reuse them, then injects the assembled system prompt and per-turn environment into the AiSdkBackend. Adds isPathInside with cross-drive (isAbsolute) and '..rules'-style guards, used by both the runtime scan and the desktop open/create management surface.

subject: fix(runtime): use local timezone for turn-tail date
body: formatDate in session-environment-prompt used toISOString().slice(0,10) (UTC), so near local midnight the injected Today's date was one day behind the user's calendar day. Switch to local getFullYear/(getMonth+1)/getDate + padStart so the date follows the process timezone. Default production path locked by a TZ=Asia/Shanghai child cross-midnight test.

subject: refactor(ui): converge layout-surface & sizing tokens (#520 PR4) (#539)
body:
* test(desktop): relax exact className match for PR3 min-width additions

PR3 (#527) added min-w-[Nrem] utilities to the error-boundary copy button
and the daily-review copy/append/save buttons (text-swap width lock for
复制中…/已复制 feedback). Two contract tests asserted the semantic class
with an exact className="X" regex that required the closing quote right
after the class, so the extra utility class broke the match and the suites
have been red on main since PR3 merged.

Match each semantic class as a whole word in the class list
(className="[^"]*\bX\b[^"]*"), the same form the daily-review test
already used for its negative maka-button check. This keeps the intent
(button uses the semantic class, not a legacy maka-button) while
tolerating the utility classes PR3 deliberately added.

* refactor(ui): converge control heights onto --h-control-* scale (#520 PR4 item 15)

The sidebar / 会话 / 设置 control heights had drifted onto off-ruler bare
px (22 / 26 / 30 / 34 / 38) while the TSX side used the 4px spacing ruler
via Tailwind h-N. The two scales never aligned, so a sidebar nav row at
34px next to a session row at 30px next to a settings nav at 38px read as
three different systems — the user's 碍眼.

Add a six-tier --h-control-* scale (xs/sm/md/lg/xl/2xl = 20/24/28/32/36/40)
on the 4px spacing ruler (var(--space-N)) so CSS var(--h-control-*) and
Tailwind h-N share one scale. Snap the off-ruler control heights to the
nearest tier and tokenize the on-ruler ones:

  .maka-list-row            30 → 32  (--h-control-lg)   会话 row
  .maka-search-modal-close  22 → 24  (--h-control-sm)
  .maka-search-modal-clear  22 → 24  (--h-control-sm)
  .settingsBackButton       38 → 36  (--h-control-xl)
  .settingsNavItem          38 → 36  (--h-control-xl)
  .settingsSelectTrigger    34 → 32  (--h-control-lg)
  .settingsSelectMenuPopup [role=option]  32 → 32 (--h-control-lg)
  .maka-model-switcher-trigger  22 → 24 (--h-control-sm)
  .maka-chat-jump-bottom    26 → 28  (--h-control-md)

subject: refactor(ui): converge visual-state & motion tokens (opacity, focus-ring, duration, transform) (#520 PR2) (#533)
body:
* refactor(ui): converge opacity onto --opacity-* tokens (#520 PR2)

4 semantic tokens (--opacity-disabled 0.5 / muted 0.65 / pending 0.8 / overlay 0.04 light→0.06 dark theme override) + 0/1 literal + keyframe exclusion. Replace ~50 bare opacity values (0.45-0.58→disabled, 0.6-0.7→muted, 0.72-0.9→pending, 0.04/0.06→overlay); 0.35 disabled (models browser-navbtn) snap disabled. maka-pulse keyframe 0.55 excluded (animation intent, not element state).

opacity-converge-contract: bans bare opacity, whitelists 4 tokens + 0/1 + literals, strips @keyframes blocks (animation intent), lookbehind excludes --shadow-*-opacity token declarations. Updated stale-sessions + artifact-pane-lifecycle test expectations (0.7→var(--opacity-muted), 0.56→disabled, 0.78→pending).

No @theme inline bridge: Tailwind opacity utilities are numeric (opacity-50), not semantic, so --opacity-* is CSS-only; TSX uses Tailwind opacity-* directly (out of CSS contract scope).

Tracking issue: #520.

* refactor(ui): converge focus-ring recipe onto --focus-ring-width/offset (#520 PR2)

2 new geometry tokens (--focus-ring-width 2px / --focus-ring-offset 2px) + 2 existing color tokens (--focus-ring strong / --ring subtle). Recipe: outline: var(--focus-ring-width) solid var(--focus-ring|--ring); outline-offset: var(--focus-ring-offset). Snap 3 outliers to 2px (skills 3px width, nav-sidebar -2px offset, models 6px offset) — no whitelist. box-shadow ring (0 0 0 2px var(--ring)) width → var(--focus-ring-width). alpha variants (oklch from var(--focus-ring) / 0.42) kept (color layer). link focus (models 962, 1px + link color) whitelisted as one-off — it's a link affordance, not the keyboard focus-ring recipe.

subject: feat(runtime): session task ledger — TaskCreate/TaskUpdate + turn-tail injection (#15 P0-task) (#537)
body:
* feat(runtime): session task ledger primitive — TaskCreate/TaskUpdate + turn-tail injection (#15 P0-task)

Main-agent session task ledger, the task-tracking slice of #15:

- core: Task contract (4-state status), defensive normalize/validate,
  TaskLedgerStore interface, shared list renderer, 200-task hard cap.
- storage: FileTaskLedgerStore at sessions/<id>/tasks.json — atomic
  tmp+rename writes serialized per session via chainWrite. Render reads
  degrade to empty; mutate reads fail closed (corrupt ledger is never
  used as a write base, so a transient read error cannot wipe tasks).
- runtime: TaskCreate (batch) + TaskUpdate tools, permissionRequired
  false (pure local session state, cf. agent_list). Schemas encode the
  subject cap so the model learns constraints at validation time. Tool
  results render the post-mutation ledger returned from inside the
  write-queue critical section (no second read, no race).
- desktop: tools wired into builtinTools; turn tail injects the current
  ledger via the existing volatile-tail path (durable system prefix
  untouched — prefix-cache discipline). Ledger text passes redactSecrets
  on both surfaces (tail + tool result) and strips literal task-ledger
  tags so a subject cannot escape the data envelope. No TaskList tool:
  the per-turn tail injection makes it redundant.

Child agents do not inherit the tools (definition allowlists). Headless
wiring and the UI panel are follow-ups.

* fix(task-ledger): strip <task-ledger> tag variants across both render faces

The turn-tail strip was the narrow /<\/?task-ledger>/, which missed
</task-ledger > (space before >), <task-ledger x="1"> (attributes),
<task-ledger/>, and </task-ledger\t>. A model-authored subject carrying any
of these could open or close the <task-ledger> data envelope early and
smuggle instruction-level text, which is the escape the PR's own comment
claims to block.

Add a shared renderSafeTaskLedgerText in core (redact + strip
/<\/?task-ledger[^>]*>/) and route both the tool-result face
(renderTaskLedger)…

across page refreshes.

The composer now calls saveGlobalInputHistoryEntry() after each sent
message, and initializes the history from readGlobalInputHistory() on
mount. This ensures input history survives page reloads and app restarts.

Drop the 'textarea must be empty' gate so up/down navigates the

The startup sequence flashed three different gray cards (preload skeleton

subject: fix(headless): resolve connection defaults from llm-connections.json (#522)
body:
* fix(headless): resolve connection defaults from llm-connections.json

When no explicit MAKA_MODEL env is set, `harbor run` previously fell through
to a hardcoded deepseek fallback, causing model_resolve_failed for any user
whose configured connection uses a different provider (e.g. Anthropic via
coproxy).

Add `applyConnectionDefaults(env)` which reads the desktop workspace's
llm-connections.json defaultSlug and injects MAKA_MODEL, MAKA_LLM_CONNECTION_SLUG,
and MAKA_BASE_URL into the process env. This makes headless runs honor the same
connection configuration that the desktop app uses.

Includes comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, skip-when-set,
missing file, disabled connections, and MAKA_CONNECTIONS_PATH override.

* fix(headless): respect explicit CLI inputs in applyConnectionDefaults

Address maintainer review feedback on PR #522:

P1: Guard applyConnectionDefaults against explicit CLI inputs — early-return
when MAKA_PROVIDER or MAKA_LLM_CONNECTION_SLUG is already set, not just
MAKA_MODEL/HARBOR_MODEL. Only write MAKA_LLM_CONNECTION_SLUG and
MAKA_BASE_URL when they are undefined (per-field respect).

P1: Move --api-key-file provider inference into a separate applyApiKeyFile()
step that runs AFTER applyConnectionDefaults, so provider is inferred from
the final resolved model rather than the pre-defaults value.

P2: Skip applyConnectionDefaults entirely when MAKA_BACKEND is fake or
pi-agent.

P2: Validate providerType from connections file against PROVIDER_DEFAULTS;
no-op on unknown provider types.

P3: Add tests for all broken combinations: --provider without --model,
--base-url without --model, --connection-slug without --model,
--backend fake, --backend pi-agent, and invalid providerType in file.

* fix(headless): cross-platform credentials + backend-flag guard for connection defaults

- applyConnectionDefaults now sets MAKA_CREDENTIALS_PATH to
  dirname(connectionsPath)/credentials.json, so readStoredMakaApiKey finds
  the API key on Windows/Linux too, not just macOS. credentials.json lives
 …

subject: fix: Gemini /v1beta baseUrl + default baseUrl persistence (#529)
body:
* fix(runtime): include /v1beta in Google Gemini baseUrl, centralize + normalize URL construction

The Google provider default baseUrl lacked the `/v1beta` version segment,
which the @ai-sdk/google library appends `/models/{model}:generateContent`
directly onto (its own default baseURL is `.../v1beta`). Chat sends 404'd.
Meanwhile the model-list fetcher and the connection probe hardcoded
`/v1beta` in their path, so adding the segment to baseUrl would have made
those two produce `/v1beta/v1beta/...`.

Align the Google default with the codebase-wide contract — baseUrl carries
the version path, like OpenAI/Moonshot/Kimi/Z.AI/MiniMax/Ollama — and
centralize Google URL construction in `googleV1BetaBaseUrl` / `googleApiUrl`
(subscription-auth.ts), mirroring the existing `anthropicV1BaseUrl` /
`anthropicV1Url` pair. `googleV1BetaBaseUrl` normalizes any base to a single
`/v1beta` suffix (strips a trailing `/v1beta` and re-appends it); all three
Google consumers route through it — chat via `createGoogleGenerativeAI`, the
model-list fetcher and the connection probe via `googleApiUrl`. The version
segment can no longer drift between consumers, and a connection that stored
the pre-fix bare-root default as an explicit override self-heals to `/v1beta`
instead of 404ing.

Tests:
- googleV1BetaBaseUrl normalization matrix (bare-root self-heal, idempotent
  /v1beta, trailing-slash, custom proxy).
- googleApiUrl: no doubled /v1beta, path normalization, key encoding, and a
  bare-root input that proves the missing version segment is supplied.
- model-factory Google chat wiring (asserts the chat path passes
  googleV1BetaBaseUrl(baseURL), not the raw effectiveBaseUrl).
- HTTP wiring test for the model-fetcher google path (asserts the request
  URL and the `models/{name}` → `{id}` response parsing).
- Update the canonical-URL example in llm-connections.test.ts to the
  `/v1beta` form.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storage): don't persist the provider default baseUrl as an explicit override

The provid…

subject: feat(desktop): watch workspace config files for external changes (#516)
body:
* feat(desktop): watch workspace config files for external changes

When external processes (maka-headless CLI, user editors, scripts) modify
llm-connections.json, credentials.json, or settings.json, the desktop UI
now refreshes automatically without requiring a restart or manual page
navigation.

Motivation:
- The headless CLI (harbor-cell.ts) shares the same credential store at
  ~/Library/Application Support/Maka/workspaces/default/credentials.json.
- Running headless eval while the desktop app is open means config can
  change under the app without notification.
- Users editing config files directly (a supported workflow since all
  config is plain JSON) had no feedback until clicking into Settings.

Implementation:
- Add config-file-watcher.ts: a zero-dependency module using Node.js
  built-in fs.watch() on the workspace directory. Filters events by
  filename, debounces 300ms to coalesce rapid/duplicate FSEvents, and
  supports self-write suppression to avoid feedback loops.
- Wire the watcher into runBackgroundStartup() with stop() on
  before-quit.
- For connection/credential changes: trigger the existing
  emitConnectionListChanged() path — renderer already handles this.
- For settings changes: add a new 'settings:externalChanged' IPC push
  event, exposed via preload subscribeExternalChanged(), subscribed in
  useAppShellBootstrapSubscriptions to refresh shell settings and
  connections.
- Fail-safe: if the watcher cannot start (e.g. missing directory), the
  app falls back to current behavior (manual refresh on navigation).

Performance:
- macOS FSEvents / Linux inotify: kernel-level, zero CPU when idle.
- Single directory watcher, 1 file descriptor.
- Callback reads 3-5 KB JSON only on actual change events.

Testing:
- 8 unit tests covering: per-file callback routing, debounce,
  self-write suppression, unrelated file filtering, stop() cleanup,
  and graceful failure on nonexistent paths.

* fix(desktop): address PR #516 review — suppress loop, CLI flag override, error handling

- Add startup grac…

subject: feat(runtime): controllable per-model thinking level for sessions (#528)
body:
* feat(core): add ThinkingLevel type and per-model variant resolver

Reasoning models think at a default depth with no way to control it. This
introduces the vocabulary and the per-model supported-set as the single
source of truth, before any runtime/UI wiring.

- packages/core/src/model-thinking.ts: ThinkingLevel (minimal/low/medium/
  high), isThinkingLevel, thinkingVariantsForModel(providerType, modelId).
  Heuristics are conservative per provider; ollama/openai-compatible/gemini-cli
  return none (cannot infer from the id).
- SessionHeader/SessionSummary gain thinkingLevel?; CreateSessionInput and
  the session store carry it. undefined = model default (UI 默认).

Refs #523

* feat(runtime): map thinking level to ai-sdk provider options

buildProviderOptions now takes a thinking level and emits the protocol-
correct ai-sdk provider option, gated by the resolver so a level the model
does not support is dropped. Existing safetySettings / store:false survive
the merge.

AiSdkBackend snapshots providerOptions at construction, so changing the
level must rebuild the backend: changesBackendConfig now treats
thinkingLevel as a backend-config field. The headless harbor-cell call
site passes the header thinkingLevel (no-op when unset).

- reasoningEffort for openai / codex-subscription / openai-compatible family
- thinking.budgetTokens for anthropic-protocol providers
- thinkingConfig (thinkingLevel for gemini-3, thinkingBudget for 2.5) for google

Refs #523

* feat(desktop): thinking level menu inside the model picker

The thinking level is now chosen inside the model picker popup as a
secondary menu, not as a separate chip in the composer (the chip pushed
the model chip around and added a second composer-bar element). Mirrors
pawwork's ThinkingLevelSection: a 思考级别 ▸ <当前> row at the bottom of
the popup opens a side flyout with 默认 + the model's supported levels;
默认 clears the variant (no override).

- The row is always rendered (even for non-reasoning models) showing
  默认 and inert, so the p…

subject: feat(runtime): render image attachments as provider image parts (#550)
body:
* fix(runtime): preserve user attachments in stored-message fallback

materializePriorMessages dropped StoredUserMessage.attachments, so multi-turn replay via the stored-message projection (used when RuntimeEvent replay is empty) lost every attachment reference; the model only saw the user text. Route user messages through the same formatTextWithAttachmentRefs helper the current-turn and RuntimeEvent-replay paths already use, so all three paths present attachments consistently.

* feat(storage): add attachment ingest/read helpers backed by ArtifactStore

ingestAttachment copies bytes into the session ArtifactStore as a
user_upload artifact and returns a session_file StorageRef; the stored
copy is the stable snapshot later reads resolve. readAttachmentBytes
resolves only session_file refs through the same path-safety check as
ArtifactStore and rejects external_file/workspace_file, so there is no
arbitrary-path read surface — workspace files go through the Read tool
and external files must be ingested (copied) first.

* feat(runtime): render current-turn image attachments as provider image parts

AiSdkBackend accepts an optional readAttachmentBytes reader. When set,
image attachments on the current user turn are read through the injected
reader (runtime never imports @maka/storage) and appended as provider
image parts alongside the text, instead of the [attachment: name (mime)]
placeholder. Non-image attachments keep the placeholder so the model can
read workspace files via Read. appendTurnTailPrompt now handles array
content; prompt-segment estimates still use the text form (image token
estimation is a follow-up). Replay/stored-fallback image parts land next.

* feat(runtime): render replayed and stored image attachments as provider image parts

The RuntimeEvent replay and stored-message fallback paths now share
appendImageParts with the current turn, so historical image attachments
stay as provider image parts across multi-turn replay instead of
degrading to [attachment: name (mime)] placeholder text. The mater…

subject: feat(composer): workspace picker, git branch switching, and composer defaults persistence (#511)
body:
* feat(composer): workspace picker with recent directories, git branch switching, and composer defaults persistence

* fix: address PR#511 review issues

- P1: Remove permissionMode from composer-defaults persistence.
  pendingNewChatPermissionMode now starts from null (never seeds from
  localStorage) to avoid silently inheriting a high-privilege mode
  across restarts.
- P1: Update project-context-badge.test.ts regex to match the current
  workspacePicker render (wp alias instead of props.workspacePicker).
- P1: Replace bare line-height: 1.25 with var(--leading-tight) in
  composer.css to comply with PR-LEADING-CONVERGE-0 governance.
- P2: Add dirty-worktree guard test for checkoutBranch: fakes
  non-empty git status --porcelain and asserts reason: 'dirty' with
  no checkout call.

* fix: address composer workspace review

* fix(desktop): bot incoming sessions use the current project root

Bot-created sessions used process.cwd(), so a bot message arriving while
the user had project A selected could start the session in the app launch
directory instead. Rename the BotIncomingMainServiceDeps.cwd() sync string
to getCurrentProjectRoot(): Promise<string>, await it at the createSession
call site, and wire main.ts to the current project root resolver.

The resolver is defined inside registerIpc(), so a module-level provider is
reassigned from registerIpc once the resolver exists; the launch directory
remains the safe fallback until then. Unifying project-root resolution
across main is tracked as a follow-up.

---------

subject: feat(ui): canonical Input/Textarea onto Base UI, retire native ui.tsx Input (#520 item 22) (#555)
body:
* feat(ui): canonical Input/Textarea onto Base UI, retire native ui.tsx Input (#22 PR10)

#22 PR10: collapse the dual Input tracks onto one canonical
primitives/input.tsx + primitives/textarea.tsx, retiring the native ui.tsx
Input/Textarea.

- primitives/input.tsx now wraps Base UI's Input primitive with maka's
  inputClasses styling ported as the default chrome. Stays a single <input>
  (no span wrapper) so caller CSS targeting `> input` / `input:focus-visible`
  still matches. unstyled gives the bare form (bareFieldClasses +
  data-maka-field-chrome="none") for Field/InputGroup embedding.
- primitives/textarea.tsx parallel shape: single <textarea> + inputClasses +
  textarea sizing. Base UI ships no Textarea, so this is a native textarea.
  Drops the Base UI Field.Control + span wrapper (caller doesn't use Field).
- ui.tsx Input, Textarea, inputClasses, bareFieldClasses retired. inputClasses +
  bareFieldClasses moved into primitives/input.tsx (exported) so number-field
  keeps its standalone NumberFieldInput chrome.
- InputGroup: InputGroupInput/InputGroupTextarea already pass unstyled (inner
  field bare, InputGroup owns the chrome — no double chrome). InputGroup CSS
  updated to key off data-slot="input"/"textarea" (the single element) instead
  of the retired span data-slot="input-control"/"textarea-control"; dropped the
  span-only `:contents` / `:before:hidden` rules.
- index.ts re-exports primitives/input + primitives/textarea as the canonical
  Input/Textarea; the 44 usages across 9 settings files keep importing from
  @maka/ui unchanged.
- Contracts: new input-canonical-contract (single element, inputClasses token
  lock, ui.tsx retired, bare-field shape); field-chrome.test rewritten for the
  unified shape (drops nativeInput/h-8.5/field-sizing-content assertions that
  pinned the old primitives/input vs ui.tsx divergence); radius-converge
  inputClasses tier repointed at primitives/input.tsx.

subject: feat(cli): wire runtime compaction pipeline into the TUI (#560)
body:
* refactor(runtime,desktop): hoist context budget policy builder

Move buildDefaultContextBudgetPolicy into @maka/runtime so both surfaces
share one implementation. The desktop main file becomes a thin
re-export; behavior is unchanged.

* refactor(runtime,desktop): hoist history compact artifact helpers

Move loadHistoryCompactBlocksFromArtifacts and
persistHistoryCompactBlocksToArtifacts (with abort-cleanup tracking and
the loader source/kind filter) into @maka/runtime. Add an optional
abortSignal field to HistoryCompactWriteInput so the helper can abort a
write and delete every artifact it created. The desktop main file
becomes a thin re-export.

* fix(runtime): rework history compact lookup + harden normal-send writes

Filter diagnostic-only RuntimeEvents out of history budgeting and compact
boundaries. Make lookup history compact lookup-only (no synthesized
fallback blocks), accept a loaded block that covers a contiguous folded
prefix, retain uncovered newer folded events only when the result stays
within budget, and fail open (reset to the original runtime context +
failedOpen diagnostics) when a durable history-compact write fails or is
skipped.

Normal-send history-compact writes now pass the turn abortSignal to the
writer so a stop can abort an in-flight write, and
mergeCompactionDecisionDiagnostics lets a failedOpen decision replace a
prior replaced decision.

* test(desktop): cover history compact abort, loader filter, and replay

Add regression tests for the hoisted helpers: an aborted write deletes
every artifact it created and leaves no replayable block or live source;
the loader skips deleted history_compact_source artifacts without
inflating the deleted skip count; and applyRuntimeEventHistoryCompact
replays a loaded block over the matching folded events.

* feat(runtime): add compactSession operation lifecycle

Thread a manual compact operation through the existing runtime lifecycle:
SessionManager.compactSession is a thin facade over
RuntimeKernel.compactSession, which runs the compaction inside …

subject: feat(ui): add prompt anchor rail for jumping between prompts (#563)
body:
A slim right-edge rail in the chat view with one tick per user prompt —
Codex / ChatGPT-style navigation for long conversations. Click a tick to
scroll that prompt into view; the tick for the turn at the top of the
viewport stays highlighted; hovering a tick previews the prompt plus the
start of its reply.

Reuses the chat view's existing `[data-turn-id]` anchors and the
scroll-into-view path; an IntersectionObserver tracks the active turn. The
tick bar draws in `currentColor` and active/hover just shift the neutral text
color (muted -> primary), so there's no brand rail or hover-surface
background. Hidden until there are 3+ prompts. Ticks are keyboard-focusable
buttons with aria-labels.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: feat(desktop): attachment add + display + vision-aware send (#556)
body:
* feat(attachments): core MIME routing + materialize projection + desktop reader

- core: attachmentKindFromMimeType(mime, fileName?) routes to image/pdf/
  doc/other; the runtime consumption split is image vs everything-else so
  only the kinds that change consumption are singled out.
- ui: materializeChat projects StoredUserMessage.attachments onto the user
  ChatItem (previously dropped), so the renderer can render them.
- desktop main: createAttachmentByteReader binds the injected reader to
  ArtifactStore (session_file snapshots only, session-scoped, 50MB cap) and
  is wired into AiSdkBackend.readAttachmentBytes so image refs reach the
  provider as image parts.
- runtime: export AttachmentByteReader type for desktop/cli consumers.

* feat(desktop): ingestAttachments resolves selected files to AttachmentRefs

Three-path ingest: images resize + snapshot to ArtifactStore (session_file
ref); workspace non-image files stay as workspace_file refs (model reads
via Read, no copy); external non-image files snapshot to ArtifactStore
(TOCTOU + deleted-temp-file safety). resizeImage is injected (Electron
nativeImage in prod, fake in tests).

* feat(desktop): attachment ingest pipeline + MIME guess + resize + IPC

- core: guessMimeFromName maps common extensions (dialog returns no MIME).
- desktop main: computeResizeDimensions (pure, tested) + resizeImageForAttachment
  (nativeImage, prod-only) keep image longest edge <=2000px to bound tokens.
- desktop main: attachments:pickAndIngest IPC opens a file picker, resolves
  selected files via ingestAttachments, returns AttachmentRef[].
- preload + global.d.ts: expose maka.attachments.pickAndIngest to the renderer.

* feat(desktop): composer attachment entry + send with attachments + chips display

- composer: + button opens picker (menu removed); drag/paste route through
  the attachment pipeline (filesToAttachmentPaths reads Electron's File.path);
  pending attachment chips render above the textarea with per-item remove.
- app-shell: pendingAttachments state + pickAttachme…

subject: feat(headless): add experimental todo task-tool baseline
body: Default-off Harbor/headless todo_write task-tool experiment for #317, with turn-tail guidance, minimal task-tool summaries, runtime-policy A/B env wiring, Harbor max-steps support, and focused headless tests.

subject: fix(runtime): bound history compact block rendering and decouple artifact reads from token budget (#565)
body:
* fix(runtime): keep history compact block rendering bounded

The model-visible rendering of a history compact block listed every folded
event's runtimeEventId, turnId, sha256 (twice), and artifactId, growing by
roughly 100 estimated tokens per folded event. Any fold beyond ~10 events
exceeded maxBlockEstimatedTokens (1024), so manual /compact on a long
session always failed open with 'skipped: max_block_tokens' - and the
metadata ate the context savings compaction exists to provide.

Render only what the model needs: block id, summary, a coverage digest
(event/turn counts, content kinds, archive count), and limitations.
Per-event provenance stays in the persisted block JSON, which is what
coverage validation and replay already read. A 500-event fold now renders
at ~120 estimated tokens instead of 10k+.

* fix(runtime): cap compact block artifact reads by storage size, not token budget

loadHistoryCompactBlocksFromArtifacts defaulted maxBytes to
maxEstimatedTokens * 4 (8 KiB), conflating the model-context budget with
the size of the persisted block JSON. The JSON carries per-event
provenance (source refs, archive refs, coverage hashes) and legitimately
grows with the folded event count, so any block folding more than a
couple dozen events was skipped with 'max_bytes' at replay time even
though its rendered context cost is small.

Default the byte cap to a defensive 1 MiB instead; token limits are
enforced on the loaded blocks' estimated tokens as before.

* fix(runtime): stop deriving history compact load maxBytes from token budget

AiSdkBackend passed maxBytes = maxEstimatedTokens * charsPerToken to the
host loadHistoryCompact loader, re-imposing the token-budget byte cap the
loader no longer defaults to. A compact block persisted from a large fold
was therefore still skipped at replay in the live send path.

Drop the derivation: the loader caps reads by storage size, and the
backend keeps enforcing token limits on the loaded blocks. Covered by an
end-to-end test that persists a 60-event block through the real a…

subject: fix(ui): theme palette leaks — derive surfaces and CTA family from base tokens (#534)
body:
* fix(ui): theme palette leaks — derive surfaces and CTA family from base tokens

Palettes ([data-maka-theme]) only override the 7 base tokens; every other
color must derive from them. A cluster of literals broke that, so palette
switches left large parts of the UI in the default zinc/blue ('换了主题色
但很多地方漏改'):

- --action/--action-foreground/--control/--control-foreground hardcoded
  blue h250 (light+dark): send button, onboarding CTA and every checked
  checkbox/radio/switch stayed blue under any palette. Now derive
  hue/chroma from --accent with min() chroma caps; the WCAG-tuned
  lightness anchors stay literal. Same bug --brand-deep had (dec85e5b).
- theme-glass darwin --color-bg-layout/-highlight/-container/-element
  hardcoded zinc hexes: on macOS the ENTIRE content pane ignored palette
  switches (the unlayered darwin block overrides reference-shell's
  var(--background) derivation). Now an l-offset ladder on --background.
- --surface-canvas hardcoded zinc: sidebar/shell plate never followed
  palettes. Now derived from --background.
- plan-reminders 系统提醒 banner: 11 hardcoded h235-245 blues → derive
  from new --system-alert-accent alias (governance contract #406 bans raw
  var(--accent) in component CSS).
- providerConfigOverlay scrim: hardcoded near-white with NO dark variant
  (dark mode got a 95%-lightness white veil) → derived from --background,
  fixing both the leak and the dark-mode bug.
- settings/QR scrims, hero user avatar, dark --chat-user-foreground,
  --color-accent/destructive-foreground: same derivation treatment.

All derivations resolve byte-identical (or visually identical) for the
default palette — verified via CDP computed styles: default detail pane
stays oklch(0.985 0 0); coral resolves hue 25, forest hue 130 across
sidebar, content pane, composer and bubbles.

Contract updates (design-system-governance-406): the action/control
subtest now pins the derived form and resolves it against the block's
literal --accent so the 4.5:1 / 3:1 WCAG gates kee…

subject: fix(ui-css): drop unlayered form font reset so font utilities reach form controls (#546) (#568)
body:
base.css carried an unlayered duplicate of Tailwind preflight's
button/textarea/input/select { font: inherit }. Unlayered author CSS
outranks every @layer rule, so the duplicate silently disabled all font
utilities (text-*, font-*, leading-*) on form controls app-wide — the
Button cva's text-sm/font-medium never actually rendered.

- delete the duplicate; preflight (@layer base) keeps the reset semantics
- pin --text-xs/sm/base--line-height to the 4-tier leading scale so the
  revived utilities carry tier line-heights, not Tailwind stock ratios
- drop the now-redundant bare leading-[1.43] from navRowVariants
- lock with renderer-form-font-reset-contract.test.ts

subject: feat(ui): migrate SearchModal + CommandPalette onto Base UI Autocomplete (#520 PR8) (#562)
body:
* feat(ui): migrate SearchModal result list to Base UI Autocomplete (#520 PR8)

SearchModal's hand-rolled roving-focus result list (activeResultIndex /
moveActiveResult / jumpActiveResult / keyboardSelectionHandledRef /
handleResultKeyDown / data-active) is replaced by Base UI Autocomplete in
activedescendant mode:

- Autocomplete.Root inline + mode="none" + autoHighlight="always" +
  filter={null}: the list renders inline in the modal body (no floating
  popup), Autocomplete does not re-filter the server-side IPC results, and
  the first result is always highlighted so Enter works without an extra
  ArrowDown.
- Autocomplete.Input renders the input via the shared InputGroupInput
  primitive (render prop); ArrowUp/Down/Enter/Escape keyboard nav is owned
  by Autocomplete (floating-ui useListNavigation).
- Autocomplete.List + Autocomplete.Item replace the hand-rolled
  <ul role=listbox> + <li><button role=option>; item onClick fires
  selectResult for both pointer click and Enter on the highlighted item.
- aria-activedescendant on the input is now managed by Autocomplete.

selectResult navigation (sessionId + turnId, restoreFocus: false), the
debounced IPC search, the inflight ticket guard, the unmount invalidation,
the clear button, the snippet rendering, and all copy/states are unchanged.

The roving-focus kbd-nav interaction (ArrowDown moved focus to the result
button) becomes activedescendant (input keeps focus, active item reflected
via aria-activedescendant). a11y is more standard; the interaction habit
shifts. Home/End now move the input cursor (Base UI ComboboxInput default);
jump-to-first/last result is not bound for now (to be confirmed by manual
testing per PR8 plan).

search-modal-lifecycle-contract: the kbd-nav it-block is rewritten to lock
the Autocomplete shape (Root props + Item onClick + selectResult navigation)
instead of the roving-focus implementation; the focus-policy it-block drops
the activeResultIndex/moveActiveResult assertions; the empty-query it-block
repoints onChange -> onValueChange. …

subject: test(css-contract): harden #568 review items (line-height pins + form-font guard) (#569)
body:
* test(css-contract): pin --text-*--line-height pairs in leading converge contract

Review P2 on #568: the three @theme inline pairs added by the form-font
fix were unlocked — deleting one, or retargeting it to a wrong-but-
whitelisted token, kept the suite green. Lock them with
assertCustomPropPinnedOnce like the --leading-* aliases.

Mutation-verified: flipping --text-xs--line-height to --leading-tight
and deleting --text-sm--line-height each fail the contract.

* test(css-contract): replace form-font block parser with selector-level guard

Review P3 on #568: the hand-written block-stack parser only flagged
selector lists made ENTIRELY of bare form types, so mixed lists
(button, body) and zero-specificity wrappers (:where(button, input))
could reintroduce the reset with the suite green.

Rewrite: drop the @layer exemption (a layered duplicate is dead weight
and one un-layering edit from the original bug), which removes the need
for block/layer parsing entirely — the detector is now an innermost-rule
scan plus one selector-item regex. Self-check covers the historical
reset, a layered duplicate, both review bypasses, and scoped-selector
negatives.

Mutation-verified: appending ':where(button, input) { font: inherit }'
to base.css fails the contract. Full desktop suite: 2114 pass.

subject: fix(desktop): 修复模型配置侧栏定位 (#570)
body:
Fix provider config sheet positioning after the Base UI Dialog migration.
Remove stale overlay flex layout that no longer controls the sheet because Backdrop and Popup are siblings.
Keep the model table layout from being squeezed by adding bounded shrink/wrap rules.
Update the provider sheet contract test to lock the new absolute-positioned sheet behavior.

subject: fix(ui): designer-audit round 1 — sidebar overlap, count mismatch, tab split, empty-state placement, vaporware chrome (#571)
body:
Fixes the P0 batch plus two P1 subtractions from the 2026-07-06 designer
audit (15-surface screenshot review):

- P0-1 sidebar 会话 label overlapped the 按状态/按项目 toggle on EVERY
  page: .maka-session-panel declared 4 grid rows for 5 children, so the
  1fr track collapsed to 0 and the toggle overflowed onto the list's
  group label. The toggle's own chrome also referenced tokens that never
  existed (--surface-secondary/-primary/-tertiary, --foreground-primary,
  --shadow-sm) and rendered invisible. Fixed with a 5-row template and by
  replacing the hand-rolled buttons with the shared SettingsSegmented
  primitive (same family as the daily-review range tabs).
- P0-2 sidebar badge said 定时任务 3 while the page tab said 4: the tab
  badge now counts non-completed reminders like the sidebar does, and the
  list's default filter is a new 进行中 view (全部/已完成 still
  selectable) so the number always matches the visible cards.
- P0-3 skills 内置 and 已安装 tabs rendered the SAME list — now split by
  sourceType (bundled vs user-added), with counts on every tab.
- P0-4 the shared EmptyState floated at 46% of the page and overlapped
  banners/cards on module pages: the absolute centering was a sidebar-
  only recipe living on the base .maka-empty-state class — now scoped to
  .maka-session-list. Daily review additionally collapses the 无活动
  empty card to a one-line note when saved reports already fill the page.
- P1-5 skills page now lands on skills the user can run (已安装 →
  内置 → market fallback) instead of the all-即将上线 marketplace;
  removed the 保持系统唤醒·即将支持 tag from the automations banner.
- P1-9 removed the dead 全部/排序：热门 pills that were styled like
  buttons but never wired.

Verified per page with CDP screenshots (segmented renders, counts match,
built-ins listed, no floating empty card). check-dead-css clean;
@maka/ui 45/45; @maka/desktop 2109/2110 — the 1 failure (history compact
artifact lifecycle) fails identical…

subject: feat(sidebar): project-grouped session list with folder-style grouping (#557)
body: Adds a status/project view-mode toggle and renders project-grouped sessions as collapsible folder sections with a 4-session preview. Project headers + show-more route through UiButton; collapsed sidebar keeps a 4-row grid; session rows align left under folders. Rebases onto main incl. #571 (SettingsSegmented) and #572.

subject: feat: wire manual /compact to LLM history summarizer
body:
- runtime: buildLlmHistorySummarizer projects folded events (incl. tool calls/results) via the runtime replay plan, builds a segment prompt, falls back to the deterministic draft on failure
- runtime: shared ai-sdk-tool-output module; summarizer emits schema-valid tool-result messages (toolName + {type,value} wrapper)
- cli: inject summarizer into writeHistoryCompact
- desktop: sessions:compact IPC -> runtime.compactSession; renderer intercepts /compact; preload exposes compact()
- tests: runtime summarizer (tool-bearing conversation -> schema-valid tool-result, no fallback) + desktop /compact routing contract across renderer/main/preload

subject: fix(ui): menu highlight uses neutral state bg, not accent (#579)
body:
The Base UI Menu primitive copied shadcn's `data-highlighted:bg-accent`
recipe verbatim. In stock shadcn --accent is a neutral hover gray, but
Maka defines --accent as the product blue reserved for emphasis, so every
menu hover painted blue — clashing with palette/search rows and the rest
of the app, which highlight list rows with --state-selected-bg.

Switch every menu item state (highlighted + submenu-open) to
bg-[var(--state-selected-bg)] and drop the accent-foreground text recolor
(neutral tint needs no contrast swap). Add a contract pinning the recipe
so the shadcn default can't leak back.

#546

subject: feat(ui): icon-only turn footer with meta tooltip; merge retry into regenerate (#546) (#583)
body:
* refactor(runtime): regenerate accepts failed/aborted source turns

First step of merging retry into regenerate (#546): regenerateTurn's
status gate widens from ['completed'] to ['failed', 'aborted',
'completed'], so it can re-run a turn no matter how the previous
attempt ended. retryTurn is unchanged here; it is removed end-to-end
in the follow-up once regenerate covers every retry case.

* refactor: remove retry, merge into regenerate end-to-end (#546)

retryTurn is gone from every layer now that regenerate accepts
failed/aborted/completed source turns (previous commit):

- runtime: drop SessionManager.retryTurn
- IPC/preload: drop sessions:retryTurn + retryTurn bridge
- core: drop RetryTurnInput type
- main: drop normalizeRetryTurnInput
- footer: collapse retry+regenerate into a single regenerate action
  (regenerate covers failed/aborted/completed; running still disabled)
- chat-view: drop the retry id + Repeat icon from the footer action set

The footer used to render two mutually-exclusive buttons (retry for
failed/aborted, regenerate for completed); now there is one regenerate
for any non-running turn. Lineage fields and badge copy are unified
in a follow-up.

* refactor(ui): unify lineage badges to 重新生成自 (#546)

retry was merged into regenerate, so the lineage badge vocabulary is
now uniform regardless of which path wrote it:

- extract badge derivation into a pure, testable module
  derive-turn-lineage-badges (the renderer view-model's relative
  imports can't run under node ESM)
- legacy retriedFromTurnId / retriedToTurnId (old sessions) are read
  back as regenerate lineages via ?? fallback - badge copy is
  重新生成自 / 已重新生成, never the legacy 重试自 / 已重试
- regenerated lineage wins over legacy retried when both present
- update stale badge-copy comments in chat-view / visual-smoke-fixture

* feat(ui): footer info action carries the turn meta summary (#546)

Add an info footer action whose tooltip is the turn meta
(model · duration · tokens · cost), giving the met…

subject: feat(cli): double-Escape interrupt, /rename, and Ctrl+T thinking toggle (#580)
body:
* feat(cli): interrupt the running turn with double Escape

While a prompt turn is running, pressing Escape twice within 600ms calls
driver.stop(), which routes to runtime stopSession and aborts the active
turn. Escape keeps meaning deny while a permission prompt is pending, and
idle Escape presses stay inert.

Part of #549 (T1 interrupt).

* feat(cli): add /rename to rename the current session

Adds renameSession to the session driver (routes to runtime updateSession
with a name patch, rejects before a session exists) and a /rename slash
command with usage error and transcript feedback.

Part of #549 (T1 session rename).

* feat(cli): expand the latest thinking block with Ctrl+T

The collapsed thinking marker now advertises the toggle, and Ctrl+T
expands/collapses the newest assistant thinking block in place, keeping
default output quiet. Session switches reset the expansion state.

Part of #549 (T1 thinking display + toggle).

* fix(cli): interrupt the running turn at most once per turn

A still-settling stop could be requested twice: after the first double-Escape
issued stop(), the time-window guard reset while turnRunning stayed true, so a
second double-Escape fired another stopSession — appending a duplicate abort
note to the session log. Guard with interruptRequested, reset at turn start and
end; a rejected stop re-arms it so the user can retry within the same turn.

Addresses review on #580.

* fix(cli): reuse the in-flight interrupt stop when closing

Double-Escape issues a stopSession and sets interruptRequested, but
close() (Ctrl-C / Ctrl-D) still called driver.stop() unconditionally.
Quitting while that first stop was still settling fired a second
stopSession and appended a duplicate abort note. close() now skips its
own stop when the turn already has an interrupt in flight.

Addresses review on #580.

* fix(cli): ignore input once the TUI has begun closing

The prior fix guarded the Esc-then-Ctrl-C order, but the reverse still
slipped through: Ctrl-C/Ctrl-D starts close(), which awaits driver.stop()…

subject: refactor(ui): composer toolbar redesign — Select pickers, unified triggers, consolidated CSS (#584)
body:
* feat(ui): simplify composer toolbar — drop dead mic, flat send, calm focus

Toolbar cleanup (#546 composer direction B):
- Remove the always-disabled voice/mic button (never functional, pure noise).
- Flatten the send button: drop the gradient + inner sheen + drop shadow
  ("machined hardware" look read heavy); flat --action with darken on
  hover/press keeps it the clear primary CTA.
- Calm the container focus state: parchment composer focus becomes a single
  accent ring (focus-ring recipe, --focus-ring-width) instead of a neutral
  ring + drop shadow; rest state drops the 1px/3px lift.
- Remove the now-dead mic-specific tool-button CSS.

* refactor(ui): permission-mode picker → Base UI Select

The permission-mode picker (composer + Settings → 默认权限模式) was built
on a Base UI Menu + a hand-styled "chip" trigger — a category error, since
it's a single-value choice, not an action menu. The bespoke chip CSS
existed to compensate. Migrate the shared picker to Base UI Select, the
semantically correct primitive that Maka already wraps (SettingsSelect)
and uses elsewhere.

- permission-mode-menu.tsx: PermissionModeMenuPopup (Menu) →
  PermissionModeSelect (Select). Standard SelectTrigger (outline control)
  + SelectValue; items render label + hint, selected item carries the
  native Select check indicator. explore still collapses to ask.
- composer.tsx + general-settings-page.tsx: both callers render the shared
  PermissionModeSelect, so option markup still can't drift.
- tool-output.css: drop the bespoke .maka-composer-mode-chip + mode-menu
  CSS (-66 lines); the model-chip geometry is preserved.
- Contracts updated to pin the Select picker; governance contrast checks
  on the token values remain.

Verification: typecheck (main + renderer + storybook) clean; 2122/2123
desktop main tests pass (the 1 fail is a pre-existing history-compact
regression unrelated to this change); affected contracts green.

* refactor(composer): consolidate card CSS, unify picker triggers, correct type scale

Three rel…

subject: test(desktop): introduce Playwright Electron E2E + CI (first version) (#581)
body:
* test(desktop): add Playwright E2E skeleton with fake-backend injection

Foundation for desktop end-to-end tests:
- resolveSessionBackend pure helper forces the deterministic fake backend
  under MAKA_E2E=1, so tests run without provider keys or network
- main.ts routes sessions:create through it, admits the fake path via the
  dev guard, and redirects userData under MAKA_E2E_USER_DATA_DIR for hermetic
  per-test isolation
- e2e/ holds the Playwright config, launch fixture (isolated userData + env),
  and a first-run spec covering the launch -> main -> renderer chain
- adds @playwright/test and `npm run e2e`

First-run spec passes (~1s); resolveSessionBackend unit tests 3/3;
existing suites unaffected.

* test(desktop): cover the chat loop and seed E2E connection state

- ensureBootstrapConnection seeds a real-looking 'e2e' connection under
  MAKA_E2E so onboarding clears and the quick-chat input is usable;
  sessions still run on the fake backend, so no real API is called
- BackendRegistry overrides 'ai-sdk' to the fake under MAKA_E2E, so every
  session-creation path (sessions:create, quickChat:start, future ones)
  routes to the deterministic fake instead of leaking to a real provider
- send-message spec: type into the quick-chat input, send, assert the
  fake backend streams its reply into the transcript

Both specs pass (~1s each).

* test(desktop): cover session create + coexistence

session-management spec: start chat A, create chat B and send in it, assert both appear in the sidebar. Empty sessions are filtered by lastMessageAt so B only lists once it has a message. Switching back via the sidebar row is deferred (rows can sit in a collapsed status group); a flaky click is worse than no assertion.

* test(desktop): cover settings theme change

settings spec: expand the collapsed sidebar, open settings, navigate to Appearance, pick the dark theme radio, assert <html> picks up the dark class.

* test(desktop): cover attachment drop; tolerate cold-start flakiness

                    moved next to the card chrome it decorates

chat-message.css was 100% empty-state hero with zero message selectors, so it
folds into hero.css (PR4 will repopulate it with the chat bubble). What
remains in chat-header.css is the header bar itself plus the chat shell
layout (.maka-chat-shell / chatViewport / chatContent / chat-jump-bottom).

Verified relocate-only: the multiset of CSS rules across the original
(chat-header + chat-message + composer) is byte-identical to the new layout
(204 rules, 0 lost / 0 added, all 5 keyframes preserved). All CSS converge
contracts and the tailwind compile contract stay green (2134 desktop tests).

Three contracts that pin selectors to chat-header.css by filename now point

subject: ci(test): run suites against pre-built dist to skip repeated rebuilds (#587)
body:
The CI test job ran `npm run build` and then `npm run test`, but the root
`test` script delegates to each workspace's own `test`, which all do
`clean && build` first. That discarded the dist just built and rebuilt
every package a second time, so the build effectively ran twice in one job
(~44s build + ~111s test, the latter mostly rebuild).

Add `test:dist`: assumes `npm run build` already ran, runs the suites
directly against dist. The six node packages run `node --test` against
their dist; headless reuses its git-isolated runner; desktop keeps its own
`test` script, because its main tests import renderer modules that `tsc`
emits under dist/renderer during `build:main`, which the subsequent
`build:renderer` (vite) clears — so desktop must `clean:main && build:main`
once more to re-materialize them before testing.

Local run: test:dist 4863 tests / 0 fail in ~25s, versus the old
rebuild+test ~111s.

subject: refactor(ui): extend typography/line-height converge contracts to TSX (#546 PR0) (#589)
body:
Close the CSS-only blind spot in the typography/line-height converge
contracts: ~64 arbitrary text-[..]/leading-[..] Tailwind utilities lived in
.tsx className strings across 6 files that the CSS scanners never read.

- css-test-helpers: add readRendererTsxFiles(); both contracts now scan
  .tsx/.ts for arbitrary font-size (text-[<num>] numeric and
  text-[length:calc(..)] length-typed calc), unitless leading-[<num>], and
  text-*/[<num>] slash line-height modifiers. var/color refs, named scales,
  and leading-[Npx] icon-centering stay allowed.
- Converge all 64 sites onto the existing scale: <=12.5px -> text-xs,
  13px -> text-sm; leading 1.4/1.45 -> snug, 1.5/1.6 -> normal.
- Update the #332 chat migration contracts (3 desktop cascade contracts +
  @maka/ui chat-primitives.test.ts): their typography anti-drift pinned
  arbitrary literals (text-[11.5px]/[12.5px]/[9px]/[12px]) and banned
  text-xs/sm. #546 PR0 intentionally converges typography onto the scale,
  so typography literals are unpinned and text-xs/sm are allowed; radius/
  spacing anti-drift is preserved.

Honest coverage noted in contract comments: the TSX scan catches literal
className strings only — not clsx/cva maps, template strings, or inline
style props.

Pre-existing (not this diff): history-compact-artifacts.test.ts fails on an
unrelated runtime artifact-size classification.

subject: ci: cut redundant installs, desktop rebuild, and vite build from the test job (#590)
body:
* ci: install ripgrep/xvfb only when missing

Make the ripgrep and xvfb install steps conditional
(`command -v X || install`): a no-op where the tool is already present,
still installs on images that lack it. No assumption about what any image
ships.

* ci(test): skip desktop rebuild via dist-renderer split + test:dist

Move the vite renderer bundle to `dist-renderer` (sibling of `dist`) so
`build:main`'s tsc side-files under `dist/renderer` (helpers imported by
main/__tests__) and the vite bundle no longer collide. `emptyOutDir` clears
only `dist-renderer`; the tsc side-files stay intact, so desktop no longer
needs `clean:main && build:main` before testing.

- vite outDir -> `../../dist-renderer`; main-window `loadFile` updated;
  check-stale-dist renderer pair updated (its header comment already
  described this two-output layout).
- desktop `test:dist` = `test:checks` (console/a11y/copy, factored out of
  `pretest` so the list is not duplicated) + `node --test dist/main`, no rebuild.
- root `test:dist` calls desktop `test:dist` instead of `test`.

* ci(test): skip the vite renderer build in the test job

`test:dist` only consumes tsc outputs (`dist/main` + `dist/renderer`
side-files). The vite bundle is never loaded by node tests: the index.html
test reads `src/`, the hygiene checks skip `dist/`, and e2e builds its own
renderer in a separate job. Add `build:test` (builds everything except the
vite renderer bundle) and use it in the test job instead of the full build.

* ci: ignore dist-renderer build output

The vite renderer bundle moved to apps/desktop/dist-renderer; .gitignore
only covered `dist`, so the new output showed as untracked. Add
`dist-renderer` alongside `dist`. (Codex review P2.)

subject: fix(renderer): fix session row actions masking and sidebar grid layout (#576)
body:
Two independent sidebar bugs, one CSS file:

1. Session row action buttons (pin/rename/archive/delete) rendered on
   top of fully-legible title text: the actions cluster's background
   gradient used `var(--state-hover-bg)` as its "opaque" stop, but that
   token is a 4% alpha tint of `--foreground` — same color as the text,
   nearly transparent. Replaced with `color-mix(in oklch, var(--foreground)
   4%, var(--background))` which flattens the tint into a genuinely
   opaque color. A 16px leading fade softens the title-to-actions
   transition without leaking text through (the old version's fade used
   the translucent token, so text was fully readable under the icons).

2. "按状态/按项目" view-mode toggle stretched to ~270px tall: PR #510
   added the toggle as a 5th grid child of `.maka-session-panel` but
   left `grid-template-rows` at 4 tracks, so the toggle consumed the
   `minmax(0, 1fr)` track meant for the session list. Added a 5th
   `auto` track for the toggle.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: fix(renderer): stop daily review page flickering from connection refresh loop (#582)
body:
The Daily Review page flickered continuously (~250 DOM mutations / 5s)
because of an unstable `connections` array reference:

1. The workspace config file watcher fires `refreshConnections()` on
   every fs.watch event (including the app's own writes to
   llm-connections.json, debounced 300ms).
2. `refreshConnections()` called `setConnections(next)` unconditionally
   — every IPC round-trip returns a fresh array reference even when the
   connection list hasn't actually changed.
3. `dailyReviewBridge = useMemo(() => ..., [connections])` invalidated
   on every new reference → all three DailyReviewPanel useEffects
   (fetchDay / listArchives / getArchive) re-ran → `setLoading(true)`
   → fetch → `setLoading(false)` → next watcher tick → repeat.

Fix: `setConnections` now uses a functional updater with a shallow
equality check (`connectionsEqual` compares slug + updatedAt per entry)
so the state reference stays stable when the content hasn't changed.
This keeps the `dailyReviewBridge` memo valid and the effects idle.

Also wraps `modelOptions` in `useMemo` inside `DailyReviewPanel` to
prevent a latent unstable-array-literal dependency (the `?? []`
fallback allocated a new empty array on every render when
`bridge.modelOptions` was undefined).

Measured: 250+ mutations/5s → 2 mutations/5s on the Daily Review page.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: feat(cli): redesign tool-result and thinking rendering (#549) (#585)
body:
* feat(cli): add TUI diff-line coloring module

* feat(cli): redesign tool-result rendering (#549)

Retain structured ToolResultContent on tool entries and render per-tool
compact summaries: Bash `$ cmd` header with exit code + tail output,
Read line/byte summary, Grep pattern + match-count truncation, colored
file_diff (compact-capped), and file_write byte summary. Replace the
single expandedToolUseId scalar with a per-session Set so earlier tools
stay expanded across later turns. Order/de-dupe tool_output_delta by seq,
group consecutive same-stream chunks, and mark redacted chunks.

* feat(cli): compact tool cards to a two-line summary (#549)

Per user feedback on PR #585, the compact card is now at most two lines:
line 1 inlines the dim input summary into the header, line 2 is a
one-line result summary with a trailing dim (Ctrl+O) hint when expanding
reveals more. Summaries by kind: terminal last output line with a
(N lines) prefix or red exit + last stderr line, file_diff +A -D path,
file_write byte note, Read line/byte count, Grep match count, otherwise
the first result line. While running, line 2 shows the latest live
output line. Expanded rendering keeps the full layout unchanged.

* fix(cli): ignore kitty key-release and repeat events in the TUI input listener

Kitty keyboard protocol terminals (Ghostty/Kitty) emit separate press
and release events. pi-tui filters releases only on the focused-component
path, but our raw addInputListener runs before that, so a release
immediately undid a Ctrl+O/Ctrl+T toggle (expansion appeared only while
the key was held) and a single Escape's press+release pair could count
as a double-Escape interrupt. Drop release events at the listener entry,
and skip key repeats for the two toggles so holding the chord does not
flicker.

* fix(cli): render thinking as ordered transcript entries

Thinking was pinned after the assistant reply text regardless of when it
happened. Store it as its own transcript entry kind pushed in arrival
order (consecutive deltas for the same message me…

subject: fix(runtime): persist thinking by emitting thinking_complete (#592)
body:
* fix(runtime): persist thinking by emitting thinking_complete

The AI SDK stream loop only pushed partial thinking_delta SessionEvents and
never a terminal thinking_complete. The RuntimeEvent read-model skips partial
events, so reasoning was dropped from the projected/materialized session:
resumed CLI and desktop conversations lost all thinking.

Emit a single non-partial thinking_complete from the backend turn-finalization
seam (alongside text_complete, same assistantMessageId), gated on accumulated
thinking text so it maps to a durable thinking StoredMessage. Capture the
Anthropic reasoning signature from the reasoning chunk providerMetadata and
forward it via a dedicated onThinkingSignature callback so signed-thinking
replay works. Multi-segment reasoning merges into one thinking_complete,
matching the existing single AssistantMessage.thinking field.

* fix(runtime): tolerate unsigned thinking in replay and persist no-text reasoning

Persisting thinking_complete for every reasoning turn (prior commit) exposed
two follow-on defects and left a gap:

1. Unsigned thinking (non-Anthropic providers, e.g. GLM) persisted an unsigned
   thinking RuntimeEvent, which buildRuntimeEventModelReplayPlan flagged with a
   BLOCKING 'unsigned_thinking' diagnostic. Because the plan spans the whole
   history, one unsigned thinking downgraded every turn's provider-native replay
   to stored-message projection, losing native tool_call/tool_result precision.
   Now: unsigned thinking is skipped from native replay items (and its semantic
   kind) with a non-blocking 'unsigned_thinking_skipped' diagnostic. Signed
   thinking still replays natively with its signature. The thinking stays in the
   UI read-model; it just never re-enters the model request.

2. thinking_complete was gated behind assistantText.length > 0, so a turn that
   ended with only reasoning (no final text) persisted neither the assistant
   message nor its thinking. Now: the turn is persisted when it has text OR
   reasoning, with an empty-text AssistantMessage pl…

subject: fix(ui): purge residual brand-green — skills banner now follows --brand-deep (#598)
body:
The default accent moved from green to logo blue (owner decision
2026-07-03), but the skills featured banner kept its fixed teal-green
family: the 199-224 hue wash gradient, border, title/body inks, sticker
card chrome, and the loudest offender — the hue-154 sticker icon that
was literally the old brand green. Comments declared the banner
'theme-independent', but after the rebrand it read as leftover green.

All 13 literals now derive hue from --brand-deep (the allowlisted
semantic alias of --accent for emphasized brand surfaces — governance
contract #406 bans raw var(--accent) in component CSS). Lightness/chroma
anchors stay pinned so the pale-wash contrast survives in both themes,
and palette switches now reach the banner (forest turns it green again,
coral warm, etc). The blue/orange companion stickers keep their distinct
hues — multicolor collage is the illustration's point; only the
brand-echo sticker follows the brand.

Repo-wide sweep confirms no other green residue: remaining hue-140-180
values are palette definitions (forest) and semantic --success status
colors (toasts, connection ok, verified badges), which are correct.

Verified via screenshot: banner renders as a blue wash under the default
palette. 2143/2143 tests pass.

subject: fix(desktop): reveal main window only after first React commit (#599)
body:
* fix(desktop): reveal main window only after first React commit

The BrowserWindow showed immediately (show: true) while React mount
waits on the onboarding snapshot prefetch (up to 2.5s), so every launch
exposed the index.html .maka-preload skeleton — users read it as a bug.

Create the window hidden and reveal it when the renderer signals its
first React commit via window:notifyRendererReady, with a 4s fallback
timer so a wedged renderer can never leave the window invisible.
Visual-smoke / E2E startHidden windows stay hidden for their whole
life; the skeleton remains as a reload-time anti-flash layer only.

* fix(desktop): defer second-instance/activate focus until first React commit

ChatGPT Pro review P2: controller focus() called mainWindow.show()
directly, so re-launching the app or clicking the dock icon during the
pre-commit window would flash the .maka-preload skeleton past the
reveal gate. Route focus() through a readiness-aware gate that defers
the request and flushes it (restore+show+focus) when the renderer
signals ready or the fallback timer fires; keepHidden (visual-smoke /
E2E) windows never show or take focus from any path.

* fix(desktop): defer saved-maximized restore until first React commit

ChatGPT Pro review P2 (round 2): BrowserWindow.maximize() reveals a
still-hidden window (verified on macOS), so restoring a persisted
isMaximized state in createWindow() bypassed the reveal gate — users
who closed the app maximized saw the skeleton again on every launch.
Route the restore through revealGate.requestMaximize(): the intent is
held until markReady, which applies it right before the reveal so the
first visible frame is already maximized. keepHidden (visual-smoke /
E2E) windows never maximize from any path.

subject: fix(ui): detail-audit round 2 — bordered actions, aligned switches, de-greened emphasis cards (#613)

Fixes from the control-form detail sweep of all 7 settings pages (user

subject: refactor(chat-message): polish code-block tier, alpha tokens, uniform turn gap, timestamp (#612)
body:
* fix(chat-message): scope code-block pre to outweigh message reset

The assistant code-block <pre> rendered at the prose body size (15px)
instead of the UI tier (--font-size-ui, 13px). The pre-existing
`[data-slot="message"] pre { margin: 0; font: inherit }` reset (#332 PR2)
has the same specificity (0,1,1) as `.maka-prose pre` and is authored
later in chat-message.css, so it won by source order and re-inherited
the prose font (size + family), leaving the `.maka-prose pre` rule dead.

Scope the code-block pre rule to `.maka-prose .maka-code-block pre`
(specificity 0,2,1) so it outweighs the reset and the UI tier holds;
the reset stays for non-code-block raw <pre> (user / system), which
should still inherit the message font. Add the
CODE-BLOCK-PRE-FONT-TIER-0 contract to lock the scoping and prevent a
future "simplification" back to a bare `.maka-prose pre`.

* refactor(tokens): govern attachment-card foreground alphas via --foreground-alpha

The file-attachment card carried four arbitrary foreground alpha
utilities (bg-foreground/[0.06], bg-foreground/[0.10] icon tile,
ring-foreground/[0.12], hover:bg-foreground/10) — scattered magic
alphas outside the font/opacity converge contracts.

Add three raw foreground-alpha tiers (--foreground-alpha-6/-10/-12)
as oklch alpha of --foreground over the backdrop, matching the
existing --border/--muted/--ring alpha convention. These are NOT the
solid --foreground-N tints (which mix with --background and would
thicken the ring / stop tracking the backdrop); the alpha form
preserves the card's existing semi-transparent rendering exactly.

Converge the card to bg-/ring-[var(--foreground-alpha-N)] (ring uses
the explicit color: type hint to avoid Tailwind's color-vs-width
ambiguity). Add the FOREGROUND-ALPHA-TOKEN-0 contract: pin the three
token definitions in maka-tokens.css and forbid bare foreground/
alphas in attachment-file-card.tsx.

* refactor(chat-message): uniform 12px turn gap (between = within)

.maka-chat (between-turn) was 24px (--space-6) and .maka-turn
(within-t…

subject: refactor(ui): re-tier font scale — heading 15 / body 13 / caption 11 (#546) (#616)
body:
Body 13 becomes the root (html font-size) and the chrome tier: ui now
aliases base, landing the app on the VS Code/macOS shape (body == chrome,
hierarchy by weight/color). The old 15px is demoted to an honest
--font-size-heading tier consumed only by title-class sites (settings
field labels, modal/card/section titles, nav-button glyph), which keep
their exact pixels. Inline code compensates 0.86em -> 0.92em so it stays
above caption size at the denser body. Contract re-pinned to the new
four-token vocabulary.

subject: fix(chat-message): prose rendering bug pass at the 13px base (#546 Phase B) (#620)
body:
* fix(chat-message): remove stale PR#212 caret hacks, clear pre-code border leak, square blockquote/table chrome (#546)

Four prose rendering defects at the 13px base, each locked by a new
PROSE-POLISH-13PX-0 contract:

- Drop the :nth-last-child(2) margin/inline hacks: they assumed the
  bubble ended with an inline timestamp child (PR #212), which moved to
  the turn footer long ago, so they instead zeroed/inlined the
  second-to-last markdown block (paragraph glued under a heading, table
  or code block). The streaming caret keeps its inline ride via a
  .maka-bubble-streaming-scoped rule.
- Reset border on .maka-code-block pre code: the inline-code pill border
  painted a rounded outline around every wrapped line box inside pre.
- Zero trailing/leading margins inside blockquote (8px/20px padding
  asymmetry).
- Table border-collapse: separate + border-spacing: 0 so the outer
  hairline and border-radius actually render (collapse voids both).

* refactor(chat-message): re-pin prose heading ladder to integer px at the 13px base (#546)

The h1-h4 em values were derived for the 15px era (22/19/16/14) and
landed on fractional pixels after the #616 re-tier: 19.07/16.47/13.87/
12.13, with h3 only 0.87px above body and h4 below body. Re-pin to
integer targets 19/16/14/13; h4 sits at 1em and reads as a heading via
weight + secondary color (GitHub's h4 convention).

Extend the LongFormArticle story with h3/h4/hr samples so the lower
ladder is visually reviewable (hr written as *** — the storybook
baseline token scan reads a quoted triple-dash as a custom property).

* fix(chat-message): shrink-wrap prose tables to content width (#546)

A short three-column table stretched to the full 72ch measure reads as
voids between columns. Adopt GitHub's markdown-table shape: display:
block + width: max-content lets narrow tables hug their cells, capped
at the prose measure with overflow-x: auto so over-wide tables scroll
horizontally. th gets white-space: nowrap so column-squeezed tables
raise their min-content width and hit the…

subject: fix(runtime): prefer PowerShell over cmd.exe on Windows and declare the shell to the model (#611)
body:
* fix(runtime): prefer PowerShell over cmd.exe on Windows and declare the shell to the model

Bash tool commands ran via spawn(shell:true), which silently picks cmd.exe
on Windows and never tells the model which shell it is in — so it guesses
the dialect and falls back to weak cmd idioms like 'dir /s /b' over
node_modules. shell-detect.ts now detects pwsh > powershell > cmd once per
process, both spawn sites (shell-exec, shell-run-manager) run PowerShell
explicitly with -NoLogo -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command, and the shell
is declared to the model in every Bash tool description plus a Shell line
in the session environment fragment. POSIX behavior is unchanged.

* fix(runtime): propagate native exit codes through the PowerShell -Command path

pwsh -Command maps the process exit code to 0/1 from $?, so a native
command exiting 42 came back as 1 (about_Pwsh documents the conversion;
reproduced against real pwsh). The spawn plan now appends a wrapper that
re-raises $LASTEXITCODE only when the final statement failed, keeping
sh -c semantics for recovered failures and cmdlet-only errors. Includes a
real-pwsh integration test, skipped where pwsh is not installed.

* fix(runtime): thread the declared ShellPlan through to every Bash execution path

The shell option on the tool builders previously only shaped the tool
description; execution still fell back to the process-wide default, so an
injected shell could declare PowerShell while running /bin/sh. The plan now
flows tool -> runShellWithBoundedTail (foreground), tool -> WorkspaceExecInput
-> LocalWorkspaceExecutor (executor path), and tool -> ShellRunBashInput ->
ShellRunProcessManager (background). The manager-level constructor override
is removed so the per-call input is the single override channel.

* test(runtime): pin exit-code wrapper boundary — earlier native code wins over final cmdlet failure

When the final statement of a PowerShell command fails, the wrapper
prefers the last native exit code over pwsh's generic 1 (exit 42 where
bare pwsh -Command wo…

subject: fix(scripts): allowlist runtime hljs/GFM classes in check-dead-css (#597)
body:
The #546 PR4 chat-message.css refactor introduced selectors for
highlight.js token classes and GFM task-list classes, which
check-dead-css reported as 29 dead classes. All 29 are runtime-generated
and never appear as string literals in source:

- hljs-* / class_ / function_: emitted by rehype-highlight
  (highlight.js 11.11.1 via lowlight `common` grammars) inside markdown
  code blocks. Verified by running lowlight from node_modules against
  sample code in ~20 languages, including hljs-class (v10-era scope
  still emitted by the php/perl/c common grammars) and the v11
  hljs-title sub-scopes class_/function_.
- contains-task-list / task-list-item: emitted by remark-gfm +
  remark-rehype on `- [ ]` / `- [x]` list items. Verified by walking
  the hast tree the pipeline produces.

Add all 29 to DYNAMIC_STYLE_HOOKS so `node scripts/check-dead-css.mjs`
returns clean on main.

subject: refactor(ui): split .maka-prose into prose.css and land the #618 prose-layer follow-ups (#626)

* refactor(renderer): split the .maka-prose layer out of chat-message.css into prose.css (#618)

blocks (font-variant-numeric folds into the pill rule) and prunes the stale
-0.012em letter-spacing archaeology (actual rule is --tracking-normal).
Contract tests re-point at the new home.

* refactor(renderer): re-home load-bearing typography from the bubble shell onto .maka-prose (#618)

color / line-height (WCAG 1.4.12 floor) / word-wrap / 72ch measure cap /
edge-margin trims move off .maka-bubble-assistant so a bare .maka-prose
consumer (tool-result bodies, PR6) is self-contained. Behavior-neutral in

same layer. PROSE-SELF-CONTAINED contract locks it.

* feat(ui): render text-kind tool results as markdown prose via .maka-prose (#546 PR6)

The generic text body swaps the mono <pre> overlay for the shared prose
layer: redaction + line cap still run on the source, the height-bounded
scroll box stays (new previewVariants prose-body part carries no
font/white-space utilities so the prose layer owns typography).
Whitespace-critical kinds (terminal / file_diff / json) keep their mono
cards. Preview contract locks the .maka-prose container.

* fix(ui): restore ARIA table semantics — scroll wrapper div + display: table for prose tables (#618)

The Phase B shrink-wrap put display: block on the table itself, which
stops it generating a table box and drops the implicit table/row/cell
roles in Chromium. markdown-body.tsx now wraps <table> in a
.maka-table-scroll div that owns the overflow-x scroller + 100% cap;
the table keeps display: table + width: max-content, preserving the
hug/wrap/scroll behavior. TABLE-A1…

subject: fix(vite): dedupe react/react-dom to prevent dual-instance white screen (#624)
body:
Vite monorepo with @maka/ui aliased to source (packages/ui/src) can
resolve 'react' to different module instances depending on the
importing file's location. Without dedupe, a fresh npm install (new
worktree, different lock resolution, etc.) triggers the classic
'Cannot read properties of null (reading useLayoutEffect)' crash
because two React copies don't share internal hook state.

resolve.dedupe is the Vite-recommended fix for monorepo React setups.

subject: feat(cli): transcript viewport, scrollback, and render memoization (#549)
body: Add an in-app transcript viewport for the CLI TUI: PageUp/PageDown scrollback over the alt-screen buffer, per-entry render memoization, and a content-anchored scroll model that keeps the reader pinned to the same text across arbitrary re-renders (growth, shrink, above/below the fold, coalesced frames). Re-pins to the tail on submit, session switch, and permission decisions.

subject: feat(desktop): system notification when a turn finishes while unfocused (#617)
body:
* feat(desktop): system notification when a turn finishes while unfocused

Raise a native OS notification once an agent turn reaches a terminal
state (completed or errored) **while the app window is not focused**, so
users who kick off a long turn and switch away get pulled back when it
lands. Suppressed while focused — the user is already looking at Maka.

Design: the renderer knows *when* a turn ends (session-event stream) and
supplies best-effort content (session name as title, start of the reply
as body); the main process owns *whether/how* — it gates on a product
toggle + `Notification.isSupported()` + window focus, sanitizes the
renderer strings, and focuses/raises the window when the banner is
clicked. Both content fields fall back to generic copy when blank
(untitled session, tool-only turn) so a notification is never empty.

- core: new `notifications.runComplete` settings section (default on),
  wired through defaults/merge/normalize with a fail-closed boolean.
- main: `notifications-policy.ts` (pure, Electron-free, unit-tested) +
  `notifications-ipc-main.ts` handler; `MainWindowController.isFocused()`.
- renderer: fire on `complete` (excluding `permission_handoff`) and
  `error`; General settings toggle bound to the new setting.

New IPC modules are added to the main-process contract source allowlist
so the IPC-surface pairing test sees the handler.

Tests: core settings contract (default/merge/normalize/fail-closed) and
desktop policy (focus/support/toggle gate, content resolution +
sanitization + fallbacks). Full core + desktop suites green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(desktop): address run-complete notification review (PR #617)

- P2 privacy: suppress the notification when `privacy.incognitoActive`
  is true — a banner carries the session name + reply/error preview
  outside the app, which contradicts incognito. Added `incognito` to the
  gate, consistent with incognito pausing local memory / search.
- P2 correctness: notify "completed" only on an `end_turn…
