subject: feat(cli): actionable first-run connection-error guidance (#549) (#630)
body:
* feat(cli): actionable first-run connection-error guidance

With no configured connection, the CLI threw NO_REAL_CONNECTION:<reason>
and printed the raw code to stderr with no guidance — the first thing a
new user hits. Translate it into what is missing and how to fix it.

- core: new connection-error-copy.ts is the canonical reason->fix-copy
  home. describeChatConfigurationReason maps each ChatConfigurationReason
  to shared Chinese fix text; chatConfigurationReasonFromError recovers
  the reason from both the bare CLI error and the IPC-wrapped form. A
  Record<ChatConfigurationReason, true> anchor fails the build if a new
  reason is added without copy.
- cli: runMakaCli catches the startup NO_REAL_CONNECTION throw and renders
  the reason-specific line plus a CLI footer (no in-app settings — open the
  desktop app, 设置 · 模型, and where the workspace lives on disk); other
  startup errors still propagate unchanged.

The desktop renderer keeps its identical inline copy (pinned by a
source-snapshot boundary test); delegating it to the new core function is
a noted follow-up.

Tests: core copy + parser unit tests (all reasons, both error forms,
unknown-reason and non-error rejection); cli formatStartupConnectionError
tests. typecheck + test green across workspaces.

* refactor(core): make connection-error copy switch compile-time exhaustive

Address pi review P3: the doc claimed a new reason without copy would fail
the build, but the switch's default branch silently absorbed it. Handle
undefined as its own case and give the switch a never tail via
assertUnhandledReason, so a new ChatConfigurationReason now fails the build
until its copy is added. The presence record keeps its runtime role (the
parser's known-token set) and its own membership guard.

* test(core): derive reason coverage from the source list; sharpen docs and guards

Address pi review P3s (all non-blocking):
- Soften the module header: it is the canonical copy, but desktop still owns
  its own copy and the boundary test substring-pins on…

subject: feat(cli): attention layer for long turns, prompts, and errors (#549) (#632)
body:
* feat(cli): attention layer for long turns, prompts, and errors

Add a small attention layer (tui-attention.ts) that drives two signals from
the runner's turn lifecycle:

- terminal title carries a state marker — ● busy while a turn/control action
  runs, ★ when something needs the user, plain when idle; attention outranks
  busy so a turn parked on a permission prompt reads as needs-you, not working.
- a terminal BEL rings when something wants the user while the terminal is not
  focused: a turn that ran past a threshold finishing, a permission prompt
  appearing, or an error surfacing.

Focus is tracked via DEC 1004 (enable on start, disable on close, parse the
\x1b[I / \x1b[O reports in the input listener). Terminals without 1004 never
report a blur, so the ring is suppressed rather than sounding while the user
watches — the title markers still work everywhere. The long-turn threshold is
injectable so tests drive the long/short split without waiting real seconds.

Tests: AttentionController unit suite (title states, focus-gated ring, no-1004
degradation) plus FakeTerminal integration through runMakaPiTui (focus
reporting enabled, unfocused long-turn and permission-prompt rings, focused and
short-turn silence). Refs #549 T1.

* fix(cli): ring attention on in-turn errors; ring once per away-episode

Codex review (P2): a prompt-stream error — a `type: 'error'` event or a thrown
sendPrompt failure — is rendered inside submitPromptToTranscript and never
reached the runner's reportError, so a short failed turn while unfocused rang
nothing, contradicting the 'errors ring' scope. Surface both via a new onError
callback and have the runner raise attention on it.

Dedupe the bell in raiseAttention: ring only on the transition into the
attention state, so an error that also ends a long turn (onError then
promptTurnEnded) rings once, and a background tab is alerted once per episode
rather than repeatedly. Clarify the ★ semantics in the docstring: it marks 'an
event fired while you were away', not a live nee…

subject: fix(runtime): persist one assistant message per AI SDK step, replay signed thinking in tool turns (#633)
body:
* fix(runtime): flush one AssistantMessage per AI SDK step

Emit a per-step AssistantMessage (text + thinking + signature) at each
finish-step boundary instead of one accumulated message at turn end, so the
ledger records the text-tool timeline at step granularity and each step's
Anthropic thinking signature stays paired with its own reasoning text.

Each step rotates a fresh assistant messageId; tool calls stamp their step id
onto tool_start (new ToolStartEvent.stepId -> RuntimeEventRefs.stepId) so model
replay can regroup a step's reasoning with its tool calls. Pure-tool steps write
no placeholder; the step-cap grace notice and abort partials flush per step.

Also count steps on the v6 finish-step chunk (the old step-finish check was
dead) and document start-step/finish-step as no-ops in the adapter.

* fix(runtime): merge per-step reasoning + tool calls in model replay

Narrow the tool-turn signed-thinking replay skip to legacy per-turn history
(tool calls with no step id); per-step turns whose tool calls carry refs.stepId
now regroup into one provider assistant message (reasoning leads, then text,
then tool calls, then tool results) — the Anthropic-valid shape, so signed
thinking from tool-calling turns replays instead of being dropped.

Read-model projectThinking pairs each step's reasoning to its own assistant row
by step message id instead of attaching to the last same-turn assistant row.

Tests: reverse the tool-turn thinking case to the per-step (merged) shape, keep
a legacy (unpaired) suppression regression, add a multi-step backend integration
test and a two-assistant read-model pairing test.

* fix(ui,desktop): render multi-step assistant messages as one answer

materialize concatenates a turn's per-step AssistantMessages (text and thinking)
in step order, keeping the first step id as the anchor and advancing durationMs
to the turn's final assistant message. The desktop Markdown export joins all step
texts. The renderer text_delta slot resets on a messageId change so a new step's
answer starts a fre…

subject: feat(cli): rewind to an earlier turn via branch (#549) (#634)
body: Non-destructive rewind: idle double-Escape (neutral editor only) and /rewind open a picker of earlier prompted turns; selecting one branches the session through that turn via runtime.branchFromTurn and switches onto the branch, so the original session is untouched and resume correctness is inherited. Excludes the conversation head turn (last message's turn), so post-compact the last real user turn stays rewindable. Dual-reviewed (codex + pi glm-5.2 xhigh): PASS.

subject: feat(cli): command discoverability and session flow (#549) (#635)
body: Add /help (registry-derived command list + keybindings) and /new (reset session id, clear transcript, keep model/thinking/permission), and fix the session picker: label rows by human name with a short id in the description to disambiguate same-named sessions, and drop the artificial 10-item cap so the scrollable SelectList reaches every session. Bare / shows the full command menu. Dual-reviewed (codex + pi glm-5.2 xhigh): PASS.

subject: refactor(cli): split pi-transcript and pi-tui-runner into focused modules (#638)
body:
* refactor(cli): split pi-transcript and pi-tui-runner into focused modules

Behavior-preserving decomposition of the two largest CLI TUI files; no
runtime logic or test assertions change (import paths only).

pi-transcript.ts (1423 -> ~770 lines) splits into:
- pi-transcript-format.ts: leaf formatting helpers (fitLine, renderIndented,
  formatToolResultContent, formatUnknown(Inline), collapseToSingleLine,
  limitText, markdownTheme) with no transcript-type deps.
- pi-transcript-tools.ts: the tool-card render subsystem (renderToolBlock and
  its compact/expanded helpers), importing the format leaf and type-only tool
  entry types.
pi-transcript.ts keeps state, ingestion, high-level render, and the non-tool
block renderers. No public export moved, so index.ts and the test file are
untouched.

pi-tui-runner.ts (1239 -> ~850 lines) splits into:
- pi-tui-layout.ts: presentational components (transcript, status line, the
  scroll/anchor Container) plus anchor helpers.
- pi-tui-pickers.ts: autocomplete provider, picker overlay, and picker item
  builders.
runMakaPiTui and its input type stay in the runner and import the two modules.

typecheck clean; all 190 tests green.

* style(cli): collapse double blank line left by the tool-block extraction

Addresses pi review P3: removing the tool subsystem left two blank lines
between renderTextBlock and renderNotice; normalize to the file's single-blank
separator. No behavior change.

subject: refactor(runtime): move the backend port and context-budget helpers to their home modules (#637)
body:
* refactor(core): move AgentBackend port contract to @maka/core/backend-types

Relocate the AgentBackend interface plus BackendCompactHistoryInput/Result
from the concrete ai-sdk-backend.ts implementation into the pure-type
backend-types module in core, where BackendSendInput/PermissionDecision
already live. Repoint the six runtime consumers (pi-agent-backend,
fake-backend, agent-run, ai-sdk-flow, session-manager, runtime-kernel) to
import the port from core instead of pulling in the ~2900-line backend
implementation for one interface. ai-sdk-backend re-exports the types for
backward compatibility. Type-only change; no runtime behavior change.

* refactor(runtime): move context-budget diagnostic helpers back to context-budget.ts

Relocate the pure RuntimeEvent/ContextBudgetDiagnostic helpers that had
accumulated at the bottom of ai-sdk-backend.ts into context-budget.ts,
where their domain and their callees (estimateRuntimeEventsTokens,
applyRuntimeEventContextBudget, searchRuntimeEventHistory,
historyCompactBlockToRuntimeEvent) already live: mergeRuntimeEventsInOriginalOrder,
buildContextBudgetDiagnosticShell, runtimeEventTurnKey,
retrieveReplayHistoryAroundSearchSource, buildHistorySearchSource,
mergeContextBudgetDiagnostic(+Patches), shouldAppendContextCompactedNote,
minimalContextBudgetDiagnostic, replaceHistoryCompactReplayBlocks, plus the
internal mergeCountRecords / mergeCompactionDecisionDiagnostics. Pure move,

(only CompactionDecisionDiagnostic added to context-budget's existing core

only re-exported them. Repoint every consumer (session-manager,

export them from the runtime barrel via tool-runtime.js, and drop the

subject: feat(runtime): session-internal CronJob scheduling (#545 + review fixes) (#639)
body:
* feat(runtime): add session-internal CronJob scheduling (Issue #15 Primitive 4)

Implements CronCreate/CronDelete/CronList tools aligned with Claude Code's
pattern. The agent can schedule prompts to fire at future times within the
same session, enabling polling, monitoring, and periodic check workflows.

New files:
- wakeup-scheduler.ts: core scheduler with timer management, cron parser,
  auto-expire (7d for recurring), jitter, idle-gate, and race-safe fire()
- wakeup-tools.ts: CronCreate/CronDelete/CronList tool wrappers
- wakeup-scheduler.test.ts: 28 unit tests covering scheduling, cron parsing,
  recurring reschedule, auto-expire, jitter bounds, idle-gate backoff, and
  cancel-during-canFire race condition

Integration:
- runtime/index.ts: export WakeupScheduler and cron tool builders
- desktop/main.ts: instantiate scheduler, register tools, wire session
  stop/archive/delete cleanup, dispose on before-quit
- ui/tool-activity.tsx: CronJob result preview cards with Lucide icons
  (Clock for one-shot, Repeat for recurring, Check for cancelled)

Closes #15

* fix(runtime): prevent unbounded CronJob record accumulation

Recurring cron jobs were accumulating ALL historical fired records
indefinitely. When CronList was called, it returned all records
(800+ observed), triggering context budget archiving and creating
hundreds of tool-result-active-* files.

Three fixes applied:

1. Non-recurring jobs: remove from records after successful fire
   (no reason to keep them; they'll never fire again)

2. Recurring jobs: reuse the same record in-place instead of
   creating a new record on each fire. The record stays in the
   map with its original id, just gets updated firesAt/status.

3. Safety cap: MAX_RECORDS_PER_SESSION = 50. pruneSession()
   drops oldest terminal (fired/expired/cancelled) records when
   exceeded.

Additionally, CronList now only returns active (pending) jobs by
default via listForSession({ activeOnly: true }), preventing the
full history from being serialized into tool results.

* review: wakeup-s…

subject: feat(ui): rework streaming output — step timeline, deep-thinking disclosure, tool trows, word fade-in (#636)
body:
* feat(core,runtime): persist tool_call stepId for step-paired UI timeline

Add optional ToolCallMessage.stepId, stamped by ToolRuntime from
getCurrentStepId() (same source as ToolStartEvent.stepId), and map it into
refs.stepId on the backfill path so post-restart model replay re-pairs a
tool call with its assistant step. The field ships with its first consumer,
the UI turn timeline (materializeTurns, next commit).

* feat(ui): materialize per-turn render timeline from step-paired messages

Replace PR1's neutral multi-step concatenation with a TurnTimelineItem
sequence (thinking / text / tools) rebuilt in storage order: each step's
thinking and text precede the tools whose stepId matches that step; legacy
stepless tools keep the tools-then-summary reading before the text; leftover
and live-only tools flush as trailing tool groups; adjacent thinking and tool
groups merge. Aggregate assistant/assistantThinking stay for copy/export/
prompt-rail consumers, with timeline as the rendering source of truth.
ToolActivityItem gains stepId for the pairing. Tests cover interleaved
multi-step, legacy single, pure-tool tail, live-only append, and merges.

* feat(ui): timeline-rendered turn body with 深度思考 disclosure + Codex tool trow

Render the turn body from turn.timeline (materialize.ts) instead of the
tools-section + single-answer layout: each step's reasoning, answer, and tools
appear in production order. Commits 2 and 3 of the plan land together because
both restructure TurnView and can't be judged apart.

- DeepThinking: one controlled Collapsible (collapsed by default; no
  defaultOpen) replacing ReasoningPanel and the .maka-turn-thinking <details>.
  Live: shimmering '深度思考' title (new TextShimmer primitive + governance
  keyframe maka-text-shimmer) and smooth plain-text body that follows the tail;
  settled: Markdown + '复制思考过程'. '已截断' pill preserved.
- ToolTrow: a contiguous tool run as one flat, borderless disclosure —
  single-tool groups render the tool's own row (no double nestin…

subject: fix(ui): decouple daily review effects from bridge reference (#582 follow-up) (#640)
body:
PR #582 fixed the most common trigger — refreshConnections() always
returning a new array reference — by adding connectionsEqual(). But
the guard compares slug + updatedAt, so it correctly passes through
connections whose updatedAt changed (e.g. provider status refresh,
credential re-verification). When that happens the bridge useMemo
invalidates and all three DailyReviewPanel effects re-fire, producing
the same loading flicker loop (#582 reported ~250 DOM mutations / 5s).

Root cause: the bridge's IPC methods (fetchDay, listArchives,
getArchive) are thin wrappers over window.maka.dailyReview.* and do
not depend on connections at all. Only modelOptions needs the
connections array, and it is already independently memoized inside
the panel. The effects should never have listed props.bridge as a
dependency.

Fix: track the latest bridge via useRef and read bridgeRef.current
inside the three data-fetching effects. Remove props.bridge from
their dependency arrays so they only re-run on actual user-driven
state changes (offsetDays, range, reloadToken, selectedArchiveId).

Measured: data-loading toggles 30×/5s → 0×/30s under stress
(149 connections.list IPC calls in 30 seconds, zero loading flicker).

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: refactor(ui): unify streaming and settled answers onto a single render path (#642) (#647)
body:
* refactor(ui): unify streaming + settled answer onto one render path (#642)

The in-flight answer no longer renders in a separate `.maka-turn-streaming`
section that gets unmounted and swapped for a committed `TurnView` at settle.
Instead the streaming buffer is injected into the tail turn's own TurnView (the
same `key={turnId}` node the committed turn settles into), so live→settled is a
data-source swap on a stable DOM node, not a two-subtree handoff.

- chat-view.tsx: `TurnView` takes an optional `liveStreaming` prop, passed only
  to the tail turn (`turns[last]` while streaming). The separate streaming
  section is deleted; a tiny fallback covers the rare "streaming before the
  optimistic user turn materialized" case. Per-frame work stays scoped to the
  tail turn — `materializeTurns` is still memoized on committed state only, so
  sibling TurnViews keep their identity and skip re-render.
- Footer is now hidden by default and revealed on hover / focus-within of the
  answer block (both the assistant turn footer and the user-message copy),
  replacing the quiet-0.72 + settle fade-in. While a turn is live its footer
  slot is a reserved-height placeholder, never an actionable regenerate/branch
  (a live tail's derived status is `completed`).
- maka-tokens.css: re-key the content-visibility override onto
  `.maka-turn[data-live-streaming="true"]`; drop the dead streaming box/margin
  rule and the `maka-footer-fade-in` keyframe.
- session-events: textless / thinking-only completion now refreshes the
  committed message in BEFORE clearing the live buffer, so the shared node's
  answer block never unmounts before the committed turn lands.

Tests: rewrite the footer-placeholder / marker-cascade / motion-allowlist
contracts for the single-node shape; add behavioral coverage for single-node
streaming, footer suppression while live, hover-gated settled footer, multi-step
and tail-selection, and textless refresh-before-clear.

* feat(ui): user-message time → absolute HH:mm, hover-gated with copy (#642)

The time …

subject: fix(desktop): status-color restraint — expected states stop shouting green (#651)
body:
The 权限与能力 page (and siblings) painted the EXPECTED state green
everywhere: granted OS rows wore a green left stripe + green icon fill +
green 已授权 badge, enabled capability cards a green border, healthy
layers green text, granted chips green pills, the granted summary count
a green number — and every toast carried a colored left stripe. None of
these were token leaks (all rode --success); they were success-color
overuse plus the stripe-on-card template trope.

Rule applied (exception-only): the normal state renders neutral; color
is reserved for states needing attention (denied/degraded/waiting),
tiny dot-tier indicators, transient feedback (copy-成功 flash), and
comparative semantics (permission diff old/new, hljs-addition).

- toast.css: per-variant left stripes removed — the tinted icon is the
  single variant signal on a neutral card
- permission page: granted/enabled/success de-tinted across rows, cards,
  chips, layers, summary tiles; denied/not_determined keep their stripes;
  已拒绝 tile only turns red when count > 0 (a red 0 is a false alarm)
  and empty tiles dim like the health page's
- health page: healthy tiles/rows keep the default hairline
- connections: verified/authenticated cards use default chrome;
  success badges/pills fall to the neutral base; OAuth logged-in badge
  neutral
- memory: save-summary box neutral card instead of green wash
- new settings-permissions visual-smoke fixture so this page finally has
  a screenshot baseline

- drag-strip: magic number 8px → var(--maka-sidebar-topbar-gap)

UiButton size=nav contributes no height; removing min-height collapsed the clickable project header below the 32px control tier. Keep min-height: var(--h-control-lg); the muted-foreground color still distinguishes it from session rows.

permission-center, health-center, and about each hand-wrote a near-identical
maka-skeleton-stack with aria-busy/aria-label. Extract SettingsSkeletonStack
(label + optional lines, snapshot preset default) and route the three pages
through it. SettingsSkeleton (settings-surface shell) is untouched.

The about-page loading contract asserted the literal aria-label source text;
synced it to the new label= prop form (same intent: skeleton carries the
accessible label while loading).

* refactor(settings): extract memory page label/format helpers to own module

The 11 React-free label/format/filter helpers (memoryOriginLabel,
memoryStatusLabel, memoryStatusTone, filterLocalMemoryEntries, the backup
summarizers, etc.) lived at the tail of memory-settings-page.tsx. Move them
to memory-settings-labels.ts and import them back.

memory-settings-labels.ts is registered in SETTINGS_SOURCE_REPO_PATHS right
after memory-settings-page.tsx so the contract source mirror still contains
the same code and the MemoryEntryList .. filterLocalMemoryEntries block
boundary still resolves (now across the two files).

* refactor(settings): extract WeChat login modals and fields to own module

BotWeChatFields, WeChatScanLoginModal, and WechatQrLoginModal (the
self-contained WeChat scan/QR login flow, ~370 lines) lived inside

subject: feat(runtime): unified Automation replaces session-internal CronJob (#643 by @hqhq1025) — adopted with review fixes F1-F3+F5 (#656)
body:
Adopts PR #643 (unified Automation: heartbeat + cron, single tool) onto
current main as a squash, preserving main's ToolCardBody dispatch and the
#647 tool-activity refactors, with the maintainer's review fixes:

- F1 (HIGH, automation-scheduler.ts): the busy-session defer budget is a
  ~45min wall-clock window (DEFER_WINDOW_MS, equivalent to the old
  wakeup-scheduler's 5s→5min exponential backoff), replacing the ~120s
  24-retry cap. A transient busy window no longer terminally expires a
  `once` automation — skipFire only runs when the window is exhausted.
- F2 (HIGH, automation-can-fire.ts): 'waiting_for_user' joins
  HEARTBEAT_IDLE_STATUSES (#639 decision — the wakeup's home scenario is
  starting a turn in place of the user); the desktop canfire test now pins
  the new set instead of the opposite.
- F3 (MEDIUM, automation-state.ts): computeJitter ported verbatim from the
  old wakeup-scheduler and wired into computeNextFire — recurring
  (interval/cron) re-schedules get up to 10% delay jitter capped at 15min,
  one-shot fires landing on :00/:30 get up to 90s early jitter, computed on
  the actual fire timestamp. Mirrored the old jitter unit tests and added
  wiring coverage; AutomationManagerDeps gains an injectable `random`.
- F5 (MEDIUM, packages/ui/tool-activity.tsx): AutomationResultPreview card
  (created/deleted/listed, localized) replaces the deleted
  CronJobResultPreview inside the shared ToolCardBody dispatch, plus a new
  desktop contract test binding the runtime tool's real output strings to
  the rendered card.
- LOW: MAX_TERMINAL_KEPT 5 → 50 (old wakeup history cap) and the
  model-facing list now surfaces deferred fire attempts (deferredFireCount),
  mirroring the old CronList fire_attempts.
- Fix: the CLI scheduler tick timer is unref()ed — the PR's always-on 5s
  tick kept the Node event loop alive, hanging any bootstrap consumer that
  exits without close() (this hung the CLI test suite indefinitely). Also
  allow-listed the PR's CLI persistence warnings in check-console.

Orig…

subject: fix(ui): switch thumb geometry + settings switch unification + permission button affordance (round 8) (#659)
body:
Three maintainer-reported defects, all CDP-measured before/after:

1. Switch thumb couldn't reach the track edge. The checked travel used a
   rem arbitrary value (translate-x-[1.125rem]) which silently shrank to
   14.625px under the app's 13px root font — Tailwind v4 spacing
   utilities are px-calc'd but rem arbitrary values are not — leaving a
   4.4px right gap vs the 2px left inset. Now translate-x-4 (16px,
   spacing scale): symmetric 2px insets. Measured 3/17 ↔ 17/3.

2. Two switch sizes coexisted inside settings: rows built from
   settingsFormRow/settingsField got the scoped 46×26 scale while 数据
   导入导出's category list fell back to the compact 36×20. The scale
   now scopes to .settingsModal [role=switch] — one switch size across
   ALL settings surfaces — and the checked translate is 20px (46 − 2×1
   border − 22 thumb − 2 inset), fixing the old 22px flush-right
   asymmetry. Measured 3/21 ↔ 21/3.

3. 权限与能力 前往系统设置 rendered variant=ghost next to the primary
   请求授权 — no edge, unrecognizable as a button. Now bordered
   secondary; contract re-pinned to ban ghost in that actions row.

Desktop suite 2281/2281; module-page switches (base scale) re-measured
symmetric as well.

subject: fix(ui): text action buttons wear an edge — ghost is icon-only (round 8b) (#660)

Applies the #613 rule uniformly: a text-labeled action button renders

subject: feat(ui): icon semantic remap + stroke unification + governance contract (#662)
body:
Sidebar icons rejected vs competitor: semantics were wrong and the glyph
family fragmented (12 different strokeWidth values from 1.5→3 across 163
call sites), nav text too weak.

Semantic remap (session-sidebar-nav.tsx):
  新任务  SquarePen → Plus
  每日回顾 LineChart → CalendarCheck
  技能    Sparkles  → Blocks
  定时任务 Clock     → Timer
  设置    Settings  (unchanged)
Blocks also replaces Sparkles on skill-entity surfaces: skills-panel skill
rows / empty-state / featured card, and the ⌘K "打开 · 技能" nav jump.
Sparkles is kept where it genuinely means AI-magic: 新建深度研究 (⌘K),
深度研究 mode pill, hero avatar / deep-research eyebrow, onboarding hint,
first-run checklist, about page. Blocks/CalendarCheck/Timer added to the
icons.tsx funnel (alphabetized); Plus/Settings already exported.

Stroke unification: deleted every per-call-site strokeWidth={...} prop
across apps/desktop/src/renderer and packages/ui/src (~163 call sites) so
all lucide glyphs ride the single governed svg.lucide weight (D5). Brand
assets (bot-brand-logo.tsx, provider-brand-marks.tsx) untouched.

Nav typography: navRowVariants base now carries font-medium (weight 500);
active rows keep semibold. Settings footer already font-medium.

Contract (icon-governance-contract.test.ts) pins: (a) no strokeWidth={ in
renderer/ui .tsx outside brand-asset files, (b) the sidebar imports exactly
Plus/CalendarCheck/Blocks/Timer/Settings from ./icons.js, (c) icons.tsx is
the only lucide-react importer in packages/ui/src. Updated two collision
pins (model-oauth-section, subagent-ui) to the stroke-less <X> form.

subject: fix(desktop): settings row switches end-align — tag-qualified selector matched nothing (#663)
body:
每日回顾 (and every settingsRow-based) switch sat at the value column's
start while inputs/selects hug the right rail. The end-align rule
existed but was written as `button[role="switch"]` — Base UI renders
the Switch root as a SPAN, so the selector silently matched zero
elements and no test could tell. Governance failure class: selector
rot with no effect-level pin.

- Rule is tag-agnostic now (.settingsRow > [role="switch"]); CDP
  measured rowRight 461px → 20px on settings-daily-review
- Contract pins the rule in tag-agnostic form AND bans tag-qualified
  role selectors in settings CSS outright (role is the contract, the
  rendered tag never is)

subject: refactor(ui): unify titled modal headers onto a shared DialogHeader primitive (#667)
body:
Three modal header languages coexisted. Collapse the titled ones onto one

title + spacer + one quiet icon-sm close button, aria-label 关闭, no border box).

- Add packages/ui/src/primitives/dialog-header.tsx (props: icon?, title,

The 新任务 row now advertises ⌘ N with a quiet kbd chip on its right

app-shell keydown handler (plain ⌘/Ctrl+N only; shift/alt combos stay
free) and listed in the keyboard-help 通用 section. Effect-stability
contract options extended. Desktop 2286/2286, dead-css clean,
CDP capture verified.

subject: refactor(ui): converge attachment thumbnail + file card onto maka radius/surface tokens (#670)
body:
The attachment surfaces were the last chat-area holdouts still styled with
raw shadcn semantic aliases and the deprecated `rounded-lg`, off the
surface-alpha + radius-token system every other chat surface uses (the
tail of #546 PR5).

- Thumbnail placeholder (chat-view): `bg-muted` -> `--foreground-alpha-6`
  (matches the file card's own placeholder fill), `border-border` ->
  `border-[var(--border)]`, and drop the `/60` magic alpha stacked on the
  50%-ink `--muted-foreground` (it dipped below the contrast floor).
- Radius: retire the deprecated `rounded-lg` alias -> `rounded-md`
  (surface, 8px) on thumbnail + lightbox; the file card's inner icon tile
  and remove button move to `rounded-sm` (control, 6px) so the nested
  corners read concentric instead of matching the outer card radius.
- Pin the converged form in attachment-frontend-contract.test.ts so the
  surfaces cannot drift back to shadcn aliases / deprecated radius.

No behavior change. Full desktop suite green (2264 tests); radius
governance + foreground-alpha contracts unaffected.

subject: feat(settings): 通用页个性化改为自动保存，去掉 保存 按钮 (#671)
body:
PR-TONE-AUTOSAVE-0: 语气偏好文本框曾是 通用 页唯一带显式 保存 按钮
（+说明行）的控件——相邻的 显示名称 / 界面语言 / 默认模型 / 开关都在
change/blur 时静默持久化。一页两套保存模型。

统一为自动保存：
- 显示名称：blur 时 flush。
- 界面语言：change 时立即持久化（并 applyUiLocale）。
- 语气偏好：停止输入约 800ms 后 debounce 保存，blur 立即 flush（blur 优先，
  清掉待触发的 timer 后立刻保存）。
- 共享 persistPersonalization(patch) 走 onUpdate，采用 NetworkProxySection
  同款 ticket + pending-count 的 last-write-wins：最新写入生效，过期响应丢弃；
  in-flight 期间同步 effect 不覆盖本地草稿。
- 失败沿用 toast.error；成功保持静默（无 toast、无 已保存 闪现）——静默就是
  本页的成功语言。移除 保存 按钮、说明行、useId、saving/savingRef 门控与
  success/warning toast。

重钉 personalization-sync 契约到新的自动保存形态：钉住 debounce+blur flush、
last-write-wins ticket、静默成功、以及个性化块内不存在行内 保存 按钮/说明行。

subject: fix(desktop): alignment governance — traffic-light-exact topbar + a machine auditor (round 13) (#672)
body:
The maintainer called out that metric misalignments kept arriving one
screenshot at a time. Two answers here:

1. The reported defect, root-caused: the LEFT topbar cluster used
   offset-y = safe-top - 18 while the RIGHT cluster uses -17 — two
   magic numbers drifted 1px apart, and neither was anchored to the
   traffic-light center by proof. Both clusters now share the -17 math;
   CDP-measured centerY 20 == the traffic-light center (14 + 6).

2. The systematic answer: scripts/audit-alignment.mjs — a CDP auditor
   that walks visual-smoke fixtures and reports, for every horizontal
   control cluster: same-type height mismatches, mixed-type centerline
   drift (>1.5px), and radius-family splits (switches exempt — pill by
   design). First full run found 定时任务's header pairing a 32px
   refresh with a 36px CTA (fixed: size icon) and re-confirmed every
   other swept surface clean. This tool is how this class of issue gets
   found from now on — not by the maintainer's screenshots.

Desktop 2286/2286 exit-code gated.

subject: fix(desktop): global radius sweep, plain-text ⌘N hint, 已阻塞 → 需要处理 (round 14) (#673)
body:
Response to the maintainer's global-view critique — the CLASS, not the
instance:

- Swept every decorative 'rounded-none' in the repo (5 hits: provider
  catalog rows ×2, OAuth cards, connection rows, onboarding first-run
  rows) — all were full-bleed row fills squaring their hover/selected
  bands against the app's rounded-fill language. providerCatalogRow
  gains radius-control. The only survivor is the input primitive's
  bare-field reset (legitimately zero inside a chrome'd wrapper).
  Static CSS scan + tsx grep used to find them all at once.
- ⌘N hint: the boxed tactile-kbd chrome came from the global kbd
  element rule; .maka-nav-kbd now explicitly resets to plain muted
  text — the reference treatment.
- 已阻塞 session status renamed 需要处理: 'blocked' means the session
  needs USER action (configure a model / re-login); the old passive
  jargon said nothing actionable. Group header + chip + tests updated.

Desktop 2291/2291 exit-code gated, dead-css clean, CDP captures
verified (rounded connection bands, plain-text hint).

subject: fix(desktop): topbar aligns to the traffic lights' MEASURED optical center (round 15) (#674)
body:
The round-13 fix used the theoretical center (position.y 14 + 12/2 = 20).
Pixel measurement of a real window capture (screencapture + PNG centroid
of the light circles) shows macOS draws the lights ~13.5pt tall with
their optical center at y=20.75 — icon glyphs at 19.8 sat visibly high,
exactly what the maintainer kept seeing. Both topbar clusters shift
+1px (offset −17 → −16): icon centers land at 21, 0.25px inside the
lights' optical center — imperceptible and integer-safe.

Lesson encoded in the comment: for OS-drawn chrome, trust the pixel
centroid, not the API math. Desktop 2291/2291.

subject: fix(desktop): 模型连接 rows speak the same row language as the catalog below (#675)
body:
The expanded provider panel painted a full-bleed 2% wash + top border
from x=0 — an edge-to-edge band running under the logo column that read
as a different component from the 模型供应商 rows below (maintainer:
'共用的解决方案却每处单独实现'). The panel is transparent now; hover /
selected state lives on the rounded row fills, inset from the edges
(mx-2 + pl-10 keeps text at the same 48px absolute column), exactly the
sidebar/session-row recipe. Spacing stays on the 4px ruler per the
spacing-converge contract (which correctly rejected my first pl-[42px]
attempt). Desktop 2291/2291.

subject: refactor(ui): collapse hand-rolled status-chip recipes onto the Chip primitive (round 1) (#681)
body:
Converge status labels onto the squared Chip primitive (tone→alpha authority
in packages/ui/src/primitives/chip.tsx), retiring bespoke CSS label recipes.

Primitive-free CSS labels → Chip (packages/ui):
- skills-panel: maka-skill-library-status-label + state_error runtime label
  render Chip size="sm"; tone derived from data-status via skillStatusChipTone
  (metadata_error/local_modified→warning, managed→info, bundled/本地→neutral).
- plan-reminder-panel: maka-plan-run-status → Chip (triggered→info,
  blocked→warning, failed→destructive).
- daily-review-panel: maka-daily-review-archive-status → Chip (ok→success,
  failed/no_model→destructive, else neutral).
Orphaned tone CSS deleted; check-dead-css stays clean.

Settings labels → Chip:
- settingsAuthActionPill: non-interactive guidance label → Chip (AccountAuthTone
  maps 1:1 to variants); the real 测试凭据 button stays a Button; preview-kind
  dashed border kept.
- settingsBotStatusPill: added a `dot?` prop to Chip (leading 6px currentColor
  round dot at 70% alpha) and migrated the hero "● 待配置" pill.
- providerCatalogBadge.is-state: gated-provider state label → Chip
  (unavailable→info, experimental→warning). Category / OAuth badges unchanged.

Contracts re-pinned to the new form (coverage preserved, never deleted):
- chip-converge-contract: file list GAINS the 5 new call sites; pins the dot
  prop + retired settingsBotStatusPillDot CSS.
- skills.test / bot-settings-ui-contract: label pins updated to the Chip render.

Status color stays exception-only; spacing on the 4px ruler.

subject: docs: UI convergence map — round 1 (Chip) shipped as #681 (#682)
refactor(desktop): wechat scan-login modals join the shared DialogHeader (#684)
body:
Both wechat login modals (direct scan + bridge QR) carried the last two
ad-hoc dialog headers flagged in #667 — one still wearing the retired
boxed settingsCloseButton, the other a bespoke grid header with its own
close recipe. DialogHeader gains an optional subtitle slot (single line
of muted instructional copy — the QR modal's 使用手机微信扫描… line);
permission-dialog keeps its richer header (status pill + meta exceed a
subtitle). Orphaned CSS deleted: both header blocks, settingsWechatQrClose,
and the settingsCloseButton family (this was its last consumer — the
style-pruning contract caught it). radius-converge row for the retired
class removed. Desktop 2291/2291, dead-css clean.

subject: feat(desktop): automation-fired turns say so — provenance chip above the user bubble (F6) (#685)
body:
A heartbeat automation injects its prompt as a user message; the chat
rendered it as if the user typed it. Turns now carry provenance:
UserMessage gains origin (persisted at the agent-run append site, fed
from the existing TurnOrigin plumbing), materialize projects it as
turn.user.automationOrigin, and the chat shows a quiet self-end chip
「⏱ 定时任务触发」 above the bubble (automation id in the tooltip —
no raw ids inline, per the lineage-badge rule). Fixture msg-user-2 is
automation-marked so the chip has a visual baseline; materialize tests
pin present/absent projection. Cron fresh sessions already carry
session-level attribution (labels + name) — unchanged.

Desktop 2293/2293 + runtime suite green, dead-css clean, CDP verified.

subject: refactor(ui): Item primitive adoption for state-fill list rows (convergence round 2) (#686)
body:
* refactor(ui): adopt the Item primitive for state-fill list rows (round 2)

Extend primitives/item.tsx with a `selected` prop (data-selected →
--state-selected-bg, the governed 6.5% foreground-alpha token) and an
`interactive` gate so inert geometry-only rows can borrow Item's
media/content/actions layout without a hover fill. Item already speaks
hover 4% / pressed 8% via Base UI useRender + render-prop composition.

Migrations:
- models 连接行 (enabledConnRow): data-default → Item `selected`; retire the
  bespoke [data-default] fill rule in models.css. #675 mx-2/pl-10/pr-6
  geometry preserved.
- daily-review archive rows: UiButton variant=quiet → Item render=<button>
  with `selected` for the active archive; ItemContent stacks title over the
  tabular-nums meta line. daily-review.css keeps only geometry + the
  selected-border accent.
- providerCatalogRow: already on Item (round 1 groundwork) — no change
  needed; its remaining CSS is pinned list-separator/logo-plate geometry.

Deferred/skipped (disproportionate contract risk, no state-fill to
converge — noted for a later round):
- enabledProviderTrigger: a Base UI AccordionTrigger header, not a list row;
  its hover already uses --state-hover-bg.
- settingsOsPermissionRow: static grid row w/ status ::before stripe, pinned
  by permissions-unified-card (grid/divider/div-actions) — Item's flex model
  conflicts, zero interactive fill to fold.
- daily-review session rows: composite (hover on the <li> container +
  non-interactive preview sibling outside the button) — not a single
  state-fill row.
- maka-skill-library-row: bespoke 3-col grid + 38px icon tile, hover on the
  parent <li>, pinned by skills.test.ts; interactive={false} support added
  so a future round can adopt without behavior change.
- sidebar .maka-list-row/.maka-nav-row: untouched by design (densest pins).

Verify: desktop 2291/2291, ui 46/46, check-dead-css clean,
audit-alignment clean (no new findings on any fixture).

* docs: mark convergence map round 2 (Item rows) shipped …

subject: docs: convergence map — round 2 (Item rows) shipped as #686 (#687)
refactor(ui): PageHeader primitive — one shell for module heroes + settings intros (convergence round 3) (#689)
body:
The app grew two independent page-header dialects that kept re-declaring the
same title/subtitle/actions structure per call site. Round 3 of the UI
convergence map extracts a single shared shell.

New primitives/page-header.tsx:
  PageHeader({ title, subtitle?, eyebrow?, icon?, badge?, as?: 'h2'|'h3',
               actions?, meta?, className?, titleId? })
plus contentClassName/iconClassName/headingRowClassName/subtitleClassName/
as_wrapper escape hatches so each call site KEEPS its existing wrapper class.
The primitive converges STRUCTURE (slot order + arrangement); per-slot
typography and surface chrome stay in the wrapper CSS, which targets the
slots via unchanged DESCENDANT selectors (`.wrap h2/h3/p`) — so no contract
re-pin was needed and radius/pruning pins are untouched.

Migrated all 6 call sites:
  - skills-panel.tsx            maka-module-main-header (as=h2, actions)
  - plan-reminder-panel.tsx     maka-plan-hero (as=h2, contentClassName=
                                maka-plan-heading, actions)
  - permission-center-page.tsx  settingsPermissionIntro (as=h3, meta:
                                RelativeTime + refresh Button)
  - health-center-page.tsx      settingsHealthIntro (as=h3, meta: Badge +
                                RelativeTime + refresh; <strong> subtitle)
  - voice-settings-page.tsx     settingsFeatureStatusHero (as=h3, icon +
                                本地自检 badge chip)
  - about-settings-page.tsx     settingsAboutHero (as=h2, icon + version/
                                channel badge fragment + tagline subtitle)

check-a11y: added the app's own product name `Maka` to the brand
allow-list (the About title prop tripped english-aria-label).

Verified: packages/ui + desktop (main/preload/renderer) builds; desktop
2293 / ui 46 tests green; check-dead-css clean; alignment audit
(AUDIT_PORT_BASE=15900) no new findings; 5 CDP captures (module-skills,
plan-reminders, settings-permissions, settings-voice, settings-about)
confirm slot typography + grid layout preserved.

subject: fix(desktop): content surface is the palette background — pure white, real contrast (#690)
body:
The darwin glass palette stepped the content container down 0.015
(measured 0.985) 'so it wouldn't glare against vibrancy', which
collapsed the card↔canvas contrast to Δ0.009 — the maintainer read it
as 'content isn't pure white and barely separates from the sidebar'.
The card is now the palette background verbatim (CDP-measured 1.0);
separation is the canvas/sidebar's job (surface-canvas −0.024 +
vibrancy), matching the reference's white-card-on-gray-shell. Dark
glass keeps its +0.035 elevation. Desktop 2293/2293.

subject: fix(cli): enable focus reporting after raw mode to stop stray ^[[I on launch (#676)
body:
Enabling DEC 1004 focus reporting before tui.start() (which sets raw mode)
meant the terminal's focus-in reply \x1b[I was echoed by the cooked-mode line
discipline, leaking a stray ^[[I onto the screen on every launch. Move the
enable to after tui.start(). Lock the ordering with a FakeTerminal write-index
contract so the regression cannot return.

subject: fix(cli): disable in-turn semantic compaction in the TUI (#677)
body:
* fix(cli): disable in-turn semantic compaction in the TUI

In-turn semantic compaction fires mid-turn and interrupts the live reply with a
"Context compacted: semanticCompact" notice for small token savings, which reads
as noise in an interactive session. Strip semanticCompact from the CLI's default
context-budget policy (keeping history/turn-boundary compaction and tool-result
pruning). Lock it with a bootstrap test asserting the backend gets no
semanticCompact policy.

* fix(cli): keep semantic-compact env opt-in instead of stripping it always

Review follow-up for PR #677 (P2).

The CLI dropped semanticCompact from the default budget policy
unconditionally, so setting MAKA_CONTEXT_SEMANTIC_COMPACT=on (or a
MAKA_CONTEXT_SEMANTIC_COMPACT_MODE) could no longer re-enable in-turn
semantic compaction — the path became impossible to exercise or debug
from the CLI.

Only strip it when the user has not explicitly opted in. buildDefault
already reflects an env-off (no semanticCompact), so an explicit on-value
now flows through to the backend. Add a test asserting the opt-in reaches
the backend.

subject: feat(cli): animate the busy tab title with a braille spinner (#678)
body:
The busy tab title used a static `●` marker, which reads as a state dot rather
than "working". Cycle it through pi-tui's braille spinner frames on an 80ms
interval while a turn or control action runs, so a glance at the tab shows Maka
is active. The spinner ticks only while the busy marker is on screen (stops when
attention '★' overrides it, when the turn ends, and on close), and the interval
is injectable + unref'd so tests drive frames deterministically and no timer
blocks exit.

subject: feat(cli): welcome block on an empty session (#679)
body:
* feat(cli): greet on an empty session with a welcome block

A fresh session opened on a blank pane with no orientation. Render a welcome
block when the transcript has no entries and nothing is pending: the maka mark,
the active model/connection/folder, and a few starting hints (input, /help,
Ctrl+O, Esc Esc). It carries no entry identity and disappears the moment the
first prompt lands. Commands are pointed at /help rather than duplicated, since
the slash autocomplete already teaches them as you type.

* fix(cli): show the welcome block on /new instead of a notice

Review follow-up for PR #679 (P2).

The welcome block only renders when the transcript is empty, but /new
pushed a "Started a new session" notice right after clearing it, so
entries were non-empty and the block never showed — /new landed on the
notice, not the welcome the PR promised.

Drop the notice: /new now leaves the transcript empty and opens on the
same welcome block as a cold start, which is itself the "fresh session,
send a prompt to begin" cue. Update the /new test to assert the welcome
block appears.

subject: feat(cli): /model across all connected providers (#683)
body:
* feat(cli): /model lists and switches across all connected providers

The TUI resolved the backend from the *default* connection (getDefault), ignoring
the session's own llmConnectionSlug, so /model could only offer the current
connection's models. Mirror the desktop app: resolve the backend from
ctx.header.llmConnectionSlug, and let /model list every model across all ready
connections and rebind the session (connection + model) on select.

- connection-target: resolveSessionTargetForSlug (per-slug) + listReadyModelChoices
  (cheap, side-effect-free readiness across all connections).
- runtime-bootstrap: backend resolves by the session's connection; expose
  modelChoices on the context.
- session-driver: setModel(model, connectionSlug?) rebinds the connection via
  updateSession (runtime already disposes the old backend on a connection change).
- runner/pickers: cross-connection /model picker, grouped by connection, updating
  the provider + thinking variants + status line on switch.

Verified against the real workspace: 11 models across 4 providers now listed
(deepseek, codex-subscription, ollama-cloud, zai) vs the current connection only.

* fix(cli): isolate per-connection credential reads in the model list

Review follow-up for PR #683 (P1).

listReadyModelChoices runs at startup (createMakaCliRuntimeContext awaits
it) and read each API/OAuth connection's secret. A single legacy or
corrupt credentials.json entry made getSecret throw, which aborted the
whole list — and thus CLI startup — even when the default connection was a
keyless local model that needs no credentials at all.

Wrap each connection's readiness resolution in try/catch so an unreadable
credential only skips that connection; the rest (and a usable default)
still launch. Add a regression test.

subject: fix(cli): flow long transcripts into native terminal scrollback (#688)
body:
* fix(cli): flow long transcripts into native terminal scrollback

The TUI windowed the transcript to roughly one screen and offered an
in-app PgUp/PgDn pager with a "N more" indicator. PgUp/PgDn do not exist
on a Mac keyboard, so long output was effectively truncated with no way
to scroll it.

Drop the windowing entirely. The transcript now renders every line; when
it is taller than the terminal, pi-tui's differential renderer scrolls
older output into the terminal's own scrollback — exactly as the upstream
Pi TUI does — so history is scrolled with the terminal/trackpad and long
output is never capped. The layout keeps only bottom-anchoring so the
editor and status line sit at the screen bottom while the transcript
fits.

pi-tui uses no alternate screen and enables no mouse tracking (only
focus reporting), so wheel/trackpad scroll reaches native scrollback.
setClearOnShrink(false) stops a shrink (collapsing tool output, a
thinking block re-wrapping) from triggering a full clear that would wipe
the scrollback the user is reading; differential rendering clears the
vacated rows without the wipe.

Removes the anchor/scroll machinery, windowTranscriptLines, the scroll
indicator, and the PgUp/PgDn handlers, plus the tests that covered them.
Adds a test locking the new invariant: a transcript taller than the
viewport is emitted in full, un-paged, with the editor pinned to the
bottom.

* fix(cli): drop stale PgUp/PgDn help, document Ctrl+O scrollback limit

Review follow-ups for PR #688:

- /help still listed "PageUp / PageDown — scroll the transcript" after the
  pager was removed. Replace it with the terminal/trackpad scroll hint so
  the help never advertises a dead keybinding; lock it with a test.
- Document the one residual scrollback interaction: a global Ctrl+O/Ctrl+T
  toggle that resizes a block above the live viewport top makes pi-tui do a
  full redraw that clears scrollback. It re-emits the whole transcript (no
  content lost, tail rebuilt into fresh scrollback) but resets scroll
  position; fully avoi…

subject: feat(skills): marketplace-grade browse — 市场 managed-source card grid + upgraded rows (#692)
body:
市场 tab is now a real marketplace grid over managed sources (was a static
即将上线 placeholder catalog):
- 3-column auto-fill grid (min 260px → 2/1 col on narrow); each card = icon
  tile + name + slug mono caption + 2-line clamped description + category Chip
  + installed state, with a quiet + install icon-button (Download) top-right.
  Card body inert; only the + acts. 官方精选 label + 导入本地 Skill action.
- Client-side category filter (全部分类 ∨) + sort (排序：名称/最近 ∨) via
  SettingsSelect on the tab row's right. Install counts OMITTED (no data).
- The old 已安装-tab 来源库 list folded into this grid.

内置 / 已安装 rows: slug moved from a visible chip into the row title
tooltip (技能：<id>); visible row is name + description only. Switch,
open-SKILL.md button, and exception-only state_error Chip all kept.
使用 button SKIPPED — no chat-prefill/invoke mechanism exists to wire it to.

Data model (backward-compatible, all optional-resolved):
- ManagedSkillSourceEntry/Record gain category (fixed 7-bucket taxonomy;
  parsed from `category:` front-matter, unknown/missing → 效率工具).
  toManagedSkillSourceEntry + module-panel-types + IPC carry it through.
- resolveManagedSkillSourcesRoot honors a dev-gated MAKA_SKILL_SOURCES_ROOT
  override so the smoke fixture seeds a deterministic catalog off-home.

Fixture: module-skills seeds 7 managed sources across categories + 2
workspace skills so the grid/filter/sort and 内置/已安装 rows render
meaningfully in CDP captures.

Contracts re-pinned: skills.test.ts (来源库 list → 市场 card grid),
visual-smoke-fixture.test.ts (+ market catalog seeding). check-dead-css
clean; audit-alignment clean (incl. module-skills). No new icons; no raw
oklch literals; new CSS scoped to .maka-skill-* module owners.

subject: fix(desktop): guidance chips carry the bare action — location moves to the detail tooltip (#693)
body:
Six account-page guidance chips all began with 在模型设置中…, repeating
the same prefix across sibling chips after #645 already gave guidance
its own visual form. Labels are the action now (保存密钥 / 拉取模型 /
退出登录 / 替换或移除凭据 / 登录 OAuth / 刷新登录); the behavioral
contract's location guarantee moved from label to detail (every
guidance detail must still say WHERE), and the one detail that lacked a
location (revoke) gained it. Desktop 2294/2294.

subject: feat(desktop): search funnel bridge — palette hands its query to the search modal (#694)
body:
Product decision for the recorded search-modal-vs-palette overlap: both
surfaces consume the same window.maka.search.thread backend, so there is
no implementation duplication — they are two interaction depths (palette
= keyboard quick-jump, modal = browse with snippets), the standard
⌘P/search-panel split. What was missing is the funnel: the palette's
content-search results now end with a 在搜索面板中查看全部结果 row that
closes the palette and opens the search modal seeded with the query
(initialQuery prop, read once on mount; topbar opens reset to blank).
Stable callbacks per the PR-FE-BUG-HUNT-0 identity rule; behavioral
tests pin the bridge row with and without wiring. Desktop 2296/2296.

subject: ci: alignment auditor joins the pipeline — portable paths + fail-on-findings (#695)
body:
The CDP alignment auditor becomes a CI gate: hardcoded machine paths
replaced (repo root derived from the script location, user-data dirs
under os.tmpdir), findings and fixture launch errors now exit non-zero,
and the e2e job runs it under the same xvfb pattern right after the
renderer it builds. Locally verified: all fixtures clean, exit 0.

subject: fix(desktop): usage/health first sweep — fixtures, edged 显示明细, 正常 goes neutral (#696)
body:
使用统计 and 健康 were the last two settings pages without visual-smoke
fixtures — added (settings-usage / settings-health), and the first sweep
found:
- 使用统计: the amber notice's 显示明细 action was a bare text ghost —
  outline per the text-buttons-wear-edges rule
- 健康: ok/正常 still rendered success-green on chips AND summary tiles,
  while the same #651 restraint rule already made 权限 已授权 neutral —
  expected states are neutral; color stays reserved for signals needing
  attention (提示/警告/错误 keep their tones)

Desktop 2296/2296, dead-css clean.

subject: fix(ui): 新任务 uses the compose icon in both sidebar states + warning tone splits from info (#697)
body:
- Maintainer: the expanded sidebar row (Plus since #662) and the
  collapsed topbar compose button (SquarePen) showed different icons
  for the same action — unified on SquarePen, the collapsed one, per
  instruction. icon-governance contract re-pinned.
- Found while sweeping 健康: --warning was literally var(--info) — the
  提示 and 警告 tiers rendered the same amber and could not be told
  apart. Warning now steps deeper + more orange (l .75→.66, h 70→55)
  with its own --warning-text mix; info keeps its amber. CDP capture
  confirms the two tiers finally read differently.

Desktop 2296/2296.

subject: docs: convergence map — R3 shipped (#689); R4/R5 run cancelled by maintainer, specs remain runnable (#698)
refactor(ui): StatTile primitive — four stat-tile recipes become one (convergence R4) (#701)
body:
The permission and health summary tiles were literal hand-rolled twins;
MetricCard (bot/usage) and the daily-review totals cell repeated the
same shape at metric-strip density. One primitive now owns the recipe:

- value slot BAKES tabular-nums (tabular-nums contract re-pinned onto

fix(ci): portable electron launch for alignment auditor (#702)
body:
* fix(ci): resolve electron binary portably in alignment auditor

#695 made the alignment auditor a CI gate but left the macOS
Electron.app path hardcoded, so ubuntu-latest e2e dies with ENOENT
before any fixture runs (breaks main and every open PR, including
#680). Resolve via the electron package export like capture-screenshots
and real-window-smoke, and fold spawn errors into the per-fixture
failure count instead of an unhandled crash.

* fix(ci): harden alignment auditor electron launch for Linux CI

Portable electron path alone was not enough: every fixture still died
with "fetch failed" on ubuntu-latest because the auditor launched unlike
capture-screenshots / Playwright e2e (no cwd, switches after app path,
stdio ignored, no Linux sandbox flags, fixed 8.5s sleep).

Align spawn with the working launchers: cwd=apps/desktop, app='.',
Chromium switches first, --no-sandbox/--disable-gpu/--disable-dev-shm-usage
on Linux, pipe stderr/stdout, poll CDP until a page target appears, and
include process tails in fixture errors so the next failure is diagnosable.

subject: feat(ui): real-time status language — first-token model-wait indicator, decoupled Stop, 继续中 hint (#646) (#680)
body:
Real-time status language for the chat turn: a first-token "正在处理…" wait indicator, a calmer "继续中…" mid-turn hint, and a Stop button that stays available for the whole turn. Fixes the head-indicator first-token race (optimistic status nudge) and decouples Stop from the wait indicator.

Follow-up review fixes folded in:
- Composer.streaming gated on (sessionAwaitingModel && turnInFlight) || activeStreamingLive so a backgrounded turn's stale arm can't leave a stuck Stop.
- Status-driven stale-transient heal (useSettledSessionTransientReconcile, keyed off [sessions]); earlier event/switch-triggered attempts read status too early.
- Optimistic-status rollback on a send that fails before the runtime.
- Heal narrowed to turn transient only (clearTurnTransientState) so a settle no longer wipes message-load-error / retry / pending-toggle state; textless refresh-before-clear marked via settlingBySessionRef so the heal doesn't clear held live thinking early.

subject: fix(cli): rewind resets to before the turn and refills its prompt (#700)
body:
Interrupting the latest turn left its aborted turn_state as the last
message, and listRewindTargets excluded the "head" turn — so the turn the
user just interrupted, the one they wanted to redo, was dropped from the
rewind list. They could only rewind to the turn before it.

Switch rewind to the semantics the gesture implies: rewinding to a turn now
resets to the state just *before* it (discarding it and everything after)
and refills the editor with its prompt for an edit-and-resend, matching how
rewind works elsewhere. With that, every prompted turn — including the
latest/interrupted one — is a valid target, and the head-exclusion (and the
bug) goes away.

- runtime: add branchBeforeTurn, the exclusive dual of branchFromTurn
  (kept unchanged; desktop still uses it). Both share createBranchSession.
  copyMessagesBeforeTurn slices by turn membership, not array position,
  because the read model does not guarantee a turn's messages are contiguous
  or that a user prompt precedes its turn_state. An empty copy (first turn)
  is a valid branch into empty context.
- cli: listRewindTargets lists all prompted turns newest-first; rewindToTurn
  branches before the turn and returns its full prompt; the runner refills
  the editor via editor.setText and updates the notice/picker copy.

subject: fix(chat): widen assistant prose to match composer width (#719)
body:
The assistant reply body (.maka-prose) carried an inner max-width: 72ch
(~480px at the 13px body font), while both the message row and the
composer share --maka-chat-measure: 680px. The assistant text therefore
stopped ~200px short of the composer's right edge, reading as wasted
space ("太窄了").

Drop the 72ch cap so prose fills the message row, which already equals
the composer measure — the assistant block now matches the composer
width. Code blocks / tables already had max-width: 100% and widen with
the row. The reading-measure decision moves to .maka-message-row (where
the 680px token already lives), so a future measure change still applies
to composer + assistant together.

Update the markdown-prose contract: it locked the old 72ch invariant, so
flip it to assert .maka-prose sets no max-width of its own. Stale
"72ch measure cap" references in chat.tsx / chat-message.css / prose.css
comments are corrected.

subject: fix(tool-trow): stabilize concurrent tool summary and settle seam (#721)
body:
* fix(tool-trow): aggregate multi-tool running summary to stop parallel jitter

A multi-tool running trow showed the active tool's description as the summary
line, cycling through each tool's intent as tools started/finished in parallel
— the 1234567 jitter. Switch to the whole-group bucket aggregation with a
"正在" prefix (e.g. "正在读取 7 个文件，搜索 2 次"), counting the whole group
including settled tools so the summary does not decrement as tools finish in
batches. Single-tool rows keep the tool's own description (locked by existing
tests, and the "what exactly is running" signal is useful when there is one).

Running summary omits the failed count (it changes mid-group as tools error);
errored tools still force-open their disclosure (trowNeedsAttention), so the
failure signal is not lost — it just stops jittering the summary line.

* fix(tool-trow): drop per-row settle fade so parallel finishes don't stack

A tool row settled with a one-shot opacity-0→1 fade (SETTLE_FADE). When
parallel tools finished together, N fades stacked into the 1234567 jitter.
The row now settles by its shimmer stopping — the same seam as the 深度思考
disclosure title (light band → static muted text), no opacity fade — so a
batch settle is N light bands dropping, not N fades stacking.

Removes the now-dead deriveToolRowMotion / ToolRowMotion (the group summary
keeps its own settle fade as the whole-group signal; the row no longer tracks
everRunning) and the two settle-fade contract tests that locked the removed
behavior. isToolRowRunning / isToolRowSettled stay (the row still uses them).

* fix(tool-trow): stabilize group icon and lock summary/row contracts (review)

Addresses Codex review findings on #721.

P3 — the group summary icon still picked the active tool's kind, so a
mixed-kind group (e.g. Read running + Grep running) flipped its icon as the
active tool changed mid-run even though the aggregated summary text never
moved. The icon now stays on the first bucket's kind (the same first-seen order…

subject: feat(runtime): Goal-based autonomous execution (Issue #15 P6) (#625)
body:
CC-style external evaluator + Codex lifecycle (pause/resume/blocked, token
budget, block cap, agent-settable goals). GoalManager + evaluateGoal (30s
timeout, evaluator-failure neutral) + handleGoalContinuation (evaluate-first,
re-entrancy guard, block cap -> stalled, token budget -> budget_limited). Wired
at the turn boundary in both cli (runtime-bootstrap + pi-tui onTurnComplete) and
desktop (goal-wiring + main.ts continuation), with an active-goal turn-tail
fragment in both system-prompt builders. Independent of Automation.

Addresses review on #625 (jackwener BLOCKERs + Astro-Han follow-ups):

- CLI interrupt now halts the loop: submitPromptToTranscript reports turn
  outcome (aborted/errored); pi-tui-runner gates onTurnComplete on a clean
  turn, mirroring the desktop turnAborted/turnError guard. An errored turn is
  non-continuable (no hammering a failing connection).
- Visible kill switch: GoalManager onChange -> 'goal-change' session event ->
  desktop header pill (turn counter, warning-tinted) with one-click clear via
  goal:get / goal:clear IPC. Rebased onto main after #643 (unified Automation).
- Evaluator runs on the session's own connection/model (not the global
  default). canContinue also rejects waiting_for_user.
- Evaluator uses maxOutputTokens (AI SDK v6), raised to 1024 so model-side
  reasoning before the JSON verdict doesn't truncate it to empty output
  (found via a real-chain e2e). Unused wait_seconds dropped from the contract.
- Goals are in-memory / session-lifetime by design (documented); a restart
  stops the loop rather than silently resuming.

subject: feat(runtime): add FormatJson builtin — in-place JSON validate + canonicalize (#658)
body: Net-new builtin (main had no FormatJson): reads a JSON file at `path`, validates it, optionally sorts keys, rewrites with 2-space canonical indent, returns only a diagnostic (valid + byte delta) — never round-trips content through the prompt. Path-contained via the workspace executor, write-locked, proto-pollution-safe key sort, no write on invalid input. Token-efficient by design (avoids Read→Write round-trip). Contributor: luojiyin1987.

Also re-learned: the desktop test suite does not compile renderer pages

subject: feat(desktop): 重新登录 lands in the OAuth connection detail sheet — expired logins re-run where they break (#723)
body:
When a Codex/Antigravity OAuth login expires, the user lands in the model
connection detail sheet, which only offered 保存修改/测试连接/删除 and an amber
banner whose sole help was prose ("请到上方 OAuth 分类完成登录…"). The one action
they needed — re-login — wasn't there. Worse, an expired token still reads
hasSecret===true, so the banner claimed 「OAuth 已登录」 while requests failed.

- Extract useOAuthLoginFlow from SubscriptionLoginModal.startLogin: the whole
  browser-loopback controller (getAuthUrl → openAuthUrl → refresh →
  completeAuthorization, one authRequestId lifecycle, synchronous one-shot
  pending guard, cancel-on-unmount, localized toast copy). Pure extraction —
  the OAuth catalog modal now renders from the hook's return; no behavior
  change (contracts re-pinned to the hook source). Claude's paste-code flow
  stays on its bespoke card (materially different: manual code + experimental
  gate), not forced through the hook.
- provider-connection-detail: map codex-subscription → codexSubscription and
  gemini-cli → antigravitySubscription; the OAuth notice gains a 重新登录 button
  (default/sm) driven by the shared flow, visible in every credential state
  except 'loading' so an expired hasSecret===true login is re-runnable. Banner
  copy re-pinned to honest states. On success it re-probes hasSecret + reloads
  the connection. Unmapped OAuth types (Claude, unknown) keep prose — never a
  dead button.
- Pull the pending guard + cancel-on-unmount into a React-free
  oauth-login-flow-guard module so both behaviors are unit-testable without a
  DOM. New behavioral test + a detail-sheet contract pin for the button.
- New oauth-relogin visual-smoke fixture (codex-subscription connection,
  focused detail sheet) so the notice+button surface is captured.

(main+renderer+storybook+core) clean; check-console/a11y/copy + check-dead-css

Target-oriented README for the shared UI package: four export surfaces

convention, the data-slot hook rule with its exceptions, where new code
goes, and the ui.tsx→primitives convergence direction. Transitional
surfaces are marked with direction + end state, not TODOs.

the main/preload/renderer split, the main naming convention, the
three-pattern IPC contract and the registerIpc() registration step, the
actual main.ts startup order, the renderer AppShell + app-shell-<scope>-<action>
split, the styles/tokens layout (with the --foreground-N wash-vs-text split),

subject: refactor(ui): remove retired streamVariants, LiveIndicator, and maka-pulse (#736)
body:
#712 retired the tool live-output stream onto the quiet ToolTrow panel, leaving streamVariants (shell cva), LiveIndicator (live dot), and @keyframes maka-pulse with zero production consumers. Remove all three and update the tests/contracts that referenced them: flip the chat-stream-cascade contract from "present" pins to a "stays removed" guard, drop the chat-primitives tests + imports, the maka-pulse keyframe + its frame contract, the 406 allowlist entry, the story demo, and the obsolete barrel enumeration + maka-pulse_ banned guard. Stale prose cross-references are deferred to a follow-up comment-cleanup PR.

Refs #712.
