subject: feat(runtime): extract shared skills module and adopt it in desktop (#749)
body:
* feat(runtime): add shared workspace skills module (#748)

Add a @maka/runtime skills module owning the runtime-facing skill read
path: scanWorkspaceSkills (scan + parse + per-workspace enablement
read), buildSkillsPromptFragment, loadSkillInstructions,
buildSkillAgentTool, parseSkillFrontMatter, readSkillRuntimeState /
writeSkillRuntimeState, and the shared path-safety primitives. Exports
RuntimeSkillDefinition (stable, no content) and ScannedSkill (adds
content + contentSha256) so a future CLI host can consume skills without
duplicating the read path or pulling in desktop governance.

11 behavior tests cover scan, prompt, lazy load, the Skill tool,
enablement state, and symlink hardening.

Refs #748 (ticket 1: runtime module).

* refactor(desktop): adopt the shared runtime skills module (#748)

Switch apps/desktop to the new @maka/runtime skills module:
InstalledSkill extends RuntimeSkillDefinition and
readInstalledSkillDefinitions calls scanWorkspaceSkills then enriches
governance (lock, provenance, managed-source status, Office seeding).
The runtime-facing functions (buildSkillsPromptFragment,
loadSkillInstructions, buildSkillAgentTool, parseSkillFrontMatter, MAX_*,
path primitives, enablement state) are deleted from desktop and
re-exported from @maka/runtime, so desktop call sites
(system-prompt-main.ts, main.ts, workspace-resources-ipc-main.ts) and the
1407-line desktop suite keep importing from ./skills.js unchanged.

Desktop behavior is preserved: the 2316-test desktop suite stays green,
matching the pre-change baseline.

Refs #748 (ticket 1: desktop adoption).

subject: fix(ui): pin Markdown heading ladder to a 0.15em step (#744)
body:
* fix(ui): pin Markdown heading ladder to a 0.15em step (#739)

The #546 Phase B ladder chased integer px targets at the 13px body base
(19/16/14/13), but h3 (1.0769em, +8% over body) was too close to body
copy — long answers relied on weight alone to distinguish H3 sections
(#739). Re-pin to a uniform 0.15em step (1.45/1.30/1.15/1) so each tier
clears the one below by +15% (CDP-verified as the minimum
distinguishable gap) and hierarchy reads by size, not weight alone.

The em values are design ratios, not px/13 rounding, so a future
body-base change does not require re-pinning the ladder the way the
#546/#616 integer-px chase did. h4 stays at 1em (GitHub's 1em h4
convention).

MARKDOWN-PROSE-HEADING-HIERARCHY-0 locks the step (>=0.15em between
tiers + h4=1em), not specific em values — a future patch may widen the
step but must not narrow it back to the #546 shape. CDP-verified:
18.85/16.9/14.95/13px.

The table-width part of #739 is not re-changed: #626 already moved prose
tables to width:max-content + max-width:100% + overflow-wrap:break-word,
CDP-verified (short/path/CJK/4-col/40-char hash/inline-code all fit 680px
and a 520px narrow column), and the fit invariants are already covered by
TABLE-A11Y-SEMANTICS-0 + PROSE-POLISH-13PX-0.

Refs #739

* fix(ui): render bare markdown elements so prose.css reaches the DOM (#739)

Streamdown's default components tag every markdown element with Tailwind
utilities (h1 "text-3xl", h3 "text-xl", th/td "px-4 py-2 text-sm", thead
"bg-muted/80", blockquote "border-l-4", ul "list-disc", ...) in the
`utilities` layer, overriding prose.css's `components`-layer rules. The
heading ladder and table padding from the previous commit never reached
the rendered DOM — CDP-verified h3 was 16.25px (text-xl) not 14.95px, and
th/td padding was 8px/16px (px-4 py-2) not 6px/12px. The issue's "16px cell
padding" was the real render all along; my earlier CDP probe used
hand-built HTML that bypassed Streamdown's utilities.

Render bare semantic elements via a bareElement helper (h1-h6, p, u…

subject: feat(runtime): host-compatibility gate for workspace skills (#748 ticket 2) (#756)
body:
* feat(runtime): add host-compatibility gate for workspace skills (#748)

Add an independent requiredTools / requiredCapabilities field to
RuntimeSkillDefinition, parsed from SKILL.md front matter alongside
allowed-tools. A new gateSkillsByHostCapabilities resolver filters skills
whose requiredTools / requiredCapabilities are not registered on the
host, before MAX_SKILLS_IN_PROMPT truncation; a missing declaredTools
entry is only an informational hint (missingDeclaredTools), while an
explicit requiredTools / requiredCapabilities mismatch hard-hides.

buildSkillsPromptFragment, loadSkillInstructions, and buildSkillAgentTool
gain an optional HostCapabilities parameter; passing it gates the catalog
and the loader (a hidden skill returns reason 'host_incompatible'),
omitting it keeps the legacy no-gating behavior so desktop call sites stay
unchanged.

7 runtime behavior tests cover required-tools/required-capabilities
parsing, scan surfacing, the gate, prompt filtering, loader rejection,
and the Skill tool. Desktop's InstalledSkill extends the new fields and
readInstalledSkillDefinitions/createStarterSkill copy them through.

Refs #748 (ticket 2: runtime gate).

* feat(desktop): declare requiredTools for bundled Office skills (#748)

Add `required-tools: [OfficeDocument, OfficeDocumentEdit]` to the three
bundled OfficeCLI skill templates so the runtime host-compatibility gate
can hide them on hosts that lack the Office tools (the CLI host in
ticket 3). Desktop keeps the Office tools registered, so it stays
unaffected.

Bumping the bundled template content also requires migrating existing
installs: LEGACY_BUNDLED_OFFICE_SKILL_SHA256 becomes a per-id list of
prior template hashes (v1 legacy `officecli open/close` plus the v2
tool-routed template without required-tools), migrateLegacyBundledOfficeSkill
migrates any unmodified install whose hash is in that list, and
isTrustedBundledSkillLockSource trusts locks whose content hash matches
either the current template or any legacy template. This keeps
user-modified legacy s…

subject: feat(cli): wire skill injection into the CLI host (#748 ticket 3) (#757)
body:
* feat(cli): wire skill injection into the CLI host (#748)

Inject the workspace skill catalog into the CLI system prompt and register
the read-only Skill tool, both via the shared @maka/runtime gated resolver.

buildCliSystemPrompt gains an explicit workspaceRoot param (distinct from the
session cwd, so the project directory is never scanned for skills) and an
optional HostCapabilities param. The prompt order is now
personalization -> skills -> workspaceInstructions, matching the desktop app.

runtime-bootstrap constructs the CLI host capability surface from its
registered tool names (builtins + automation + goals) and passes it to
buildSkillsPromptFragment and buildSkillAgentTool. The CLI has no Office tools,
so bundled Office skills (requiredTools includes OfficeDocument /
OfficeDocumentEdit) are hard-hidden without the CLI seeding them — desktop
owns Office seeding. MakaCliRuntimeContext.tools now returns the actual allTools
passed to the backend (builtins + automation + goals + Skill), so tests can
verify the Skill tool is registered.

Tests: the skill catalog is injected from a workspaceRoot separate from cwd,
gated by host capabilities (Office skills auto-filter on the CLI host); the
Skill tool is registered on the CLI host; existing prompt tests pass workspaceRoot.

Closes #748 (ticket 3 of 3: wire CLI skill injection).

* fix(runtime): gate hides legacy v2 Office skills without required-tools front matter (#748)

The host-compatibility gate filters by requiredTools, but ticket 2's v2 -> v3
template migration only runs when the desktop starts. A host that runs the CLI
before the desktop migrates would see Office skills with empty requiredTools
(the v2 template had no required-tools front matter) and fail to hide them,
advertising skills whose tools the CLI cannot call.

Add a product-metadata fallback map (officecli-docx/xlsx/pptx ->
[OfficeDocument, OfficeDocumentEdit]) used by gateSkillsByHostCapabilities when
a skill's requiredTools front matter is empty. This hides legacy v2 Office
installs on hosts…

subject: fix(ui): collapse verbose tool failure details behind a disclosure (#741) (#753)
body:
* fix(ui): collapse verbose tool failure details behind a disclosure (#741)

An errored tool rendered a human-readable failure banner followed by the
full raw payload (repeated messages + schema details), so one failed turn
could grow to ~2631px and repeat the same failure several times. The
ToolErrorBanner already truncates errorText to 240px and offers a copy
action, but the shared output panel still rendered the full raw result
alongside it.

Add a ToolErrorDetails Collapsible (closed by default, keyboard-reachable
trigger "显示原始诊断") that owns the shared-panel raw result
(quietJson.body / non-owned ToolResultPreview) when errored, and gate the
shared-panel raw on !errored so it no longer duplicates the banner summary.
Owned structured previews (automation / terminal / web_search / connector —
already bounded cards) stay rendered; only the verbose shared raw folds.

Secret redaction and size caps stay enforced upstream (redactSecrets, the
banner's 240px truncation, and the result preview's own caps). A
renderToStaticMarkup contract pins the collapse: the banner shows the
first 240px, the tail of a long error is not rendered alongside it, and
the disclosure trigger is present.

Refs #741

* fix(ui): close empty errored output panel + verify expanded disclosure

Two follow-ups from the #741 codex review:

- P2: args:{} + errored + non-owned used to render an empty destructive
  tool-output box (the raw moved to the disclosure, but showResult kept the
  shared panel open with nothing in it). Gate hasSharedPanelContent's
  showResult on !errored so the shared panel only opens when it has content.
  Added a DOM regression for the args:{} validation-failure shape.
- P3: ToolErrorDetails gets a controlled `open` prop (internal useState
  fallback when omitted) and is exported, so the contract can render the
  expanded state in static markup and prove the raw payload actually mounts
  when open — not just that it is hidden by default. disclosure-collapsible-
  contract still passes (controlled open, not …

subject: refactor(ui): converge remaining sidebar row indicator sizes (#743) (#754)
body:
* refactor(ui): converge remaining sidebar row indicator sizes (#743)

The session-row indicator path used bare 8/14/24px literals that bypassed
the spacing and control-height vocabularies. Converge them onto tokens:

  .maka-list-row-streaming-dot  width/height 8px → var(--space-2)
  .maka-list-row-unread         width/height 8px → var(--space-2)
  .maka-list-row-text           min-height 24px → var(--h-control-sm)
  .maka-list-row-status-icon    width/height 14px → documented dense exception (kept)

The 14px status icon stays a documented exception: --icon-size is 16px
(chrome glyphs on nav/button icons), and 16px here would degrade the session
row's density. A comment on the rule + the new sidebar-indicator-size-
contract pin that one selector so a NEW bare 14px elsewhere in the
indicator path still needs its own exception.

Evaluated extracting a shared streaming/unread primitive (both repeat
width/height + border-radius + background + the 3px halo): not extracted —
streaming-dot owns a pulse animation that overrides box-shadow per keyframe,
and merging the selectors would change the CSS structure the density
contract scans. Converging the literals onto tokens already removes the
off-ruler drift the issue tracked; the duplication is geometry, not a
scale mismatch.

No visual change: 8px and 24px map to var(--space-2)/var(--h-control-sm)
which resolve to the same px. streaming/unread/stale/active/reduced-motion
states are untouched.

Refs #743

* refactor(ui): converge status icon to var(--icon-size) + scan all row indicators

Three follow-ups from the #743 codex review:

- P2 (status icon SVG): the 14px wrapper on .maka-list-row-status-icon never
  clipped the SVG — buttonVariants' [&_svg]:size-[var(--icon-size,1rem)] on
  the row's UiButton overrides the <Icon size=12> presentation attribute, so
  the SVG was always 16px. Removed the misleading 14px width/height; the icon
  now converges to var(--icon-size) (16px) like every other in-button glyph.
  No visual change (SVG was already 16px).
- P2 (…

subject: feat(runtime): multi-path skill discovery (.agents/skills + .maka/skills) (#758)
body:
Scan skills from the four standard discovery paths per the Agent Skills spec
(agentskills.io), plus the existing {workspaceRoot}/skills/ for backward
compatibility. scanSkills(source) replaces the single-root scan;
resolveSkillDiscoveryPaths(cwd, workspaceRoot) assembles the five paths in
precedence order. Desktop call sites are unchanged.

Three rounds of Codex review (max effort) addressed: ancestor symlink
containment, relativePath derivation, id-before-name activation matching,
source-precedence-preserving sort order, and case-insensitive dedup.

Refs #424.

---

This is the first PR authored, reviewed, and merged entirely within Maka own CLI - a milestone of the agent improving itself.

subject: fix(ui): tighten the composer's constant footprint (#740) (#752)
body:
* fix(ui): tighten the composer's constant footprint (#740)

Compact the empty composer from ~200px to ~164px by statically tightening five
constant vertical levers (textarea min-height, composer/inner padding, inner
gap, actions margin-top). No idle/active mode switch — the Composer is the
highest-frequency surface at the bottom of the chat column, so an idle↔active
height change would jump the conversation. The textarea already auto-resizes
with content up to the 240px cap, so content-driven expand is the textarea's
own growth. Controls and the workspace/branch row stay mounted; the workspace
row margin is NOT tightened (stability).

Lock the constant geometry with a CSS-scan contract
(composer-constant-footprint-contract) pinning each lever exactly-once —
catches a later rest block, a selector-list companion, and a same-block
duplicate — plus two lock-against-regression invariants (textarea min-height
follows --h-composer-min; workspace-row margin stays var(--space-2)).

CDP on turn-narrative (820px): composer 200→164, textarea 56→44, inner 128→104,
workspace-row 36 (mt 8, unchanged).

* refactor(ui): single-source composer geometry + lock no-mode-switch

Codex fresh-eye review follow-ups (round 1):

- P2: docs/design-system.md + packages/ui/stories/spacing.stories.tsx still
  listed --h-composer-min as 56px while the token moved to 44px; synced both to
  44px and clarified the doc row as the textarea/input min height (not the
  whole-composer height).
- P3: .maka-composer in maka-tokens.css carried a redundant rest padding
  (var(--space-4) var(--space-6), overridden by .composer in composer.css) and
  the textarea kept a redundant Tailwind min-h-11 (overridden by min-height:
  var(--h-composer-min)). Removed both so composer.css is the single geometry
  source (the contract's exactly-once claim is now true, not cascade-lucky).
- P3: the contract pinned each lever exactly-once but did not lock the core
  #740 invariant — no state selector may change vertical geometry. Added a
  state-geometry …

subject: fix: converge chrome icon size and color across sidebar and titlebar (#823)
body:
* fix: converge chrome icon size and color across sidebar and titlebar

Sidebar nav glyphs were pinned to --muted-foreground (50% ink) by
unlayered CSS while titlebar icon actions sit at --foreground-secondary
(80% ink), and the same unlayered color silently killed the tone/active
icon utilities in session-sidebar-nav.tsx. The settings gear also
carried a qualified 18px + 24px-track override, off-size and off-axis
from the nav rows.

- .maka-nav-icon: drop the color declaration; glyph color now lives in
  navRowVariants, pinned to --foreground-secondary for every row (the
  darwin glass override forces row text to full foreground, so icons
  must not inherit), active rows elevate to foreground
- settings gear: drop the 18px override, share the nav rows' icon
  column (--icon-size track, px-1.5) so it sits on the same axis
- app-shell-chrome-actions: remove dead size={15}/size={16} props that
  buttonVariants' [&_svg]:size-[var(--icon-size)] always overrode
- icon-system contract: every .maka-nav-icon selector must stay free of
  px sizes and color declarations
- design-system §1.9: register the 80%-ink chrome glyph color rule

Verified via screenshot pixel measurement (sidebar-long-sessions,
light-1280): all chrome glyphs render at 16px boxes, gray 55
(--foreground-secondary), gear centered on the nav icon axis (x=21.8).

* test: close false-pass gaps in the icon chrome contract

Codex review (P2): the contract only scanned renderer CSS, so deleting
the navRowVariants glyph-color utilities or reverting the settings grid
track to 24px would still pass. Lock all three invariants:

- assert the 80%-ink base and data-[active=true] glyph utilities exist
  in session-sidebar-nav.tsx (negative-tested: removing the base
  utility fails the suite)
- assert .maka-sidebar-settings-button tracks var(--icon-size)
- allow multi-line selectors in the .maka-nav-icon scan and require
  width/height values to be exactly var(--icon-size) (closes the
  calc(18px) bypass)
- fix the inaccurate 'glyphs inherit' wording in messages and…

subject: fix(ui): disable permission-mode picker while the composer awaits a permission decision (#822)
body:
The composer's PermissionModeSelect disabled condition keyed off
`permissionModePending` and `permissionModeDisabledReason` but not
`props.disabled`. `props.disabled` is driven by `Boolean(activePermission)`
in app-shell.tsx, while `permissionModeDisabledReason` is keyed off
`activeSessionForView?.status === 'waiting_for_user'` — and the two are NOT
fully coupled. A pending permission can set `props.disabled` before the
session status flips to `waiting_for_user`, leaving a window where the
composer is frozen (permission-hint pulsing, attach button + textarea
disabled) but the mode picker stays clickable. Letting the user reconfigure
the global permission mode mid-decision is incoherent (it doesn't resolve
the pending tool call) and breaks the freeze-state symmetry.

Add `props.disabled` to the Select's disabled condition so the picker
respects the composer's own frozen state directly, not only the
separately-computed disabledReason.

CDP-verified on the live `permission-destructive` fixture: before the guard
the Select trigger read `disabled=false`, `pointer-events:auto`; after,
`disabled=true`, `pointer-events:none`, `opacity:0.45`. The `model-processing`
(streaming) case is unchanged — still disabled via the disabledReason
streaming branch, no regression.

Updated `session-status-presentation` contract to pin the new disabled
expression with a comment documenting the gap.

Found by the #546 Composer combined-state audit. Refs #546

subject: fix(ui): tokenize composer branch-picker min-height + cover it in the control-height contract (#821)
body:
The branch-picker carried a bare `min-height: 24px` while the adjacent
workspace-picker used `var(--h-control-sm)`. Both resolve to 24px (a visual
no-op — CDP-verified on the live model-processing fixture: both pickers
render min-height 24px, content height 36px), but the bare literal is exactly
the drift the `control-height-converge` contract exists to prevent. The
branch-picker was simply missing from its CONTROL_HEIGHT list (the
workspace-picker was already covered there).

Fix at the owner: swap the bare 24px for the token, and add
.maka-composer-branch-picker to the control-height-converge list so the
existing contract guards both pickers (no bare px, expected --h-control-sm
tier) instead of needing a parallel guard.

Found by the #546 Composer combined-state audit. Refs #546

subject: fix(ui): give BrowserPanel a narrow bottom-sheet layout (#546) (#818)
body:
BrowserPanel had no @media(max-width:990px) rule. Its grid auto row sized to
the panel's max-content (toolbar height); the strip's flex:1 1 auto
contributes 0 height without a definite container height, so the rAF loop
mirrored a ~zero-height rect to main and the native WebContentsView was
sized to nothing — the embedded browser disappeared beside a ~470px empty
gap. ArtifactPane already had an explicit narrow bottom-sheet; BrowserPanel
did not.

Add a <=990px bottom-sheet rule for .maka-browser-panel mirroring ArtifactPane
(full width, min-height:220px, max-height:min(42dvh,360px), border-left:0,
top border + lift shadow). min-height:220px is the load-bearing declaration:
it forces the auto row (and thus the strip) to a usable non-zero size so the
native view is visible. width:100% turns the 520px right rail into a bottom
sheet. BrowserPanel is always the second grid child (chat / browser /
artifact), so it already occupies the explicit auto row; no grid-row change
is needed (ArtifactPane as the third child uses an implicit auto row, which
defaults to auto).

Locked by a narrow PostCSS-based CSS contract test (the repo's established
contract-test parser) that locks ONLY the declaration preventing the
disappearing-browser defect: exactly one min-height:220px on
.maka-browser-panel in the 990px media block, with no !important. min-height
is genuinely exclusive to the narrow rule (the base rule does not declare
it), so a source contract locks its effective value within the block. The
test does NOT simulate the CSS cascade and does NOT claim to lock
width/border-left (also declared by the base rule, won only by source order —
not verifiable by a text contract), max-height (a space cap), or
border-top/box-shadow (visual) — those belong to visual smoke review. A
synthetic test locks the helper's collection/guard logic (catches a second
overriding rule, a duplicate property, !important, and removal); the
integration test fails if min-height is removed or overridden (verified). The
existing ArtifactPane narrow con…

subject: docs(ui): correct BrowserPanel narrow-layout comment to reflect #824 (#826)
body:
Follow-up to #818. The narrow-layout CSS comment said "the user recovers by
collapsing ArtifactPane or closing the browser (tracked in the PR notes)",
which overstates reachability at the shortest window heights (closing the
browser alone usually recovers the chat column, but at extreme short heights
the ArtifactPane collapse control may be clipped, so recovery may require
both) and points at the ephemeral PR notes instead of the durable tracker.

Replace with the wording from the #818 review consult: the chat row (not
"column") can collapse from the two 220px minima, the shared-height policy is
tracked in #824, and recovery may require closing the browser and/or
collapsing ArtifactPane. Doc-only; the browser-panel-layout contract test
strips comments and stays green (2/2).

Refs #546.

subject: refactor(ui): move section routing out of ChatView (#831)
fix(ui): stabilize long-session scroll geometry for content-visibility turns (#828)
body:
* fix(ui): warm up content-visibility turns to stop endless upward scroll

Never-rendered turns in a remounted long session occupy only the 250px
contain-intrinsic-size placeholder, so scrolling up inflates the document
turn by turn while scroll anchoring keeps compensating — the top keeps
receding. Verified against the live app: a seeded 24-turn session mounted
at scrollHeight 14640 vs 31872 real and bounced through 51 anchor-corrected
steps to reach the top; Chromium never renders the skipped turns on its own.

Add a chunked, bottom-up idle-time warm-up (createTurnSizeWarmup) that
forces each turn through one rendering opportunity so the browser records
its last remembered size, then releases it back to content-visibility:auto.
After warm-up the same session reports exact geometry after ~2 idle chunks
and scrolls to the top with constant height and monotonic scrollTop.

* fix(ui): keep pinned-to-bottom through render-skipped turn inflation

Opening a long session pins the viewport against placeholder geometry
(content-visibility turns still at their 250px estimate). The turns then
inflate to real heights with no DOM mutation and no scroll event, so the
one-shot pin plus the mutation-only follower left the viewport stranded
mid-document — verified live at scrollTop 5408 of an eventual 31872.

Give createPinnedBottomFollower a second channel: a ResizeObserver on the
content's element children (re-synced when mutations add turns), so any
height growth re-pins while the user is pinned. Verified live: a remounted
24-turn session mounts at distanceFromBottom 0, stays 0 while warm-up grows
the document 23256 to 31872, and an immediate user scroll-up is respected
(distance preserved exactly, no yank-back).

* fix(ui): record warm-up sizes from the final layout

The scroll-geometry E2E contract caught the warm-up remembering sizes from
a transient mount-time layout: until the lazy markdown-body chunk commits,
every bubble is the plain-text Suspense fallback (~7.5px taller per turn),
so each turn scrolled into view c…

subject: fix(ui): reserve chat height when both auxiliary panels open at narrow widths (#830)
body:
At <=990px both ArtifactPane and BrowserPanel become bottom sheets, each
with a fixed min-height: 220px (from #818). When both are open and the
detail area is short (<=~440px), the two fixed minima squeeze the chat row
`minmax(0, 1fr)` to 0 and the composer is clipped — a reachable state
since the 320px restore minimum in window-state.ts was restore-only and
BrowserWindow set no minHeight, so runtime drag could go shorter.

Fix with two coordinated changes:

1. chat-detail.css: add a :has()-scoped rule inside the 990px block that,
   when BrowserPanel AND a non-collapsed ArtifactPane are both present,
   scales each panel's min-height to min(22dvh, 220px) so the chat row
   keeps the remainder. min(22dvh, 220px) shrinks below 220px at short
   heights and never grows above 220px at tall heights, so >=1000px tall
   (where 22dvh == 220px) is unchanged; below that the panels yield
   proportionally. Single-panel (or a collapsed ArtifactPane) keeps the
   fixed 220px / 34px floor, so the rule scopes to both-present via :has()
   (already used in 8 other places) and skips the collapsed artifact.
   Recovery (close browser / collapse artifact) stays reachable because
   each panel's toolbar sits at the top of a non-zero-height row.

2. main-window.ts + window-state.ts: extract SAFE_MIN_WIDTH / SAFE_MIN_HEIGHT
   from window-state.ts (consolidating the 480x320 literals already used by
   sanitizeBounds + visualSmokeWindowBounds) and set BrowserWindow
   `minHeight: SAFE_MIN_HEIGHT` so the runtime resize floor matches the
   sanitizeBounds restore floor. This makes the 320px floor the dvh fix
   relies on actually enforced at runtime, not just at restore.

Tests:
- browser-panel-layout.test.ts: both-present shared-height contract locking
  the :has() rule's exact `min(22dvh, 220px)` value, both-panel coverage,
  and per-selector that each selector carries the full both-present +
  non-collapsed condition (rejects an unconditional panel selector that
  would leak dvh into single-panel, a collapsed-condition leak, a wro…

subject: feat(ui): add BrowserPanel chrome visual-smoke fixture + source contract (#819) (#838)
body:
BrowserPanel renderer chrome had no deterministic coverage, so layout/
a11y regressions (e.g. the #818 narrow-layout defect) could only be
caught by manual real-window smoke. Add two deterministic layers:

- VisualSmokeState.liveBrowserSessionIds + applyVisualSmokeFixture
  wiring + a `browser-empty` scenario that seeds the active turn
  session as a live browser session so BrowserPanel mounts. With no
  native WebContentsView in visual-smoke mode, browser.getState
  resolves null → EMPTY_STATE → the empty-state chrome (toolbar
  all-nav-disabled + <Empty> strip), exactly the #818 defect surface.
  Reuses the always-seeded turn session; no browser-specific on-disk
  seed.

- browser-panel-chrome source contract (symmetric to
  artifact-pane-layout.test.ts) locking the 5 declarative chrome
  invariants that don't need a screenshot: back/forward :disabled
  track canGoBack/canGoForward; reload/stop icon + aria-label +
  onClick swap with loading; address bar snaps to live URL on blur
  when not editing; empty state present when !hasPage. The wiring IS
  the rendered DOM for these declarative bindings, so a source regex
  is a reliable proxy without a render harness (the repo has no
  jsdom/testing-library; BrowserPanel is IPC-driven).

Loaded/loading/nav chrome states are intentionally not screenshot-
fixture'd: their layout is identical to empty (#818 is a layout
defect, not a state defect), loading/nav are transient, and their
state invariants are locked by the source contract. Seeding
BrowserState for a loaded screenshot would need a production test-
seam (main-side visual-smoke browser:state hook or BrowserPanel
visual-smoke read) for no layout-regression value.

8 baseline PNGs (1280/990 × light/dark × motion/reduced) generated
in apps/desktop/tests/screenshots/browser-empty/ (gitignored
generated captures) as a stable-subset candidate, pending visual
review + promotion to screenshots-baseline/ via a separate baseline
commit (the established pattern — baselines are committed separately
from fixture cod…

overlay-scrollbars contract test reads the file and asserts its content;

knip runs in the CI typecheck job for both workspaces. See
notes/frontend-simplification-map-2026-07-13.md for the full rationale.

subject: refactor(css): consolidate token strata into maka-tokens (Round C) (#869)
body:
Two parallel token systems coexisted: the --color-bg-*/--color-text-*
family (reference-shell.css + theme-glass.css) vs the authoritative
--background/--foreground family. Nobody knew which was canonical.

Census (machine-generated, var() consumers across all CSS+TSX):
- reference-shell.css: 23 defined -> 16 dead (removed), 3 live color
  aliases (--color-bg-container/--color-border-tertiary/
  --color-text-quaternary) moved to maka-tokens, 4 live layout-locals
  (--agents-layout-bg/--agents-content-area-bg/-gap, --sidebar-width)
  kept in-file with their layout rules.
- theme-glass.css: 2 darwin token overrides (--color-bg-container
  light+dark, --color-text-quaternary) relocated to maka-tokens; all
  glass-material + text/label rules stay.

maka-tokens.css now owns every token definition and carries a
token-authority README (authoritative --background family vs
compatibility --color-* aliases + alias policy). Both themes migrated
(:root + .dark + darwin light/dark). Superseded hue-80 archaeology
condensed (it named the deleted tokens).

Pure reorganization: no computed value changed. The shipped
`--color-bg-container: var(--background)` exception kept as-is.
maka-tokens internal zero-consumer tokens are governance-contract scale
members (z-index/control-height/spacing/radius/406) and were re-pinned,
not deleted.

Gates: desktop 2397/2397, ui 125/125, typecheck 0, check-dead-css
clean, knip desktop+ui 0, auditor exit 0. CDP light+dark captures
(turn-narrative/module-skills/settings-general/first-run) before/after:
7/8 byte-identical, 1 rasterization-drift-only (eyeball identical).

subject: refactor(renderer): shared useMountedRef hook + last formatBytes fork removed (Round D-1) (#871)
body:
The mounted-guard pattern (ref set true on mount, false on unmount, read
before every post-await setState/toast) was hand-rolled per component.
One hook in @maka/ui now owns the flag; components keep separate cleanup
effects only for their own companion refs. Contracts re-pinned to the
shared-hook form (first-run, settings-form-a11y, general-settings,
daily-review, roadmap-cleanup). voice-settings-page's formatVoiceBytes
was the last local fork of formatBytes — replaced with the @maka/ui
helper (round-21 dedup rationale). Map updated with the real ×38 census;
long tail staged as Round D-2.

subject: refactor(renderer): app-shell state-cluster decomposition — 4/5 blades (Round B) (#876)
body:
* refactor(app-shell): extract useKeyedPendingRegistry for the four pending-action sets

Replace the four hand-rolled keyed de-dup registries (turn-footer actions
with state+timers, session-row actions, per-session permission-mode and
model changes) with one generic useKeyedPendingRegistry. Only the
turn-footer instance opts into the React-visible state mirror + 5s
auto-clear timers; the other three stay ref-only and keep clearing in
their action factories' finally blocks. keysRef stays a stable Set so the
action factories and the unmount cleanup effect operate on the same object
— zero behavior change. Re-pin the source-slice contracts (sticky-model,
permission-mode presentation, row-actions fail-soft) to the registry form.

Blade 1 of Round B app-shell decomposition.

* refactor(app-shell): extract useAppShellProjectContext

Move the workspace / project-picker cluster (appInfo + git status, branch
list + pending flag, recent-workspace history, project-picker pending state
and its dedup refs) plus the createAppShellProjectActions wiring into a
dedicated hook. Seeds still come from the persisted composer defaults;
the picker refs are returned for the bootstrap unmount cleanup. Pure move,
zero behavior change. Add the two new hook files to the renderer shell
source allowlist so combined-source contracts keep seeing the moved decls.

Blade 3 of Round B app-shell decomposition.

* refactor(app-shell): extract useAppShellModuleData

Move the two sidebar-module data clusters — skills / managed sources /
bundled catalog and plan reminders — plus their createAppShellSkillActions
and createAppShellPlanActions wiring into one hook. The surface-active
predicates are injected so mutation error toasts stay scoped to the
foreground module, and the plan getter still reads the latest reminders per
call. Pure move, zero behavior change.

Blade 4 of Round B app-shell decomposition.

* refactor(app-shell): extract useShellConnections

Move the LLM-connection cluster — the connection list, default-connection
slug, connectio…

session-history-list, and daily-review-panel to useMountedRef, imported
from ./use-mounted-ref.js per intra-package house style. permission-dialog's
mount effect did nothing but the flag, so the whole effect is deleted. Re-pin
the 10 contract suites (definition + effect blocks) to the shared-hook form.

model+label, thinking-variant lists, sticky-pick validation, both pending

subject: feat(headless): wire synthesis cache into Harbor + benchmark it (#976)
body:
* feat(headless): wire synthesis cache into Harbor cell + A/B toggle

Headless Terminal-Bench runs recomputed synthesis blocks instead of
reusing them: harbor-cell never injected the synthesis-cache callbacks
or built the policy. Wire it end-to-end, gated by
MAKA_CONTEXT_SYNTHESIS_CACHE so it is A/B-toggleable via
runtime-policy-ab-run.

- Lift the artifact-store glue (load/persist synthesis cache blocks)
  from apps/desktop into @maka/runtime, mirroring the sibling
  history-compact-artifacts module. Typed against a structural
  SynthesisCacheArtifactStore interface (core types only) so runtime
  stays storage-agnostic; @maka/storage's ArtifactStore satisfies it.
  Desktop main now imports the shared implementation.
- harbor-cell: build the synthesisCache sub-policy from
  MAKA_CONTEXT_SYNTHESIS_CACHE[_MODE|_MAX_BLOCKS|_MAX_TOKENS|
  _MAX_BLOCK_TOKENS], add the keys to HARBOR_CELL_CONTEXT_ENV_KEYS
  (runtime-policy-ab-run derives its arm allowlist from it), include the
  policy in the early-return guard, inject load/write callbacks backed by
  createArtifactStore(storageRoot), and surface it in the policy snapshot.
- Tests: runtime unit test (in-memory fake store, all skip reasons) and a
  headless synthesis-cache-only arm test (guard + snapshot).

Wiring + measurement only; no synthesis-cache behavior change.
Child of #481. Refs #578.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(runtime): benchmark synthesis-cache replay-token savings + doc

Add a deterministic A/B (mock model, no network, CI-reproducible) showing
the synthesis cache cuts replay tokens 72.9% (2818 -> 764) by injecting
the compact synthesis block instead of re-hydrating the archived tool
result. Document it in docs/economic-mechanisms-benchmark.md alongside the
live Harbor activation evidence (synthesisCacheEnabled + write gate
reached) and why the live cost/pass-rate A/B is deferred: a Terminal-Bench
run collapses to one turn, so nothing is archived-then-retrieved to feed
the write path (source_missing) -- a workload mismatc…

subject: feat(runtime): star orchestrator→worker expert teams (#971)
body:
* feat(runtime): add star orchestrator→worker expert teams

Expert teams let a lead persona fan a task out to tool-scoped member
experts (child agents), then synthesize their results — the star
topology reverse-engineered from WorkBuddy's `team` experts and
QoderWork's sub-agent pipelines, rebuilt entirely on Maka's existing
child-agent machinery (no new orchestration engine, no mesh mailbox).

Runtime (@maka/runtime):
- expert-catalog.ts: ExpertDefinition / ExpertTeamDefinition, data-driven
  members that inherit a capability archetype (tool scope + permission
  mode) and may only narrow its tools — never widen. Members materialize
  into ordinary AgentDefinitions with a deterministic id
  (expert:<teamId>:<memberId>) so the child-turn machinery runs them
  unchanged and a spawn resolves statelessly from the id.
- expert-tools.ts: the team-bound `expert_dispatch` tool (member enum +
  roster), a thin wrapper over spawnChildAgent; parallel fan-out reuses
  the existing per-turn concurrency; fan-in is the child summary +
  artifact-id pointers.
- Pluggable definition resolver: spawnChildAgent / startChildTurn resolve
  a spec id via requireResolvedAgentDefinition (builtin ?? expert),
  preserving builtin error messages.
- Ships a read-only Code Review Team (correctness / simplification /
  test-coverage reviewers) that runs within current capabilities.

Core (@maka/core):
- expert-team.ts: the `mode:expert-team:<teamId>` session-label vocabulary.

Desktop:
- Lead system-prompt fragment + `expert_dispatch` tool gated on the label
  (main session only — members never get nested teams).
- expertTeam:list / expertTeam:start IPC + preload bridge + window typings
  to start a read-only team session.

Tests across core / runtime / desktop-main. Docs in docs/expert-team-*.md.

* feat(ui): composer "+" opens a menu — add file/dir + expert team

The composer "+" button attached files directly. Turn it into a small
menu (reusing the existing Menu primitive, same as the workspace/branch
pickers):

- 添加文件或�…

Tests: trigger-detection boundary matrix + matcher; workspace file
search (git/walk/containment/cap/no_project); popup contract
(Enter-intercept precedes send, Esc closes popup only, SSR inert).

subject: feat(core): derive provider conformance matrix from registry declarations (#974)
body:
* feat(core): add registry-derived provider contract matrix plan

Phase 7 of #860: derive rows from every ready registry entry and resolve
each contract dimension to generated / named-override / explicit-N/A,
as a pure function with no runtime behavior change.

* test(runtime): execute the generated provider conformance matrix

Phase 7 of #860: run every generated cell against scripted local
protocol servers, assert named overrides are registered, execute
fallback reverse assertions, and fail CI on any coverage gap.

* test(runtime): enforce derived contracts in generated conformance cells

Review hardening for the Phase 7 matrix (#860): discovery cells now assert
the declared path, query, and credential placement before responding (public
discovery must carry no credential; array-or-data runs both fixtures);
Anthropic/Google/Cohere wire runners assert the second turn actually replays
the tool result tied to the first-turn call id, matching the OpenAI runner;
override registry entries bind to exact hand-written test titles so deleting
or renaming an owned test fails the matrix instead of drifting silently.
Every new assertion was mutation-verified red before restore.

* fix(core): derive auth none for providers with no credential to send

The plan derived discovery auth 'default' for authKind 'none' providers
(lm-studio), forcing the runtime executor to second-guess the plan via
providerAuthSupportsApiKey. Derive 'none' at the plan layer instead and
drop the executor workaround: a generated 'none' cell now uniformly means
the request must carry no credential.

* test(runtime): verify Copilot second-turn wire and harden override bindings

Rereview round two on the Phase 7 matrix (#860): the GitHub Copilot
conformance test now asserts the second turn carries the exact model id,
replays the assistant tool_calls id and reasoning_content verbatim, and
links the tool result via tool_call_id; the github-copilot:discovery
override rebinds to the dedicated picker/endpoint-gating test in
model-fetcher.test.ts (bindings now…

subject: refactor(settings): present model connections as first-class rows (#988)
body:
* feat(ui): add accent variant to Chip primitive

Brand-accent marker for default-connection style flags, reproducing the
retired .enabledDefaultTag visual (nav-active text on a nav-active /14
wash). Kept outside the StatusTone scale that the other variants mirror.

* refactor(settings): flat provider connection rows (#864)

Replace the per-provider accordion grouping in Settings → 模型 with one
top-level row per connection, following the bot-chat channel-row template
(logo · name + 默认 chip · provider · status chip · chevron). Rows keep
bridge.list() order; the 已配置 header counts connections only.

- Add chipStatusTone() beside chipStatusText(), branching in lockstep so
  the status dot color never disagrees with the copy; drop the retired
  rollupForGroup()/GroupRollup group-collapse logic.
- Prune the accordion CSS (.enabledAccordion/.enabledProvider*/.enabledRollup/
  .enabledConnStatus/.enabledDefaultTag/…) down to .connectionList/.connectionRow
  (scroll container, 0.06 hairline, control radius, disabled dimming).
- Sync the style-pruning, oauth-section, and connection-status contract
  tests to the removed accordion hooks and helpers.

* fix(settings): keep failure signal on disabled connections via single chip status helper

PR #988 review P1: oauth-model-connections-main.ts failDiscovery()
persists enabled:false + lastTestStatus:'error', and the split
chipStatusText/chipStatusTone helpers duplicated the state machine —
the tone's !enabled branch short-circuited before error, washing the
red signal out to neutral. Merge both into connectionChipStatus()
returning { label, tone } from one branch order:

- needs_reauth → 需要重新登录 / info (wins over disabled, unchanged)
- !enabled + error → 暂不可用 · 上次连接失败 / destructive (was lost)
- !enabled → 暂不可用 / neutral (disabled+verified lands here on
  purpose: no stale green on an unusable connection)
- verified / error / default → success / destructive / neutral

The aria-label and title now carry the fa…

subject: feat(cli): TUI rendering polish — statusline density, picker widths, truncation fixes (#990)
body:
* fix(cli): show ellipsis on truncated lines via fitLine

fitLine used truncateToWidth with '' ellipsis, producing hard cuts
with no visual signal that content was truncated. Switch to '…' so
every truncated line (status line, tool headers, summaries, notices)
shows the standard ellipsis, matching the convention already used by
renderPtyTerminalRows.

* fix(cli): unify tool duration display to milliseconds

Bash shell_run cards showed integer seconds (Math.floor(ms/1000)s)
while every other tool showed raw milliseconds. A 900ms Bash round-trip
displayed as 0s, losing all precision. Unify to milliseconds across all
tool types for consistent scanning.

* fix(cli): place tool status before input summary in card headers

The status (running/done/error/aborted) was at the end of the compact
tool card header, so it was the first thing truncated at narrow widths.
A long command input could push the status off the line entirely,
leaving the user unable to tell if a tool was still running. Move
status right after the tool name, before the dim input summary, so it
survives truncation.

* fix(cli): align file_write compact and expanded wording

Compact cards said 'wrote 42 bytes out.txt' (lowercase, no 'to') while
expanded cards said 'Wrote 42 bytes to out.txt' (capitalized, with 'to').
Toggling Ctrl+O changed the wording, making the same result look like a
rewrite. Align both to 'Wrote N bytes to path'.

* fix(cli): shorten rewind picker title, move explanation to hint

The rewind picker title 'Rewind — 回到选定轮次之前（丢弃该轮及之后，
prompt 回填输入框）' was ~40 display columns. On an 80-column terminal
it barely fit; on narrower terminals it was hard-truncated mid-sentence.
Shorten the title to 'Rewind' and pass the CJK explanation as the
picker hint line, which renders dim below the title and truncates more
gracefully. Also add hint support to showSelectPicker.

* fix(cli): bold permission prompt key tokens for scannability

The permission action line was entirely dim — the user had to re…

Mirrors the DeepInfra template: models.dev facts + registry entry, per-model
thinkingOptions, the reasoning_effort wire (off maps to none), headless
credential env, a byte-exact Lobe Icons color mark, and tests across every

model discovery uses the fallback-models filter; the base URL already

subject: test(e2e): replace per-provider add-flow tests with representative journeys and registry contract tests (#991)
body: * test(core): add provider catalog registry invariants Data-driven structural invariants over CATALOG_PROVIDER_TYPES (label, catalog group, endpoint source, default-model shape) that auto-cover new providers with zero manual updates, replacing the per-provider add-flow E2E clones as the source of provider-fact coverage. Refs #989 * test(desktop): consolidate per-provider add-flow E2E into representative journeys Collapse the per-provider add-flow clones in providers.spec.ts (29 expanded cases) down to four: one canonical API-key journey, the account-scoped Cloudflare form, the no-auth local runtime, and the keyboard-focus journey. Both brand-mark render contracts (color and currentColor mask) stay covered. Per-provider facts now live in the core registry contract; add a desktop brand-mark completeness invariant so every catalog provider still resolves to a real mark, not the generic fallback. Refs #989 * test(core): drop redundant default-model assertions The per-id loop already asserts every shipped id is non-empty, and fallbackModels[0] trivially belongs to fallbackModels, so the block asserted nothing. Refs #989 * fix(settings): lowercase provider type before deriving the default slug nextSlug swept uppercase letters into '-' without lowercasing first, so the mixed-case provider types 'MiniMax' / 'MiniMax-cn' derived '-ini-ax' / '-ini-ax-cn' — slugs validateSlug rejects — leaving the add form unable to save those providers with its pre-filled slug. Prerequisite for the new default-slug derivation contract (PR #991 review finding 4): the data-driven assertion that every catalog provider's derived slug passes validateSlug goes red without this fix. nextSlug's only call site is the add form's create-time initializer, so existing stored connections are untouched and no migration is needed. Refs #989 * test(core): align provider catalog contract with production gates Address PR #991 review findings 1-3: - Judge concrete baseUrl and resolved baseUrlTemplate values with validateConnectionBas…

subject: test(desktop): stop flaky gateway SSE stream-count race by retaining response refs (#994)
body:
The "rejects excess SSE streams" test opened 10 held-open SSE fetches but only
kept each AbortController, dropping the Response objects. undici aborts a fetch
connection when its unread response body is garbage-collected, so under load
(heavier GC) the client tore down already-registered streams before the
activeEventStreams assertion ran, intermittently reading 2-7 instead of 10.

Retain the whole opened stream (controller + response) through the assertions so
the bodies stay reachable and the connections stay open. Product code is correct:
the gateway registered all 10 streams and only removed them on client-initiated
req 'close'.

Refs #993

subject: feat: expose thinking level for all Ollama Cloud reasoning models (#995)
body:
* feat: expose thinking level for all Ollama Cloud reasoning models

Ollama Cloud's OpenAI-compatible endpoint globally accepts reasoning_effort
(none/low/medium/high/max), but the thinking-level switcher only appeared for
qwen3.5:397b because models.dev does not carry reasoning_options for this
provider. Derive thinkingOptions for every reasoning-capable ollama-cloud model
from the generated metadata snapshot, with GPT-OSS declared separately because
it only accepts low/medium/high and cannot be fully disabled.

Also adds max to qwen3.5:397b, which was documented but missing.

* fix: exclude deprecated Ollama Cloud models from thinking options

Ollama publishes concrete retirement dates for deprecated models — all
current deprecated entries were retired on 2026-06-16 and are already
gone from the API. Filter them out of the derived thinkingOptions so the
switcher does not surface a knob for a model the provider will reject.
This matches the existing provider-registry filter for fallback models.

* refactor: unify ollama-cloud thinking options into single derivation

Fold GPT-OSS thinking options into the same derivation map instead of
excluding the prefix and then manually restoring two exact IDs. Future
GPT-OSS reasoning models added to the snapshot are now handled
automatically — the family-level effort difference is a single branch
in the map, not a separate hardcoded entry per id.

subject: feat(runtime): mid-turn capacity compaction with a single final-payload verdict owner (#996)
body:
* feat(runtime): extend history compact checkpoint protocol to mid_turn phase

Add a phase (pre_turn|mid_turn) and head-anchor reference to the V2
HistoryCompactCheckpoint so a checkpoint can fold a contiguous prefix that
reaches into the current turn's completed steps while re-rendering the
covered head anchor (the current turn's user message) verbatim on replay.
Coverage stays a contiguous event prefix so the digest math is unchanged;
pre_turn checkpoint ids stay byte-stable. projectHistoryCompactCheckpointReplay
centralises the deterministic [block, head anchor, tail] projection.

* feat(runtime): add pure mid-turn capacity measurement and safe-boundary engine

Turn-agnostic, side-effect-free helpers for the active-turn context invariant:
estimateNextRequestTokens anchors on the last step's real provider usage plus a
char/4 tail delta (whole-projection char/4 on cold start); exceedsHighWater and
exceedsContextWindow gate the two failure tiers; selectMidTurnSafeBoundary picks
the largest covered prefix that ends on an immutable non-partial event and never
straddles a tool call/result pair, reporting no_safe_completed_span otherwise.

* feat(runtime): add mid-turn history compact policy surface (default off)

HistoryCompactPolicy.midTurn carries enabled + reserveTokens + reserveTailEvents.
MAKA_CONTEXT_HISTORY_COMPACT_MID_TURN opts in (default off, PR 3 sinks it on),
reusing the shared MAKA_CONTEXT_HISTORY_COMPACT_RESERVE_TOKENS (16384) high-water
reserve. A standalone revert leaves every surface's behavior unchanged.

* feat(core): add context_budget_exhausted complete outcome

A first-class CompleteEvent.stopReason for when the runtime cannot produce a
provider-safe request even after mid-turn compaction, with a detail field
(no_safe_completed_span | summarizer_failed | head_anchor_exceeds_capacity).
failureClassFromCompleteStopReason maps it to a distinct failure class so the
turn is recorded as an explicit budget outcome rather than a provider error.

* feat(runtime): add mid-turn capacity compaction orchest…

subject: fix(ui): restore quiet composer picker triggers (#999)
fix(ui): keep in-flight live turn armed when persisted history covers all steps (#1000)
body:
Symptom: the desktop composer's "in progress" indicator flickers off during
a running turn. At every step-to-step lull, when all tool/thinking evidence is
already covered by the persisted transcript, the busy state drops to idle until
the next event recreates the projection.

Cause: reconcileTerminalLiveTurn deleted the whole live-turn projection
(returning undefined) whenever the filtered steps array became empty, even for
a NON-terminal projection. app-shell calls it on every messages/activeLiveTurn
change mid-turn, so the projection vanished and turnInFlight (projection exists
&& !terminal) went false.

Fix: an empty result only deletes the projection when current.terminal, mirroring
the existing precedent in settleLiveTurnStep. A non-terminal projection survives
as { ...current, steps: [] } with its arm preserved.

subject: refactor(providers): rename codex-subscription to openai-codex with live model discovery (#1005)
body:
* refactor(providers): rename codex-subscription to openai-codex with alias migration

Rename the codex-subscription provider to openai-codex to point at the
endpoint it actually drives (chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex) and match the
provider id used by hermes and pi. The rename is non-destructive:

- PROVIDER_TYPE_ALIASES + normalizeProviderType() map the legacy persisted
  providerType to the new id, so connections stored before the rename keep
  working without an on-disk migration. The normalization is wired into
  migrateConnectionV1ToV2 and the headless harbor read path
  (applyConnectionDefaults) so legacy codex-subscription connections resolve
  PROVIDER_DEFAULTS correctly.
- The persisted connection slug (codex-subscription) and credential-store
  key are intentionally left untouched so existing OAuth tokens remain
  reachable; only the in-memory providerType is normalized.

Split isSubscriptionExperimentalEnabled out of claude-subscription-service.ts
into claude-subscription-helpers.ts (mirroring the existing
openai-codex-helpers.ts split) so oauth-model-connections-main.ts no longer
pulls Electron ESM at module load, which unblocks behavior-testing the OAuth
model sync path.

The Codex OAuth model sync (syncOpenAiCodexConnection) is renamed in place
but keeps its existing fallback-only behavior; live /models discovery lands
in a later commit.

* feat(providers): add gpt-5.6-sol to OpenAI Codex OAuth with accurate context windows

Add gpt-5.6-sol as the lead fallback model for the openai-codex (ChatGPT/
Codex OAuth) provider, and align the OAuth model metadata's context windows
with the OpenAI codex CLI models.json - the authoritative source for the
chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex path.

Slug: the ChatGPT/Codex OAuth backend serves the 5.6 generation as
gpt-5.6-sol/-terra/-luna, never as a bare gpt-5.6 (per codex CLI models.json
and hermes DEFAULT_CODEX_MODELS). gpt-5.5-pro is dropped: it is a
platform-API slug the OAuth backend does not serve.

subject: feat(runtime): reactive context-overflow compact-and-retry recovery (#1017)
body:
* feat(runtime): classify provider context-length overflow errors

Add an exclusion-first ContextLength bucket to classifyError, matching the
raw provider error message against a ported overflow-pattern table (the
providers Maka ships) while excluding throttling/quota wording that merely
mentions tokens. This is the reactive-recovery trigger for issue #882 PR 2;
the status-based classes still win, so an explicit 429/5xx never lands here.

* feat(runtime): reactive context-overflow compact-and-retry recovery

Second line of defense for issue #882 (PR 2). A request-level provider
failure surfaces as a fullStream error chunk — both when the transport
throws (finishReason then rejects with NoOutputGeneratedError) and when it
streams an error part. The old pump caught the rejected finishReason as
`stop` and emitted a fabricated end_turn completion with success telemetry.

The pump now captures that error chunk and, at most once per send, folds the
durable turn ledger and resends on a context-length overflow — a pi-style
single compact-and-retry latch reusing the PR 1 mid-turn compaction machinery
(planMidTurnCapacityCompaction, the checkpoint protocol, the anchor-tail
decoration, and the replay-admissibility gate), tagged with reason 'overflow'.
When recovery is impossible or spent — a non-context-length error, no mid-turn
seam, no safe completed span, or a second overflow — the real provider error
becomes the terminal outcome, never a fabricated success and never a
synthesized context_budget_exhausted (the provider, not the runtime, rejected).

The compaction core is extracted into computeMidTurnCompactionReplacement,
shared by the proactive prepareStep hook and the reactive path so there is one
fold implementation, one acceptance standard, and one persist order.
errorReasonFromClass maps ContextLength to a context_overflow error reason.

* fix(runtime): classify overflow on original-error fields and tighten fallback patterns

Review findings P2-1/P2-2: match the overflow signatures against the
composite orig…

subject: feat: runtime-owned context compaction defaults across surfaces (#1018)
body:
* feat(runtime): default mid-turn capacity compaction on, runtime-owned

The mid_turn capacity invariant becomes a runtime-owned default: whenever
history compaction is enabled the runtime derives midTurn from the selected
model window (contextWindow - reserve), so Desktop, TUI, and headless inherit
it without copying config. MAKA_CONTEXT_HISTORY_COMPACT_MID_TURN=off stays as
the explicit escape hatch.

The backend still gates activation on the checkpoint seams, the persisted head
anchor, and a KNOWN context window, so a session without model metadata or a
child session with no anchor seam never misfires even though the default is on
(covered by new backend safety guards).

Refs #882

* feat: default semantic compaction off, runtime-owned across surfaces

Semantic compaction is the #981/#986 attention-first experiment, not the
standard historyCompact mechanism. Make the runtime the owner of its default:
it stays off unless a surface explicitly opts in (MAKA_CONTEXT_SEMANTIC_COMPACT
truthy, or a non-off MAKA_CONTEXT_SEMANTIC_COMPACT_MODE).

This lets the CLI drop its local semanticCompact strip and eat the runtime
default directly, so the surfaces stop duplicating the same off decision.
Desktop and headless inherit the off default without any per-surface wiring.

Refs #882

* test(runtime): lock the shipped default's proactive long-turn journey

Wire buildDefaultContextBudgetPolicy — the exact default every surface now
inherits — into the streaming backend and prove a long turn near the window
engages mid-turn compaction and continues to a clean end rather than being
truncated or surfacing a raw provider error.

The fake-backend Playwright suite cannot exercise this path: it is a separate
canned-event backend that never runs AiSdkBackend's prepareStep machinery, so a
desktop E2E could only fake the very compaction under test. This deterministic
runtime-layer test is the honest representative journey for the proactive path.

Refs #882

* fix(runtime): bound the derived compaction reserve by the model window

* chore: introduce Biome linter with CI gate (#415)

Installs @biomejs/biome 2.5.4 (exact) and adds biome.jsonc: formatter
(2-space, 100-col, single quotes, semicolons, trailing commas), linter
starting from the recommended preset, VCS-aware file discovery, and
Tailwind CSS 4 directive parsing for apps/desktop's renderer stylesheet.
Adds `npm run lint` (biome lint .) and `npm run format` (biome format
--write .) scripts, and wires `npm run lint` into the CI typecheck job
right after `npm ci` for a fast-fail gate.

Import-sorting/organize-imports assist actions are left disabled
(assist.enabled: false) — that lands in a follow-up auto-fix PR per #415.

64 recommended lint rules had pre-existing violations on this tree and
are disabled (each with its violation count as a comment) so `npm run
lint` passes without touching any source file. Top offenders:
noNonNullAssertion (1274), useTemplate (137), useExhaustiveDependencies
(110), noAdjacentSpacesInRegex (84), noUnusedImports (74),
noUnsafeOptionalChaining (74), noControlCharactersInRegex (67),
noExplicitAny (60), noLabelWithoutControl (51).

`npx biome format .` (check-only) reports 1299 of 1555 scanned files
would be reformatted — deferred to the follow-up auto-fix PR, not
applied here.

The local npm config points at a npmmirror.com mirror, which leaked 9
resolved URLs for @biomejs/* packages into package-lock.json; repin

subject: fix(ui): restore circular composer icon buttons via governed Button pill shape (#1028)
body:
#901 retired the composer consumer-layer button CSS
(maka-composer-tool-button / maka-composer-context-plus /
maka-composer-send-button) onto the shared Button, but the shared
primitive had no governed replacement for the circle — its only radius
is the control-tier rounded-sm — so the "+" affordance (hover included)
and the send button silently went square.

Add a `shape` axis to buttonVariants (default / pill) so the pill tier
lives on the governed primitive, opt the composer "+" and send buttons
into shape="pill", and extend the radius-converge contract with
`alsoTiers` so a component can legitimately serve more than one radius
tier (buttonVariants: control + pill). The utility-primitives contract
now asserts the composer buttons carry shape="pill", and button.test.ts
locks that tailwind-merge resolves pill over the base rounded-sm.

badge, so the two never read as the same kind of control.

Both READMEs now open with one badge row under the title: CI first, then
the language switch, in shields' default flat style to match the CI badge.
Blue keeps it from reading as a second pass/fail signal next to CI green.

* fix(ui): keep errored tools collapsed instead of force-expanding

An errored tool no longer counts as an attention state: the trow group
and the boxed card stay collapsed when a tool fails, and an earlier
manual collapse is no longer overridden by the error transition. The
failure signal stays on the summary line ("N 个失败" in destructive
color, red kind icon), and the error banner + raw diagnostics remain
one click away behind the disclosure.

Permission prompts keep the force-open behavior — they are actionable
and a collapsed summary would hide them.

ToolActivity gains a controlled `open` prop (the ToolErrorDetails
pattern) so tests can render the expanded body in static markup; the
#741 error-collapse contract now pins the collapsed default and the
expanded body separately.

* fix(ui): keep the failure signal textual in live groups and errored rows

Codex review on the collapsed-error change found two spots where the
failure signal degraded to a destructive tint once errors stopped
force-opening:

- summarizeTrowTools suppressed the "N 个失败" clause for live groups.
  The suppression was justified by the force-open fallback that no
  longer exists, and the failed count is monotonic mid-group, so the
  clause now rides the live summary too (the live option only adds the
  正在 prefix).
- An errored ToolTrowRow inside an expanded group showed only a red
  intent. The row label now carries a visible "· 失败" word.

* test(ui): restore errored-branch coverage via the ToolActivity open prop

The collapsed-error change had switched five body-rendering tests to
waiting_permission as the static-markup mounting seam, which silently
moved them off the errored branches they pin (error banner, raw-details
disclosure, pty_control banner suppression). With the open prop added
in this PR, the tests can keep status: 'errored' and mount the panel
explicitly — byte-identical to the old auto-open renders.

The automation preview contract is status-agnostic (it exercises the
result parser), so it now uses the…

body:
The three provider-editor borders referenced var(--border-subtle), a token
defined nowhere in the repo. With no fallback, the undefined custom property
made the border shorthand invalid at computed-value time, so border-style fell
back to none and the dividers/outline rendered with no border. Point them at
--border, matching the hairline-divider convention used elsewhere in the file.

Closes #1040

subject: test(headless): deflake harbor-cell settlement-deadline continuation test (#1049)
body:
The 'does not start a continuation turn after the settlement deadline
latches' test used settleAfterMs: 25, letting the deadline fire during
run.begin() before the backend's send was dispatched. That lands on the
runtime-runner 'abort signal already set before dispatch' early return,
which skips backend.send entirely, so the prompt is never recorded while
the run is still cancelled by the deadline. The assertion then reads [].

Raise settleAfterMs to 1_000, matching every other reliable deadline test
in the file, so the first send always starts before the deadline latches.

Fixes #1048

subject: fix(ui): remove the dark composer edge halo (#1050)
body:
* feat(ui): lift the docked composer with a theme-correct shadow

The composer read as a flat band welded to the bottom of the conversation.
Layout stays with the layout engine — the .mainColumn grid keeps the
composer in its own bottom row, so the message stream can never be
occluded. Only the paint layer changes, and it splits by theme the way the
design system's shadow recipe prescribes:

- Light: the card's own ring plus a restrained, foreground-derived drop so
  it reads as lifted off the floor.
- Dark: elevation collapses to the ring. A blur drop on a dark shell reads
  as a dirty halo (pure black) or a glow (foreground-derived), so dark
  carries elevation as a border ring, not a shadow.

Every layer derives from --foreground (no pure black), per
PR-BOX-SHADOW-CONVERGE-0. The empty new-task surface keeps the flat
resting card.

* fix(cli): map failed tool status to danger disc and preserve duration on overflow

Address Codex + Claude Opus review findings on #1056:

- toolDisc now maps `failed` (background shell_run failed/timed_out/
  orphaned) to the danger disc alongside error/aborted, instead of the
  muted done disc. The disc is the sole status carrier on the collapsed
  row, so a failed run was previously indistinguishable from success.
- renderCompactToolBlock now allocates width so the disc, name, duration,
  and `›` are always preserved; only the input and summary text truncate
  on overflow. A long command previously hid elapsed time and the expand
  marker - a regression from the single-line reorder, which moved duration
  from before the input to after it.

Adds regression tests for the failed danger disc and for duration/`›`
surviving a width overflow. 366 tests pass.

shows "Working… Ns" while an agent turn runs and a blank reserved row when

subject: fix: align session health notice with send/rebind authority (#1062)
body:
* fix: align session health notice with send/rebind authority

The composer health notice decided from a renderer-side proxy
(default exists && enabled) plus lastTestStatus, which disagreed with
the actual send gate in both directions (#1038):

- locked sessions (has history) hid the notice behind a ready-looking
  default even though the send cannot rebind and will fail;
- an enabled-but-secretless default hid it even though rebind fails;
- a deleted connection showed a destructive notice even when another
  persisted connection could serve the send;
- lastTestStatus=error rendered as a destructive hard block even
  though it never gates send (E4).

The send/rebind decision now lives in a single pure core projection
(projectSessionSendOutcome) shared by both sides: the desktop send
gate resolves the async facts, delegates the decision, and keeps only
the side effects (rebind mutation, canonical error copy); the notice
maps the same outcome to actionable copy. SessionSummary exposes
connectionLocked so the renderer works from the same facts, and a
connections:hasSecret probe replaces the exists-and-enabled proxy.
lastTestStatus stays as an intentional pre-send reminder: warning
tone only, copy that never claims send is blocked, and silent when
the projection rebinds away from the connection.

* fix: keep send-gate fact resolution staged and rebind order deterministic

Review on PR #1062 found that ensureSessionCanSendOrRebind gathered
facts with a single Promise.all over every candidate connection:

- an unrelated connection's getApiKey failure rejected an otherwise
  healthy send;
- every send triggered OAuth near-expiry network refreshes for all
  connections just to compute readiness;
- a getDefaultSlug/listConnectionSlugs failure could drop the session's
  own connection from the candidate set, misjudge it as
  connection_missing, and silently rebind a healthy session;
- parallel push made the rebind winner depend on async completion order
  instead of the persisted connection order.

The gate now resolves fa…

subject: fix(cli): statusline density, color fallback, and activity-strip spacing (#1066)
body:
* fix(cli): statusline density, color fallback, and activity-strip spacing (#1064)

Seven chrome fixes for the TUI after the #1053 visual grammar epic:

1. NO_COLOR / color capability fallback — tui-ansi.ts reads NO_COLOR /
   COLORTERM / TERM and downgrades 24-bit truecolor to 256/16/no-color.
   Benchmark: codex supports-color 3-level, pi theme.ts 256 fallback.

2. ctx fallback — when contextRemaining is absent, use the last
   token_usage input as an estimate of used tokens (#1053 spec fallback
   that was never implemented).

3. ctx color warning — >80% yellow, >95% red, dim otherwise.

4. cwd shortened to ~-relative path instead of full path.

5. thinking:default omitted — only an explicitly set thinkingLevel shows.

6. Activity strip blank separator — Working… no longer touches the last
   agent-work (thinking/tool) transcript line.

7. AskUserQuestion CJK IME cursor fix — UserQuestionTextOverlay sets
   editor.focused = true so IME candidate windows position correctly.
   Reference: ~/.pi/agent/extensions/question.ts.

* fix(cli): address Codex review findings for #1064

- P1: Remove ctx lastInput fallback — token_usage.input is a billing-
  cumulative sum across tool-loop steps, not the last request's context
  size. Using it as a proxy for 'used context' produces misleading
  percentages (potentially >100%). Revert to omitting ctx segment when
  contextRemaining is absent.
- P2: Fix NO_COLOR empty-string handling — per the NO_COLOR spec, only
  a non-empty value disables color. NO_COLOR= (empty) no longer strips
  color.
- P2: Add direct unit tests for detectColorLevel — the env detection
  function (NO_COLOR / TERM / COLORTERM combinations) was previously
  only tested indirectly through render output.

Findings verified from Codex xhigh review (timed out but intermediate
analysis confirmed the input semantics issue against
ai-sdk-backend.ts mergeNormalizedUsage).

body:
streamText's default onError is console.error(error), so a provider
failure (e.g. network disconnect after retries) painted the raw error
object — full util.inspect dump with stack, nested retry errors, and
request bodies — straight onto the terminal, bypassing the TUI
transcript.

Stream failures already reach the user through the fullStream error
chunk -> ErrorEvent -> generalizedErrorMessage path, which renders a
short classified notice. Pass an explicit no-op onError so the SDK
default no longer leaks.

subject: fix(runtime): emit contextRemaining in token_usage events (#1072)
body:
* fix(runtime): emit contextRemaining in token_usage events

Capture the last completed step's inputTokens from the finish-step handler
and compute contextRemaining = max(0, contextWindow - lastStepInputTokens) when
constructing the TokenUsageEvent. The TUI statusline ctx segment already
gates on contextRemaining; this fills the pipeline from the source.

totalUsage.inputTokens is cumulative across tool-loop steps and would produce
misleading >100% percentages, so the per-step value is captured instead.

Closes #1067.

* fix: fail closed on lastStepInputTokens and precompute contextRemaining

Review feedback (P2+P3):

P2 — lastStepInputTokens was only set inside 'if (stepUsage)', so a
missing final step's usage would leave a stale value from an earlier
step. Fix: assign lastStepInputTokens = stepUsage?.inputTokens on every
step boundary (fail closed — undefined when stepUsage is missing).

P3 — Replace the IIFE in the object literal with a precomputed
contextRemainingForUsage variable, matching the repo's conditional-spread
convention.

Closes #1067.

subject: fix: restore green CI on main (runtime prune regression + non-hermetic CLI color tests) (#1077)
body:
* fix(runtime): let active prune cover the newest step under mid-turn capacity

#986 unconditionally excluded the newest completed step from active
tool-result pruning, breaking three reviewed capacity-rescue invariants
(mid-turn finding C, capacity-replacement re-convergence, overflow
round-3 P1) and CI on main. Include the newest step exactly when
mid-turn capacity compaction is active, keeping the keep-newest-raw
default for every other configuration.

* test(cli): pin color level so ANSI assertions are hermetic

tui-ansi detects color capability from TERM/COLORTERM at module load, so
the pi-transcript and pi-tui-runner ANSI assertions inherited ambient
terminal capability: truecolor locally, level 0 on CI runners with no
TERM/COLORTERM, where every color function is a no-op and the #1064/#1066
color assertions fail deterministically. Pin level 3 via the existing
_setColorLevelForTesting seam, matching tui-ansi.test.ts.

subject: feat(cli): status-colored tool rows with parenthesized outcome annotations (#1082)
body:
Rework the compact tool row to `● Tool  target (annotation)`:

- The disc now carries the status color: green for done, blue for
  running, red for failed/aborted, grey for detached/unavailable
  (done was indistinguishable grey before).
- No `·` separators, no `›` expand marker, no `0s` durations, and
  no output content on the row. The parenthesized annotation reports
  fixed-shape outcomes — counts (5 matches, 3 lines), sizes (42
  bytes), diff tally (+12 -3), red (exit 1), (running 12s) — whose
  shapes already say whether there is anything to expand.
- Bash rows show the first real command line instead of a leading
  '#' comment; Read drops the byte count; Write/Edit no longer repeat
  the path already shown as the target.
- Short annotations are reserved whole when a row overflows, so a
  long command can never hide an exit code.

body:
* feat(cli): fold background-task polls at tool_start and announce settles

A Read / StopBackgroundTask aimed at a ref a visible Bash card owns is
internal polling: it no longer renders a transient row between tool_start
and tool_result, so an active polling loop stops flickering cards in and
out of the transcript. An errored poll still surfaces as a normal card.

A live running -> settled transition now appends one transcript notice
(completed / stopped as info, failed / timed_out / orphaned as error),
fired exactly once whether the settle arrives through the event-driven
ShellRunUpdate channel or a folded poll. Stored replay stays silent.

Refs #1096

* fix(cli): keep settle notices live-only and honor errored polls

Review on #1110 (Codex + Claude, both FAIL) surfaced two contract
violations:

- P1 (both): hydration catch-up replayed durable settle state through the
  live-update entry point, so every session attach re-announced old
  settles — after a CLI restart, orphaned tasks spammed a red Error on
  every open. The runner now marks listShellRunUpdates catch-up as
  announceSettle: false; only the runner knows update provenance. Live
  subscription updates (including those buffered during hydration) still
  announce exactly once.
- P2 (Codex): the poll fold never read tool_result.isError, the call-level
  authoritative status — an errored poll carrying a well-formed shell_run
  payload folded silently, and materialization overwrote the error state.
  Folding now requires !isError on both fold paths, and errored results
  keep status 'error'.

Also from review: clear pendingShellRunPolls on abort/error (P3), and
render timed_out as 'timed out' in the notice text (P3).

Declined: attention pull on failed settles (aligned out of scope in
shaping); resume replaying an aborted poll as an Interrupted card
(pre-existing materialize behavior for all result-less tool calls).

Refs #1096

* fix(cli): keep errored background-task polls from folding on stored replay

A stored Read/StopBackgroundTask poll whose res…

subject: feat: mid-turn steering and queued follow-up messages in the TUI (#1087)
body:
* feat(runtime): steering and followup message queues with step-boundary injection

Add two authoritative per-session queues in the runtime kernel so a user can
act on a turn while it runs:

- steer(sessionId, text): queue a message injected into the running turn at the
  next step boundary. The backend leases it via BackendSendInput.pullSteering,
  echoes it as a first-class `steering_message` (persisted as a user event,
  rendered in place), and injects it into the model context wrapped in a
  steering envelope so it continues the same turn. With no live steering owner,
  steer returns a `fallback` outcome so the caller opens a fresh turn and the
  message is never lost.
- queueMessage(sessionId, text): queue a followup that opens the next turn;
  drainFollowup joins them with blank lines at turn end.
- retractQueue / interrupt clear both queues; queue changes emit a `queue_update`
  snapshot into the active turn's stream, including the final stranded-steering →
  followup migration at ownership release (emitted before the stream closes).

Delivery is a lease state machine whose single atomic commit point is the
pull:

- pullSteering moves messages to an in-flight set; retract/clear reclaim
  QUEUED messages only. A pulled lease is committed to the running turn and
  settles exactly once, decided solely by the persistence fact: the backend
  acks after the echoed event is durably consumed AND in the injection set,
  and nacks only when the event provably never persisted (never pushed, or
  the consumer detached first) — the dying request never carries it. An
  ambiguous append error is settled by reading the ledger back: present ⇒
  durable (ack path); a store that fails both the append and the read-back
  leaves the message on the queue side (nack). An
  abort does not settle a pushed lease: the wait rides the teardown's
  convergence, so a durably appended event is never nacked into a second
  delivery — the ledger owns it and the next turn replays it. Snapshots keep
  counting in-flight messages as p…
