* refactor: make workspace test runner the single source of truth

- scripts/run-workspace-tests-parallel.mjs now reads workspace list from root package.json and supports --serial

- root test: clean + build + test:scripts + parallel runner (headless runs serially after parallel batch to avoid cross-workspace races)

- root test:dist: test:scripts + serial runner, removing duplicated workspace list

- root test:fast: build + test:scripts + parallel runner for local fast path

- Measured: npm run test ~44s (down from ~75s), test:fast ~36s, test:dist unchanged ~51s

* fix: clean all workspaces and build:test for local test paths

Root npm test now depends on a complete clean + dist-only build. Derive
clean-build targets from package.json workspaces so packages/computer-use
cannot be left out, and use build:test so local test no longer pays for
the vite renderer bundle that test:dist never consumes.

* refactor: own dist tests in packages and harden workspace runner

Each workspace now declares test:dist; the root runner only schedules
npm run test:dist. Add spawn error handling, multi-failure aggregation,
and a contract test for parallel/serial partitioning.

body:
* chore: upgrade Electron from EOL 39 to 43.1.1

Electron 39 reached end-of-life on 2026-05-05. Bump the desktop app to the current stable major so Chromium security fixes continue to land.

tooling fidelity matches the desktop runtime. Bumps setup-node in the

subject: refactor(ui): govern segmented chrome as maka-segmented with hover/focus states (#1128)
body:
* refactor(ui): govern segmented chrome as maka-segmented with hover/focus states

The segmented control (sidebar 按状态/按项目 toggle, daily-review range
tabs, usage/appearance settings) had governed behavior (Base UI
ToggleGroup) but ungoverned chrome: the .settingsSegmented recipe lived
in the bot-settings subpage stylesheet, reached by cross-surface
consumers only through cascade accident, and offered no hover, focus,
or pressed feedback.

- Rename the primitive SettingsSegmented -> Segmented and its class to
  .maka-segmented; it stopped being settings-specific long ago.
- Re-home the recipe to styles/segmented.css and add hover, active,
  keyboard focus-visible ring, disabled, and quick transitions, all on
  existing tokens (--state-hover-bg, --focus-ring, --opacity-disabled).
- Lock the governance with a contract test: shared recipe has
  hover/focus/pressed states and must not move back into a settings
  subpage file.

* fix(desktop): move segmented control to the ui type tier

The 11px caption size was a leftover from the recipe's settings-page
origin. Per the #546 type scale, caption is the dense-meta tier
(timestamps, badges, kbd); interactive controls sit on the 13px
ui/chrome tier — which is also what every neighboring sidebar element
(session names, group labels) uses. Lock the tier in the segmented
contract test: the vocabulary contract only enforces token usage, not
which tier a control sits on.

* fix(desktop): wrap segmented options whole at narrow widths; harden contract

Codex review round on PR #1128, adjudicated:

- At <660px the usage page's five-option segmented broke labels inside
  buttons (39px two-line pills). Owner is the recipe: options now wrap
  as whole pills (flex-wrap on the group, nowrap on buttons), verified
  live at 620px — every option stays 22px and reachable.
- The chrome contract moved to its own segmented-contract.test.ts and
  now asserts against styles/segmented.css itself (location can't drift
  to another imported file) plus the states the previous asserti…

subject: feat(desktop): retire Electron safeStorage as OAuth credential authority (#1129)
body:
* feat(desktop): add store-authority OAuth token persistence with one-shot safeStorage import

The shared credential bridge becomes the real persistence layer for
subscription OAuth tokens (#1125): CredentialStore-backed save/load/
delete that fail closed, plus a one-shot import that decrypts legacy
safeStorage token files into the store and tombstones them. The
best-effort try* export helpers remain until services switch over.

* feat(desktop): make CredentialStore the OAuth token authority for Claude and Codex

Claude and Codex subscription services now persist tokens only in the
shared workspace CredentialStore: reads, writes, and logout go through
the shared bridge, force-refresh reuses the runtime's provider
refresher instead of a private duplicate, and the in-memory token
cache is gone so a pure-Node refresh can't be shadowed by a stale
rotated-out refresh token. Desktop startup imports pre-#1125
safeStorage token files once and tombstones them; logout also clears
any legacy file the import could not process. Contract tests now pin
the store-authority behavior instead of the safeStorage one.

* feat(desktop): move Cursor and Antigravity tokens to the shared CredentialStore

Both preview services gain the credentialStore dependency and persist
through the shared bridge like Claude/Codex; their legacy safeStorage
token files join the one-shot startup import, and logout clears the
authoritative store entry plus any leftover legacy file. Retires the
B-SWEEP-3 unlink-corrupt-file contract, superseded by the bridge's
corrupt-entry cleanup.

* docs: describe the single CredentialStore OAuth authority and pin it with a no-safeStorage contract

SECURITY.md, README, and README.zh-CN now describe one credential
model — credentials.json as the only token authority with safeStorage
reduced to the one-shot legacy import — and a contract test asserts no
module under oauth/ imports or invokes safeStorage and main.ts only
passes it to the legacy importers. Stale safeStorage comments in core
and onboarding updated to mat…

subject: fix(runtime): unblock session resume after a run ends with an error event (#1131)
body:
* fix(runtime): keep ledger replay when a run ended with an error event

A provider error or an app-restart recovery commits an error-content
RuntimeEvent to the ledger. The replay planner classified it as
unsupported_content — a blocking diagnostic — so every later turn of the
session degraded to the stored-message projection and, on
Anthropic-protocol providers, failed outright. Error content is a
failure fact, not model conversation: classify it as the non-blocking
error_content_diagnostic_only and keep provider-native replay.

* fix(runtime): skip empty assistant texts in the stored-message fallback

The degraded stored-message projection replayed every assistant row
verbatim. Thinking/tool-only steps project assistant rows with empty
text, and an empty text content block is a hard 400 on
Anthropic-protocol providers — a session on the degraded path could
never complete another turn.

* fix(runtime): drop dangling tool calls from ledger replay

A crash during tool execution persists the function_call but never its
function_response; recovery appends the terminal error event but cannot
invent the result. Replaying the dangling tool_use without a tool_result
is a provider 400. Drop the unmatched call from the replay plan with the
non-blocking unmatched_tool_call diagnostic — the mirror of the existing
unmatched_tool_result handling — so items-only consumers (materializer,
compact summarizer) stay provider-valid too.

Found by external review after the error-event reclassification exposed
this ledger shape to provider-native replay.

subject: refactor(desktop): converge settings sidebar tier and row primitives (#1132)
body:
* fix(desktop): align settings sidebar rows with the session-list lg tier

The settings nav rows sat on the 36px xl control tier while the
session-list rows sit on the 32px lg tier, so the two sidebars read as
different rhythms. Drop .settingsNavItem and .settingsBackButton to
--h-control-lg and move the 'settings nav row' example in the token
comments accordingly.

Also fixes three latent nav-item issues found in the same review:
- the always-semibold .settingsNavItem strong rule shadowed the active
  rule's weight change (dead declaration); weight now lives on the item
  (medium, semibold when active — same recipe as .maka-list-row-name)
- the 24px glyph track + 17px svg override contradicted the 16px icon
  the TSX renders and offset nav labels 8px from the back button label
- leftover grid-template-columns on a flex container

* refactor(desktop): consolidate settings row primitives into rows.css

The .settingsRow/.settingsRows primitives (settings-rows.tsx) had their
styles scattered across four files — base layout in bot.css, states and
hint in connection.css, label/value typography in provider-editor.css,
and width variants plus the whole .settingsFormRow/.settingsField/
.settingsFormGrid family in nav-sidebar.css. That let the two row kinds
drift: on the 通用 page, .settingsFormRow titles (隐身模式) rendered
15px while adjacent .settingsRow titles (默认模型) rendered 13px, with
different padding.

Move everything into styles/settings/rows.css as the single style home
for the row primitives, and converge the contract:
- row titles: one tier (--font-size-heading / medium) for both kinds
- row padding: space-5/space-6 for both kinds
- hints: one rule (--font-size-base, muted)
- field labels (.settingsFormGrid / .settingsField): --font-size-ui,
  one tier below row titles instead of sharing the heading tier

* test(desktop): move settings nav rows to the lg tier in the height contract

The control-height contract pinned .settingsNavItem/.settingsBackButton
to --h-control-xl; they now sit on --h-…

subject: fix(cli): anchor Ctrl+O/Ctrl+T expansion to the live viewport so toggles never clear scrollback (#1130)
body:
* fix(cli): anchor Ctrl+O/Ctrl+T expansion to the live viewport so toggles never clear scrollback

Expansion becomes per-entry state stamped from the expandAll defaults at
creation. The global toggles retarget only entries whose first rendered
line sits inside pi-tui's live viewport: entries above it live in terminal
scrollback, which cannot be rewritten, so resizing one forced pi-tui's
differential renderer into a full redraw that emitted ESC[3J, clearing
pre-Maka scrollback and resetting the scroll position.

renderMakaPiTranscript records each entry's first line and
MakaPiLayoutComponent records the viewport top (monotonic max, mirroring
pi-tui's previousViewportTop, which never scrolls back up short of a full
redraw), so overestimation only makes the toggles more conservative.

Fixes #1097

* fix(cli): shadow pi-tui's full-redraw rule so the viewport estimate re-anchors

The monotonic-max viewport estimate never fell after pi-tui re-anchored its
real viewport in a full redraw, so after /new, a session switch, a deep
rewind, or a terminal resize every entry of the shorter document sat
permanently "above the viewport" and Ctrl+O/Ctrl+T found nothing to toggle.

MakaPiLayoutComponent now mirrors pi-tui's own rule: reset the estimate to
the document tail exactly when pi-tui full-redraws (width or height change,
a changed line above the current top, or a shrink below it) and keep it
monotonic otherwise, so shallow truncations still hold the old top just
like pi-tui's deleted-lines path does.

Found by external review of #1130.

* fix(cli): harden the viewport shadow diff against re-review findings

Four gaps between the shadow diff and pi-tui's real doRender, all found by
a focused external re-review of 992be0170:

- After a wholesale transcript replacement, entry positions are unknown
  until the next render; a toggle in that window could rewrite lines above
  the still-scrolled real viewport. entryFirstLine is now undefined in that
  state and the toggles stay inert until a render records positions.
- Under Term…

subject: refactor(runtime): centralize filesystem-containment guards (#1136)
body:
* refactor(runtime): centralize filesystem-containment guards

The path-safety guards existed as private copies across the runtime skill
reader and the desktop main process. Move them verbatim into one shared
leaf module, `packages/runtime/src/path-containment.ts`, re-exported from
`@maka/runtime`, so a future fix to a containment check lands in one place.

- `isContainedPath` / `isSafeSkillId` were byte-identical in
  `runtime/skills.ts` and `managed-skill-sources.ts`; both now import the
  shared home (desktop main already depends on `@maka/runtime`).
- `explore-agent-tool.ts`'s `isInside` / `toRelative` are a distinct,
  separator-aware family (verified not byte-identical to `isContainedPath`);
  moved verbatim and imported, with the semantic divergence documented in
  the shared module so it stays a deliberate, behavior-changing decision.

Behavior-neutral: guards moved verbatim, no logic changed. Public
`@maka/runtime` exports stay stable so the later `skills.ts` split composes.

Refs #1084

* refactor: correct containment-guard doc and dedupe office-document-tool copy

Review follow-ups on the containment-guard dedup:

- Rewrite the path-containment.ts header: it overclaimed a complete,
  name-discriminated two-family taxonomy and single-home status. Runtime
  still holds local `isInside`-named checks with `isContainedPath`
  semantics (workspace-executor.ts, filesystem-worker/operations.ts,
  additional-permissions.ts) and a tested separator-aware `isPathInside`

  follow-up consolidation. Also fix `..foo` wording: it is a child entry

subject: refactor(desktop): split registerIpc into per-domain register*Ipc modules (#1138)
body:
* refactor(desktop): split registerIpc into per-domain register*Ipc modules

Migrate the un-migrated main.ts registerIpc() domains into dedicated
register*Ipc(deps) modules following the existing *-ipc-main.ts pattern,
so registerIpc becomes pure composition and each domain is testable in
isolation instead of only via module-side-effect source greps.

New modules: app-ipc-main (window + app info/openPath/project-select +
visualSmoke), git-ipc-main, workspace-search-ipc-main,
workspace-instructions-ipc-main, onboarding-ipc-main,
session-entry-ipc-main (quickChat + expertTeam), sessions-ipc-main,
permissions-ipc-main (permissions + capabilities + health),
settings-ipc-main (settings/bots/network), gateway-ipc-main. The shared
"current project root" selection moves into project-root-controller so a
single authority owns the state now that its readers and the picker
handlers live in separate modules.

Pure behavior- and visually-neutral move: handler bodies and shared
wiring (streamEvents, emitSessionsChanged, ensureSessionCanSend,
getReadyConnection, applySettingsRuntimeEffects, ...) are unchanged and
injected as deps. main.ts drops from 2447 to 1590 lines.

Co-migrate the source-reading contract tests: the wiring contract now
enumerates every extracted registrar, the combined-source helper lists
the new modules, and the direct main.ts readers switch to the combined
source. Allow-list the relocated visual-smoke capture log.

Refs #1084

* test(desktop): scope project-root contract to owning files and unit-test the controller

Review follow-up on the registerIpc split (#1084):

- project-context-badge contract: the earlier redirect to the combined
  main-process source let `selectedProjectRoot = projectPath` match the
  controller's internal implementation, so dropping the pickers'
  projectRoot.setSelected(...) calls would have passed silently. Scope
  every main-process assertion to the narrowest owning file
  (app-ipc-main, project-root-controller, sessions-ipc-main,
  workspace-instructions-ipc-main, main-window,…

subject: fix(cli): flip the expansion default and explain when Ctrl+O/Ctrl+T finds no block in view (#1140)
body:
* fix(cli): flip the expansion default and explain when Ctrl+O/Ctrl+T finds no block in view

A block expanded past the terminal pushes its own head into scrollback,
which the #1097 contract forbids rewriting, so the second Ctrl+O/Ctrl+T
found no target, returned false, and silently left both the block and
the expansion default stuck.

The toggles now always flip the default when any candidate entry exists
and positions are known; when every candidate sits above the viewport
they append an info notice saying the content stays readable in
scrollback and which state new output starts in. Toggles stay fully
inert while entry positions are unknown after a wholesale replacement,
and entries above the viewport stay byte-identical as before.

Closes #1134

* docs(cli): state why unscrolled unknown geometry keeps the toggles live

subject: fix(cli): freeze off-screen entry renders to prevent scrollback-clearing redraws (#1139)
body:
* fix(cli): freeze off-screen entry renders to prevent scrollback-clearing redraws (#1135)

Off-screen transcript entries live in terminal scrollback, which is
immutable. Five event paths mutated their rendered lines in place,
forcing pi-tui's differential renderer into a full redraw
(ESC[2J ESC[H ESC[3J) that clears pre-Maka scrollback and resets the
scroll position:

- refreshRunningShellRunElapsed: 1s ticker rewrote off-screen running
  cards every second
- shell-run settle/ownership: applied status/result changes to off-screen
  Bash cards
- setThinking: late thinking_complete replaced streamed text in place
- tool_result poll-card splice: removed an off-screen entry, shifting
  subsequent line numbers
- tool_progress/tool_output_delta: appended to expanded off-screen cards

The fix introduces a single render-layer freeze: when an entry's first
line sits above the live viewport (already recorded by #1130's
renderGeometry), renderTranscriptEntryMemoized serves the cached render
matching the current width instead of re-rendering. The underlying entry
state still updates — only the visual output is frozen, so session
switch, resume, and re-expand stay correct.

The splice path gets a separate entryInLiveViewport guard: splicing an
off-screen entry shifts line numbers above the viewport, which the freeze
alone cannot prevent. The stale entry stays in place until the next
session switch.

The ticker skips off-screen running cards entirely, so
refreshRunningShellRunElapsed returns false when no visible card needs an
update and the 1s interval stops.

* fix(cli): address review findings — freeze only fully off-screen entries

Codex review found three issues in the initial freeze approach:

P1: Freezing an entry whose first line is in scrollback but whose tail
is still visible stopped streaming text from appearing. The invariant
is that scrollback lines are immutable, not that the entire entry is
frozen. Now only entries sitting entirely above the viewport (firstLine
+ height <= viewportTop) are frozen; straddlin…

subject: feat(storage): add compare-and-set write to the credential store (#1141)
body:
Add an optional compareAndSetSecret capability to the file credential
store: a read-verify-basis-then-write specialization of the existing
read-modify-write, run under the same never-stolen withCredentialFileLock
so the basis check and the write are one serialized step.

The result distinguishes the two loser cases the OAuth refresh lifecycle
must tell apart: current === null means the entry is gone (a terminal
delete, e.g. a logout, that the caller must not resurrect) and a string
means a concurrent winner the caller adopts instead of overwriting.

Exposed as an optional member of the structural CredentialStore type so
third-party implementations and future credential_provider backends stay
source-compatible; callers fall back to an unconditional setSecret when
it is absent. No lease is held across any network I/O by design.

Step 1 of #1133. Refs #1133.

AttachmentImage previously cast window and called the desktop preload's
window.maka.attachments.readBytes directly, hard-coupling @maka/ui to the
Electron host. Thread an injected onReadAttachmentBytes reader from the

subject: refactor(desktop): extract useOptimisticSettingsDraft for settings pages (#1146)
body:
* refactor(desktop): extract useOptimisticSettingsDraft for settings pages

Four Settings pages had each hand-copied the same optimistic
last-write-wins draft block: a local draft mirrored on a ref, a
persistedRef, a pendingSaveCount, a monotonic saveTicket, a commitDraft
helper, and a prop->state sync effect. Extract that machinery into a
shared hook so no page reinvents the async-correctness contract.

- Add a pure createOptimisticDraftController (no React/DOM) that owns the
  ticket/pending/commit bookkeeping, plus the thin useOptimisticSettingsDraft
  React shell that wires it to state + useMountedRef.
- Route NetworkProxySection, OpenGatewaySettingsPage, UsageSettingsPage,
  and the personalization block through the hook; delete the per-page
  draft refs and sync effects.
- Unit-test the controller's last-write-wins ordering (a stale save must
  not clobber a newer draft), rollback, in-flight sync deferral, and
  dispose invalidation.
- Re-anchor the source-grounded Settings contract tests onto the shared
  hook; the pure async-correctness invariants they used to pin per page
  now live in the controller unit test.

Refs #1041

* refactor(desktop): keep personalization draft flow local

* fix(desktop): reconcile optimistic settings drafts safely

Track the highest confirmed save as the rollback baseline, apply deferred persisted snapshots when the final save settles, and invalidate every late path on dispose. Narrow the hook to the three whole-object Settings drafts and let it own batch saving state.\n\nRefs #1041

* fix(desktop): survive StrictMode draft replay

body:
* refactor(ui): extract composer draft + history hooks (#1044)

Pull the per-session draft store (draftStoreRef/activeDraftKeyRef/
hasDraftText + the draftKey swap effect) into useComposerDraft, and the
up/down prompt-history navigation state machine into useComposerHistory.
The pure store/navigation logic stays in composer-helpers.ts; the hooks
are the React seam that applies results to the uncontrolled textarea.

sendCurrent now clears the submitted key via clearDraft(submittedDraftKey)
+ saveCurrentDraft(''); the composer-send-guard contract is co-migrated to
pin the same invariant across the composer/hook seam.

* refactor(ui): extract composer mention hook + workspace row (#1044)

Move the @// mention-popup state machine (trigger detection, items,
active index, post-insertion suppression, selectionchange listener) into
useMentionPopup; Composer keeps only the keydown routing, driving the
hook through identically-named bindings so the mention contract reads

Move the workspace + branch picker row into ComposerWorkspaceRow with

Co-migrated contracts pin the same invariants across the seam:

subject: refactor(desktop): share settings action guards and connection-test helpers (#1156)
body:
* refactor(desktop): route settings one-shot action guards through shared hooks

Settings pages each hand-rolled the same synchronous xRef.current
re-entrancy guard (check, set before the first await, clear in finally,
reset from an unmount cleanup) instead of using the tested seam behind
useOAuthLoginFlow; provider-connection-detail.tsx alone carried six
parallel refs cross-checked in five functions, and the memory /
workspace-instructions controllers duplicated a keyed owner-token
variant of the same pattern.

- Add a framework-free createKeyedActionGuard (per-key acquire with
  owner-checked release, beginExclusive for mutually excluding sheet
  actions, reset for teardown) so the multi-latch cases are unit-testable
  without a DOM.
- Add useActionGuard (wrapping createOneShotActionGuard) and
  useKeyedActionGuard hooks that hold the guard in a ref and release it
  on unmount, replacing every per-page guard-reset cleanup effect.
- Migrate all hand-rolled guard sites: provider-connection-detail (six
  latches + the Copilot relogin notice), provider-add-form, web-search
  (four latches), general defaults card + network proxy, usage refresh,
  voice capture smoke, data actions, account connection test, daily
  review (two labeled owners), permission center (two sites), open
  gateway copy, about env copy, password-input copy, and both keyed
  controllers.
- Re-anchor the source-grounded contract tests onto the shared hooks;
  the keyed guard's semantics are pinned by new unit tests and the
  hooks' unmount-release by a new source contract.

Refs #1041

* refactor(desktop): share connection-test failure classification in settings

The Models connection sheet and Account page each hand-copied the same
statusCode/errorClass → Chinese troubleshooting table; only the auth and
final-fallback copy differed by surface.

- Export connectionTestFailureFallback/Message from provider-panel-shared
  with injectable ConnectionTestTroubleshootingCopy (auth + recheck).
- Route ConnectionDetail and AccountSettingsPage through t…

subject: refactor: extract tool-activity result projection and stabilize palette command list identity (#1152)
body:
* refactor(ui): move pure result projection out of tool-activity.tsx

Relocate the non-component logic (extractErrorText, resultOwnsOwnPanel,
resultHasCapturedStreams, withLiveStreamFallback, plus the status/result
predicates they interlock with) into tool-activity/result-projection.ts,
and summarizeErrorText into tool-activity/preview-utils.ts, so
tool-activity.tsx holds only components. Behavior-neutral; refs #1045.

* refactor(desktop): keep the command list identity stable while the palette is open

app-shell rebuilds commandOptions on every render, which rebuilt the
whole command list per render and busted every memo inside
CommandPalette. Build the list once per palette open/close transition in
AppShellOverlays, reading the latest options through a ref (the same
stable-ref pattern as openSessionInChatRef), and stabilize the
onOpenSearchModal wrapper plus useCommandPalette's open/close handles so
the palette's memo chain stops recomputing on unrelated renders.
Refs #1045.

* refactor(ui): drop unneeded export on resultHasCapturedStreams

Its only references are the definition and the internal call from
withLiveStreamFallback inside result-projection.ts. Refs #1045.

* fix(desktop): keep export/session rows live while the palette is open

Freeze-at-open captured messages/sessions at build time, so export/save
while the palette stayed open used a stale snapshot, and session catalog
rows stopped updating on background create/rename. Base commands still
freeze per open/close with run() reading the latest options ref; session
rows rebuild on visibleSessions + activeId only. Extract pure builders for
unit tests and co-migrate contracts. Refs #1045.

subject: feat: explicit skill invocation via /skill:<name> tokens in the TUI (#1150)
body:
* feat: explicit skill invocation via /skill:<name> tokens in the TUI

Users had no way to invoke a skill explicitly: the TUI advertised the
catalog and wired the Skill tool, but triggering one meant asking the
model in prose and hoping it called the tool.

Runtime gains the shared, client-agnostic contract (Refs #1148):
listInvocableSkills / resolveSkillInvocations /
composeSkillInvocationMessage, plus loadSkillInstructionsFromScan so
several invocations resolve against one scan. The TUI consumes it
end-to-end: /skill:<name> tokens anywhere in the input (multiple,
deduped), brand-accent highlight for invocable tokens (no highlight IS
the inactive state), dynamic autocomplete, a /skill picker that only
inserts the token, and deterministic CLI-side injection at submit —
the transcript shows what the user typed while the runtime receives
the composed message. Resolution never blocks a send: failed tokens
stay literal with at most a non-blocking notice.

Refs #1148

* fix: separate display and model text for skill invocation turns

Persist optional displayText on user messages so titles, rewind, previews,
and transcripts keep the typed /skill: tokens while the model still receives
the composed envelope. Hold editor.disableSubmit during async skill prep,
and tell the model not to re-call Skill for already-invoked blocks.

* fix: carry displayText through runtime projection and tighten skill submit

Persist human-facing displayText on initial user RuntimeEvents so resume,
rewind, and regenerate keep the typed prompt after the canonical read model
replaces JSONL. Preserve significant whitespace when stripping skill tokens,
skip turn start if the TUI closed mid-prep, and export Markdown via
userFacingText.

body:
* refactor(runtime): move isPathInside into path-containment

Relocate the separator-aware `isPathInside` guard and its `PathInsideApi`
injection point from `system-prompt/workspace-instructions.ts` into
`path-containment.ts`, the shared home #1136 established for filesystem-
containment guards. Pure move: the implementation, its cross-platform tests
(migrated to `path-containment.test.ts`), and the `@maka/runtime` public
re-export are unchanged in behavior and signature.

This makes `isPathInside` the single authority for the separator-aware family
so the bare-`startsWith('..')` variants can migrate to it next.

Refs #1145

* refactor(security): unify containment guards on isPathInside

Migrate every caller of the bare-`startsWith('..')` containment family to the
separator-aware `isPathInside` and drop the retired variants, so
`path-containment.ts` is the single authority for "inside or equal to root"
checks.

- runtime: `isContainedPath` (skill reader + managed sources) and the private
  `isInside` in `workspace-executor.ts` and `filesystem-worker/operations.ts`,
  plus `pathWithinRoot` in `additional-permissions.ts`, now call `isPathInside`.
- headless: `workspace-executor-adapter.ts`'s `isInsidePosix` is replaced by
  `isPathInside(root, target, <posix pathApi>)`, reusing the injectable
  `pathApi` for the POSIX sandbox paths.
- desktop main: `skills.ts`, `managed-skill-sources.ts`, `explore-agent-tool.ts`,
  and `office-document-tool.ts` switch from `isContainedPath`/`isInside` to
  `isPathInside`.
- `path-containment.ts` keeps only `isPathInside` (+ `PathInsideApi`),
  `isSafeSkillId`, and `toRelative`; the `@maka/runtime` public surface drops
  `isContainedPath` and `isInside`.

Behavior change: a child entry whose own name begins with `..` (e.g.
`root/..foo`) is now correctly treated as inside, where the bare-`startsWith`
variant rejected it as an escape. `..foo` is a legitimate directory name, not a
parent reference, and skill-id safety is enforced separately by `isSafeSkillId`.
The `..rules` case is alr…

subject: refactor(desktop): stabilize app-shell action factory identities (#1162)
body:
The 9 createAppShell*Actions factories ran unmemoized in the AppShell
render body, so every render allocated fresh handler identities. The
one observable harm: the streaming-settle fallback effect lists
settleAssistantStreaming in its deps and therefore tore down and
re-armed its 1s timer on every render while armed (#1043).

Introduce useStableActions: the factory still runs every render (so
closures always capture the latest deps, identical to calling it
bare), but consumers receive a once-created delegating facade whose
method identities never change. Publication to the facade happens in
useLayoutEffect, so only the latest COMMITTED render's actions are
reachable — an interrupted or discarded concurrent render cannot leak
its closures to event handlers, timers, or subscriptions (render-phase
ref writes are limited to the lazy initialization React permits). The
settle timer now arms once by construction, and any future effect-dep
or memoized-child consumer is safe by the same mechanism.

- createDelegatingActions (pure, React-free) pinned by node:test unit
  tests; source-contract tests pin the wiring at all 9 call sites and
  forbid the render-phase publication this design rejected
- remove the hand-rolled sessionRowActionHandlersRef mirror superseded
  by the facade; the fail-soft contract moves to the facade pattern
  with unchanged semantics (dedup registry, no fire-and-forget void)
- createAppShellStopAction stays unwrapped: it returns a bare function
  and only feeds JSX props, never effect deps

body:
Step 2 of the #1043 plan. Two self-contained state slices that lived
inline in AppShell move into their own hooks, following the existing
use-shell-connections / use-shell-chat-model seam:

- useShellExpertTeams: the Composer "+" menu catalog. A static
  load-once effect with no AppShell deps - pure relocation.
- useShellMemoryPill: the chat-header memory-visibility flag plus the
  fire-and-forget refreshMemoryActive. Takes toastApi (like
  useShellConnections), returns { memoryActive, refreshMemoryActive }.

AppShell now calls the hooks at the original state-declaration sites
(hook order preserved) and threads the results to the same consumers:
memoryActive -> chat-header pill; refreshMemoryActive ->
useAppShellBootstrapSubscriptions (mount recompute) and closeSettings
(Settings-close recompute). expertTeams -> Composer prop.

Pure code motion - no behavior change. refreshMemoryActive keeps its
exact source shape (2-space indent, same body) so the
renderer-startup-fail-soft contract still matches it after the move;
the two new hook files are registered in renderer-shell-source-helpers
so the combined-source contract keeps covering them. The stale
"refreshed when activeId changes" comment is replaced with the two
verified recompute triggers.

AppShell calls the hook at the original state-declaration site (hook

Step 3b of the #1043 plan. The Settings modal surface state (open flag,

AppShell calls the hook at the original state-declaration site (hook

subject: feat(cli): trigger /skill: completion mid-message (#1176)
body:
A `/skill:<name>` token that begins mid-message (after whitespace) now
surfaces skill completions, not only at line start. Typing a bare `/`
mid-message immediately offers all `/skill:xxx` completions (filtered by
the text after `/`); `/skill:<query>` refines by name. Selecting a mid-message
skill inserts `/skill:name ` and returns instead of submitting; line-start
`/skill:` keeps the existing select-to-invoke behavior.

Only `/skill:` is completed mid-message. Plain commands (`/compact`,
`/model`, ...) only execute at line start (`handleSlashCommand` checks
`parts[0]`), so completing them mid-message would insert dead text. `/skill:`
is a parseable invocation token and has value anywhere in the message.

Two halves, both repo-only (no pi-tui fork/patch):
- Trigger: MakaSkillHighlightEditor.handleInput calls pi-tui's
  tryTriggerAutocomplete (TS-private but runtime-public; pinned by a
  contract test) when a `/`-token begins mid-message on the first line.
- No-submit: the provider returns a slash-less prefix (just the text after
  `/`) for mid-message `/skill:` tokens, so pi-tui's select-confirm guard
  does not fall through to submit. Line-start keeps `/skill:query`.

Regressions caught by Codex review and fixed:
- A bare mid-message `/` with no skill match no longer falls through to the
  file provider: that path returns a `/`-prefixed prefix, which pi-tui's
  select-confirm guard auto-submits, sending the unfinished message.
- The printable-input trigger guard now detects whether super.handleInput
  actually changed the editor text, instead of re-deriving pi-tui's printable
  decoding. This covers Kitty CSI-u and xterm modifyOtherKeys terminals
  uniformly (the previous ESC-prefix guard skipped both), and drops a
  duplicated decoding definition.
- A bare mid-message `/` returns the original (non-lowercased) query as the
  replacement prefix; toLowerCase can change UTF-16 length (e.g. "İ" -> "i̇"),
  which would make applyCompletion over-delete the original text.

Closes #1100

subject: feat(cli): standalone first-run API-key onboarding in the TUI (#1177)
body:
* feat(cli): /setup wizard for API-key provider onboarding

End-to-end vertical slice for #1098: a TUI wizard that adds an API-key
connection without leaving the CLI.

- /setup lists API-key providers (listApiKeyOnboardableProviders); after
  picking one, the wizard collects the API key in the editor and persists
  the connection + secret via setupApiKeyConnection, then makes it default.
- setupApiKeyConnection: gate on api-key providers and a non-empty key,
  create the connection, store the secret, probe models non-blocking.
- MakaOnboardingSurface: the TUI collects provider + key; the host
  (runtime-bootstrap) owns the stores and runs the real setup, mirroring
  the skills surface pattern.
- FakeTerminal end-to-end tests cover the picker and the key→setup path;
  two slash-autocomplete tests updated for the new /setup command.

Out of scope (next slice): first-run auto-trigger — createMakaCliRuntimeContext
throws before the TUI starts when no connection exists, so /setup today adds a
connection once the CLI can launch; OAuth subscription loop, baseUrl prompt,
and in-wizard model selection are also deferred.

* fix(cli): run first-run onboarding when no connection exists

Fresh installs hit NO_REAL_CONNECTION:missing_default_connection and exited
with a "configure in Maka Desktop" message, so the standalone CLI could never
bootstrap itself — the very gap #1098 exists to close.

- cli.ts catches missing_default_connection, runs the in-TUI onboarding wizard
  (runFirstRunOnboarding) over a stub session driver, then retries
  createMakaCliRuntimeContext and launches the normal TUI.
- runMakaPiTui firstRun mode auto-opens the provider picker on launch and
  closes the TUI (onConfigured + beginClose) once a connection is configured,
  handing control back to the host for the retry.
- createFirstRunSessionDriver: a stub MakaSessionDriver whose chat/runtime
  methods are unreachable (the wizard only collects provider + key via the
  onboarding intercept); driver.runtime is unused by the TUI.

A connection confi…

The darwin glass overrides forced .appFrame transparent so native
vibrancy became the main rail's effective color (~white over a light
desktop), while the Settings page kept its opaque --agents-layout-bg
backplate (oklch 0.976) — the two sidebars visibly disagreed and the
main rail lost all contrast against the pure-white content card. The
backplate now paints the shared token on every platform; the rail keeps

subject: feat(cli): /recap and idle-return auto-recap — one-sentence session summary (#1182)
body:
* feat(cli): /recap and idle-return auto-recap — one-sentence session summary

Implements #1055. A tool-free generateText call over the session's own
projected history (never written back to it), mirroring the goal
evaluator's connection resolution. Manual /recap always shows; the
idle-return path fires on the first prompt after >=3min idle with >=3
main turns, at most once per main turn, suppressed when the raw output
exceeds 500 bytes, and discarded when a later prompt supersedes it
(synchronous prompt-seq epoch). Requests persist as session artifacts
best-effort. Front-trims the projected history to an 85%-of-context
budget, dropping trailing dangling tool placeholders first.

* fix(cli): address #1182 review — authoritative projection, locking, scoping, language

- Recap now projects through the authoritative RuntimeEvent seam
  (RuntimeReadModel -> applyRuntimeEventContextBudget with the backend's
  own policy capped at 85% - 4096 -> buildRuntimeEventModelReplayPlan ->
  replayPlanItemsToModelMessages), so tool calls keep full inputs and
  outputs; the lossy StoredMessage projection is deleted. Exports the two
  existing runtime functions from the barrel.
- In-flight lock is acquired synchronously before the first await and
  released in one finally covering every early return.
- Staleness is session-scoped: the session id is captured at entry and
  revalidated before any display (success or failure), and the auto-recap
  watermark is keyed by session id.
- Slash commands no longer consume the idle-return gap: the activity
  clock refreshes only for prompts that open a turn.
- The recap artifact persists the actual bounded request messages.
- The instruction requires the recap in the language of the user's most
  recent substantive message, with openings expressed semantically.

subject: feat(cli): redesign onboarding wizard around the input field (#1181)
body:
Redesign the TUI onboarding wizard so provider search, API-key entry, and
verification status all happen around a single input field, replacing the
old SelectList picker + armed-editor flow.

- OnboardingWizard: editor.onChange filters providers (rebuilds SelectList,
  which has no setItems); an internal search→key state machine;
  setVerifying/setResult runner hooks; a status line; attempt-token guarded
  setup; Ctrl+C cancels the whole wizard while Esc returns to search.
- Runner: showSetupWizard drives the overlay; submitPrompt is the single
  first-run choke point (Enter, Alt+Enter, and /exit all route correctly);
  submitWizardKey/closeWizard orchestrate setup, probe-error retry, slash
  routing, and first-run close.
- setupApiKeyConnection: setDefault runs only after a successful model
  probe so a cancelled first-run attempt can't trap the next launch.

subject: fix(skills): unbox the skills module — drop the outer card frame (#1185)
body:
The whole module (featured banner + tabs + installed list) was wrapped
in a second card (bg + hairline + radius + shadow) that the MCP page
never had; inner surfaces already carry their own borders, so the
wrapper read as box-in-box and its top edge crowded the module
subtitle. Plain layout container now, content flush with the header
like the MCP page. radius-converge entry dropped with the established
unboxing note.

body:
* feat(cli): bare quit/exit words, maka --resume, and an exit resume hint

Typing exactly 'quit' or 'exit' on its own line closes the TUI
gracefully — embedded mentions still send as prompts. /quit dispatches
as a hidden alias of /exit (new MakaSlashCommand.aliases field) rather
than a second menu entry. On exit the CLI prints 'Resume this session
with: maka --resume <session-id>' when a session exists, and the new
top-level --resume flag reopens that session in the TUI through the
same switchSession path as /session <id>, falling back to a fresh
session with a notice when the id is unknown or its cwd is gone.

* fix(cli): address #1183 review — resume preflight, strict args, mid-turn exit, ctx window

- --resume now preflights the stored session header before the runtime
  context resolves any connection, anchoring startup to the session's own
  connection/model/cwd (resolveTuiResumeBootstrap); an unknown id errors
  on stderr with exit 1 without launching the TUI.
- parseMakaCliArgs rejects an option-like or missing --resume value and
  any trailing arguments.
- The four exit spellings (quit/exit//quit//exit) are recognized before
  the turnRunning steering branch, so they close the TUI instead of being
  sent to the model mid-turn (isExitPrompt shared with the idle path).
- applySwitchResult resolves modelContextWindow from modelChoices by the
  switched-to connection slug + model, fixing the statusline ctx total
  after resuming or /session-switching across connections/models.

* fix(cli): address #1183 second review — preflight scope and stale ctx window

- resolveTuiResumeTarget now selects only the stored connection/model;
  cwd stays process.cwd() and a missing session silently falls back to
  the default bootstrap so the runner's switchSession failure path (the
  transcript notice + fresh session) remains the single owner of
  resume-failure handling.
- applySwitchResult clears modelContextWindow on a real target change
  when the switched-to model has no exact modelChoices match (curated-out
  his…

subject: feat(plans): 保持系统唤醒 — real powerSaveBlocker capability for scheduled tasks (#1207)
body:
* feat(desktop): keep-system-awake capability for scheduled tasks

Scheduled reminders run off an in-process timer that freezes when the
machine sleeps, so tasks silently never fire. Add a keepSystemAwake
setting that holds an Electron powerSaveBlocker to keep background
scheduled work running.

- core: add SystemSettings { keepSystemAwake } to AppSettings, mirroring
  notifications.runComplete end to end (default, merge, patch surface,
  fail-closed boolean coercion in normalizeSettings) + contract tests.
- main: keep-system-awake controller wraps powerSaveBlocker with
  'prevent-app-suspension' (system stays awake WITHOUT forcing the
  display on) — Electron-free + injected blocker so it unit-tests under
  node --test, with a double-start guard on the blocker id.
- wiring: apply on launch if enabled, start/stop the instant the toggle
  flips via applySettingsRuntimeEffects (rides the existing settings
  channel), and reconcile on external settings.json edits.

* feat(ui): 保持系统唤醒 capability row on the 定时任务 page

Replace the placeholder-honesty gap (designer audit P1-5) with the real
control: an informational row above the plan list — Info icon + 定时任务
仅在电脑保持唤醒时运行 copy, right-aligned 保持系统唤醒 label + switch.

- Status-color restraint: neutral passive surface (settingsNotice passive
  recipe: --foreground-5 / --muted-foreground, hairline border,
  radius-surface), NOT the reference's saturated blue banner. Replaces
  the old maka-plan-system-alert accent recipe.
- Switch reflects reality: optimistic local state initialized from the
  persisted snapshot, revert-on-error + Chinese toast, useMountedRef +
  pending-ref guard so a slow IPC write can't double-fire or set state
  after unmount.
- Fail-soft: the row hides entirely unless the host wires both the value
  and the setter (bridge present).
- Re-pin the panel async-action contract with the new toggle invariants.

- use-keep-system-awa…

subject: feat(storage): foreign session scanner + digest reader for Claude Code and Codex (#1057 part 1) (#1208)
body:
* feat(storage): foreign session scanner + digest reader for Claude Code and Codex (#1057 L1+L2)

Read-only library slice of #1057. Core gains foreign-session.ts — the
single untrusted-input gate (sanitize: NFC + C0/C1/bidi controls +
zero-width strip + code-point caps; digests redact secrets and carry
only user-authored text, assistant text, and tool file paths — never
tool outputs, system prompts, or thinking blocks) plus defensive
parsers for Claude transcript records and Codex thread rows / rollout
envelopes, and an escape-proof prompt envelope renderer.

Storage gains foreign-session-store.ts: a scanner over
~/.claude/projects (JSONL head/tail windows, sidechain filter, cwd
filter, title priority customTitle > aiTitle > summary > lastPrompt >
first user message) and ~/.codex (node:sqlite read-only with PRAGMA
column introspection, rollout-walk fallback), capped at 50 sessions /
30 days, plus a digest reader with a 2MB read ceiling. The store never
writes, scopes all reads under the two source roots, and
realpath-confines every transcript path — including re-confinement at
digest time so a post-scan symlink swap cannot read outside the root.
Per-source env kill switches: MAKA_IMPORT_CLAUDE_CODE / MAKA_IMPORT_CODEX.

Verified against real local stores: both sources detected, 50-cap
enforced, full scan in <500ms, cwd filter and digest extraction
correct. TUI picker integration and the handoff flow are PR2.

* fix(storage): harden foreign-session import — injection, silent-drop, and Codex-format fixes (#1057)

Consolidated fixes from a 16-agent adversarial review plus a behavioral
diff against xai-org/grok-build's foreign_sessions module.

Security (prompt-injection through the handoff envelope):
- every foreign field, including cwd and file paths, is now envelope-
  stripped AND JSON-stringified in renderForeignSessionDigestForPrompt;
  cwd previously bypassed both and could close the data block early.
- stripEnvelopeTags loops to a fixpoint so a reassembly payload
  (</foreign-<...>-digest>) cannot sur…

subject: feat(cli): resume Claude Code / Codex sessions from the TUI picker (#1057 part 2) (#1221)
body:
* feat(cli): resume Claude Code / Codex sessions from the TUI picker (#1057 part 2)

Selecting a foreign session in the /session picker distills it into a
handoff and continues it in a fresh Maka session.

- core: FOREIGN_SESSION_HANDOFF_INSTRUCTION + buildForeignSessionHandoffMessage
  compose the model-facing first-turn text — the instruction (frames the
  digest as untrusted reference DATA, demands working-tree verification
  before trusting it) followed by the render-gated digest envelope.
  foreignSessionHandoffDisplayText is the short transcript line.
- cli: the runtime context builds a createForeignSessionStore() and threads
  it to the TUI. showSessionList scans foreign sessions for the current cwd
  and appends them (keyed foreign:<source>:<id> to avoid id collisions,
  labeled 'resume from <tool>'). Selecting one reads the digest, opens a
  fresh session via the existing startNewSession + first-prompt path, and
  seeds the first turn — the handoff as sendText (model-facing), a short
  line as the display prompt. Not run inside runControl (runAgentTurn owns
  busy); a scan failure degrades to no rows.

No separate LLM call: the handoff IS the first turn, so the untrusted
digest flows through the normal turn wrapped in the envelope + instruction.
Integration test drives /session → select foreign → readDigest →
startNewSession → handoff turn and asserts the display/model text split.

* fix(cli): harden foreign-session import per review (#1057 part 2)

High-effort review of the resume path:

- isSyntheticClaudeUserText narrowed from '/^<[a-z]/' to an explicit
  allowlist of Claude Code's synthetic wrappers (command-*/local-command-*
  /bash-*). The broad regex dropped real prompts opening with a lowercase
  angle-bracket token (<button> …, <ref> …) from both the digest and the
  title, losing the actual task.
- importForeignSession now claims busy + an activity lease + disableSubmit
  SYNCHRONOUSLY before the async readDigest (the submitPreparedUserPrompt
  pattern), so a Goal auto-continuat…

subject: style: Biome format baseline + check-mode CI gate (outside apps/desktop) (#415) (#1122)
body:
* build(lint): re-enable Biome formatter with a check-mode CI gate (#415)

Follow-up to #1019, which introduced the linter but deliberately left the
formatter out. Restores the formatter config (2-space, 100-col, single
quotes, semicolons, trailing commas — matching dominant existing style),
adds `format` (write) / `format:check` scripts, and a `npm run
format:check` CI step in the typecheck job.

Formatter-scoped exclusions (all still fully linted):
- apps/desktop/** and packages/ui/**: the UI surface's source-contract
  tests read .ts/.tsx source (desktop's own and packages/ui's) and
  regex-match exact single-line shapes; a whole-tree reformat broke 145
  desktop-source assertions plus ~45 contracts introspecting packages/ui.
  UI-surface formatting lands as a follow-up once those contract tests
  are format-tolerant.
- Byte-sensitive provenance artifacts (SOURCE.json is SHA-256'd and
  size-checked by scripts/prepare-cua-driver.mjs) and bundled-tools.json.

The mechanical baseline reformat is the next commit; the check gate goes
green only with both included.

* style: apply Biome format baseline outside the UI surface (#415)

Regenerated on top of current main (the previous baseline commit was 90
commits stale and conflicting; a whole-repo mechanical reformat is
rebased by re-running the formatter, not by resolving 660 files of
textual conflicts). Same scope as before: biome.json excludes the
apps/desktop UI surface.

Two adjustments the reformat itself required: a @ts-expect-error in
proxy-test.ts moved onto the property line it suppresses (the reformat
split the fetch call, displacing the directive), and a second formatter
pass over two files where Biome's first pass is not idempotent.

* chore: add .git-blame-ignore-revs scaffold for the format baseline (#415)

* test(runtime): make RunTracePhase contract regex layout-tolerant (#415)

subject: fix(runtime): drop top-level anyOf from the Read tool provider schema (#1231)
body:
The Read tool built its provider-facing schema by spreading the strict
file-vs-ref Zod union and overlaying `type: "object"`, leaving the union's
top-level `anyOf` on the wire. Anthropic rejects that tool definition
before inference (regression from #1124).

Split the two contracts: the provider now sees a single top-level object
with path/offset/limit/ref all optional and their mutual exclusivity
stated in the descriptions, while the strict union stays the authoritative
runtime validator via `jsonSchema(..., { validate })`. The regression test
now asserts an object-rooted, union-free provider schema alongside the
existing strict validation cases (path/ref accepted; empty and mixed
rejected).

Fixes #1228.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

body:
Unwrap official AI SDK RetryError instances before classifying provider evidence, while keeping abort semantics and fail-closed behavior for malformed or spoofed wrappers.

Cover rate limits, provider outages, context overflow, aborts, empty wrappers, spoofed errors, and ModelAdapter projection.

subject: fix(mcp): render real brand marks for 钉钉 / 飞书 / macOS 应用 (#1236)
body:
The MCP catalog's last three text-mark tiles (钉/飞/⌘) now render official
library brand marks on the neutral data-logo plate, matching the simple-icons
tiles. Unifies the mark model behind a per-source viewBox so 24-viewBox and
896-viewBox sources coexist without rescaling.

- macOS 应用 → Apple: siApple from the existing simple-icons dep (hex 000000;
  the dark-plate luminance gate flips it to currentColor → white in dark).
- 钉钉 → DingTalk: new dep @ant-design/icons-svg (Ant Group's first-party lib;
  DingTalk is an Alibaba product). Deep-imports only the DingtalkOutlined asn
  AST (tree-shakes; +1.3KB to index, no separate chunk). Chose the OUTLINED
  bare-pigeon silhouette over DingtalkCircleFilled so it reads like the other
  bare-glyph tiles instead of a solid disc. Filled DingTalk blue #0089FF.
- 飞书 → Feishu: monochrome mark vendored byte-for-byte from ByteDance's own
  @douyinfe/semi-icons (MIT; Feishu is a ByteDance product) as path constants
  — mirrors provider-brand-marks' single-mark vendoring rather than adding a
  React-coupled 3k-file dep. Filled Feishu blue #3370FF.

Cleanup: drop the 钉/飞/⌘ text marks (mark is now optional); remove the dead
--brand-dingtalk / --brand-feishu tokens + their tinted-plate CSS (these tiles
now ride the neutral logo plate). Refresh the icon-governance allowlist
rationale to name all three mark sources.

Verified: typecheck, desktop suite (2729), ui suite (191), knip ×2,
check-dead-css, audit-alignment (module-mcp clean) all green; CDP light+dark
captures show all three real marks (Apple flips white in dark; DingTalk/Feishu
stay blue).

body:
* feat(cli): add searchable cross-connection /model picker

The cross-connection /model picker listed every ready model with no way
to narrow it, so finding one across many connections meant scrolling.
Issue #1098 (seam 2) asks for one bottom search field that matches model
id, provider label/type, and connection name/slug, while keeping the
existing cross-connection rebinding and typed /model <id> path.

Add a focused ModelSearchOverlay (one search field + a bounded
single-select list, mirroring the OnboardingWizard search phase) and
wire it into /model's cross-connection path. The list rebuilds in place
on every keystroke; Esc/Ctrl-C close without rebinding; an empty filter
shows an in-frame 没有匹配的模型 state. The models-only fallback for
minimal hosts/tests stays on the non-searchable PickerOverlay, and
typed /model <id> still sets the model directly without opening the
picker. No second picker system or shared abstraction is introduced.

Covered: live filtering by model id, the empty state, selecting a
filtered model rebinding across connections, Esc cancel leaving the
model unchanged, typed /model <id> still bypassing the picker, and the
overlay's bottom-anchored placement above the editor + status line.

Refs #1098

* test(cli): cover every /model search match criterion

The seam-2 /model search lists five match criteria (model id,
connection name, connection slug, provider type, provider label), but
the committed suite only positively exercised model id; a regression
deleting any other criterion's line in matchesModelChoice would have
shipped green (noted in Pi k3 review of #1239, P3).

Add one test with a three-provider fixture (openai / zai / google) and a
query per criterion, each isolating exactly one line: `zai` exercises
the providerType line alone (its label `Z.AI` is not a substring) and
`gemini` exercises the PROVIDER_DEFAULTS label line alone (its type
`google` is not), so deleting either line fails its assertion. Mutation-
checked: removing the providerType or providerLabel line turns …

subject: refactor(runtime): split ai-sdk-backend compaction orchestrator (#1249)
body:
* refactor(runtime): extract compaction cache load/write into AiSdkCompaction

Move the compact/synthesis-cache load and write family
(loadHistoryCompactBlocks, loadSynthesisCacheBlocks, writeSynthesisCacheBlocks,
writeHistoryCompactCheckpoint, writeHistoryCompactBlocks) and their exclusive
incrementRecord/mergeCountsInto helpers from AiSdkBackend into a new
AiSdkCompaction collaborator (packages/runtime/src/ai-sdk-compaction.ts).
AiSdkBackend instantiates the collaborator and delegates the call sites in
compactHistory, buildPriorMessages, and prepareContextBudgetPolicy to
this.compaction.*.

Turn-scoped priorRequestShape is no longer read inside the write methods; it is
threaded per call as requestShapeHashBefore, read at the stay-side call sites,
preserving the "previous turn's shape" semantics (cleanupAfterTurn still does
not clear it). The five methods change private -> public on the collaborator
because AiSdkBackend now calls them cross-class; the contract types stay in
ai-sdk-backend.ts (the collaborator type-imports AiSdkBackendInput).

Behavior-neutral pure move: runtime build + 2073 tests pass (0 fail, 7 skipped),
parity with the pre-split baseline. Part of #1084.

* refactor(runtime): move manual history-compaction family into AiSdkCompaction

Move compactHistory, buildManualHistoryCompactPolicy, hasHistoryCompactWriter,
and hasHistoryCompactCheckpointWriter from AiSdkBackend into AiSdkCompaction.
historyCompactAbortController now lives on the collaborator (it is
manual-compaction-operation-scoped); AiSdkBackend.stop delegates to a new
compaction.abortHistoryCompact() hook, so the stop-then-new-send-overlap
behavior is unchanged. compactHistory becomes a public one-line delegate on
AiSdkBackend (the AgentBackend interface method) that threads
this.priorRequestShape?.requestShapeHash into the collaborator.

hasHistoryCompactWriter / hasHistoryCompactCheckpointWriter stay public on
the collaborator because buildPriorMessages (which stays in AiSdkBackend) also
calls them; those two call sites now go throug…

subject: chore(settings): R7 connection-detail decomposition + byTool aggregation fix — campaign close (#1254)
body:
* fix(storage): aggregate usageStats byTool globally, not per-session

Settings → 使用统计 → 工具统计 repeated tool-name rows (multiple Bash
rows) because byTool was built via sessions.flatMap(toolStatsFromMessages),
producing one aggregate row per tool PER session. byProvider/byModel were
already global (aggregateBy over the flattened modelLogs), so only byTool
was affected.

Replace toolStatsFromMessages with aggregateToolStats(sessions, since):
tool_call.id ↔ tool_result.toolUseId matching stays session-scoped (ids
are only unique within a session) while calls/success/errors/durations
merge into a single row keyed by tool name. Rows sort by call count desc
(tool name tie-break) to match the byProvider/byModel convention and give
deterministic output. Row shape and the sole consumer (UsageToolsPanel)
are unchanged.

Regression test: two sessions each calling Bash (+ one Read) yield one
merged Bash row (calls 2, success 1, errors 1, avg duration 30).

* refactor(settings): extract connection-detail controller + enabled-model manager (arch R7)

provider-connection-detail.tsx was the renderer's densest state cluster
(983 lines, 12 useState in ConnectionDetailInner + a shared keyed action
guard + lifecycle gate). Split following the settings house pattern:

- use-connection-detail.ts (controller hook, ~520 lines): owns the whole
  mutually-exclusive action state machine (save / test / fetch-models /
  save-enabled-models / set-default / delete, all gated through one
  useKeyedActionGuard), the credential-presence probe, the prop-sync
  effects, every derived flag, and the pure snapshot/equality helpers +
  oauthLoginServiceFor. Returns a controller object; ZERO behavior change,
  every identifier and statement moved verbatim.
- provider-enabled-model-manager.tsx (~187 lines): the roving-tabindex
  model-list editor (owns query + activeRowId) — an independent cluster.
- provider-connection-detail.tsx (983 -> 373 lines): thin view that
  destructures the hook, plus ConnectionEndpointField / the OAuth …

subject: polish(settings): lift every surface to the 使用统计 bar — DataTable primitive, dense health/permission rows, dialect cleanup (#1274)
body:
* feat(ui): extract DataTable primitive from usage stats table

Promote the #1252 hairline table recipe out of usage-settings-page into a
shared DataTable primitive (@maka/ui) now that it has health + permission
consumers. The primitive owns the table markup: muted+medium scoped col
headers, first-cell-as-scoped-row-header, hairline row separators,
right-aligned tabular-nums numeric columns, and one grow column that absorbs
slack while the rest size to content on one line.

UsageStatsTable becomes a thin wrapper: it keeps the usage-local empty-tab
routing (EmptyState icon+copy) that a table primitive deliberately omits, and
delegates the non-empty branch to <DataTable>. Rendering is pixel-identical —
every utility class and the settingsUsageTable pin class are preserved.

Re-pin: the moved table markup is now guarded by packages/ui data-table.test
(renderToStaticMarkup); settings-usage-contract's stats-table assertions follow
the split — keep the wrapper's typed-column + EmptyState signature, assert
delegation to <DataTable>, and forbid a hand-rolled <table>.

* refactor(settings): kill the .settingsAboutPrivacy blue-header dialect

The uppercase brand-blue h3 + brand-deep tinted card was a fourth section-
header voice, used by 关于's privacy card AND (confusingly) 数据's 配置导入
导出 header — the reason 数据's export section wore a blue header.

Converge both onto the sanctioned language:
- 关于 privacy → SectionHeader (as=h3) + Alert variant=passive holding the
  privacy points (a quiet 'just so you know' callout; matches the passive
  Alert language already on the data page's backup notice).
- 数据 config → SectionHeader (as=h3, configHelp as subtitle) over the form
  controls, no card chrome (it's a form, not a callout).

Widen SectionHeader's `as` to accept h3 (a section under a page h2). Delete
the .settingsAboutPrivacy CSS block (incl. the green ::marker) for neutral
gap containers. Re-pin radius-converge (drop the stale selector) and
settings-app-info (name now scopes at the sec…

subject: test(runtime-host): connect before loser election to avoid idle-grace race (#1296)
body:
The 'elects one owner...' test started the winner with a 250ms idleGrace
and ran a full loser-candidate election before its one-shot
connectRuntimeHost. On a loaded CI runner that front-side window can
exceed the grace, so the winner's idle timer committed shutdown (removing
the registration, closing the listener) before connect landed, yielding
{ kind: 'unavailable' }.

Connect first instead: a resident handshake cancels the idle timer, so
the loser candidate's storage work can no longer race with idle drain.
Election outcome is unchanged because ownership is independent of client
residency, and connect-before-contention is already this file's pattern.

subject: fix(ui): drop the docked composer resting drop-shadow wash (band seam) (#1300)
body:
The docked, unfocused composer card carried a two-layer foreground-derived
drop-shadow (`0 2px 6px` + `0 10px 22px`) on top of its 1px ring. Because the
card is ~measure-wide and nearly fills the dock, that soft grey drop read as a
light-grey band with a boundary line beneath the composer — the "色差" the
maintainer flagged — and it showed only while the composer was NOT focused
(focus swaps in the accent ring), which is exactly the reported "sometimes".

CDP autopsy (turn-narrative fixture, light+dark, default / narrow-stacked /
focused / streaming / permission variants) confirmed there is no full-width
tinted footer layer: `.composer` outer is transparent and the card fill is
`--agents-content-area-bg`, identical to the panel, and the composer row is
always enclosed within the white `.maka-panel-detail` surface. The only grey
around the composer was this resting drop-shadow (pixel-sampled lum 249–254
below the card vs 255 content).

Converge the resting docked card onto the pure-white content surface: keep AT
MOST the card's own hairline ring, no wash. Dark mode already carries no
resting drop (its one-step tone lift is the only cue) and is unchanged.

Proof: band == content-above now equal in both themes — light
rgb(255,255,255)==rgb(255,255,255); dark rgb(31,31,33)==rgb(31,31,33).
Gates: desktop 2751/2751, ui 202/202, typecheck, check-dead-css,
box-shadow/no-gradient/composer-single-source/constant-footprint contracts,
audit-alignment (AUDIT_PORT_BASE=26300) all clean.
